This review synthesizes current successes, identifies persistent challenges, and outlines future research priorities-including edge computing, transfer learning, human-in-the-loop systems, and regulatory frameworks-to support the scalable and secure deployment of AI in renewable energy systems. By addressing these barriers, AI can play a pivotal role in accelerating the transition to a resilient and net-zero energy future.

title: Supply Chain Attacks in Cloud Native Web Applications
authors: Thoufeeq Ahamed, Akash Saminathan, Balasubramanian Prabhu Kavin
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Intelligence, Security, and Artificial Intelligence (IntelliSecAI)
abstract: One of the biggest cybersecurity risks in recent years has been supply chain attacks using cloud-native web applications. North Korea launched an attack in mid-2025 that used fictitious job interviews to release 67 malicious NPM packages, resulting in over 17,000 downloads and infecting developer computers with malware families like BeaverTail, XORIndex Loader, HexEval, and InvisibleFerret. Because the attack reveals untrusted third-party dependencies that have the potential to infect entire ecosystems, it highlights the vulnerability of contemporary CI/CD pipelines. Here, we present an AI-Augmented Dependency Trust Verification Framework that integrates three defense in-depth layers: sandboxed verification at runtime prior to being added in cloud-native build pipelines; static and dynamic AI-powered malware detection as the initial indication of suspicious code patterns; and cryptographic package signing and provenance veerification at the Supply-chain Levels for Software Artifacts (SLSA) and Sigstore levels. To combat social engineering attack vectors, the framework also includes zero-trust developer onboarding guidelines. With the suppression of unsigned dependencies, obfuscated payload detection, and enforcement against malicious action exe- cution, the study demonstrates that the multi-layered protection would have easily destroyed the North Korean NPM attack. The system offers a useful template for cloud-native applications' software supply chain security.

title: Thermal Analysis of TIG Welding: Experimental Study and Comparison with FE and FD Numerical Methods
authors: Abdel Halim Zitouni, Mouloud Aissani, Lotfi Beghdadi, Younes Benkheda
publish: 2025 International Conference on Recent Advances in Mathematics and Informatics (ICRAMI)
abstract: This work presents a thermal analysis of the TIG (Tungsten Inert Gas) welding process. In the experimental phase, 304L stainless steel sheets were welded and thermal cycles at various locations were recorded using thermocouples. The second phase involved developing a mathematical model to describe the heat flux generated by the TIG arc welding process without filler metal. Two numerical methods were used to implement this model: the first employed a Finite Difference (FD) approach using a custom FORTRAN program, while the second utilized the Finite Element (FE) method via COMSOL Multiphysics. The main objective was to compare the experimental data with the results from both numerical approaches to evaluate the accuracy, strengths, and limitations of each. The resulting heat distribution calculations enabled the identification of critical regions affected by the welding process, including the Heat-Affected Zone (HAZ) and the Melted Zone (MZ).

title: Forecasting Electrical Outages in Adrar, Algeria: Towards a Smart Energy Management System
authors: Samia Bentaieb, Driss Nehari, Ammar Neçaibia, Messaoud Hamouda
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy (AIESRE)
abstract: In this study, we present a predictive modeling framework for anticipating power outages using temporal, meteorological, and historical outage data. The proposed methodology gathers aggregate temporal and contextual data from multiple sources, and then engineers features based on time, binary indicators, and derived features. A Random Forest classifier is developed using a train-test partition and the model robustness is established through cross-validation. The model is designed to predict outages based on patterns in the energy consumption and environmental conditions. The performance of the model is evaluated using several metrics, it reached an accuracy of approximately 99 %, with precision, recall, and F1-scores all exceeding 98 %. The findings of this study provide evidence that supports the proposed approach for anticipating power outages to create smarter grid management and better reliability in power systems.

title: Designing a Coding Schema and Generating Sequence for Recompression Compressed Data Files
authors: Bekir Tevfik Akgün
publish: 2025 International Conference on Recent Advances in Mathematics and Informatics (ICRAMI)
abstract: Our compression suite includes 5 different compression programs for recompressing compressed files. Here is a hierarchical partitioning schema designed for converting an input data file into fixed-sized segments. The pages of a segment are compressed by executing our skip second word (SSW) compression algorithm in SSW programs, which are recursive, incremental, and iterative compression programs. There is an off-the-shelf compression program for non-SSW programs, which are heuristic and sequence compression programs. The SSW compression algorithm starts to find two identical words placed on the same page. The first word is kept as the original value and position in the output file, while the second word is skipped. There is an addressing issue of restoring these skipped words in their original locations during decompressing. An encoded addressing information (EAI), which contains the locations of both original and skipped words, must be included in the output file. The bit size of encoded values of locations for each selected page will be crucial for the compression performance. In this paper, a variable-length and mixed-type coding schema, which has flags, Huffman coding, binary coding, and combinadics, is designed. The non-SSW programs require deterministic selections of pages that will be recompressed. The heuristic program contains a heuristic algorithm for testing and selecting pages with encoded page locations that is a subset of the EAI coding schema. Whereas, the sequence program contains a constant number sequence for selecting pages. Therefore, there is no need to save page locations. In the previous work, this constant sequence was generated using random functions of the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{C}$</tex> standard library. For enabling the compilation of the source code using different compilers for different operating systems or rewriting it in different languages, this constant sequence must be saved. So that the same file can be obtained by decompression. Two portable generator functions are introduced in this paper. Here, we do not need to fulfill all requirements of the random number generation. We tested three number generators for the sequence program. As a result, the compression performance of the proposed sequence generator is better for selected examples, mostly.

title: Atomic Orbital Search with Convolutional Neural Network Based Cloud Assisted Centralized Prediction Model for Enterprise Financial Risks
authors: Pavan Nutalapati, Geol Gladson Battu, Rajeev Agarwal, Venkata Sai Nageen Kanikanti, Ashanand, Rahul Chauhan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Intelligence, Security, and Artificial Intelligence (IntelliSecAI)
abstract: With the rapid growth of financial technology and the enlargement of enterprise financial environment, financial risk prediction's effectiveness, timeliness and accuracy have become important factors to guarantee enterprise stability and sustainable growth. However, traditional financial risk assessment models tend to have the disadvantages of poor adaptability and inadequate accuracy in predicting financial risks using large-scale and dynamic financial data. In order to overcome these problems, a cloud-aided centralized financial risk prediction model based on convolutional neural network (CNN), optimized by atomic orbital search (AOS) algorithm, is proposed in this study. In this type of model, the enterprise's financial data is encrypted securely with an encryption algorithm and stored the data in the cloud environment to provide data confidentiality and integrity. The cloud hosted financial data is then run through the CNN to predict potential financial risks wherein the AOS algorithm is used to optimize the hyperparameters to enhance the learning proficiency and prediction performance of the CNN model. The annual reports of enterprises are used as the primary data source to validate the effectiveness of the proposed model. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed cloud-assisted AOS-CNN model achieves superior accuracy, recall, and F1 score compared to conventional financial risk prediction methods.

title: Behavioral Profiling–Driven Zero-Trust Security Framework for Smart City IoT Ecosystems
authors: Uman Ahmed Mohammed
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Intelligence, Security, and Artificial Intelligence (IntelliSecAI)
abstract: Over the past few years, the intensive growth of city IoT systems has triggered the prevalence of heterogeneous devices, thus creating complex and highly dynamic ecosystems that significantly increase the size of the potential attack surface. Traditional perimeter-based security concepts and passive, identity-based controls are insufficient to ensure such environments are protected against advanced adversarial approaches, hacked devices and abnormal post-authentication activities. Based on which, we offer a Behavioral Profiling-Driven Zero-Trust Architecture (BP-ZTA) that integrates machine-learning-assisted behavioral analytics with zero-trust concepts to provide a continuously adaptive and context-aware security to smart-city IoT networks. The framework uses multi-layer behavioral modelling at the edge, fog, and cloud layer, using anomaly detection, graph-based learning, and reinforcement learning to generate dynamic scores of device-trust. A policy engine then consumes these trust scores and coordinates real-time micro-segmentation of access-control decisions. It is designed to support both hybrid-cloud and multi-hyperscaler deployments, with the containerised microservices, infrastructure components prepared with AI, and federated learning pipelines. This architecture guarantees scale ability, privacy and availability. Empirical assessments show that the BP-ZTA framework significantly increases the accuracy of anomaly-detection and the false-positiveness rate as well as makes smart-city infrastructures more resilient without compromising latency and resource-effective use. The study provides an end-to-end security model consistent with the current enterprise architecture and customizable to a broad spectrum of applications of smart cities.

title: Cost-Effective Reliability Analysis of RE Rich Grid with BESS by Horizon-Optimal Technique
authors: Prahlad Mundotiya, Arun Kumar Nayak, Santosh Kumari, Nirmala Yadav, Chandra Prakash Naga, N.K. Gupta
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: The growing integration of renewable energy sources, particularly wind and photovoltaic systems, has transformed modern power system operations, introducing both environmental benefits and operational challenges due to their stochastic and weather-dependent nature. Accurate forecasting of renewable generation has thus become crucial for reliable and economical grid operation. This paper proposes a horizonoptimal linear programming framework that integrates probabilistic renewable forecasts directly into the battery energy storage system dispatch problem. Unlike deterministic or computationally intensive stochastic optimization methods, the proposed model incorporates multiple forecast scenarios within a rolling horizon, enabling adaptive, uncertainty-aware decision-making in real time. The approach jointly minimizes operating costs while maintaining system reliability and tractability. Simulation studies conducted on the IEEE 39-bus test system demonstrate that the proposed framework effectively balances economic performance and reliability under renewable uncertainty, outperforming traditional rule-based dispatch strategies. The results reveal that optimal BESS scheduling achieves up to a 13 % reduction in operational costs and a significant reduction in renewable curtailment compared to the no-storage scenario.

title: Smart Home Energy Management System Considering Battery Degradation Cost
authors: Vinay Kumar Yadav, Basant Kumar Sethi, Chandani P. Gor
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: Since, smart home energy management systems (SHEMS) are becoming more popular, the homeowner has the opportunity to maximize energy utilization and make use of renewable resources effectively. However, the increased adoption of SHEMS leads to frequent battery charging and discharging, that effect life of the battery significantly. To deal with this issue, effective scheduling strategies must be considered for battery degradation costs along with homeowner requirement. The major objective of this method is to coordinate the operation of smart home appliances, energy resources, and the battery itself to achieve an overall reduction in household energy expenditure. Apart from it, the approach simultaneously integrates a model for battery degradation. The study rigorously investigates the effects of strategic, efficient demand scheduling on battery longevity and overall energy expenditures.

title: Technoeconomic Analysis and Smart Scheduling of Neighbourhood EMS Considering Shading Effect of PV and Ageing Effect of ESS
authors: Anchal, Sandeep Kakran, Ratna Dahiya
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: With the global rise in the integration of solar photovoltaics (PV) with the grid, consideration of energy storage systems (ESS) becomes important for efficient utilization of energy for the prolonged sustainability. ESS may include battery for storing and providing the energy generated from distributed energy resources (DERs). Consideration of degradation cost and life cycle of battery becomes an important aspect while including ESS in the system. This article includes solar PV, battery energy storage systems (BESS) and electric vehicles (EV) as DERs in the presence of peer-to-peer (P2P) energy exchange across the prosumers within the residential community. The research work further explores the impact of shading on the energy scheduling of a neighbourhood energy management system (NEMS) and also evaluates the performance of the BESS considering various parameters during the life cycle of battery. Battery capacity fade due to calendar-ageing and cyclicageing based degradation is calculated to estimate the end-of-life of the battery. This paper also analyses the payback period of PV coupled with battery system. The formulated problem is solved using SBB solver of General Algebraic Modelling Systems (GAMS) to optimize the cost function under Time of Use (TOU) demand response scheme. After optimization, 0.59 % decrement is obtained in the amount of cost of energy consumed by the community along with 9.1 % reduction in the amount of energy generated by solar PV due to consideration of shading effect in the system.

title: Modulating Emotional Perception through Colour in Neurodivergent Individuals
authors: Ruoxi Yin, Peter Raynham, Jemima Unwin Teji
publish: 2025 IEEE Sustainable Smart Lighting World Conference &amp; Expo (LS25)
abstract: An increasing number of studies suggests that neurodivergent individuals may experience differences in emotion perception, with atypical visual processing proposed as a contributing factor. Despite colour is a salient component of visual input, its role in shaping emotion perception in neurodivergent populations remains underexplored. This pilot study employed a two-stage design in which 29 neurodivergent participants first selected a preferred colour and then rated the perceived intensity of facial emotional expressions presented under personalised chromatic and achromatic conditions. Results showed an emotion-specific effect: angry expressions were perceived as significantly less intense under personalised colour conditions than under achromatic presentations. These findings provide preliminary evidence that personalised colour context can selectively modulate perceived emotional intensity in neurodivergent observers, with potential implications for inclusive lighting and built-environment design.

title: HPC Controlled Algorithm for Solar PV Systems Driving Non Linear Load
authors: Nawaz Rahman, Sonali R Nandanwar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: This paper introduces an innovative approach toward enhancement in grid-interfaced three-phase solar photovoltaic (PV) systems, with advanced control strategies. The aspect of maintaining voltage stability, together with minimizing harmonic distortions and ensuring the efficient extraction of power in variable environmental circumstances, is becoming more critical with the increasing penetration of solar PV systems worldwide. Two systems are analyzed here: System I, which employs the conventional Perturb and Observe (P&O) algorithm for MPPT; and System II, which proposes the use of a hybrid P&O-Cuckoo Search Algorithm (HPC) for enhancing efficiency and robustness. The performance of the systems is evaluated using MATLAB/Simulink-based simulation under dynamic irradiance and non-linear loading conditions. System I suffer from frequent voltage oscillations and higher Total Harmonic Distortion (THD), which severely compromise power quality. By comparison, System II shows good voltage regulation and THD reduction, thus validating the appropriateness of the HPC approach. Some recent MPPT approaches are also reviewed and benchmarked, such as using Fuzzy Logic, Dynamic Group-Based Cooperation Optimization (DGBCO), Regression Models, and metaheuristic optimizations such as Firefly and Antlion algorithms. The findings support that the proposed hybrid method could efficiently address the partial shading and harmonic disturbance implementations. The results suggest accepting hybrid optimization techniques in the areas of future smart grids, particularly for areas characterized by variable solar irradiance and non-linear load characteristics.

title: Core Temperature Estimation in Lithium-Ion Batteries Using EKF and Offline-Based Thermal Parameters
authors: Keyur K. Patel, Mahmadasraf A. Mulla, Sanjay Tolani
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: In Battery Management Systems (BMS), accurate estimation of the core temperature of Lithium-Ion Batteries (LIBs) is crucial for ensuring operational safety, enhancing performance, and preventing thermal runaway. Direct measurement of core temperature is impractical due to sensor placement limitations and reliability concerns. Therefore, model-based estimation techniques are employed. This study proposes a method that integrates an Extended Kalman Filter (EKF) with a twostate lumped thermal model to estimate core temperature. The Bernardi model is used to characterize heat generation, accounting for electrochemical over potential and entropy changes. This method uses offline parameter calculations based on cell material properties or datasheet values for parameter identification rather than Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO). This greatly lessens the need for lengthy experiments and computation time. The corresponding root mean square errors (RMSE) between estimated and measured core and surface temperatures are approximately 0.052° C and 0.155° C, respectively.

title: Deep Learning Based Forecasting Model for Solar PV Power Generation Prediction in Highly Uncertain Environments
authors: Anandhi Govindarajan, Arjun Deo, Sonali R. Nandanwar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: The Solar energy serves is an efficient alternate to conventional energy sources. Solar energy exhibits a high level of uncertainty due to weather conditions. This paper proposes a deep learning-based model aimed at forecasting solar energy output. The proposed model optimizes the training time and reduces the operational costs in a high-dimensional uncertain environment. Experimental results demonstrate that the model achieves consistent training and validation accuracies of 0.94 and 0.92, respectively, from epoch 7 onward. These findings indicate that 7 epochs can be considered an optimal point for balancing the accuracy and computational efficiency and to reduce the operational cost.

title: Innovative Iot Applications for Monitoring Undergrown Cows in Large-Scale Smart Farms
authors: Devendra Kumar Misra, Jagriti Pal, K. M. Nilu Gupta, Sapana Sidarth
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: The growth in demand for effective livestock management in large-scale farming environments has led to increased adoption of IoT technologies, as this technology can be used to develop and implement new IoT applications that can monitor underdeveloped or “under-grown” cows in smart farms. The IoT application developed includes integrating different types of sensors such as RFID tags, accelerometers, weight sensors, and temperature sensors to continually collect data from cow health, cow activity, and cow growth. The collected data is then transmitted through low-power wide-area networks (LPWAN) to a cloud computing platform where it can be analyzed in real time using machine learning-based growth tracking, cow health issue detection, and cow undergrowth condition prediction. The application also offers the farm manager a user-friendly dashboard to view cow growth and cow health status and to receive notifications if there are any abnormalities detected in the cow data so that the farm manager can make timely intervention. Field testing demonstrated the effectiveness and scalability of the system in large-scale farming environments.

title: A Comparative Study of GA, PSO, and SA Algorithms for Improving UAV Endurance through Metaheuristic Optimization
authors: B. Ravi Chandra, Y Sravanthi, K Mounika, P Suneetha, Ajay Roy
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: The endurance of Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) continues to be a significant constraint in extended missions that encompass surveillance, transportation, and environmental monitoring. This study introduces an intelligent UAV endurance optimization approach that combines a windsensitive, airspeed-dependent energy model with adaptive charging control over several charging pads. The suggested model realistically links aerodynamic impacts to power use by linking propulsion energy to airspeed instead of ground speed. Three metaheuristic algorithms-Genetic Algorithm (GA), Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO), and Simulated Annealing (SA)-were executed under uniform conditions, utilizing a simulated wind vector of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$[8.0,-3.0] \mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}$</tex>. The simulation results demonstrated that the model was consistent because all three methods attained the same optimum endurance of 20.62 minutes. The most effective in terms of computation was PSO, which took 0.56 seconds to identify the global optimum, followed by SA at 1.79 seconds and GA at 2.98 seconds. According to these findings, PSO is superior to GA and SA in terms of realtime mission adaptation.

title: Deep Learning Based Late Blight Disease Detection in Potato Using UAV
authors: Taimoor Qamar, Abdullah Bin Masood, Syed Ali Ishaq, Muhammad Talal Saeed, Hafiz Zia Ur Rahman
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Innovations in Information and Communication Technologies (IICT)
abstract: This paper presents an advanced drone-based system for real-time detection of late blight disease in potato crops, leveraging high-resolution imagery and deep learning. A fine-tuned ResNet50 model was trained on a curated dataset of 1,703 potato leaf images, captured using both mobile phones and a drone-mounted ESP32-CAM under real-world field conditions. Rigorous testing demonstrated that the system achieves reliable disease identification with an F1 score of 77.80 % and a Recall of 70.51 %, providing advantages over manual inspection. By enabling scalable, automated monitoring, this approach addresses key limitations of manual scouting, offering a practical solution to enhance crop yield and reduce disease-related losses in precision agriculture.

title: A New Method for Measuring the Zero Position Angle of Motor Resolvers
authors: Zhijun Liu, Yu Mo, Ruixue Wang, Haihong Pan, Zhenwei Liao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechatronics, Automation and Intelligent Control (MAIC)
abstract: To address the long calibration cycle and limited production efficiency of the traditional self-learning method for resolver zero position angle measurement in motor manufacturing, this study proposes a rapid measurement method based on an angular marker instrument. The method determines the resolver zero position angle by acquiring the motor's back electromotive force (back-EMF) and the resolver's sine and cosine signals, and calculating their phase difference—eliminating the need for the motor self-learning process. Experimental results show that the proposed method achieves an average deviation of only 0.008 rad compared with the traditional self-learning method, with a maximum difference within <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 0.035$</tex> rad and a repeatability error below <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 0.004$</tex> rad. Torque performance tests further indicate that when the zero position angle deviation is controlled within <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 1^{\circ}$</tex>, the torque output error remains within <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 3 \%$</tex>, meeting automotive-grade precision requirements. In addition, the new method reduces the measurement cycle from 40 s to under 5 s, improving efficiency by approximately eightfold. Overall, the proposed approach maintains high measurement accuracy while significantly enhancing production efficiency, providing an effective and reliable solution for automated, electronics-centric mass end-of-line (EOL) calibration of motors.

title: Interpretable Stacking Ensemble Framework for Adaptive PID Control Optimization in Direct Torque Control Systems
authors: Sitong Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechatronics, Automation and Intelligent Control (MAIC)
abstract: This paper proposes an interpretable ensemble learning framework for optimizing PID controllers in direct torque control (DTC) systems of induction motors. A stacking architecture combining seven heterogeneous base learners achieves 96 % test accuracy in predicting optimization success across 700 operational scenarios. SHAP dependency analysis with polynomial threshold fitting identifies critical control regimes: speed overshoot <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<4.5$</tex> RPM, ISE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\in[0.010,0.022]$</tex>, torque ripple 0.2 0.4 Nm, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Kd}<1.25$</tex>. Waterfall plots reveal transient response metrics contribute 68 % to prediction, guiding data-driven parameter tuning. The framework reduces ISE by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 2 \%}$</tex> compared to manual tuning while maintaining computational efficiency for real-time implementation.

title: Autonomous Navigation for Multiple Wheeled Robots Based on Lightweight LiDAR Mapping and Dynamic Priority
authors: Wenlei Fan, Qiyu Zhang, Zhenqi Zheng, You Li
publish: 2025 International Ubiquitous Positioning, Indoor Navigation and Location-Based Services Conference (UPINLBS)
abstract: Multi-robot collaborative planning and autonomous navigation are widely applied in unmanned mapping, warehouse logistics, and emergency rescue. However, they face challenges in safety and efficiency within complex environments, particularly in obstacle-dense and highly dynamic scenarios, where issues such as trajectory oscillation, heavy communication loads, and insufficient coordination often arise. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a distributed planning framework based on dynamic priority. The method builds upon lightweight mapping and single-robot planning. First, to overcome the limitations of 2D SLAM in capturing vertical information, a novel 2D occupancy grid map construction method based on real-time 3D point cloud projection is proposed, which reduces computational load while enhancing environmental representation completeness. Second, feasible trajectory generation for individual robots is achieved through integrating Dijkstra global search with Timed Elastic Band (TEB) local optimization. On this basis, a dynamic priority mechanism is introduced at the multi-robot level, which considers conflict intensity, environmental congestion, and remaining task distance, and employs lightweight UDP communication for priority status sharing, minimizing data throughput and supporting responsive coordination. Individual robots then dynamically adjust their local TEB planning based on this priority information, enabling differentiated obstacle avoidance strategies and maintaining collaborative order. Experimental results show that the proposed lightweight mapping method outperforms representative approaches such as Gmapping, Karto, and Cartographer in terms of obstacle completeness and geometric fidelity, providing a more reliable environmental representation for multi-robot planning. Compared with baseline methods without priority mechanisms, the simulation results demonstrate that the proposed dynamic priority approach reduces average path length by approximately 15.1% and task completion time by about 12.9%. This effectively mitigates deadlocks and detours in scenarios like head-on encounters and grid-like intersections, thereby significantly lowering collision risks and improving overall efficiency.

title: RLF-VLN: A Training-Free Vision-Language Navigation System Powered by Large Language Models and Robust Semantic Mapping
authors: Ruilin Wang, Guiyong Zheng, Mingjie Zhang, Juepeng Zheng
publish: 2025 International Ubiquitous Positioning, Indoor Navigation and Location-Based Services Conference (UPINLBS)
abstract: Vision-Language Navigation aims to enable robots to follow natural language instructions using visual perception. Despite recent progress, most methods rely on environment-specific training and cannot be generalized to unknown scenes or novel instructions. In this work, we present RLF-VLN, a robust training-free Vision-Language Navigation system that eliminates the need for task-specific training and enables robots to directly leverage the reasoning and perception capabilities of large language models. Our approach leverages large language models to decompose complex instructions into structured sub-tasks and reason about spatial-semantic constraints that link language instruction with geometric feasibility. A multi-layer Value Map then fuses visual observations with these constraints to guide navigation decisions, while a robust semantic mapping module enhances landmark recognition through multi-frame fusion and contextual consistency. Experiments demonstrate the navigation capabilities in different environments, highlighting the potential of large model reasoning for scalable and training-free embodied navigation.

title: A Portable, CNN-Driven Health Monitoring System for Real-Time Vital Sign Analysis and Offline Anomaly Detection
authors: Muhammad Talal Khan, Muhammad Afrial, Muhammad Rafiq, Numan Asim, Amir Shahzad, Malaika Yasrab
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Innovations in Information and Communication Technologies (IICT)
abstract: Worldwide, health issues such as cardiovascular diseases, respiratory illnesses, and lifestyle-related conditions are increasing rapidly. Monitoring vital signs is therefore critical for early diagnosis and timely intervention. However, many existing healthcare monitoring systems are limited by low accuracy, high cost, a reliance on constant internet connectivity, and limited functionality, which restricts their adoption, particularly in low-resource environments. To address these challenges, this study presents a portable, cost-effective, and accurate health monitoring system driven by a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN). The device monitors heart rate, electrocardiogram (ECG), internal/external body temperature, and blood oxygen levels (SpO2) in real-time. The system integrates MAX30100 (heart rate/SpO2), DS18B20 (temperature), and AD8232 (ECG) sensors with a Raspberry Pi Pico W microcontroller for efficient data acquisition. Data is transmitted to the ThingSpeak IoT platform for remote monitoring and to a local SD card for secure storage, ensuring data continuity without internet. A key innovation is the integration of a pre-trained CNN model for electrocardiogram (ECG) on the Raspberry Pi, which provides offline, instant analysis of health data. This model categorizes the user's condition and provides immediate notifications for abnormal readings, including advice on when to seek medical attention. When validated against a clinical-grade device, the system demonstrated over 97 % accuracy in its real-time, on-device predictions. The housing, designed in SOLIDWORKS and 3D-printed for durability, makes the system easy to use and budget-friendly. With its immediate response, timely anomaly detection, and real-time alerts, this system is an ideal solution for inclusive and robust personal and clinical health monitoring.

title: A Hybrid Machine Learning and Deep Learning Framework for Early Detection of Depression from Social Media Text
authors: Abhay Bairagi, Anu Sayal, Saurabh Singh, Avisha Gupta, Indu Bala, Madhuri Gokhale, Dinker Dubey
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical, Communication, and Computing Technologies (iCONECCT)
abstract: The increasing importance of early depression identification using social media text is one of the main uses of artificial intelligence in digital mental health. However, existing Deep Learning models frequently suffer from overfitting, interpretability issues, and a lack of emotional context, particularly when affective cues like emojis are removed. This study introduces a hybrid lexical-contextual ensemble that combines DistilBERT and TF-IDF + Logistic Regression to capture both lexical and semantic data. By using a weighted integration approach to balance the trade-off between bias and variance, the ensemble minimizes false negatives by almost half when compared to individual models, achieving an F1 score of 0.866 and a ROC-AUC of 0.9385. In order to preserve affectrich information typically lost with standard text normalization, an emotionally intelligent preprocessing pipeline is also provided, which distinguishes emojis from their semantic equivalents. The system further emphasizes computational efficiency by using DistilBERT, which requires only forty percent of BERT's parameters, which is suitable for lowresource settings. Empirical results from the Kaggle Twitter Depression Dataset prove that the introduced framework surpasses current single-model baselines that are interpretable, lightweight, and computationally efficient methods of early depression identification. However, current transformer-based models have issues with interpretability and efficiency. This study investigates this gap with a hybrid lexical-contextual ensemble framework that balances accuracy, explainability, and computational cost.

title: Driver Braking Intensity Prediction Based on TCNFormer with Surface Electromyography
authors: Yan Liu, Xiaoqing Xu, Yu Tang, Qian Cheng, Feiyang Liu, Lin Li, Jie Ma, Kongming Jiang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechatronics, Automation and Intelligent Control (MAIC)
abstract: Accurate prediction of driver braking intensity (DBI) is essential for enabling advanced driving assistance systems (ADAS) to detect potential human errors, i.e., insufficient braking intensity, especially under hazardous conditions. However, existing research on DBI prediction predominantly focuses on classifying braking behavior into emergency versus normal events, ignoring the inherent uncertainty of DBI during the continuous modulation of braking intensity. To address this limitation, DBI is depicted by the brake pedal force, as well as a lightweight Transformer with temporal convolutional network (TCNFormer) is proposed for DBI prediction. Surface electromyography (sEMG) signals from driver lower leg are collected and employed as the physiological basis for DBI prediction. Considering the dotproduct attention in canonical Transformer lacks the ability of precepting local context, a temporal convolutional network (TCN) is introduced into the embedding module to enhance its local awareness. Experiments show that TCNFormer can predict DBI 200 ms in advance with (2.19 N) and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{2}(0.89)$</tex>, while achieving the inference latency of 0.015 s on NVIDIA Jetson TX2 platform, indicating suitability for deployment on in-vehicle edge computing units. Comparative evaluations further confirm that TCNFormer surpasses traditional deep learning baselines in both accuracy and real-time performance. These findings underscore the promise of our proposed DBI prediction framework for integration into future human-centric ADAS.

title: PD Sliding Mode Control of the Fast Steering Mirror
authors: Lidong Guo, Lixin Yang, Nan He, Liang Xu
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: Aiming at the control challenges of the Fast Steering Mirror (FSM) system, such as nonlinearity and external disturbances, a PD sliding mode control method is proposed. This method is compared with traditional PID control and traditional sliding mode control through simulation. Simulation results demonstrate that, in contrast to PID control and sliding mode control, the PD sliding mode control reduces the rise time by 87.98% and 66.67% respectively, with no overshoot, thus showing significant advantages in dynamic response. Meanwhile, tracking test results indicate that it improves accuracy by 94.23% and 63.87% relative to PID control and sliding mode control, which greatly enhances steady-state performance. This study confirms that PD sliding mode control can effectively address the control challenges of the FSM system and provide reliable technical support for its application in the field of high-precision optics.

title: AgriShield: A Hybrid NLP and Machine Learning Framework for Plant Disease Prediction
authors: Ritik Saini, Vikas Maurya, Shubham Singh, Tarun Shukla
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: Plant diseases continue to be a significant threat to the world's food security system, and crops are lost yearly. Conventional diagnosis approaches require professional intervention and are not always available to smallholder farmers in developing countries. The paper presents AgriShield, a unified system using Natural Language Processing (NLP) and Machine Learning (ML) to predict plant diseases. The farmers can enter textual queries describing the symptoms or submit images of the crops, and AgriShield guesses at the most likely disease and preventive and remedial actions. The system uses transformer-based NLP models to extract symptoms and ensemble classifiers, utilising textual and visual input. Some of the experimental outputs of AgriShield on benchmark datasets indicate that AgriShield has an accuracy of 90 %, which is better than textonly and image-only systems.

title: Smart Energy Communities: Business Models and P2P Trading for Sustainable Power
authors: Muhammad Waseem, Khizer Aftab, Muhammad Waleed Asghar, Waqas Asghar, Amy Fahy, Fabiano Pallonetto
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Innovations in Information and Communication Technologies (IICT)
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive analysis of various off-grid and grid-connected hybrid renewable energy systems (HRES) to support the development of smart energy communities in the municipal committee (MC) Burewala, district Vehari, Pakistan. The study aims to improve residential electrification through cost-effective and sustainable power solutions, while enabling future integration of peer-to-peer (P2P) energy trading mechanisms within community-based energy networks. Centering on techno-economic performance, the Hybrid Optimization Model for Electric Renewable (HOMER) Pro software is employed to design, simulate, and optimize multiple HRES configurations. Sensitivity parameters including nominal discount rate, inflation rate, and annual capacity shortage are examined to refine the net present cost (NPC) and levelized cost of electricity (LCOE). Results indicate that the grid-connected configuration comprising solar photovoltaic (PV), diesel generators (DG), and battery storage yields the highest cost efficiency, with an NPC of 1,677,396 USD and an LCOE of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.02519 \text{USD} / \text{kWh}$</tex>. To evaluate the robustness of this solution, a sensitivity analysis is performed to verify its proximity to the global minimum. Finally, the study discusses key social, technical, and economic barriers that may affect large-scale deployment of smart energy communities and P2P trading frameworks, and offers recommendations to facilitate their successful implementation in sustainable energy systems.

title: Inventory Demand Forecasting Using Cat Boost Regression: A Machine Learning Method
authors: Elluru Bhavana, A. Vyshnavi, Sugandha Saxena, B.S. Likitha Sreeja, C. Druthi
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Sales forecasting plays a important role in the part of retail management and decision making. This research uses past operations and data. The dataset contain numerical and categorical attributions that shows different operations. The workflow involves the preprocessing, like handling missing values, removing duplications, and conducting EDA to find patterns and collaborations. Ww train regression algorithms like Linear regression, Lasso, Ridge and XGBoost regressor to identify most efficient model. We use model performance using Mean Absolute Error(MAE). Experimental results show ensemble based models like XGBoost, outperform traditional regression methods: they give lower prediction errors and better generalization. This work implemented machine learning techniques gives a useful framework for modeling sales dynamics and significantly improve inventory management, demand planning and business decision support in the retail sector.

title: A Novel Deep Learning Framework for High-Accuracy Brain Tumor Diagnosis in Medical Imaging
authors: Sachin Chawla, Deepa Gupta, Sanjeev Kumar Pippal
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Brain Tumors (BT) are abnormal clusters of neuronal cells that spread through all brain areas, and their presence can disrupt essential brain operations. Accurate assessment of BT is crucial because it enables timely treatment of this medical condition. Early BT assessment relies on established testing methods, which fail to deliver precise results because they depend on the abilities of the radiologist and the actual patient situation, and they encounter difficulties when assessing fragile tumor characteristics. The research developed a powerful Deep Learning (DL) framework that improves BT detection and classification from Magnetic Resonance Imaging scans. The proposed framework included a complete preprocessing system, which used brain stripping, bias field correction, z-score normalization, histogram equalization and data augmentation to achieve uniform image quality throughout all procedures. The attention net architecture performed tumor segmentation, while deep feature extraction was managed through a pre-accomplished efficient net model. The ensemble system employed Vision Transformer (ViT), XGBoost, and Support Vector Machine (SVM) models, which achieved optimal performance through hyperparameter tuning based on Bayesian Optimization. The framework achieved 99.85% accuracy during testing with the BraTS dataset, which surpassed multiple existing top methods.

title: SCOUT: An AI-Powered Vision-Based Fall Detection System for Elderly Safety
authors: Satyam Singhal, Ujjwal Dwivedi, Rohit Verma, Laxman Singh, Rekha Kashyap
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: Falling represents one of the largest public health threats to the elderly, and falls have become a major issue for the world's healthcare systems. This research project introduces SCOUT, a home-based, continuous, computer-vision based fall detection system which uses a YOLOv8n deep learning model for the purpose of privacy preserving monitoring at home. The SCOUT system integrates a lightweight object detector with temporal heuristics, and an optional pose verification module, to provide both fast and accurate fall alerts, while providing strong user privacy protection by locally computing the object detector results and minimizing transmitted data. Because of its design for edge-based deployments, the SCOUT system also has scalable, affordable, and reliable operations in all resource limited environments. A publically available dataset was curated and expanded for training the SCOUT model, specifically for use in detecting falls in older adults. These experimental results show that SCOUT achieved the desired performance metrics of detection accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=93 \%$</tex>, precision <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.94$</tex>, recall <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.945$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex> score <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=\mathbf{0. 9 5}$</tex>, while demonstrating robustness across various lighting, occlusion, and camera angle challenges. The results shown above clearly support that SCOUT is capable of adapting to different use cases, and therefore applicable to Smart Home and Elderly Care Monitoring applications.

title: A Review on FPGA Based Real Time Sobel Edge Detection for Medical Imaging Using PYNQ-Z2
authors: A. Manonmani, U. DhanushKumar, B. Kelvin Jacob
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical, Communication, and Computing Technologies (iCONECCT)
abstract: The use of edge detection to identify diagnostic features in an X-ray is a critical initial processing step in medical imaging, increasing the number of diagnostically meaningful objects, including bone fractures in X-ray images. Previous literature has shown the usefulness of FPGA-based accelerators in medical imaging applications, such as Sobel, Canny edge detection, because they are inherently parallel and have low latency relative to a CPU or GPU-based implementation. Based on these methods, this paper outlines a hardware implementation of a Sobel based edge detector with pixel enhancement on the PYNQ-Z2 FPGA using Verilog HDL. The given design takes X- ray data in grayscale format and provides the refined results to a display device. The FPGA implementation is resource efficient and provides real-time performance comparatively to conventional software-based techniques and therefore is a cost effective solution to portable diagnostic system development. This paper will show that reconfigurable hardware can be used to support medical imaging applications by offering preprocessing of high speed and low power that can be used in point of care healthcare facilities.

title: Reinforcement Learning-Based Dual-Arm Push-and-Grasp Coordination
authors: Shuai Xie, Xiaohua Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechatronics, Automation and Intelligent Control (MAIC)
abstract: This paper presents a dual-arm grasping framework based on pixel-wise deep reinforcement learning. Building upon the idea of learning manipulation behaviors directly from visual observations, we extend the action representation to support coordinated grasping actions executed by two robotic manipulators. The system models grasping as a Markov Decision Process and employs a fully convolutional Q-network that maps RGB-D heightmaps to dense pixel-level action-value predictions. At each step, the optimal grasp pose and arm assignment are selected by maximizing the learned Q-function. The policy is trained entirely through self-supervised interaction, where grasp outcomes provide sparse binary rewards without human labeling. We evaluate the proposed framework on a dual-arm robotic platform using randomized tabletop scenes. Experimental results, including grasp success rates and learning curves, demonstrate that the policy progressively improves through experience and achieves reliable grasping performance in cluttered environments. These findings indicate the effectiveness of pixel-wise Q-learning for dual-arm grasping and highlight its potential for scalable, autonomous manipulation learning.

title: Development of Digital Twin Models of Transport Facilities Taking into Account Their Life Cycle
authors: Anatoly A. Rogov, Andrey S. Shinkaruk, Natalia A. Telyatnikova, Ivan A. Vishnyakov, Elena V. Mitryukhina, Alina A. Olenich
publish: 2025 International Conference on Quality Management, Transport and Information Security, Information Technologies (QM&amp;TIS&amp;IT)
abstract: The article is devoted to the problem of ensuring the efficiency of cargo transportation logistics management based on information technologies. It is proposed to use digital twins as an important tool for monitoring and managing key transport objects using rolling stock as an example. The life cycle of a transport object and the life cycle of an intelligent software product are analyzed. The cyclic model of development and implementation of a software product is studied, which requires the use of change management and configuration management methods over a long period of supporting the operation of a transport object. The configuration of an information system containing digital twins and their management is proposed. An example of the operation of a refrigerated container as a complex cargo transportation object requiring monitoring and optimal online management in the process of providing a transport service is considered. Proposals are given for the development of digital twins of transport objects in order to improve the efficiency of logistics activities of transport companies.

title: TBCFormerNet: A Multi-Scale Dilated and Spatially Reorganized Network for Tuberculosis Cough Classification
authors: Huichu Xiong, Dandan Yan, Shihong Che, Baiying Li, Qianqian Liu, Xiaomin Lou
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: Tuberculosis remains one of the leading infectious causes of death worldwide, posing a persistent threat to public health. Early and accurate diagnosis is crucial for controlling transmission and improving patient outcomes. Existing screening methods such as the Tuberculin Skin Test (TST), Interferon-Gamma Release Assay (IGRA), and Chest X-ray (CXR) have limitations in cost, operational convenience, or accuracy. This study proposes TBCFormerNet, a deep learning model based on cough sound analysis, to distinguish tuberculosis patients from non-tuberculosis subjects. The model converts cough audio into Mel spectrograms, integrates multi-scale features through a Multi-scale Dilated Fusion Attention (MDFA) module, and incorporates a Global Grouped Pyramid Attention (GGPA) mechanism to enhance global modeling capability. Validated on a dataset containing 3000 cough sound segments from 651 subjects, TBCFormerNet achieved an accuracy of 94.95% and an F1-score of 94.96%. This solution offers the advantages of low cost and ease of operation, making it particularly suitable for resource-limited healthcare settings and providing a scalable new approach for tuberculosis screening.

title: Review of Key Technologies for High-Efficiency Magnetic Coupling Resonance Wireless Power Transfer System
authors: Zhongxin Xu, Liangdeng Hu, Qingsong Wang
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: Wireless Power Transfer (WPT) technology, as a key technology overcoming the constraints of wired power transmission, exhibits significant application potential in various scenarios such as mobile devices and electric vehicles. Among them, Magnetic Coupling Resonance Wireless Power Transfer (MCRWPT) has become a hotspot in research due to its distinct advantages. This paper systematically reviews the research progress of MCR-WPT technology in terms of high-efficiency and large-capacity power transmission, focusing on analysis from three core dimensions: coil parameter and structural optimization, compensation network design, and frequency control and optimization. Specifically, regarding coil design, it discusses the influence of coil size, material, and structure on the coupling coefficient and transmission efficiency; in the aspect of compensation networks, it compares the topological characteristics and application scenarios of low-order and highorder compensation networks; as for frequency control, it expounds the role of three strategies-dynamic tuning, frequency tracking, and coil reconstruction - in addressing frequency mismatch and splitting problems. Finally, it is pointed out that the problems of magnetic flux leakage loss and reactive power loss caused by the loose coupling of magnetic components remain key bottlenecks limiting system performance, providing guidance for future research directions.

title: REFINE-IoT: A Multi-Task Machine Learning Framework for Reliable and Energy-Aware IoT Network Optimization
authors: Naween Kumar, Chhotelal Kumar, Ankit Dubey, Rohan Dabas
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Multimedia, Signal Processing and Communication Technologies (IMPACT)
abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) is a network of interconnected devices, which are featured by such characteristic features as energy efficiency and reliability of the data. The article presents REFINE-IoT, a multi-task machine learning model that will help to overcome these problems. The framework includes three activities, reliability filtering, which is assisted by classification models to forecast the efficiency of optimization strategies, energy-aware scheduling, which is assisted by regression models to forecast residual energy, and opportunistic fusion, which is assisted by an unsupervised autoencoder, to identify redundancy and anomalies in information. The success of the proposed strategy is evidenced by the results of the experiments. XGBoost has the best accuracy (91 percent) and F1-score (0.69), and this could be relied upon as a filter. Random Forest model was found to be more suitable in the energy sensitive scheduling with the mean absolute error rate of 0.104. The opportunistic fusion autoencoder detected an anomaly in the data and the mean reconstruction error was low (0.839). These results confirm that REFINE-IoT is an all-inclusive solution to make the IoT networks more efficient, reliable, and valuable.

title: Techno-Economic Evaluation of Solid-State and Sodium-Ion Batteries in E-Mobility Using a MATLAB Tool
authors: Nicole Klewicz, Heiko Fechtner, Benedikt Schmuelling
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology and Engineering (i-COSTE)
abstract: The selection of suitable battery technologies is a key lever in shaping the global transition to sustainable mobility. This study investigates the techno-economic potential of two emerging battery types - solid-state batteries and sodium-ion batteries. While solid-state batteries promise higher energy densities, sodium-ion batteries offer advantages in material availability and potential cost stability due to the abundance of sodium. A MATLAB-based evaluation is used to conduct a holistic analysis focusing on their application within the Tesla Model Y and the VW ID.3. The assessment considers key indicators such as energy density, specific costs, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CO}_{2}$</tex> emissions, and achievable driving range. The results highlight technological trade-offs and demonstrate how different battery choices impact the ecological and economic performance of electric vehicles. Moreover, the flexibility of the modeling approach enables its application across a range of current and future vehicle configurations, supporting strategic decision making in battery selection.

title: Operational Testing and Error Budget Management in the Architecture for Large Language Models Integrated Systems
authors: Sergey V. Makshakov, Valentina G. Sidorenko
publish: 2025 International Conference on Quality Management, Transport and Information Security, Information Technologies (QM&amp;TIS&amp;IT)
abstract: The integration of large language models (LLMs) into information systems requires new architectural solutions to ensure security, reliability, and quality control. This paper analyzes vulnerabilities and testing challenges specific to LLMintegrated systems. We propose a new class of architecture tailored for LLM usage, based on a microservice paradigm with a centralized orchestrator. Special attention is given to operational testing as an architectural component for identifying defects in real-world environments, and to the use of fault budgets as a dynamic quality control mechanism. This approach enhances system adaptability and resilience under real-world usage and rapid AI evolution.

title: Large-Kernel Convolutional Multi-Scale Fusion for Traffic Sign Detection
authors: Tuo Wang, Yiding Zhao, Qiang Ai
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: This paper presents an improved framework for traffic sign detection based on the YOLOv12 backbone, integrating MS-CA, LKC-FF, and an RT-DETR-based decoder to enhance detection of small and distant targets. The MS-CA module dynamically emphasizes discriminative channels, LKC-FF performs deep cross-scale feature fusion using large-kernel convolutions, and the RT-DETR decoder leverages learnable queries and multi-head attention for global context modeling. In addition, the Alpha-IoU Loss is employed to improve bounding box regression, making the model more sensitive to subtle localization errors of small objects. Experiments on the TT100K dataset demonstrate that the proposed method achieves mAP@0.5 of 76.8% and mAP@0.5: 0.95 of 58.6%, outperforming baseline models and showing significant advantages in detecting small and distant traffic signs, providing an efficient and robust solution for real-world intelligent transportation systems.

title: Performance Guarantees in High-Load Serverless Systems: A Queuing and Prediction-Based Approach
authors: M. Shamma Afzal, Umamageswaran Jambulingam
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: Serverless computing simplifies application deployment by managing infrastructure and auto-scaling. However, latency-sensitive workloads are hindered by cold starts, where idle functions require re-initialization, introducing unpredictable delays. This paper presents a framework that integrates machine learning–based traffic prediction with queuing-theory analysis to proactively reduce cold-start latency. The system incorporates a traffic predictor, latency estimator, dynamic warm-pool manager, and smart request router. Simulation results show that the approach reduces cold starts by up to 50% and improves average latency by 30% compared to reactive provisioning. These findings indicate that predictive and analytical techniques can provide reliable performance guarantees for high-load serverless systems at low cost.

title: Performance Evaluation and Empirical Atmospheric Modeling of OFDM-FSO Links Using MATLAB
authors: A. Kasi Vishwanathan, R.P Nirmal Kumar, R.P. Niranjan Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: Free Space Optics (FSO) is a promising candidate for 6G backhaul networks. In this paper, we evaluate the performance of FSO communication systems that employ Orthogonal Frequency-Division Multiplexing (OFDM) under a range of atmospheric conditions. Atmospheric turbulence is modeled using the Gamma-Gamma distribution, while signal attenuation is estimated with the ITU-R model for four weather scenarios: clear sky, haze, rain, and fog. System performance is assessed using five different modulation schemes-BPSK, QPSK, 16-QAM, 64-QAM, and 256-QAM-using MATLAB R2024b simulations. Inter-Symbol Interference (ISI) is compensated by incorporating a Linear Minimum Mean Square Error (LMMSE) equalizer. The results indicate that BPSK exhibits superior performance in terms of Bit Error Rate (BER) under severe weather, while 256-QAM achieves the maximum throughput of 1.42 Gbps under clear air conditions but suffers poor BER performance. Our results form the basis of weather-adaptive OFDM-FSO systems for high-speed 6G communication networks.

title: XAI-Enhanced Hybrid Models for Effective Chronic Kidney Disease Prediction
authors: Pijush Kanti Roy Partho, Suchi Karmoker, Tushar Emran, Dipraj Sarker, Delowar Hossain, Eui-Nam Huh, Muhammad Aminur Rahaman, Tangina Sultana
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology and Engineering (i-COSTE)
abstract: Chronic Kidney Disease (CKD) is a major global health concern that requires early and accurate detection to prevent progression and improve patient outcomes. This study presents a predictive framework integrating traditional machine learning and deep learning with an ensemble approach for robust CKD diagnosis. To address data imbalancing, three resampling techniques, namely SMOTE, SMOTE-Tomek, and SMOTE-ENN, were proposed to balance the dataset. A single SHAP-based feature selection via XGBoost was applied to each resampled dataset, and the top features were aggregated across all three techniques. We also performed a comprehensive statistical analysis using SPSS software to identify the most important features. Nine machine learning classifiers and two ensemble strategies were trained and applied alongside several deep learning models, including Attention Autoencoder with XGBoost for latent features, TabNet, TabPFN, LightCNN, MLP, and Deep and Cross Network (DeepCrossNet). DeepCrossNet achieved the highest accuracy (97.38 %) among all deep learning models; meanwhile, the stacking ensembles model reached 97.50 %, and Random Forest led among traditional models with the accuracy of 97.71 %. Explainable AI, likely SHAP and LIME analysis, confirmed that the features GFR (glomerular filtration rate) and serum creatinine are the most influential predictors, supporting clinical interpretability. t-SNE and UMAP projections demonstrated clear class separation and highlighted ambiguous cases. These results demonstrate that combining aggregated, SHAP-selected features from resampled datasets with ensemble and deep learning methods yields accurate and interpretable CKD predictions.

title: Techno-Economic Analysis for Placement of Wind and Energy Storage with Multi-Period UC Decision
authors: Munazza Shaikh, Kusum Verma, Akanksha Shukla
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: Modernization and technology advancement has led to increased demand of electricity in the recent decades. Also, due to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CO}_{2}$</tex> emissions and less dependency on fossil fuel, renewable energy penetration into the power grid at transmission level is also increasing. Renewable Energy Sources (RES) integration poses many challenges in terms of stability, reliability and finances. Energy Storage System (ESS) can mitigate these challenges by balancing the output in terms of generation, losses, costs, voltage regulation and more. This paper proposes an approach to determine the best candidate bus for co-located placement of Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) and wind in power transmission network using multiperiod stochastic Unit Commitment (UC) decision. Three wind scenarios with transition probability, contingency and variable load profile are considered for this study. The best and worst candidate bus is determined on the basis of technical and economic parameters of the system. Real power losses and generation cost at each bus are identified and results are compared to analyze the best candidate bus with UC decision. The study is carried out on IEEE 30 bus system using Mixed Integer Linear Programming (MILP) algorithm determining optimal UC decision. The generation cost of best candidate bus is compared with other research work to highlight the best results. It is found from the results that the generation cost is reduced to $ 28386.82 with storage as compared to $ 34696.87 without storage.

title: A Method for Evaluating Polymerization Degree Based on Terahertz Spectroscopy of Insulating Oil
authors: Yanxiang Wang, Yuan Li, Chenjun Guo, Qingbo Wang
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: To address the drawbacks of traditional methods for detecting the aging degree of insulating paper, such as destructive testing and complicated procedures, this paper employs terahertz technology to achieve nondestructive detection of aging stages. Insulating oil-paper samples with different aging levels were first prepared in the laboratory, and their THz absorption spectra were measured. Terahertz feature parameters were then extracted using the Total Variation-regularized Least Absolute Shrinkage and Selection Operator. Based on these spectral features, a diagnostic model for insulating paper aging stages was constructed using the Shape-Constrained Additive Model. The model was further validated with samples from in-service transformers, achieving an accuracy of 85%. The results demonstrate that the proposed model can effectively and nondestructively evaluate the aging degree of insulating paper.

title: Impact of Light Quality on the Complexity Measured in a Scene
authors: Rahma Jaber, Noël Richard, Ludwig Lepage, Christine Fernandez-Maloigne
publish: 2025 IEEE Sustainable Smart Lighting World Conference &amp; Expo (LS25)
abstract: The shift from halogen to LED spotlights has transformed stage lighting, offering efficiency and flexibility but also raising questions about visual quality. While traditional light quality metrics describe colour accuracy, they fail to capture how changes in light spectra alter the overall perception of a scene. In this study, we analyse the complexity variation of textile aspects in response to changes in light spectra. First, complexity is assessed in the perceptual CIELAB space using a fractal-based model (fractional Brownian motion). Then, after defining and validating it, we analyse the complexity response of different textiles to 8 light configurations. Results show the approach's interest and the directions for measurement improvement.

title: Statistical and Temporal Analysis of Electrical Vehicles Charging Behavior at Caltech ACN
authors: Aramchehr Zare, Seyed Mahdi Miraftabzadeh, Michela Longo, Dario Zaninelli
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Driving and Smart Transportation (IDST)
abstract: The accelerating adoption of Electric Vehicles (EVs) presents emerging challenges for charging infrastructure operation, load management, and user behavior analysis. This study conducts a comprehensive data-driven visualization of the Caltech Adaptive Charging Network (ACN) dataset, which provides detailed session-level records including arrival and departure times, energy demand, and charging duration. The primary goal is to identify and interpret temporal and behavioral patterns in EV charging activity through systematic data preprocessing, statistical characterization, and visualization. By generating interpretable plots, the analysis detects key behavioral features, e.g., peak arrival and departure periods, dominant charging durations, and variability in delivered energy. Results indicate distinct temporal dynamics, with arrival peaks around 2:00 PM and departures concentrated near midnight patterns that diverge from conventional workplace charging behavior. These findings reveal noticeable temporal regularities associated with campus and commuter usage while highlighting the diversity of user charging profiles. The insights derived from this analysis enhance the understanding of real-world EV charging dynamics and provide a quantitative foundation for future studies on demand forecasting, adaptive charging strategies, and infrastructure planning.

title: Coating Thickness Testing of Gas Insulated Composite Electrical Appliances based on Terahertz Technology
authors: Juan Yi, Lei He, Jiankan Zhou, Guofang Luo
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: The damage and corrosion of the shell caused by corrosion and unevenness of the paint film may lead to internal flashover faults in GIS. The fully sealed structure of GIS makes it difficult to locate and repair faults, and the maintenance work is complex. Terahertz technology provides a new method for on-site in-situ evaluation of GIS paint film quality. The pulsed terahertz waves emitted by terahertz time-domain optical systems providing a convenient and fast online application solution for the detection of cover layer thickness and uniformity. This article uses reflective terahertz time-domain spectroscopy technology to detect the thickness distribution of GIS coatings, verifying the ability of terahertz technology to detect GIS coating thickness in a non-contact manner.

title: Modeling and Simulation of a Rear-Wheel-Drive Electric Vehicle: Case Study on Tesla Model Y
authors: Tommaso Robbiano, Matteo Fresia, Simone D'Ambrosio, Stefano Bracco, Andrea Di Martino, Nicoletta Matera, Michela Longo
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Driving and Smart Transportation (IDST)
abstract: This work presents the development and experimental validation of a forward-facing longitudinal model for a rear-wheel-drive battery electric vehicle (Tesla Model-Y RWD). The MATLAB/Simulink simulator is organised into modular subsystems: Driver; Brake System (including regenerative braking); Permanent-Magnet Synchronous Motor (PMSM); Driveline; Battery; and Vehicle Longitudinal Dynamics. Validation is performed against on-road measurements (urban profile, 18 km, 60 s sampling interval) of traction power, energy consumption, vehicle speed and state of charge (SOC). Across the urban route, the model achieves a mean absolute percentage error of less than 5% on energy consumption and SOC evolution. Discrepancies are primarily due to the resolution of the data sampling and the omission of battery thermal dynamics. Therefore, the simulator is suitable for energy analysis, what if studies and control prototyping, and it can be easily adapted for other electric vehicle platforms.

title: Integration of Quality Management and Production Organization Based on Digital Technologies
authors: Victor A. Vasiliev, Svetlana V. Aleksandrova
publish: 2025 International Conference on Quality Management, Transport and Information Security, Information Technologies (QM&amp;TIS&amp;IT)
abstract: The development of modern industry leads to increased requirements for the quality of products and their production processes. This is especially true in high-tech industries such as precision engineering, electronics, instrumentation, aircraft manufacturing, etc. Improving the quality of products and production processes is achieved in various ways. Quality assurance and management issues are based on modern quality management systems (QMS). The quality of processes is ensured within the framework of information systems of the production organization (ERP, MES, etc.). In recent years, digital technologies have been widely developed in relation to the topic under study - artificial intelligence, big data, blockchain, digital twins, etc. The concepts of Industry 4.0 and Quality 4.0 began to be used. In this paper, possible integrated methods of product and process quality management based on digital technologies are considered. The options for integrating the basic principles of quality management, the process approach to the organization of production and digital technologies are shown. The combination of the considered approaches can be considered as quality management 5.0.

title: Design and Implementation of a Micro Bionic Butterfly Robot Based on Intelligent Actuation and Biomimetic Optimization
authors: Yuxin Lin
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: This paper presents the design, development, and experimental validation of a bionic flapping-wing micro aerial vehicle (FW-MAV) inspired by the flight mechanics of butterflies. The system employs a dual coreless motor drive integrated with a three-stage planetary gear reduction mechanism, eliminating conventional linkage-based transmissions to reduce redundant mass and improve maneuverability. The wing structure comprises a carbon fiber vein frame and a polyimide film membrane, yielding a total vehicle weight of only 10.2 grams. A flexible hinge between the primary and secondary wings introduces a controlled phase difference during flapping, optimizing lift distribution. The onboard control system, built around an STM32F103 microcontroller and ExpressLRS protocol, enables real-time attitude adjustment and multi-degree-of-freedom flight control. Experimental results, supported by ANSYS Fluent CFD simulations, confirm stable flight performance with a flapping frequency of 13 Hz and a lift force of 1.4 N. The design offers a novel platform for applications in covert reconnaissance, search and rescue, and ecological monitoring.

title: MIMO-Enabled Robust Communication Architecture for Fault-Tolerant Smart Grids Under Renewable Uncertainties
authors: Shubhalaxmi Mohapatra, Satyadeep Das, Debasis Nanda, Bibhu Prasad Sahoo, Sandeep Gupta, Kamalakanta Shaw, Priyanka Jethi, Subhendu Sekhar Sahoo
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: The rapid growth of renewable energy generation and the increasing dependence on cyber-physical communication infrastructures have introduced new challenges to the reliability of modern smart grids. Renewable sources such as solar and wind exhibit stochastic variability that leads to rapid changes in power system operating conditions, resulting in electromagnetic interference, transient disturbances, and communication bottlenecks during critical events. Conventional single-input single-output (SISO) communication links often fail to maintain the latency, throughput, and outage performance required for protection, control, and wide-area monitoring. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a MIMO-enabled robust communication architecture designed specifically for smart grid environments subject to renewable intermittency and grid faults. The framework integrates multi-antenna transmission, adaptive switching between spatial diversity and spatial multiplexing, CSIaware real-time beamforming, and spatial interference suppression. A grid-event-driven adaptation mechanism is introduced, enabling the communication system to reconfigure transmission parameters based on fault severity, renewable ramp rates, and instantaneous channel conditions. Comprehensive simulations using MATLAB/Simulink demonstrate significant improvements in BER, SNR stability, outage probability, latency during protection events, and throughput during renewable ramp disturbances compared to SISO, OFDM, and fixed MIMO systems.

title: Suppression of Low Frequency Oscillation by Optimal Mechanical Steam-Turbine Controller
authors: Samarjeet Satapathy, Rojalin Sahu, Anshuman Bhuyan, Ashutosh Gantayat, Satyakam Mohanty, Narayan Nahak, Alok Mishra, Akshaya Kumar Patra
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: Oscillations in power system aggravations day by day with uncertainty in load variations and disturbances such as renewable penetrations. The present study proposes deployment of steam governors to supper oscillations in power system. The steam governor is equipped with fractional compensated lead lag action, where the gains are set by improved salp swarm algorithm (ISSA). The ITAE based objective function being considered with the remote signal speed deviation of generator taken as input for controller action. Sudden and discrete variations in reference voltage to generator, load and plug in electric vehicles (PEV) have been the source of low frequency oscillations and proposed steam governor being employed to suppress these oscillations. The tunning efficacy of ISSA has been contrasted with PSO and SSA techniques.
