The variations in speed have been analyzed for different disturbances with system eigen distributions and justification of proposed action has been established. Low frequency modelling of power system along with steam governor has been taken at an initial operating state. Time domain simulations have been carried out to analyses system behavior and it has been observed that proposed approach is much effective to oscillation suppression in power system.

title: Educational and Career Outcome Forecasting Through Machine Learning Analytics
authors: Suha Khalil Assayed, Padmesh Tripathi, Pridhi Arora
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Understanding the factors that have impacts on salaries are more and more important in data-driven era. Factors such as education, experience, age, gender, and career choices play a key role in determining salaries. This study applies machine learning (ML) methods to explore the impact of these factors, providing insights that can guide employers and help students make informed career decisions. Multiple machine learning models were applied to predict employee salaries based on degree level, age, experience, gender, and career type. The dataset was obtained from a public source, data.world, and is derived from the US Adult Census data. Three ML algorithms were used: support vector machine (SVM), logistic regression (LR), and random forest (RF) classifier to predict whether salaries are above or below 50K. The models were then evaluated and tested using unseen data to assess their performance. The results exposed that LR classifier attained the highest accuracy in comparison to the other classifiers, whereas in terms of precision and F1-score, the RF performed better. In future work, neural network algorithms, including recurrent neural networks (RNNs) with activation functions like sigmoid and softmax, will be applied to classify predicted salaries as either above or below 50K.

title: Scalable Credit-Card Fraud Detection Using a RAAE and XGBoost
authors: G. Sindhu Sri, Ravuri Lalitha, Ch. Sai Viswanth, V. Lakshmi Sravya, S. Rehana Sulthana
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Credit-card fraud is a widespread worldwide issue that costs banks and customers billions annually and requires real-time identification so all transactions can be filtered through in seconds. Good modelling is difficult to accomplish because fraudulent activity is very rare in relation to valid transactions, fraud methods constantly change, and streams of transactions may contain noise or missing data. Traditional deep-learning approaches tend to overfit and are not transparent, which makes them too unreliable for deployment. This paper introduces Recency-Attentive Autoencoder with Extreme Gradient Boosting (RAAE+XGBoost) to solve these problems. The autoencoder learns the manifold of valid transactions and generates reconstruction-error features, whereas an attention mechanism weighs more heavily on recent and informative transactions. Other short-term statistical features maintain temporal trends. These enhanced features are input to an XGBoost classifier that can deal with extreme class imbalance and extract intricate nonlinear relationships. Model choices are justified by means of SHAP values to satisfy financial-sector interpretability standards. Stepwise ablation from a vanilla Autoencoder to AE + XGBoost and finally to full RAAE + XGBoost illustrates the way recency weighting, attention, and gradient boosting improve precision-recall performance stepwise. On the review-voted European credit-card dataset, the method achieves the highest precision-recall AUC and lowest false-negative rate among all the tested baselines in machine learning and deep learning, offering a accurate, scalable, and transparent framework for real-time fraud detection for banking and e-commerce settings.

title: Brown Bear Optimization-Based Error Minimization for Reduced-Order Modeling of Off-Grid Microgrids
authors: Richa Chaudhary, V. P. Singh, Akhilesh Mathur
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: Microgrid systems are the interconnection of various subsystems that consist of distributed energy resources, energy storage units, and loads that integrate to form complex higher-order systems (HOSs). However, assessing and controlling such complex HOSs shows significant challenges. To address these issues, reduced-order models (ROMs) are proposed to solve the HOS while conserving its essential system characteristics. In this paper, an approximation of the off-grid microgrid (OM) system is developed by employing the time moments (TMs) and Markov parameters (MPs). The proposed off-grid microgrid reduced-order model (OMROM) for the off-grid microgrid higher-order system (OMHOS) is obtained by minimizing the errors between the TMs and MPs of the OMHOS and its OMROM. For this error minimization, an objective function is developed and enhanced using the brown bear optimization (BBO) algorithm. The suggested OMROM is obtained under the criteria of the Hurwitz stability criterion and steady-state matching conditions. The effectiveness and applicability of the developed OMROM for representing the OMHOS are demonstrated through tabular analyses and graphical responses.

title: HAR70+W: Cross-Dataset Alignment of Thigh Back and Wrist Sensors for Elderly Activity Monitoring
authors: Abha Tewari, Dhananjay Kalbande
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: We present HAR70+W, a cross-dataset fusion approach for elderly activity monitoring that combines complementary inertial signals from two public sources: HAR70 + (thigh/back) sensors and WEDA-Fall wrist-worn signals. The proposed pipeline performs cross-dataset alignment and window-based segmentation to handle differences in sampling characteristics and recording protocols before learning activity representations. We benchmark several deep architectures, including 1D-CNN,LSTM,CNN+BiLSTM, Transformer, and a hybrid CNN+Transformer model. We introduce window based signal segmentation to enhance the performance of the recognition process. The model based on CNN+Transformer shows a considerable advantage over others at 90.62% accuracy and macro F1-score of 0.867. This framework is an attempt to overcome these problems, as shown by heterogeneous sampling rates and label alignment, and demonstrates that hybrid sensor fusion with advanced architectures has the potential to make a difference in practical elderly care use cases.

title: Self-Evolving AI Framework for Adaptive Threat Detection in Cloud-Integrated Cyber Environments
authors: Mohit Garg, Isan Sahoo, Vivek Banerjee, Keshav Kaushik, Vijayent Kohli, Mukesh Soni
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: This paper has discussed a self-improving AIbased model developed for identifying threats in cloud computing environments. The model evolved as it learned on its own after each cycle. The model applied both genetic principles and reinforcement learning in order to adapt quickly to emerging forms of attacks. The simulated results showed the improvement in threat detection accuracy and the decline in false positive alarms over time. The model seemed to be continually operating as a cyber security “brain” that is constantly learning and never stops thinking. The research showed that this model could enhance the defenses of cloud based networks through automated means without requiring a user's intervention; although the model was tested via simulation, the results were very similar to those expected from a live cyber security system.

title: Advancing Multivariate Time Series Generation Using Hybrid Transformer-Based and Generative Models- A Comparative Study on Weather Data
authors: Gaganjot Singh, Karuna, Inderpal Singh, Anshu Vashisth, Gagandeep Kaur, Abhishek Kumar
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: Transformer-based models and generative models are compared in this paper when applied to the weather time-series data. We created synthetic weather sequences with the help of Variational Autoencoders and Adversarial Autoencoders and tested the performance of Artificial Neural Network, Long Short-Term Memory, Time-Transformer, and Temporal Fusion Transformer forecasting models on them. Data realism, temporal continuity and statistical accuracy under controlled conditions were investigated with a single test situation. A <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 8}$</tex>-variable dataset of greenhouse weather was experimented on, and these experiments represent complex relationships between environmental factors including temperature, humidity, radiation, and concentration of CO 2. These findings indicate that Adversarial Autoencodersgenerated data are more likely to maintain time dynamics, cross-variable relationships and distributions than Variational Autoencoders-generated synthesis. Moreover, the effectiveness of transformer-based forecasting models, especially the Temporal Fusion Transformer and Time-Transformer, was consistently in terms of high predictive accuracy and robustness in both real and synthetic data, and attribute to their ability to balance trade-offs between accuracy, computational efficiency and stability to practical real-world weather forecasting systems.

title: A Comprehensive Review of Virtual Power Plants as an Enabler of Energy Future
authors: Sairam Challa, Ubaid Qureshi, Neha Tak
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: The increasing integration of distributed energy resources (DERs) such as solar PV systems, wind turbines, battery storage, electric vehicles, and responsive demand technologies is significantly altering the architecture and operation of modern power systems. These decentralized components are disrupting the conventional hierarchical model of electricity generation and distribution, introducing complex challenges in demand-supply balancing, grid stability, and overall system efficiency. To address these challenges, the concept of Virtual Power Plants (VPPs) has gained prominence. VPPs aggregate diverse DERs into cohesive and controllable units, enabling flexible operation and market participation through sophisticated digital platforms. By coordinating various distributed assets, VPPs facilitate optimized scheduling and centralized or decentralized control. This paper presents an in-depth analysis of the core principles and current developments related to VPPs. It examines the primary architectural approaches highlighting their respective strengths and limitations regarding scalability, communication needs, interoperability, and system resilience. Furthermore, the paper discusses emerging technologies set to advance VPP functionality, including artificial intelligence for predictive control, blockchain for secure energy transactions, and digital twins for detailed simulation and system management.

title: Voltage and Frequency Stabilisation of A Deregulated Power System Using Multi-Stage Controller
authors: Subhadra Sahoo, Narendra Kumar Jena, Kumari Kasturi, Binod Kumar Sahu
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: In the present scenario, there is continuous increase in electricity demand in almost all sectors of industries. This huge load requirement is to be satisfied only by introduction of renewable energy sources. But the RES units due to lack of inertia and again due to uncertain inputs makes the system more unstable and complicated. Hence it becomes a great challenge to maintain the system stability of large interconnected power system in the presence of the RES units. Further to satisfy the consumers by providing reliable supply with less cost the systems are modified to a horizontally integrated system commonly known as deregulated system. In such complex system to maintain the voltage and frequency at a desired level, more reliable and efficient secondary controllers are to be designed. In this work, a two stage PID+1PI controller is designed for the dynamic stability of a two-area hydro-thermal-wind power system. Its result is compared with result of PID controller and proves its better performance. The controller parameters are selected through Selfish Herd Optimisation technique. The robustness of the controller is validated by inserting a random load.

title: Approximate Non-Uniform FRM-FIR Filter Bank Architectures: 6-Band and 8-Band Realizations for Digital Hearing Aid
authors: Ramya R
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: The growing demand for energy-efficient hearing aids has intensified research on low-power audio signal processing in digital VLSI design. A major challenge in hearing aid devices is the filter bank, which demands large amount of power because of the computationally intensive Multiply and Accumulate (MAC) operations. This paper presents the design and implementation of non-uniform FIR filter banks for hearing aid applications using an approximate multiplier. Two configurations, namely a 6-band and an 8-band filter bank, are implemented for processing speech signals in the audio band. To validate the filter bank results on an FPGA, a 16-bit approximate multiplier was integrated into the MAC unit to perform the convolution operation for filtering. The design was implemented on a Zynq-7000 FPGA SoC development board, where Block RAM was used for storing filter coefficients and audio samples. System performance was evaluated with real audio signals, including music mixed with interference tones, and also tested for audiogram matching across different hearing loss profiles. Experimental results demonstrate significant reductions in power and area while maintaining acceptable accuracy, confirming the suitability of the proposed filter bank design for next-generation hearing aids.

title: Design of an Integrated Model for Intelligent Energy Aware Routing in High QoS Wireless Networks
authors: Pradnya A. Jadhav, Dinesh B. Bhoyar
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: Wireless networks face energy shortages, unanticipated traffic, and growing QoS expectations. Current clustering and routing algorithms react late to node mobility and energy deterioration as the topology changes, causing performance collapses in real time scenarios. Many published models assume static link behavior and simple routing decisions, resulting in theoretical improvements a real system cannot sustain in process. We offer a sequential, analytically grounded intelligent routing design to close that gap. We employ AMANP to choose cluster seeds using a learned “Network Sustainability Index,” reducing early energy imbalance that shortens cluster lifespan. QIFCO uses these characteristics to explore many stable cluster arrangements for the most energy-efficient one by treating membership uncertainty as a quantum superposition. Spatial-temporal energy and delay patterns are used by Deep Graph Routing Predictor (DGRP) to anticipate future paths that maintain QoS during mobility. Since routing may fail later, Stochastic Energy Equilibrium Validation (SEEV) evaluates longhorizon energy fairness using Markov state transitions. Finally, EQRO alters routing algorithms if throughput, latency, or jitter exceed constraints. Significant performance enhancements include <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim \mathbf{4 7 \%}$</tex> longer network lifetime and consistent delay under unpredictable node movement. These results demonstrate that predictive learning and analytical equilibrium validation can create wireless systems that are self-aware infrastructures rather than fragile link networks.

title: Enhanced Spatio-Temporal Feature Learning for Dorsal Hand Vein Recognition Using CNN-RNN Architecture on Video Input
authors: Rohit Rana, Rajendra Kuma
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: The main focus of this study is to use Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), more importantly on ResNet-18, as an option to extract spatial details. It uses a bidirectional Gated Recurrent Unit (Bi-GRU) network for extracting temporal information from vein image captured from frames taken from videos captured by NIR Camera. These spatial and temporal aspects are then combined to fully grasp the dynamics of vein structures. This assisted in improving the accuracy and stability of the dorsal hand vein recognition system. The system shows remarkable improvement in performance compared to common single-frame CNNs and temporal modelling-based systems. It recorded 98.8% accuracy and an Equal Error Rate (EER) of 1.1%. Feasibility and novelty of the system were verified against recent peer-reviewed works of different researchers, such as CNN-based and temporal biometric models. The proposed model not only improves dorsal hand vein recognition but also sets up a platform for temporal video-based recognition systems that can be deployed in realworld scenarios.

title: CyberStrike: A Gamified AI Tool to Build Cyber Safety Awareness
authors: Charanjeet Dadiyala, Harshala Shingne, Prachi Ingale, Om Gatlewar
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: In today's digital age, it is the people, not technology, who remain the leading source of cybersecurity risk. Standard training practices very often do not captivate users and as a result, they can hardly memorize the important security measures. This work introduces “CyberStrike”, a creative Learning Management System that uses AI and gamification to cover the shortage of cybersecurity awareness. By taking advantage of generative AI tools: specifically, Gravitywrite.ai for teacher scripting and Steve.ai for video production, the platform provides high-quality, animated educational content. The system employs the Odoo framework to support a well-organized learning path made up of multimedia modules and Multiple-Choice Question (MCQ) tests. A preliminary study shows that the emphasis on accessibility and interactive feedback of the platform can fill the gaps that exist in traditional EdTech tools, especially in relations to mixed demographic groups.

title: A Comprehensive Review of Optimization Techniques For Transformer Based Rainfall–runoff Modeling
authors: Neha S. Chavhan, Pranay Saraf, Nekita Chavhan
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: The increasing demand for accurate hydrological forecasting the modeling rainfall and runoff processes has become very important for water resource management and flood prevention. However, traditional physical models and recent neural architectures like ANN, CNN and LSTM struggle to capture long-term dependencies effectively. Transformer-based models use self-attention mechanisms that help to provide enhanced temporal feature learning and parallel computation capabilities. This paper presents a systematic review and simulated comparative analysis of Transformer optimizers, including Adam, RAdam, AdaBelief, Lookahead and Lion in rainfall-runoff forecasting. The study compares the convergence, generalization, and stability of optimizers, emphasizing AdaBelief and Lion for higher predictive stability and quicker convergence. The results offer important insights into the optimization strategies used in transformer-based hydrological models and also address the shortcomings of existing methods and offer ideas for future research, such as cross-catchment generalization and hybrid physical transformer models.

title: Cloud-Native Multimodal Semantic Search and Recommendation for Large-Scale Digital Commerce
authors: Amit Kumar Padhy, Tejas Pravinbhai Patel, Vinay Soni, Arun Kumar Elengovan, Gajendra Babu Thokala, Nandagopal Seshagiri
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: The explosive growth of large-scale digital commerce platforms has seen the need for intelligent product search systems that can comprehend textual and visual information. Traditional keyword-based retrieval methods and text only neural retrieval models may not be able to capture semantic intent, as well as visual similarity, resulting in less than optimal user experience and lower conversion rates. The purpose of current research effort is to design and test a cloud-native multi-model semantic search and recommendations framework to scale-text and image representations. The proposed methodology includes a combination of transformer-based multimodal encoders, distributed dense vector retrieval and microservices-based deployment orchestrated using Kubernetes. The system breaks down ingestion, embedding, indexing, retrieval, ranking and personalization processes into independently scalable services to deal with spiky e-commerce workloads. Extensive experiments on an actual product catalog show that the proposed framework has a higher retrieval effectiveness, gains up to 11.5 % in Precision@10 and 9.8% in nDCG@10 as compared to strong multimodal baselines, while saving almost <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 0 \%}$</tex> of p99 latency as compared to monolithic deployments. These results prove that cloud native multimodal retrieval is a practical and scalable solution for next generation digital commerce systems.

title: Plenary Talks
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 IEEE Afro-Mediterranean Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AMCAI)

title: HandBridge: A Bidirectional Indian Sign Language Translation App Using Mediapipe and Neural Language Models
authors: Rajeshwari Dandage, Supriya Mhaske, Smit Salalkar, Princekumar Prasad, Gaurav Rachalwar, Priyanka Raut
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: Communication problems between hearing and Deaf peoples often arise from limited familiarity with sign language. In India, around 63 million people face challenges with hearing, and for many members of the Deaf community, Indian Sign Language (ISL) is a vital part of their everyday communication. ISL is not just a language; it's a bridge that connects people, allowing them to share their thoughts, stories, and emotions, fostering a sense of belonging and understanding among friends and family. To address this challenge, we present HandBridge, a bidirectional ISL translation application that employs camerabased keypoint extraction, machine learning for sign recognition, and contemporary speech technologies. The system converts ISL gestures to spoken language and vice versa, featuring on-device processing for enhanced privacy and offline functionality. In this paper, we'll explain our system's design, how we developed it, and how we plan to evaluate it. We'll also discuss the ethical issues involved and the real-world situations where it can be used.

title: Assessment and AI Analytics of Students' Motivation and Skills: Case of Blending Different Methods in Learning Art and Design
authors: Rym Bouhamed
publish: 2025 IEEE Afro-Mediterranean Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AMCAI)
abstract: This paper studies the integration of artificial intelligence (AI) in evaluating interdisciplinary pedagogical projects within a blended learning environment in art and design. We propose an AI-driven semantic analysis framework to decode student feedback from case studies. Using natural language processing (NLP) and machine learning algorithms, we analyze textual data from 60 student reports collected via a learning management system (LMS), complemented by self-assessment surveys and eight academic performance indicators. Our pedagogical methodology combines project-based learning, classroom relocation, flipped classroom, peer collaboration, and active and collaborative learning. Leveraging AI tools, this paper demonstrates how blended approaches-mixing physical classroom interactions and virtual LMS participation-enhance critical thinking, social skills, and student motivation. In fact, semantic analysis reveals many interesting key insights. The statistical results show that AI-enhanced teaching strategies significantly improve academic performance. The success of the hybrid model comes from its ability to contextualize theoretical concepts through realistic design challenges while exploiting AI to personalize feedback. Our research aligns with global developments in AI-driven pedagogy, advocating ethically designed tools that balance technological innovation with human-centered learning experiences.

title: Building Trust in Smart Cities: A Secure, On-Premise AI Chatbot for Urban Planning Applications
authors: Avraam Bardos, Theoktisti Marinopoulou, Antonios Lalas, Konstantinos Votis
publish: 2025 IEEE Afro-Mediterranean Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AMCAI)
abstract: The rise of large-scale language models has reshaped the landscape of natural language processing (NLP), transforming how users interact with information. While widely known services such as ChatGPT have demonstrated remarkable capabilities across many domains, their dependence on cloudbased infrastructure can often raise concerns about privacy, customization, and operational flexibility. In this work, we present Regulation Extractor Assistant (Rexa), a locally-based chatbot leveraging retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) to extract and interact with regulatory and planning documents. Rexa combines file processing, vector-based retrieval, and structured prompt engineering to deliver accurate and context-aware responses. This approach supports transparency and data security, which are critical in scenarios such as urban planning and regulation. Our experiments, conducted on an in-house dataset of questions based on an urban planning document, demonstrate that Rexa outperforms leading commercial chatbots across multiple metrics, even when those chatbots are guided by Rexa's optimized prompts. These findings underscore the value of domain-specific, adaptable AI-tools in supporting reliable and informed decision-making.

title: Differential Performance and Contextual Integrity in Speech Privacy
authors: Poppy Welch, Natalia Tomashenko, Sneha Das, Jennifer Williams
publish: IEEE Security & Privacy
abstract: Speech technology challenges traditional privacy frameworks due to the complexity of speech and diverse contextual norms. We apply contextual integrity to three speech case studies, showing its promise for speech privacy and recommending preliminary assessments of signal, speaker, and system characterizations.

title: Confidence-Aware Test-Time Adaptation for Fault-Resilient Sound Source Localization Guided by Lightweight Diagnosis
authors: Inkyu An
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Deep learning-based sound source localization systems typically rely on specific microphone array configurations, making them highly vulnerable to hardware failures. Unexpected sensor faults cause severe structural signal degradation, leading to substantial deterioration in localization accuracy. To address this challenge, we propose a robust Fault-Resilient Sound Source Localization framework that autonomously diagnoses sensor faults and adapts the model at test time without requiring ground-truth labels. The proposed system integrates two core modules: 1) a Lightweight Fault Diagnosis module, which employs a computationally efficient Frequency-Time Projection Network with a Fault Queue mechanism, and 2) a Diagnosis-Guided Confidence-Aware Test-Time Adaptation strategy, which leverages the highly reliable diagnosis results to apply interventions and utilizes a confidence thresholding mechanism to generate refined hard pseudo-labels for stable self-training. As a result, the proposed system reduces the mean absolute error, averaged across diverse models and fault conditions, from 21.99° to 9.91°, achieving a significant error reduction of 54.92%, thereby ensuring reliable robotic perception even in the presence of sudden hardware malfunctions. The proposed framework is further validated in a real-environment experiment using a physical microphone array with an authentic hardware fault, confirming its generalizability beyond synthetic fault conditions.

title: Economic Impacts and Mitigation for the Grid Connected Electric Vehicle Charging: A Technical and Operational Overview
authors: Oyakhilome Ereme Ikhajangbe, Himavarsha Dhulipati, Shichao Liu
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The rapid growth of residential electric vehicle (EV) adoption is reshaping distribution networks, shifting load patterns from relatively dispersed background demand to synchronized, high-amplitude charging events. When charging is unmanaged, these concentrated loads can exceed thermal and voltage limits, accelerate transformer aging, intensify harmonic stress, and prompt costly infrastructure upgrades. Unlike prior reviews that examine technical, economic, and coordination aspects separately, this paper brings them together within an integrated technical–economic analytical review that considers thermal limits, voltage security, and battery state-of-charge constraints simultaneously. Transformer loss-of-life, harmonic–thermal interactions, peak amplification, and renewable–EV timing mismatch are quantitatively analyzed alongside infrastructure deferral benefits and ancillary-service revenues. Economic performance is evaluated by balancing upgrade deferral, V2G revenue, battery degradation costs, and coordination overhead. A comparative assessment of ARIMA, LSTM, and reinforcement learning methods highlights practical trade-offs between forecasting accuracy and computational scalability under evolving charging behavior. Evidence from field pilots reports 10–30% peak reduction, 20–65% charging cost savings, and $100–$500 per EV-year gross V2G revenue, depending on market structure and incentives. Overall, this review provides an integrated analytical foundation to support resilient and economically efficient EV–grid integration under high-penetration conditions.

title: Bayesian Deep-Learning Processor for Real-Time Bio-Applications With Structured Monte Carlo Dropout for High-Volume Sample Generation
authors: Han-Gyeol Mun, Jeong-Min Woo, Seunghyun Moon, Byungjun Kim, Jongmin Lee, Jae-Yoon Sim, Hyunwoo Son
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems I: Regular Papers
abstract: This work presents a Bayesian neural network (BNN) processor for real-time, edge-based medical applications, designed to generate high-volume inference outputs efficiently, providing rapid and precise uncertainty estimation. To address the computational and memory-intensive demands of BNNs, we propose a structured Monte Carlo (MC) dropout method and an efficient processing technique for depth-wise separable convolution, thereby minimizing both the memory and computational burdens. The proposed processor, implemented in 28-nm LP CMOS, is benchmarked on a 12-lead ECG dataset and demonstrates significant improvements in energy efficiency, achieving a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$12.9\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> enhancement over conventional MC dropout methods. Furthermore, the processor incorporates a model-switching technique based on uncertainty estimation, resulting in <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$3.2\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> lower energy consumption while maintaining robust and accurate ECG classification, even under challenging conditions involving noise and motion artifacts.

title: Learning to Generate Preferences for Multiobjective Deep Learning
authors: Peixin Huang, Yu Sun, Gang Wang, Yaoxin Wu, Qiong Wu, Wen Song, Yew-Soon Ong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics
abstract: Multiobjective optimization (MOO) is important for deep learning applications with multiple conflicting objectives. Pareto front learning (PFL) methods learn a single model conditioned on the preference of objectives and can be applied to any preference at inference time. However, existing PFL methods use predefined strategies (e.g., uniform sampling) to generate preferences, which could result in unevenly spaced solutions since the shape of Pareto front is largely ignored. In this article, we propose a lightweight and model-agnostic method to train a preference generator for a given PFL model, which learns to generate proper preferences from uniformly sampled ones, such that the resulting solutions are evenly spaced on the Pareto front. Compared to previous works, our method enables a more rational allocation of preferences, which can either be utilized to enhance a pretrained PFL model or be seamlessly integrated into the PFL training process to improve efficiency. We apply our method to state-of-the-art PFL methods with various backbones (e.g., multilayer perceptron, convolutional neural network, transformer) and validate the significance of preference generation across various tasks, from multitask supervised learning to multiobjective reinforcement learning-based neural combinatorial optimization. Experimental results show that our method improves the backbone algorithm in most settings, showing its effectiveness and general applicability.

title: Hybrid Noise and Jamming Modulation: An Efficient Anti-jamming Perspective
authors: Yuxin Shi, Xinjin Lu, Yusi Zhang, Cheng Li, Chen Han, Fanggang Wang, Symeon Chatzinotas
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: Recently, the noise modulation scheme and active anti-jamming (AAJ) scheme have shown the potential advantages, such as high anti-jamming performance, low transmitter complexity and low power consumption. Inspired by the prior schemes, this paper proposes a novel hybrid noise and jamming modulation (NJM) scheme that enables reliable communication under diverse jamming scenarios. Specifically, this scheme encodes information bits through a noise modulated transmitter and the amplification factor of a programmable gain amplifier (PGA), achieving robust anti-jamming performance. Theoretical bit error rate (BER) is conducted to derive the optimal decoding thresholds for different jamming strategies, and the approximate optimal threshold proposed for practical implementation using training symbols. Furthermore, we formulate and solve the power allocation optimization problem to enhance BER performance by properly splitting power between the two modulation components. To comprehensively evaluate the scheme, we provide the capacity analysis for the hybrid NJM scheme under three jamming strategies. Simulation results show that the theoretical results match well with the simulated ones, which demonstrates the correctness of our BER performance analysis. Moreover, the hybrid NJM scheme can achieve the superior BER performance and the channel capacity under the jamming attacks compared with the benchmark schemes.

title: Theoretical Gain-Bandwidth Limit of Parametrically Tuned Electrically Small Receiving Antennas
authors: C. G. Blosser, K. Schab, K. C. Kerby-Patel, J. E. Ruyle
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: Theoretical limits on the gain and bandwidth of a class of receiving, electrically small antennas (ESAs) loaded with periodically time-varying (TV) reactances is analyzed. The TV reactance produces parametric amplification and its average reactance tunes the ESA to resonance. A multi-band circuit model is developed that is suitable for designing these ESAs. The model is analyzed using conversion matrix techniques to obtain expressions for maximum transducer gain and half-power bandwidth. The trade-off between maximum gain and bandwidth is analyzed, and the circuit parameter values required to approach the maximum gain for a fixed bandwidth are determined.

title: Efficient Incremental Updates for Encrypted Mobile Apps
authors: Lewei Jin, Kui Zhang, Tong Sun, Jiamei Lv, Yi Gao, Wei Dong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing
abstract: The growth in mobile applications and user numbers has significantly increased the volume of app updates on the network. For example, TikTok has garnered 825 million downloads in 2024 and maintains a nearly weekly update frequency. To reduce the data traffic, incremental update has become a common solution, as it calculates and transmits only the differences between two versions of an app. However, the widespread adoption of application encryption has introduced new challenges for efficient incremental updates. Even minor changes in files or encryption keys can result in substantial differences between app versions that were initially highly similar. This paper presents EncDiffPatch, the first incremental update system tailored for encrypted mobile apps. EncDiffPatch leverages two key techniques to enable efficient updates. First, it uses an updating-aware key sharing mechanism, which utilizes file-level encryption and dynamically shares the encryption keys for files that remain unchanged across versions based on the content of these files. Second, it incorporates the decryption process with a selective decompression approach. This approach performs only the Huffman decoding process of decompression and decompresses only those bytes estimated to contribute to differencing benefits. Evaluation shows that EncDiffPatch achieves a 51% reduction in compression ratio without introducing any performance overhead. When incorporating the decryption process, EncDiffPatch achieves a 73% reduction in compression ratio, outperforming the state-of the-art algorithms while requiring only 45.3% of the reconstruction time. Our security analysis shows that EncDiffPatch maintains an equivalent level of security to existing mobile systems.

title: Evaluating the Impact of Jitter on Collaborative High-Speed Aerial and Railway Networks
authors: Ziyue Liu, Yue Xiao, Enzhi Zhou, Shuting Chen, Xianfu Lei, Xingwang Li, Sotiris A. Tegos, Panagiotis D. Diamantoulakis, George K. Karagiannidis
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: With the rapid growth of high-speed rail (HSR) networks, reliable communication is increasingly challenging due to complex terrain and coverage gaps in remote areas. Uncrewed aerial vehicles (UAVs), with their mobility and flexibility, provide aerial relay support to bridge these gaps, enhance signal strength, and improve HSR communication reliability. However, their performance in millimeter-wave (mmWave) systems is significantly degraded by mechanical jitter caused by environmental factors such as wind and turbulence, which adversely affects beam alignment and overall communication quality. To address these challenges, this work introduces a comprehensive analytical framework. We first develop a statistical model that characterizes the relationship between beam gain and jitter intensity. Subsequently, closed-form expressions are derived for the outage probability and ergodic data rate of UAV-assisted HSR mmWave systems under jitter influence, taking into account both co-located (CA) and distributed antenna (DA) configurations. Furthermore, we propose an adaptive beamwidth design that maximizes the average ergodic rate by adjusting the beamwidth according to UAV jitter severity. Numerical simulations verify that this approach significantly improves system capacity and robustness compared with conventional static beamforming, confirming its effectiveness.

title: A Method for Slip Angle Estimation of Intelligent Tire System Based on Hierarchical Temporal Fusion Network
authors: Jinghan Huang, Xiaolong Zhang, Chao Liu, Licheng Yan
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Accurately estimating the slip angle under dynamic conditions is critical for enhancing vehicle stability and safety. Intelligent tire systems and data-driven deep learning methods have been adopted to slip angle estimation. However, existing approaches often suffer from inadequate temporal feature extraction and limited model–data compatibility. To address these limitations, we perform a temporal dependency analysis of tire acceleration data, revealing strong local and global correlations. Based on this, we propose a novel feature extraction algorithm and a Hierarchical Temporal Fusion Network to fully capture these multiscale temporal correlations. Additionally, an Intelligent Linkage mechanism is designed to enhance model convergence speed and generalization capabilities. Linear probing and principal component analysis further confirm that the network learns highly discriminative and physically continuous representations. Experiments demonstrate that the proposed method achieves 99.38% prediction accuracy with only 11,842 parameters and processes more than 96,000 samples/s, outperforming baseline networks in both accuracy and efficiency. On-road validation confirms its applicability, reaching over 92% agreement on both dry and wet pavements against a kinematic reference.

title: Average-Case Benchmarking of Optimizers With Environmental Impact Metrics
authors: Jamel Raddaoui, Yassine Ben Salem, Walid Mechri
publish: 2025 IEEE International Multi-Conference on Smart Systems &amp; Green Process (IMC-SSGP)
abstract: This paper addresses an extensive empirical analysis of Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) models, trained systematically on diverse benchmark datasets for image classification using a broad spectrum of optimization techniques. For each distinct model-dataset-optimizer triplet, we rigorously record a multidimensional suite of performance indicators, encompassing accuracy, training time, energy consumption, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CO}_{2}$</tex> emissions, F1-score, precision and recall. The full set of performance measures was averaged to ensure stability. This study not only identifies the most effective optimizer for each architectural and dataset configuration but also offers a comparative analysis of the overall optimization efficiency and learning performance, providing actionable insights into the trade-offs between accuracy and resource efficiency in deep learning model training.

title: AI Tutor Study: Linking Student Questioning Patterns to Learning Gains Through Bloom's Taxonomy and Intent Analysis
authors: Tsun Chow
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: This paper investigates whether patterns in student questioning within an AI-powered tutoring system can predict learning outcomes. In a small exploratory pilot (Phase 1), a prototype tutor for principal component analysis (PCA) was used with ten graduate students in Business Analytics; six complete dialogue sessions were analyzed. Questions were coded along Bloom's taxonomy and communicative intent categories by ChatGPT, and validated by the author manually. Learners who engaged across a broader range of Bloom levels and displayed diverse intent types tended to achieve higher gains on a post-quiz. These observations informed three hypotheses for Phase 2: (1) that cognitive breadth predicts learning gain; (2) that diversity of question intents also predicts learning; and (3) that their interaction offers the strongest model of engagement. Phase 2 will involve a larger, naturalistic dataset, automated tagging validation, and regression modelling to test these hypotheses. Together, the study aims to provide a rigorous, data-driven approach to assessing cognitive engagement in AI-supported learning environments.

title: Modeling and Optimizing Dynamic Coalitions in Energy Markets Using Game Theory
authors: Diogo Ribeiro, José Baptista, Tiago Pinto, Adelaide Cerveira, Tiago Soares
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: This study provides a comprehensive review of how game theory can be applied to model and optimize dynamic coalitions in contemporary energy markets. With the increasing decentralization of energy systems driven by technologies such as solar photovoltaics, home energy storage, and electric vehicles, consumers have begun to play a more active and influential role in the market. In this new context, where cooperative and collective decision-making is gaining importance, game theory emerges as a valuable tool for analyzing and structuring these interactions. The primary objective of this work is to systematically review existing models, assess their methodological strengths and limitations, and identify open research gaps that hinder their applicability to real-world settings. By synthesizing the current state-of-the-art, this study aims to highlight pathways toward the development of more realistic and effective models that capture the dynamic and interdependent behaviors of energy consumers and the coalitions they form. Ultimately, this review seeks to provide an updated overview of this growing field, serving both as a basis for future research and as a foundation for the design of solutions that promote fairer, more efficient, and more participatory energy markets, especially for small-scale consumers, who now have greater voice and power of choice.

title: A Multiple-Decomposed Recursive Generalized Extended Least Squares Algorithm for High-Order MISO CARARMA Systems
authors: Roya Fathi Sabri, Munya Ali Arwin, Nasar Aldian Ambark Shashoa
publish: 2025 IEEE International Multi-Conference on Smart Systems &amp; Green Process (IMC-SSGP)
abstract: This paper concerns the identification challenge in high-order system Controlled Autoregressive Autoregressive Moving Average (CARARMA) systems. Utilizing a decomposition-based approach, a novel multiple-decomposed recursive generalized extended least squares (RGELS) algorithm is used to estimate system parameters efficiently. A key difficulty in this process arises from the presence of unknown variables within the noise information vector. To overcome this, the algorithm replaces these unknowns with their estimated counterparts, leading to the formulation of the multiple-decomposed RGELS method. Compared to the conventional RGELS algorithm, the proposed technique offers superior computational efficiency by partitioning the system's covariance matrix into multiple smaller sub-matrices. Ultimately, the outcomes of the simulation results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm has higher estimation accuracy under low noise variance, and model validation methods illustrates that the Best Fit Criterion and the Histograms indicating a high level of algorithm performance.

title: A 0.5 V 2.4 GHz Digital PLL with a Sub-Feedback Loop Technique
authors: Wen-Hsuan Cheng, Bo-Jun Huang, Jen-Chieh Liu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: This paper presents a digital bang-bang phase-locked loop (BBPLL) for low-supply-voltage applications. The proposed BBPLL utilizes a five-stage ring oscillator, which incorporates a forward-interpolation sub-feedback loop to achieve a higher output frequency under a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.5-\mathrm{V}$</tex> supply. A 4-bit secondorder delta-sigma modulator (DSM) is integrated into the digitally controlled oscillator (DCO) to enhance time resolution and reduce jitter. The prototype was implemented in TSMC <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 0 - n m}$</tex> CMOS technology and occupied a core area of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$59 \mu ~\mathrm{m} \times 110 \mu ~\mathrm{m}$</tex>. Measurement results showed that the BBPLL operated at 2.4 GHz with a peak-to-peak jitter of 23.46 ps and an RMS jitter of 2.68 ps, while consuming <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$283.4 \mu ~\mathrm{W}$</tex> from a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.5-\mathrm{V}$</tex> supply.

title: Decentralized Energy Management in Residential Microgrids: Simulation and Optimization for Homes Grouped by Proximity
authors: Fatma Ben Youssef, Yassine Ben Salem, Nihel Chekir, Ali Snoussi, Houcine Bendaoud
publish: 2025 IEEE International Multi-Conference on Smart Systems &amp; Green Process (IMC-SSGP)
abstract: The deployment of distributed renewable resources in residential microgrids calls for efficient energy management strategies. This paper compares two decentralized control approaches applied to a 20 -house microgrid equipped with photovoltaic (PV) generation and battery storage. In the first method, energy is shared globally among all households, whereas in the second, the system is organized into four clusters of five, enabling intra-cluster exchanges prior to inter-cluster coordination. A MATLAB-based Energy Management System (EMS) is developed to simulate power flows, battery dynamics, and energy transfers. Performance is assessed using standard indicators such as self-consumption, self-production, and surplus utilization. Results show that clustering reduces energy losses, improves demand-generation matching, and enhances overall system resilience, demonstrating its effectiveness for sustainable residential microgrids.

title: Optimized IIR Filter Design Using Genetic Algorithm and Theoretical Methods with Symmetric Coefficients
authors: Sidi Mohammed Meriah
publish: 2025 IEEE International Multi-Conference on Smart Systems &amp; Green Process (IMC-SSGP)
abstract: This article presents a novel approach to designing IIR filters by combining theoretical methods for deriving filter coefficients with an optimization-based approach. The denominator coefficients are constrained to be symmetrical, and only half of them are optimized using Genetic algorithm (GA). This reduces the computational complexity of the optimization process while maintaining filter performance. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves comparable or superior performance to traditional design techniques, such as Butterworth, Chebyshev Type II, and elliptic filters, with significant savings in computational resources.

title: Dynamic Parameter Extraction for Battery Discharge Characteristics Using Intelligent Algorithms Under Real Measurement
authors: DEGLA Aicha, REHOUMA Youssef, DANOUNE Mohamed Bilal, MEZIANE Fares, BOUMECHETA Samer, HAZIL Omar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Multi-Conference on Smart Systems &amp; Green Process (IMC-SSGP)
abstract: Accurate modeling and simulation of lead-acid batteries are essential for understanding their internal behavior and optimizing battery-based systems. However, the scarcity of realistic models in the literature remains a significant limitation. This study proposes the biogeography-based heterogeneous cuckoo search (BHCS), a hybrid metaheuristic algorithm that combines heterogeneous cuckoo search (CS) with biogeography-based optimization (BBO). The BHCS is designed to accurately estimate the unknown parameters of the battery's equivalent circuit by effectively balancing exploration and exploitation. Its performance is assessed on a lead-acid battery and benchmarked against four advanced algorithms: IJAYA, Slime Mould Algorithm (SMA), Whale Optimization Algorithm (WOA), and conventional CS. Statistical analysis demonstrates that BHCS provides the most precise modeling, achieving a discrepancy of less than 0.034 A between predicted and experimental data.

title: When Agents React: Synthetic Social Resonance for VR Communication Training
authors: Armin Grasnick, Quintus Stierstorfer
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: In this work-in-progress paper, we present a concept of an AI-powered VR training system that leverages a synthetic audience - an adaptive population of generative agents to deliver real-time, emotionally grounded feedback to speakers. Operating in token time, multimodal interaction tokens from the presenter update agent-level affective states and a population-level resonance field, which in turn generates coherent audience reactions. Unlike traditional VR systems that only measure human responses passively, our closed-loop, co-adaptive design transforms audience feedback into an active training signal by simulating social resonance through generative audience models. The framework establishes a bidirectional learning loop in which speakers and audiences adapt together to optimize social communication performance. For learners, the synthetic audience enables on-demand, repeatable practice with lifelike reactions and automated feedback, accelerating skill acquisition. For training providers, it scales delivery without synchronous facilitators, lowers per-session cost, and yields standardized analytics for program improvement.

title: Implementation of Sharding to Improve the Performance of Blockchain
authors: Divyesh Bhatnagar, Anil Gupta
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Blockchain is used in many number of industries such as supply chain, healthcare and cryptocurrency because it is having decentralised Ledger. [1] [2] Blockchain is reliable, but as the users' increases, the blockchain faces scalability issues, such as storing nodes and validating transactions. It can be solved by sharding. [3] In sharding large blockchain network is divided into smaller nodes, which allows parallel processing. [4] This makes the system faster, but there can be security issues if too many shards are used in the node. By this study, we can analyses the security of sharded blockchain. The study has been conducted on the database, which is extracted from a blockchain network, to simulate real-world node and structural patterns. The result of this study gives information about optimising transaction speed in a sharding-based blockchain system. Our findings will conclude how sharding can be effectively implemented within blockchain network which will improve overall performance and solve the problem in the transactional blockchain system.

title: Bi-Fluid Synergy: Unlocking Unprecedented Efficiency in Hybrid Photovoltaic/Thermal Collectors via Numerical Simulation
authors: Chaima Goubar, Mama Bouchaour, Laarej Merad
publish: 2025 IEEE International Multi-Conference on Smart Systems &amp; Green Process (IMC-SSGP)
abstract: This study presents a detailed numerical analysis of the performance of a hybrid photovoltaic/thermal (PV/T) collector that simultaneously uses two heat transfer fluids: air and water. The objective is to evaluate and optimize the overall energy conversion efficiency of the system through a comprehensive thermal model implemented in Python. The model is based on steady-state energy balances applied to each component of the collector. Simulations demonstrate that the dual-fluid configuration significantly enhances both thermal and electrical efficiencies compared to single-fluid systems, due to more effective heat extraction and improved cooling of the photovoltaic cells. Results show that thermal efficiency reaches up to 58.6% with water, while electrical efficiency increases to 13.1% at high flow rates. The combined airwater configuration achieves a maximum overall efficiency of 71.7%, confirming the benefits of dual-fluid cooling in hybrid solar systems. This work highlights the strong potential of bi-fluid PV/T systems as a sustainable energy solution, particularly in regions with high solar irradiance.

title: Assessing Vulnerability of Deepfake Detection Models to Noise-Masked Attacks
authors: Artyom Galimov, Artem Chirkovskiy
publish: 2026 28th International Conference on Digital Signal Processing and its Applications (DSPA)
abstract: Audio deepfake detection models perform reliably in clean audio conditions, but may struggle in noisy environments. Moreover, fraudsters may enhance deepfake samples with genuine background noise to fool an audio deepfake model. This paper introduces a novel metric to quantify the robustness of the model against noise-masked attacks. Using the In-the-Wild (ITW) dataset as a foundation, we construct two large-scale benchmarks consiting more than 380,000 samples each, spanning four SNR levels (6-24 dB) and diverse noise categories from MSSNSD and DEMAND noise datasets. Unlike standard evaluation protocols that re-optimize thresholds per condition, our proposed metric employs a fixed threshold calibrated on clean data to measure three complementary aspects: overall degradation (noise vulnerability), class asymmetry (noise tolerance), and integrated robustness (noise influence). We evaluated three state-of-the-art open source models to test the proposed metric. Our results showed, that the metric is able to capture vulnerability of the models to noise-masked attacks and to measure it. The proposed metric provides a standardized framework for assessing model resilience, revealing that single-source evaluation yields incomplete and potentially misleading conclusions.

title: Integrating Generative AI Into Total Quality Management for Public Health Improvement
authors: Astri Indriyati, Wikan Danar Sunindyo
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Ensuring service quality and satisfaction in public healthcare remains challenging due to limited resources and fragmented data utilization. This study proposes an AI-Enhanced Total Quality Management (TQM) framework that integrates Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI) and Explainable AI (XAI) to support predictive and evidence-based quality improvement. Using an exploratory sequential mixed-methods design, qualitative insights on organizational readiness and ethics were combined with quantitative survey data and machine learning models. Synthetic data generated via GANs were employed for privacy-preserving analytics, while Random Forest, XGBoost, and Artificial Neural Networks were evaluated using cross-validation and statistical significance testing. Results from a public dental hospital case study show that XGBoost achieved the highest predictive performance, and explainability analysis highlights leadership commitment and employee involvement as key determinants of satisfaction. The findings demonstrate that integrating GenAI and XAI into TQM enables transparent, data-driven, and human-centered quality governance in public healthcare.

title: Real-Time Markerless Hand and Object Tracking with Occlusion Awareness for Augmented Reality
authors: Amulya Sakhamuru, Sandeep Vasireddy, Bhavsingh Maloth
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: Augmented reality (AR) applications demand precise and real-time markerless tracking of hands and objects to enable natural interaction. Existing markerless approaches, while effective under controlled conditions, struggle with occlusion, leading to performance degradation in dynamic and cluttered environments. Additionally, many deep learning-based models are computationally heavy, limiting deployment on mobile or edge AR devices. This work aims to design a lightweight, occlusion-aware tracking framework that achieves high accuracy and robustness while maintaining real-time performance on edge platforms. The proposed system introduces an Occlusion-Aware Perception module, which explicitly integrates segmentation, depth ordering, and visibility reasoning to handle partial and severe occlusions. A hybrid CNN-Transformer architecture fuses spatial and temporal features with visibility cues for robust markerless tracking. Training is performed using the Rendered Hand Pose Dataset (RHD) for pretraining and validated on DexYCB and EgoGesture datasets. Optimization through quantization and pruning ensures real-time inference on AR hardware. Experimental evaluations demonstrate a reduction in tracking error by up to 35% under high occlusion compared to CNN-only, LSTM-only, and Transformer-only baselines. The framework sustains <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>30$</tex> FPS with latency below 35 ms on Jetson Nano and HoloLens 2 devices. Case studies in AR-assisted surgery and AR-retail validate practical usability, achieving over 90% accuracy. The proposed framework delivers a compact, robust, and deployable solution for real-time occlusion-aware markerless tracking, bridging algorithmic innovation with practical AR applications.

title: Lightning AutoML Agent: An Offline Framework for Automated Machine Learning
authors: Gold Sharon R, Khalid Abdul Jaleel, Nivi V
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: The high level of manual knowledge to preprocess data, engineer features, select with the use of models, and tune hyperparameters makes training dependable machine learning models difficult. These steps also tend to slow down, be inconsistent and hard to scale once it comes to converting raw data into a deployable model. We present Lightning AutoML Agent, an AutoML agent which is fully automated to resolve these challenges by simplifying the entire machine learning pipeline. At this base, the system consists of a Large Language Model (LLM)-powered cognitive engine that is able to study datasets and comes up with task-specific medical preprocessing and feature engineering plans. This intelligence layer is paired with a high-performance backend implementation based on modular PyTorch implementations of the training and background hyperparameter optimization based on Optuna. The framework favors the supervised learning (classification and regression), and the unsupervised tasks (clustering and the association rule mining). The main contribution of this work is the combination of the decision-making process guided by LLM and scalable AutoML architecture which leads to less manual intervention and less adverse model behavior. Reproducibility and accessibility are enforced by an extensive reporting, open-ended preprocessing and generation of leaderboards, which are the result of automated generation. In general, the Lightning AutoML Agent makes machine learning much more accessible to high-quality due to both strategic and computational intervals being automated.

title: Unraveling Parkinson's Progression with AI: Multi-Model Predictive Analysis and Explainable Diagnostic Biomarker Insight
authors: Aarya Apte, Vibhav Gogate
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Parkinson's Disease (PD) is a progressive neurodegenerative disorder whose clinical progression remains difficult to model due to its heterogeneous symptomatology and limitations in existing tracking methods. This study evaluates the effectiveness of supervised machine learning (ML) techniques for forecasting PD progression over a 3-year time window using high-dimensional, longitudinal biomarker data from the Parkinson's Progression Markers Initiative (PPMI) dataset. The nine predictive models are trained and compared, including linear regression, ridge regression, random forests, gradient boosting, and neural networks on curated input features and clinically validated progression scores. Ensemble models such as Random Forest and Gradient Boosting achieve the highest performance, yielding prediction accuracies between 94.8% and 96.4%. In contrast, deeper models exhibit no performance benefit, highlighting the suitability of simpler architectures for structured clinical data. To enable interpretability, SHAP and LIME are applied, which identify baseline clinical scores, age, REM Sleep Behavior Disorder metrics, and Body Mass Index as the most influential features across both global and patient-specific contexts. These results demonstrate that accurate PD progression prediction is feasible using standard ML methods trained on well-structured biomarker data. Moreover, the integration of model-agnostic explanation techniques facilitates biomarker-level insight, providing a basis for early intervention and individualized treatment planning.

title: Distributional Information Embedding: A Framework for Multi-Bit Watermarking
authors: Haiyun He, Yepeng Liu, Ziqiao Wang, Yongyi Mao, Yuheng Bu
publish: 2025 Asia Pacific Workshop on Data Science and Information Theory (APWDSIT)
abstract: This paper introduces a novel problem, distributional information embedding, motivated by the practical demands of multi-bit watermarking for large language models (LLMs). Unlike traditional information embedding, which embeds information into a pre-existing host signal, LLM watermarking actively controls the text generation process-adjusting the token distribution-to embed a detectable signal. We develop an information-theoretic framework to analyze this distributional information embedding problem, characterizing the fundamental trade-offs among three critical performance metrics: text quality, detectability, and information rate. In the asymptotic regime, we demonstrate that the maximum achievable rate with vanishing error corresponds to the entropy of the LLM's output distribution and increases with higher allowable distortion. We also characterize the optimal watermarking scheme to achieve this rate. Extending the analysis to the finite-token case with non-i.i.d. tokens, we identify schemes that maximize detection probability while adhering to constraints on false alarm and distortion.

title: Additional Reviewers
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 Asia Pacific Workshop on Data Science and Information Theory (APWDSIT)

title: Staged Training for Robust Crop and Weed Detection Under Temporal Domain Shift Using YOLO26
authors: Dmitriy Poleshchenko, Ilia Mikhailov
publish: 2026 28th International Conference on Digital Signal Processing and its Applications (DSPA)
abstract: This paper investigates temporal domain shift in crop and weed detection from aerial imagery during the growing season using the PhenoBench dataset. We demonstrate that the YOLO26m model, trained exclusively on an early phenological stage, experiences significant degradation in mean Average Precision (mAP) on subsequent stages due to changes in plant morphology and increased scene complexity. To improve robustness, we propose a staged training strategy that sequentially trains the detector on temporal domains corresponding to different acquisition dates within the same field and crop. Experimental results show that staged training exceeds the baseline (training only on the initial date) by +22.3 p.p. in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{m A P}$</tex> at late-season stages and approaches the oracle scenario trained on all dates. Quantitative analysis of domain differences via Frechet Distance in YOLO26m's feature space demonstrates that temporal domain shift magnitude correlates with observed detection quality degradation, highlighting the effectiveness of staged training for accounting for phenological scene variability.

title: Forecasting Regional Oil-Hazardous Waste Generation in the Philippines Through Arima Modeling
authors: Mayolito III Gulleban, Lheann Cabase, Roel Andrei Carreon, John Archilles Deduyco, Florence Jean Talirongan, Roseclaremath Caroro, Phoebe Claire Hermione Gation, Jazzen Keithly Yusop, Jon Kristian Luste, Earl RJ Hocoy, Femarie Sumile, Prince Rainier Lomo
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: The improper management of oil-hazardous waste presents escalating environmental and public health risks in the Philippines amid rapid industrialization and urban expansion. This study clustered regional oil waste data and forecasted waste generation trends across Philippine regions from 2025 to 2029 using k-means clustering and an Autoregressive Integrated Moving Average (ARIMA) model. Kmeans clustering using 2020 and 2024 data to identify similar patterns resulted in 3 regions in the first cluster and 14 in the second cluster. Moreover, in the forecasting results, critical regional disparities were revealed: Region X and Region XII showed persistent increases due to industrial expansion, while National Capital Region, Cordillera Administrative Region, Region III, and Region VII exhibited decreasing patterns. Ten regions displayed volatile patterns with fluctuations in 2025, followed by alternating trends that eventually stabilized or showed slight upward trends from 2027 to 2029. The Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM) was excluded from the forecasting analysis only due to the lack of sufficient historical data required for the ARIMA model. The study features the significance of data-driven forecasting models in supporting evidence-based policymaking and environmental planning strategy for maintainable waste management in the Philippines.

title: Physiological Stress Detection and Artificial Intelligence: A Survey
authors: Kawthar Alsayed, Hamza Ghandorh, Wael M.S. Yafooz
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Stress gradually weakens both the body and mind, contributing to reduced immunity, sleep disturbances, and serious conditions such as depression, anxiety, and cardiovascular diseases. In addition to health effects, stress is also a factor that decreases work performance in terms of decision-making and productivity. Thus, timely identification of stress will be significant in avoiding negative consequences. Nonetheless, the traditional measurement of psychological scales and self-reports is biased and situation-specific, and it can be different in people and environments. Recent researches respond to this by exploring ways of detecting stress using physiological measurements that are recorded in non-invasive and unobtrusive means. Thus, the initial search in this paper determined 76 relevant studies (2019-2025), which were then screened and finally 25 papers were selected to be thoroughly analyzed concerning physiological-signal-based stress detection using computational and learning-based stress detection. The review is a summary of publicly available datasets and commonly used signals and features (EDA, ECG, and PPG), metrics of reported evaluation, and most critical gaps pertaining to validation procedures, robustness, and real-world implementation. The survey gives a very concise overview of datasets, signals, methods, metrics and open challenges to physiological stress detection.

title: Deep Segmentation of Retinal Layers and Fluid in Diabetic Retinopathy OCT with UU-MAMBA
authors: Rolf Alexander Klæboe, Raju Shrestha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Accurate segmentation of retinal layers and pathological fluid regions in Spectral-Domain Optical Coherence Tomography (SD-OCT) images is critical for early diagnosis and monitoring of retinal diseases. However, this task is challenged by severe class imbalance, low contrast boundaries, and complex anatomical variations. In this paper, we propose a modified UU-Mamba architecture tailored for fine-grained retinal layer and fluid segmentation on the Duke SD-OCT dataset. Our approach integrates gated Mamba blocks for efficient global context modeling and introduces a customized Uncertainty-Aware Loss (UAL) function that strategically combines Tversky loss, class-specific Focal loss, and Cross-Entropy loss to mitigate class imbalance and enhance minority class sensitivity. We further employ Sharpness-Aware Minimization (SAM) during training to improve generalization across underrepresented structures. Experimental results demonstrate that our method achieves competitive performance, surpassing human expert segmentation, with an average Dice Similarity Coefficient (DSC) score of 0.85 compared to the human expert's 0.81. Extensive ablation studies validate the individual contributions of each proposed component, confirming the efficacy of our tailored loss design and regularization strategy in enhancing segmentation quality and improving precision for challenging structures like fluid regions.

title: Towards Autonomous Spectral Mapping for Soil Analysis: Development and Validation of a Robot-Deployable DIY Spectrophotometer
authors: Ghouti Abdellaoui, Anisse Chiali, Fayçal Dergal, Mohamed Ramzi Bousmaha, Maissara Bellouati
publish: 2025 IEEE International Multi-Conference on Smart Systems &amp; Green Process (IMC-SSGP)
abstract: This paper presents the complete design, implementation, and experimental validation of a DIY spectrophotometer embedded on an autonomous mobile robot specifically developed for in-situ soil analysis. The system employs accessible components including Raspberry Pi, USB camera, and diffraction grating, integrated with a custom-designed mobile robotic platform featuring articulated suspension for agricultural terrain navigation. A comprehensive Python-based software framework was developed to automate image acquisition, spectral extraction, and analysis of absorbance and transmittance curves based on Beer-Lambert law principles. Experimental validation against a commercial Horiba LabRAM HR Evolution Raman spectrometer demonstrated the system's capability to detect characteristic spectral peaks of aspirin, silica, and malachite green with 90-97% qualitative agreement. The integrated system enables automated georeferenced spectral mapping, providing a robust and costeffective solution for precision agriculture and environmental monitoring applications.

title: Hybrid Machine Learning Based Model for Early Parkinson's Disease Detection
authors: Rafael Sotelo, Carlos Perez, José Santisteban
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Early detection of Parkinson's disease remains challenging due to the absence of accessible biomarkers and objective diagnostic tools. This study presents a hybrid machine learning model that combines structured data and graphical biomarkers through a multimodal late-fusion approach. The proposed method combines Gradient Boosting and Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) models, achieving improved interpretability and computational efficiency compared to attention-based fusion models. Results show that Gradient Boosting achieved 95.25% accuracy, while CNN achieved 97.40% accuracy. Their adaptive late fusion attained 98.27% accuracy and a 96.6% F1-score. A 5-fold crossvalidation confirmed robustness and absence of overfitting, demonstrating potential for cost-effective and reproducible early diagnosis of Parkinson's disease in clinical contexts across Latin America.

title: Artificial Intelligence and Leadership in Small Businesses: A Hawaiˋi-Based Perspective with Broader Implications
authors: Eduard O. Merc, Michael Rose
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: This study examines how artificial intelligence (AI) is being adopted by small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) in Hawaii and explores the cultural, organizational, and leadership factors that shape this adoption. Drawing on survey responses from 87 SME leaders, the research identifies clear patterns in how AI is currently used, with adoption strongest in marketing analytics and customer service automation and considerably lower in leadership-oriented applications, consistent with global SME trends [1], [2], [3]. The findings show that AI integration in Hawaii is not driven solely by technological readiness but is deeply influenced by cultural values emphasizing trust, interpersonal relationships, and community reputation [4], [5]. Leaders consistently expressed strong support for AI as an augmentation tool, particularly for improving insight, forecasting, and decision quality, aligning with established research on AIassisted leadership rather than AI-driven automation [6], [7], [8], [9]. Barriers including cost constraints, limited technical expertise, and data security concerns further restrict adoption, reflecting widely documented challenges in SME digital transformation [1], [2], [10], [11]. Overall, the study underscores the importance of culturally informed, human-centered AI strategies for SMEs operating in relationship-based or geographically isolated contexts. These insights contribute to a broader understanding of how AI can be integrated responsibly and effectively within culturally distinct leadership systems.

title: A New Scheme for Continuous and Discrete States Estimation Under MDADT Constraints
authors: Rafika El Harabi, Manel Atitallah, Mohamed Naceur Abdelkrim
publish: 2025 IEEE International Multi-Conference on Smart Systems &amp; Green Process (IMC-SSGP)
abstract: This paper aims to design a new robust estimator scheme dedicated to switched linear systems subject to unknown disturbances and noises. This algorithm is able to estimate both continuous and discrete behaviors owing to the unknown synchronous switching signal estimator block consisting of eventtriggered mechanism and the unknown input observer to estimate the system states directly. Using linear matrix inequalities (LMIs), novel stability criteria is formulated taking into account the mode-dependent average dwell time (MDADT) constraints. This later is able to reduce the conservativeness of stability results. Finally, a numerical example is proved to illustrate the applicability of the proposed framework.

title: Process Control Block Designs in Embedded Operating Systems: A Comparative Review
authors: Binh T. Nguyen
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Embedded operating systems (EOSes) are the foundation of modern real-time and Internet-of-Things (IoT) devices, where deterministic task scheduling and efficient memory use are critical.
