To handle renewable intermittency and rapid load power variations, the system uses a battery for high energy support and a supercapacitor for fast power transients in DC microgrid applications. A modified droop control technique (MDCT) is developed for power sharing between the storage units, maintaining stable DC bus voltage and limiting excessive current stress on the battery. The control strategy allocates transient power to the supercapacitor and steady-state power to the battery according to their operating dynamics. In addition, a power management scheme is implemented to ensure proper power balance in DC bus and seamless grid interaction under different operating conditions. MATLAB/Simulink simulations were conducted to validate the proposed method in the microgrid system. The results demonstrate improved voltage regulation, reduced battery current ripple, and enhanced system stability.

title: Design and Performance Analysis of a Wild Goat Optimization Tuned PID Controller for AP Applications
authors: Akshaya Kumar Patra, Smitta Ranjan Dutta, Manoj Kumar Debnath, Anuja Nanda, Alok Kumar Mishra, Sanjeeb Kumar Kar
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: The current investigation presents the design and performance evaluation of a PID controller tuned through the Wild Goat Optimization Algorithm (WGOA) to achieve accurate Blood Glucose (BG) regulation within a closed-loop Artificial Pancreas (AP) system for individuals living with Type I Diabetes Mellitus (TIDM). In this framework, WGOA is utilized to fine-tune the PID parameters, enhancing the controller's ability to manage nonlinear behavior, physiological uncertainties, and external disturbances associated with glucose-insulin interactions. A validated physiological patient model is employed to evaluate controller performance under varying conditions such as meal disturbances, changes resulting from daily activity levels and sensor noise. WGO-based PID controller is examined basing on key performance indicators including overshoot, undershoot, settling time, robustness, and insulin delivery efficiency. Simulation outcomes show that the proposed strategy successfully regulates glucose to normo-glycemic levels with steady and trustworthy control behavior. Furthermore, comparisons with other optimization-driven control methods highlight the superior robustness and performance of the WGO-PID approach, indicating strong feasibility for real-time biomedical control applications.

title: Reinforcement Learning-Based Optimal Load Shedding Techniques for Smart Grids to Lower Dynamic Load Alteration Attacks
authors: Aditi Chatterjee, Barnali Kundu, Pritam Chandra
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: Cyberattacks on the smart grid can have severe repercussions, such as data barriers, system outages, grid operations manipulation, and even physical harm to vital infrastructure. By altering the system frequency, dynamic load alteration attacks are a type of cyber-physical attack that might jeopardize the stability of power systems. Matlab/Simulink software was used in this study to model and simulate an IEEE-14 bus system. By adding dynamic load changes to three of the grid's generators, an attack model was successfully constructed. Frequency instability brought on by the load alteration attack signal ultimately resulted in 100% load shedding of the generators. A reinforcement learning mitigator agent was used to lessen the frequency instability problems and the losses brought on by 100% load shedding. After a short transitory period, the frequency eventually settles down to its steady state value, which is 60 Hz, as the reinforcement learning agent cleverly sheds part of the loads to stabilize the bus frequency. The effectiveness of the suggested reinforcement learning agent is confirmed by the simulation results.

title: Tariff-Responsive Battery Dispatch for Residential Rooftop PV Systems: Cost Reduction and Grid Relief Under Time-of-Use Pricing
authors: Ashutosh Malik, Rajesh Kumar Sarkar, Chirodeep Bakli
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: Residential rooftop solar photovoltaic (PV) systems with battery storage increase reliability and alleviate peak grid demand. Integrating these systems effectively requires Time-of-Use (TOU) tariffs to ensure optimal operation. Existing literature often relies on complex optimizations with limited practical use or focuses on price arbitrage without accounting for resilience during grid outages. This paper presents a tariff responsive battery dispatch framework for residential PV—battery systems that balances electricity cost reduction, grid relief, and backup capability. The framework introduces a partitioned dispatch strategy where battery capacity is structurally divided into a reserved segment for outages and a dynamic segment for tariff-driven use. Discharge decisions are coupled with TOU charges for cost-aware optimization based on the battery's state of charge. Benefits are maximized by using PV to meet loads during high solar availability and deploying battery energy during high-tariff periods. Three cases, midday peak, evening peak, and uniform pro-file, are analyzed under realistic Indian load patterns and solar irradiance. Across all profiles, the hybrid system achieves lower costs than a flat-rate, grid-only scenario. The evening-peak case shows maximum savings due to the generation-load mismatch. Simulations also indicate reduced grid imports and peak demand alleviation without requiring explicit grid-level control. By retaining a dedicated reserve for outages within a tariff-responsive strategy, this approach reconciles economic gains with the practical need for supply reliability. The control logic is intentionally transparent, making it suitable for real-world residential deployment. This framework serves as both a household energy management strategy and a reference for designing tariffs and incentives under initiatives like India's PM Surya Ghar Yojana.

title: Hardware-Intelligent Flux Damping Based Fault Ride-Through Control for DFIG-Integrated Wind Energy Systems
authors: Subhendu Sekhar Sahoo, Prinkle Vohra, Jharana Behera, T. Kokilavani, Priyabrata Mohapatra, Ritambhra Katoch, M. Krishna Kishore
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: Due to higher efficiency and increased dependability, wind energy systems are becoming more popular. Wind energy conversion systems that use a doubly fed induction generator (DFIG) show exceptional performance and efficiency, outperforming other generator kinds. Their benefits are further enhanced by the cost-effectiveness of a partially rated converter. The system becomes more vulnerable, when the stator terminal is directly connected to the grid, and any disruption can negatively impact the generator's output. To enhance the stability of wind energy systems based on DFIGs, this research creates a ride-through plan. Based on the fault scenario, a effective ride-through method that makes use of a advanced demagnetizing method in circuit is suggested for fault situations. In a MATLAB-based environment, the system is simulated. To reduce uncertainties efficiently, a control scheme is presented. To assess its effectiveness, the suggested plan's performance is also compared to that of established methods. The result shows the efficacy of the system by showing the restriction of stator current, rotor current, DC Link over voltage and electromagnetic torque oscillations.

title: A Novel Compact Two-Port MIMO DRA for Sub-6 GHz Application
authors: Manoj Kumar Vishwakarma, Jugul Kishor, Jayanta Ghosh
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: The proposed work shows a compact circular dielectric resonator antenna (CDRA) based two-port MIMO antenna. This antenna has a rectangular RT duroid substrate on which two CDRAs are placed. Each DRA is excited via a slot on the ground plane, which has a rectangular geometry, and a microstrip feed line, which is modified just below the slot. This excites the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{H E M}_{11} \delta$</tex> mode,-resulting in an isolation better than <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1 5 ~ d B}$</tex> for this MIMO antenna. The antenna operates within the frequency range <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{3}. \mathrm{2}-\mathrm{3}. \mathrm{7} \text{GHz}$</tex>, which covers the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{5 G ~ N R}$</tex> n78 band. The antenna provides high gain, and its radiation pattern shows pure polarization purity. The MIMO DRA demonstrates strong diversity performance, as validated by measurements of MIMO parameters ECC, MEG, TARC, etc. The structure neither has DGS on the ground plane nor does it use any decoupling structure; hence, it is simple and compact. The measured results closely match the simulated ones, validating the design and making it suitable for Sub-6 GHz 5G base stations and portable devices.

title: Systematic Survey of Deep Learning Algorithms for Multi-Class Skin Disease Detection
authors: Sachin U. Balvir, Aditya C. Dukare, Vansh G. Gonnade, Ankit S. Wandhe, Sahil M. Nimje, Devvrat A. Welekar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical, Communication, and Computing Technologies (iCONECCT)
abstract: Dermatological conditions are challenging and this implies that there are difficulties relating to accurate and timely diagnosis of dermatological conditions. The high visual similarity of many classes of skin lesions has led to modern medicine in the related field due to poor distinctness on an individual basis surrounding the subject. Although early diagnosis plays a pivotal role in effective treatment and the diagnoses of malignant diseases in particular, the diagnosis after the onset of the disease is quite significant. The manual diagnostic procedure may be subjective and it may be restricted by expertise availability dermatologists. This has prompted automated systems development, deep learning being one of them developed as a potent model toward this work. This paper is the systematic survey of the deep learning algorithms of the highest achievable levels precisely used in multi-class classification skin diseases. In our review, we found a number of important facts: the profession is largely defined by through transfer learning based on Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), taking advantage of established constructions like ResNet, DenseNet and MobileNet. Concurrently, newer architectures like Vision Transformers (ViTs) are beginning to show promise. Despite significant progress persistent challenges remain most notably the scarcity of large, publicly available datasets which leads to significant data imbalance issues and the inherent “black box” nature of deep learning models which hinders clinical trust and adoption. Therefore, the future outlook for the field points towards the development of more robust models that require less data an increasing focus on model interpretability through Explainable AI (XAI) and the deployment of efficient and validated algorithms on mobile platforms to assist both clinicians and patients in preliminary diagnosis.

title: Barriers and Enablers of Digital Twin Adoption for Sustainable Construction: A Systematic Literature Review
authors: Muhammad Ahsan Soomro, AbdulLateef Olanrewaju, Shalini Sanmargaraja
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Innovations in Information and Communication Technologies (IICT)
abstract: Construction significantly contributes to a country's GDP. As this growing sector expands, adopting sustainable strategies becomes essential to ensure operational longevity and minimise environmental impacts. Digital Twin (DT), which offers a virtual replica of physical assets and processes, can serve as a transformative tool for enhancing sustainability throughout the building lifecycle. This study presents a systematic literature review (SLR) on the key enablers and barriers to implementing DT in construction for sustainability. The review utilises recent publications from 2020-2025, sourced from Scopus and other databases. It identifies five major barriers: technological, economic, organisational, data-related, and regulatory. Similarly, it highlights the main enablers: technology, organisational, economic, and regulatory, along with their sub-factors. The findings demonstrate that DT and operational construction resources have the potential to support sustainability from environmental, economic, and social perspectives. However, barriers such as high costs and data interoperability issues limit adoption, while a shortage of expertise remains a significant obstacle to wider DT implementation. The study suggests practical steps for future research on DT and construction 4.0, aiming to foster a digital, resilient, and more sustainable construction industry.

title: Prescan-Based Migration and Optimization Framework for Autonomous Driving Detection Algorithms
authors: Yumei Zhou, Yongjie Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechatronics, Automation and Intelligent Control (MAIC)
abstract: Development of self-driving cars still faces competition to increase speed, accuracy, and dependability of the detection algorithms as a top major research issue. On the other hand, the real-world context is intricate and multifaceted. Furthermore, data gathering is resource intensive, and the costs of annotation are unacceptably high. Moreover, although Prescan has the ability to create synthetic data at scale, it does not sufficiently bridge the gap between real and virtual worlds. To address these issues, this paper outlines the Prescan-based migration and optimization framework which is comprised of four core modules: data processing, adaptation of the domain, optimization, and validation. The framework supports effective migration and enhancement of algorithm performance due to the application of transfer learning to address cross domain issues. The experiments conducted under this framework showed that the application of the proposed solution improved the influence of domain shift, obtaining an mAP of 0.85 and a frame rate of 42 FPS. This represents a major enhancement in accuracy.

title: Spartan-7 FPGA Based Implementation of ChaCha20 Accelerator for RISC-V Systems
authors: Yusuf Ahmad, Kamlesh Kumar Singh
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: ChaCha20 is a stream cipher widely adopted in modern communication systems due to its simplicity and strong cryptographic properties. While software implementations are common, hardware acceleration is often required in embedded and resource constrained systems to meet performance and power constraints. This work presents a register transfer level implementation of a ChaCha20 encryption core designed with an emphasis on low dynamic power consumption. The proposed design employs a serialized 32 bit data path, reducing switching activity at the cost of increased latency, which is acceptable for low throughput and control oriented applications. The architecture is organized around a memory mapped interface to support integration as a peripheral accelerator in processor based systems. The design is described entirely at the RTL level and evaluated through synthesis and power analysis. Implementation results are used to examine the impact of architectural trade offs on power, timing, and resource utilization without modifying the underlying cryptographic algorithm.

title: Silver-Graphene Nanocomposite Flexible Strain Sensors for Detecting Phonetic Events in Audible and Silent Speech
authors: Ishant G. Patil, Ting Yang Ling, Khong Wui Gan, Suan Hui Pu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Sensors and Nanotechnology (SENNANO)
abstract: Clear speech detection, unaffected by external noise or hearing impairments, is essential for effective daily communication. Strain sensors offer a promising approach by recognizing the subtle vibrations of throat muscles near the vocal cords when a person speaks. Silent communication, which relies on these muscle movements rather than audible sound, is especially valuable for individuals unable to produce vocal signals. In this study, sensitive and flexible graphene-silver nanocomposite strain sensors were designed and fully fabricated via inkjet printing for human speech recognition without external acoustic noise interference. A baseline characterization workflow was developed and implemented to ensure repeatable sensing performance, focusing on sensor placement on the larynx region to improve speech recognition accuracy. Preliminary results revealed that the meander patterned strain sensors struggle to capture complex phonetic features such as plosives and fricatives. To address this limitation, we introduced and evaluated an enhanced meander pattern strain sensor that demonstrated improved responsiveness to neck muscle deformation during the articulation of both audible and silent phonemes. This work highlights the baseline studies of enhanced patterns that significantly improve the speech detection sensitivity, particularly for plosives, fricatives and complex words.

title: An ANOVA-Driven Deep Feature Optimization Approach for Strawberry and Cherry Leaf Disease Detection Using VGG-16 and EfficientNet-B5
authors: Lakshya Kumar Tyagi, Lucky Sharma, Manish Patel, Raghav Gupta, Sarvachan Verma
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: Plant disease identification plays an essential role in improving agricultural output and ensuring crop security. This paper introduces a reliable and efficient automated system for detecting strawberry leaf scorch and cherry powdery mildew using Convolutional Neural Networks on a dataset of 6000 images classified into four classes. VGG-16 and EfficientNetB5 were used as pretrained feature extractors on a curated dataset of strawberry and cherry leaf samples. Features extracted from both models were concatenated and subjected to ANOVA F-test based feature selection to retain the most statistically discriminative features. A Support Vector Machine with an RBF kernel was then applied for final classification. Experimental observations show that combining EfficientNet-B5 and VGG-16 with feature extraction, feature selection, and SVM classification delivers highly accurate and consistent predictions. The proposed hybrid model achieves 99.8 % accuracy, outperforming VGG16 alone at 91.5 % and EfficientNet-B5 alone at 95.6 %. The hybrid approach demonstrates superior performance, minimal overfitting, and strong generalization compared to using either backbone individually.

title: Green Synthesis of Aliphatic Esters Using Acid-Treated Perlite Catalysts Under Microwave Irradiation: Optimization and Mathematical Modelling Using RSM
authors: Monika Sharma, Sakshi Kabra Malpani, Dipti Singh, Deepti Goyal
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: The present study investigates optimized green synthesis of aliphatic esters via a series of microwave assisted Fischer esterification reactions of propionic acid and aliphatic alcohol using acid-treated perlite catalysts. In this study, the % yield of reaction products were predicted and optimized using a mathematical modelling approach- Response Surface Methodology (RSM). The central composite design (CCD) of RSM was used to determine the effect of independent reaction variables like time, temperature, catalyst dose, power output on % yield of reaction products using Expert Design software. Afterwards, experimental results were analyzed using RSM technique. The sufficiency of the developed model was examined through ANOVA, coefficient of determination R(0.97), R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>(0.95), adjusted R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>(0.94), and error analysis MSE (1.76). Further the modelling results were validated with experimental data. It shows that the modelling results are in good agreement with the actual experimental results.

title: Low-Cost IoT-Integrated Wearable Platform for Home-Based Sleep Apnea Screening and Posture Analysis
authors: Hira Amjad, Muhammad Noman, Danish Memon, Azam Jan
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Innovations in Information and Communication Technologies (IICT)
abstract: Sleep apnea is a common sleep disorder that is connected to hypertension, cardiovascular diseases, and a decreased quality of life, yet it is still underdiagnosed. The current clinical gold standard for sleep apnea diagnosis, polysomnography (PSG), is expensive, invasive, and impractical for large-scale screening. This creates a need for inexpensive, non-intrusive alternatives. This paper describes a multimodal, Internet of Things (IoT) wearable system that consolidates SpO 2 and heart rate monitoring (MAX30102) using photoplethysmography, inertial posture tracking (MPU6050), and edge AI for snoring detection. Cloud and smartphone connectivity using ESP32 microcontrollers permits real-time health data visualization. Experimental health data validated against clinical oximeter standards <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{SpO}_{2}:97.8\%\pm 1.2$</tex>; heart rate: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$79 \pm 2$</tex> BPM), during monitoring of 15 participants, demonstrated correct posture classification during supine and lateral positioning, and recorded high-performance snoring detection (training accuracy 93 %, testing accuracy 98.8 %). The suggested design improves the availability and practicality of sleep tracking due to the accessibility of inexpensive sensors, mobile garnet feedback, and several monitoring methods. This work illustrates the potential for sleep apnea detection at early stages due to the ability to continuously and non-invasively monitored, and paves the path toward wider clinical validation.

title: ALMS Based Reduced-Switch UPQC Integrated Bidirectional Converter Supported Electric Vehicle Charging Station
authors: Mrutyunjaya Mangaraj, Rishikesh Sreepathi
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: The increasing penetration of electric vehicle (EV) charging infrastructure and power-electronics-based distributed generation has exacerbated power quality (PQ) issues in modern distribution networks, including voltage disturbances, harmonic distortion, and reactive power imbalance. This paper proposes a reduced-switch nine-switch Unified Power Quality Conditioner (UPQC) as an effective alternative to the conventional 12-switch UPQC and standalone Distribution Static Compensator (DSTATCOM) for three-phase three-wire (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3 \mathrm{P}-3 ~\mathrm{W}$</tex>) systems. The proposed topology achieves simultaneous voltage and current compensation without increasing the switch volt-ampere (VA) rating, thereby reducing semiconductor count, converter complexity, and system cost. An isolated bidirectional DC–DC converter is integrated to facilitate EV charging applications, enabling grid-to-vehicle (G2V) operation, DC-link voltage regulation, and dynamic PQ compensation under variable grid conditions. Simulation studies carried out in MATLAB/Simulink demonstrate that the proposed configuration significantly improves PQ indices, ensures stable DC-link voltage regulation, and provides reliable energy management, validating its suitability for sustainable EV charging stations.

title: Simulation Study of Metal - Doped Graphene/Hexagonal Boron Nitride for Ammonia Sensing Application
authors: Ainun Khairiyah Taib, Zaharah Johari, Shaharin Fadzli Abd Rahman, Mohd Fairus Mohd Yusoff, Fatimah Khairiah Abd Hamid, Suhaila Isaak
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Sensors and Nanotechnology (SENNANO)
abstract: Graphene/Hexagonal Boron Nitride (hBN) has gained the attention of numerous researchers developing sensing devices due to its excellent physical and electrical properties. This study uses Density Functional Theory (DFT) simulation to investigate the effect of metal substitution on Graphene/hBN for adsorption of ammonia, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{NH}_{3}$</tex> gas molecules. The energy band gap and density of state (DOS) are simulated to determine the changes in the conductivity. The charge transfer, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$Q$</tex>, adsorption energy, E<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ads</inf> and recovery time, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\tau$</tex> are determined to evaluate the sensing interaction between the molecules and Graphene/hBN. Different types of metal, namely Platinum, Pt and Aurum, Au, are used to evaluate the gas adsorption behavior. Within the investigated simulation condition, results shows that metal doping affect the energy band gap. Most notably Pt and Au doped on Graphene/hBN significantly influence <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{E}_{\text{ads.}}$</tex> A single <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{P t}$</tex> doped improve <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{E}_{\text{ads}}$</tex> from +0.9 eV (undoped) to −1.41 eV. An additional metal substituted exhibit slight reduction to −1.28 eV and −1.21 eV for bi - and tri - Pt doped. Single Au doped on Graphene/hBN shows very minimal adsorption (+0.02 eV). This is markably improves to - 1.46 eV and −1.59 eV for additional bi - and tri - Au doped. A single Pt - doped exhibits a recovery time that longer than Au doped likely due to stronger binding of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{NH}_{3}$</tex> molecules on the Graphene/hBN surface. These findings provide insight in Graphene/hBN surface treatment approach for developing high performance sensor.

title: Design of Metamaterial MSPA for Wireless Applications
authors: Jayasukumari. I, Themozhi. G, Amali. C
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: A radiating patch on one side of the dielectric substrate and, consequently, the ground plane on the other side makes up a metamaterial coplanar waveguide patch antenna. Pure copper is utilised as the ground plane (conducting material) and as a patch, whereas FR-4 is used as the substrate (dielectric material). Several feeding methods are used in the design and simulation of the Metamaterial MSPA (Microstrip Patch Antenna). The frequency range in which the antenna operates is 3 to 6 gigahertz. The microwave studio of Computer Simulation Technology (CST) was used to create the high frequency Metamaterial coplanar waveguide patch antenna. In antenna design, the metamaterial is utilised to make the antenna smaller. In order to boost signal strength, which is crucial for long-range wireless networks, the antenna is examined for a number of criteria, including radiation pattern, gain, directivity, and efficiency, using microstrip line feed and coplanar waveguide feed.

title: Evaluating YOLOv12 and RT-DETR for Robust Brain Tumor Detection in MRI Scans
authors: Satyendra Yadav, Isha Saini, Vidushi Sharma, Rajiv Ratn Shah
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Accurate and quick detection of brain tumors from MRI scans is critical for its timely diagnosis and treatment planning. The evaluation of MRI images manually is strenuous and susceptible to variability among experts. Deep learning-based object detection models have turned up as promising solutions for automating tumor localization and classification. In this research we have compared two state-of-the-art architectures, YOLOv12 and RT-DETR, for the detection of brain tumors on two MRI datasets, containing multiple tumor classes. Both the models are trained and assessed under baseline and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3 \times$</tex> augmented conditions. This assessment is based on metrics such as Precision, Recall, mAP@0.5, mAP@0.5:0.95, and Accuracy. Our experimental results proved that data augmentation remarkably raises model performance, improving both precision and generalization. YOLOv12 demonstrates higher overall precision, while the RTDETR shows great performance improvements on augmented dataset due to its design based on transformers. These findings further extend the importance of augmentation within medical imaging and validate YOLOv12 as a strong, efficient solution toward the automated detection of brain tumors in MRI scans.

title: Hybrid Force and Torque Control of a Four-Finger Robotic Gripper Using Pid and Tactile Feedback in Simulink Environment
authors: Azam Ameen, Mishkat Nizami, Mohd. Suhaib
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: This paper presents the design and simulation of a four-fingered robotic gripper with hybrid force/torque control using MATLAB Simulink and Simscape Multibody. There are three revolute joints in each finger, thus the 12-DOF system is tendon-driven by PID controllers. In virtual tactile sensing, the spatial actuating force blocks can be used for real-time imitating. Position and force-controlled modes are switchable dynamically by the finite state machine implemented inside Stateflow. The system adapts the gripping of five object geometries- sphere, cube, cylinder, disc and ellipse. Simulations reveal true force control (5N ± 0.2 N), small angle error (<3°), and good torque control (~ 0.25 Nm). These observations revealed the effectiveness of the system to model “human-like dexterity” and thus “intelligent robotic manipulation in unstructured scenarios” can be realized through the system.

title: Landslide Detection and Warning System Using NB-IoT
authors: Muralidharan Perumal
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: This project describes about Landslide detection and warning system using IoT sensors, Narrow Band - IoT (NB-IoT) and Wireless Sensor Networks (WSN). NB-IoT are deployed along with BLE (BlueTooth Low Energy) with STAR Topology of sensore nodes. This project encompasses possibilities of early warning system, installtsion of monitoring system, setting up of appropriate alarm levels and alerting system to public and Govt authorities like District Administration (Ex: District Collector), Natural Disaster Management Authorities. Each node is deployed with distance between them as 20 mts. These sensor nodes will communicate via BLE 5 (Nordic nRF 52840 device) with Ultrs low power for communication. Among multiple sensors, one will be the master/gateway (Nordic nRF 9160) which will communicate with cloud and pass necessary crucial data like GPS, sensor data, node conectivity info etc. Also it will monitor and communicate with slave device's connectivity status to central control room. In the event of landslide event, based on the expert judgement, the possibility of landslide is hinted and alerting sytem sends alerts to public and govt authorities. Visible danger light and sound like siren alerts are provided in nearby roads to alert passing vehicles. Considering alert system, the nearby living public are alerted via SMS. Government authorities like District/Village administration, Natural disaster management authorities are alerted via email.

title: Design and Development of a Mobile Application for Homework Supervision and Management
authors: Mritha Ramalingam, R. Puviarasi, Elanchezhian Chinnavan
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: There is a growing trend of diminished motivation among school students to complete homework assignments, as they frequently devise methods to circumvent these responsibilities. Educators encounter difficulties in overseeing students' activities outside the classroom, and there is a prevailing belief that parents may not enforce strict homework guidelines. Additionally, students often resort to providing excuses or misleading their parents about the status of their assignments. This research introduces a strategy to enhance communication between educators and parents, thereby minimizing students' opportunities to neglect homework obligations. The central objective of this study is to create a mobile application that records and disseminates information regarding homework assignments, allowing both educators and parents to effectively track students' progress. The application is designed with intuitive interfaces to ensure user accessibility. Considering the extensive use of smartphones, a mobile platform was chosen for the development of this application.

title: A Real-Time Audio-Visual System for Indian Currency Authentication Using Probabilistic Neural Networks in Python
authors: B. Ravi Chandra, P Akshay Babu, P Abdul Subhan, R Dilip Kumar, Ajay Roy
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Fake money continues to be a significant economic risk in the modern digital age. The demand for a low-cost software-driven solution is made clear by the high cost and inaccessibility of conventional hardwarebased detectors. This article presents a real-time audiovisual Indian currency authentication system that makes use of a probabilistic neural network (PNN) that is written in Python. The model makes use of hybrid feature extraction, including color histograms, Sobel, LBP, and HOG, along with CLAHE for light normalization. A trained PNN uses live video from an IP camera to classify notes worth between ₹10 and ₹2000, giving immediate vocal input. The experimental findings demonstrate great precision, inexpensive computation, and excellent applicability for real-time application in banking and retail settings.

title: AI-Driven Hybrid Framework for Lung Cancer Detection Using Deep and Machine Learning Techniques
authors: Shilpa Sharma, Vijay Mohan Shrimal, Shubham Sharma, Tapasya Sharma, Girish Paliwal, Kanta Prasad Sharma, Madani Hamdi Mohammed Rashed
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Globally, lung cancer continues to rank among the leading causes of mortality. The lack of sophisticated technology choices for screening and the fact that the sickness is typically discovered at the final stage are the main causes of this. Consequently, this work creates a hybrid AI-based system that combines machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) methods to make lung cancer detection quick and accurate. A machine learning classifier such as Random Forest or Support Vector Machine (SVM) is used for the final diagnosis after the created system uses convolutional neural networks (CNN) for automatic feature extraction from computed tomography (CT) images. Such a hybridization allows the model to leverage the deep networks' superior feature extraction ability and also, maintain the interpretability and the efficiency of traditional ML algorithms. The proposed model through rigorous experiments on benchmark datasets is reported to achieve classification accuracy, sensitivity, and specificity of 97.8%, 96.5%, and 98.2%, respectively, thus, outperforming the conventional standalone methods. Besides, the designed scheme is perfect in differentiating malignant and benign lung nodules under various imaging conditions.

title: Psycho-Intelligent Dialogue Agents for Enhancing Emotional Self-Regulation in Autistic Teenagers
authors: Celestine Iwendi, Negin Aboutorabi, Adedeji Edward Adesola, Collins Lemeke, Gladys Chisom Okoro, Vandana Sharma
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Autistic adolescents often experience the inability to identify their emotions and self-regulate them, thus creating the impulse for the construction of intelligent assistive technologies. Building on this premise, this work proposes a novel Psycho- Intelligent Dialogue Agent (PIDA) system, which attempts to incorporate advances in affective computing, contextualised rea-soning, and psychotherapeutic dialogues, in aiding emotional self- regulation with teenagers with autism spectrum disorder (ASD). This system integrates a visual emotion recognition model with an adaptive conversational bow. To train the emotion classifier for real time application trained using transfer learning techniques based on the VGG16 architecture of deep convolutional neural networks, it was trained on a specialised dataset comprising of autistic children's facial expressions and achieved an accuracy of 71% at a 5-emotion recognition task. The Effect recognition module serves the context-aware dialogue manager in real time adapting and personalising the emotional regulation frameworks to be employed. PIDA's dialogues are based on the principles of clinical psychotherapy, with psychotherapeutic techniques and intervention strategies which are individually tuned to the emotional state and contextual parameters of the situation. The system was designed and built salted with caregiver integration features to enable guardians to monitor progress and active participant in the personalization of the intervention. Primary experimental results reflect the feasibility of this dimension in emotional awareness and emotional regulation and coping strate- gies. To support we provide uninterrupted emotional assistance to autistic young people and offer flexible support resources during and in between emotional therapy appointments.

title: Back Cover Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 IEEE Afro-Mediterranean Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AMCAI)

title: Fuzzy PID Control Method for Finished Cigarette Packaging Robots
authors: Chenle Liu, Shoufeng Jin, Jie Zan, Ziran Zhang, Dian Pan, Linjiang Li
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: To address the issue of low precision in the motion trajectories of robots during the packaging process of finished cigarettes, this paper proposes a control method for robot packaging using a fuzzy PID controller. By analyzing the packaging process of finished cigarettes, a fuzzy PID control model for the robot is established. The fuzzy PID controller is designed based on the robot's D-H parameters, kinematic, and dynamic equations. Fuzzy rules are designed to dynamically adjust the PID parameters, and a simulation experiment is conducted to analyze the robot's trajectory tracking using a sine wave test signal. The experimental results indicate that the proposed method can improve the accuracy of the robot's trajectory tracking. Error analysis shows that the tracking error is reduced by an average of 97.47%. Under external interference, the absolute error of the robot's joint angles is controlled within 1.96°.

title: Research and Application of Online Detection Technology in the Inspection of Adsorption Towers in PSA Devices
authors: Dapeng Wang, Xiushan Shi, Pengcheng Gan, He Yan
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: The adsorption tower plays a crucial role in the normal operation of the PSA unit as the core equipment. Due to the special structure of the adsorption tower and the particularity of the adsorbent, it is generally only conduct in-material detection externally. In this paper, the author identified its damage pattern based on the working conditions of the adsorption tower. On this basis, the online detection method of the system has been proposed, then conducts targeted detection on the defect types and defect locations that are prone to occur in this equipment. The results show that the proposed damage mode and online detection method can effectively identify harmful defects and provide targeted treatment suggestions, offering reference and guidance for the safe and stable operation of the adsorption tower.

title: Segment-Based Advanced Image Encryption Scheme for Secure Image Transmission
authors: Jaishree Jain, Saroj Kushwah, Updesh Kumar Jaiswal, Anuradha Dhumale, Mani Dublish, Aastha Sharma
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: The necessity to safeguard picture data from unwanted access has grown due to the quick development of digital media, especially in industries like online content distribution, military communication, and medical imaging. In this paper a new approach toward image encryption and decryption for attaining increased security in both storage and transmission. Our approach breaks down the images into segments before the process of encryption application on the images increases complexity and strength of the encryption process. Every segment is encrypted separately by using a 16 character key, which is supposed to be provided by the user. Thus, ensuring that any partial decryption attempt would be futile without an accurate key. Our approach is very simple and user friendly, without compromising on security. This approach uses a web application interface developed using HTML and JAVA SCRIPT for user interaction like uploading images. This work demonstrates a Python implementation for the encryption system that uses high modularity and scalability. Advanced segmentation- based encryption guards sensitive image data from unintended access. The technique, in particular, is great for secure image transfer. It is used to privately store confidential data. More importantly, this application technique provides a practical solution for modern cybersecurity issues.

title: Precise Ammonia Injection Zoning for SCR Denitrification Systems Based on CFD Simulation
authors: Zhanqing Li, Zhaoxia Zhang, Xinying Xu, Haolin Xiong, Ping Dong, Zhenzhu Dong
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechatronics, Automation and Intelligent Control (MAIC)
abstract: In response to practical challenges in coal-fired power units, such as non-uniform flow distribution, fluctuating denitrification efficiency, and excessive ammonia slip under variable load conditions during deep peak shaving, this study employs a multi-condition simulation and intelligent zoning optimization method based on CFD. A CFD simulation model was developed to characterize the flow fields under five typical load conditions. A three-dimensional representation function integrating load weighting and NOx concentration distribution was constructed to achieve refined zoning of the ammonia injection grid. The proposed method significantly improved the uniformity of NOx distribution at the outlet of the SCR system. Compared with the uniform ammonia injection strategy, the overall NOx concentration at the outlet cross-section was reduced, and its RSD decreased by 59.04%. This approach effectively controls ammonia slip while enhancing denitrification efficiency, providing a theoretical basis for precise ammonia injection in coalfired power plants across a wide load range.

title: Estimating Electric Vehicle Fast-Charging Infrastructure Needs Under Uncertainty: Brebemi
authors: Daniele Martini, Cristian Giovanni Colombo, Michela Longo, Dario Zaninelli, Gianfermo Lupi, Giuseppe Mastroviti
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Driving and Smart Transportation (IDST)
abstract: Electric vehicle (EV) adoption accelerates globally, resulting in a growing demand for charging infrastructure along highway corridors. The proposed Monte Carlo framework provides a flexible and behaviorally grounded method for estimating charging infrastructure needs under uncertainty. By explicitly modeling driver behavior, vehicle characteristics, and long-term technology trends, the approach supports infrastructure planning that balances robustness and economic efficiency. The value of stochastic methods is to capture probability distributions rather than deterministic estimates. The results show that higher levels of electric vehicle penetration reduce the ratio between variance and mean in infrastructure demand, thereby increasing the utilization rate for a given level of service. This methodology enables decision-makers to assess infrastructure resilience across different service level thresholds and to evaluate the marginal benefits of incremental capacity additions, supporting more robust and cost-effective infrastructure investment strategies.

title: Machine Learning Based PV Estimation and Generation Across Diverse Locations for Hybrid Microgrid Applications
authors: Meenu, Pawan Kumar, Smarajit Ghosh
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: Energy efficiency of hybrid microgrid systems depends on the active and passive energy boosters. An active energy booster involves external energy sources that can be integrated with existing networks. Usually, the active energy boosters are solar PV, wind energy, and other small-scale distributed generation systems that can be operated with changes in network topology and loading patterns. However, PV generation is independent of network topology and loading patterns; instead, it needs to be estimated with changes in environmental conditions so that the distributor operator can manage the system configuration for enhanced energy efficiency. Several conventional and machine learning (ML) based models for PV estimation exists in the literature. Here, ML models are explored for PV estimation across diverse climate zones, and the best-suited ML model is proposed to be implemented on a location basis. The performance of the proposed ML models is compared with historical data and conventional transposition models using annual and hourly optimal tilt angles. For robustness, the performance indicators, namely RMSE, MAE, U95, Er-max and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{2}$</tex>, are used and found to be <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.59,0.45,1.12$</tex>, 2.61 and 0.9999.

title: Utilization of Biomass Accessible for Electrification and Several Domestic Purposes Specific to Rural Extents in India
authors: Satyam Patel, N.P. Patidar, Vaibhav Kumar Sharma
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: The growing mandate for sustainable energy access in rural areas of India has highlighted the necessity to explore alternate energy sources beyond conventional fuels like firewood and charcoal, which contribute suggestively to deforestation and health risks. Biomass, derived from agricultural, animals, forestry, industrial, and aquatic residues, offerings a capable and underutilized renewable energy resource. This study explores the potential of biomass utilization from village areas for decentralized energy production. It examines multiple biomass conversion technologies including combustion, gasification, anaerobic digestion, pyrolysis, fermentation and briquetting. Furthermore, procedures for assessing biomass potential using remote sensing, GIS-based spatial mapping, and techno-economic assessments are considered. The study uses data-driven analysis and case comparisons to identify the most appropriate strategies based on biomass availability, technology adaptability, community acceptance, and economic feasibility

title: Multimodal Fake News Detection: A 6-Way Classification Approach Using Image-Text Pairs on the Fakeddit Dataset
authors: Dishant Kumar Soam, Utkarsh Goel, Harsh Panwar, Rohit Kumar Kaliyar
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: The proliferation of false information online has created an urgent need for automated fake news detection systems. This paper presents a novel multimodal approach for detecting fake news by jointly analyzing text and image content from social media posts. We utilize the Fakeddit dataset to build a 6-way classification system that identifies not only whether content is true or false, but also categorizes different types of misinformation including misleading content, manipulated content, false connection, and satire/parody. Our proposed model incorporates cross-modal attention mechanisms to effectively bridge the semantic gap between textual and visual information, achieving an 88% accuracy on the test set. Experimental results demonstrate that our multimodal fusion approach significantly outperforms unimodal baselines, with particularly strong performance in identifying false connection and manipulated content categories. This research contributes to the growing field of multimodal fake news detection and offers practical solutions for combating misinformation online [1], [3], [17].

title: Advanced Multilevel Inverter with Integrated Fault Tolerance: A Novel Approach
authors: Gulshan Yadav, J. Venkataramanaiah, Suresh Lakhimsetty
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: Reliability is a paramount concern for multilevel inverters, particularly in sensitive industrial applications. Conventional multilevel inverters are susceptible to abnormal operating conditions and potential severe equipment damage when a fault occurs in one of their semiconductor switches. This paper presents an efficient single DC source based fault-tolerant(FT) inverter topology to address these limitations. in this topology one half bridge leg are connected to another legs via bidirectional switch. additionally, The proposed FT control scheme achieves rapid fault recovery, operating within a single fundamental cycle of the inverter. Moreover, it require only one current and one voltage sensor for fault diagnosis, and its operation is independent of the underlying switching technique. The proposed fault-tolerant converter topology and its associated control method will be rigorously validated through comprehensive MATLAB/Simulink simulations.

title: Experimental Validation of Street Lighting Digital Twins Using Virtual Luxmeter and HDR Imaging
authors: Ghassen Ben Hnia, Pascal Dupuis, Laurent Canale, Georges Zissis
publish: 2025 IEEE Sustainable Smart Lighting World Conference &amp; Expo (LS25)
abstract: Street lighting digital twins (DTs) promise interactive optimization of urban illumination. This paper evaluates the photometric fidelity of a Unity-based DT by cross-checking Relux and DIALux simulations against perceptually relevant, in-situ HDR measurements. First, we implement an in-engine virtual luxmeter in Unity URP to sample and validate Relux-generated illuminance maps. We then validate the digital twin at two complementary levels: (i) a quantitative illuminance validation against Relux simulations using MAE and RMSE, and (ii) a perceptual comparison with in-situ HDR luminance measurements reported in the literature. The results demonstrate that real-time rendering engines, when combined with measurement-oriented sampling and calibration, can support validation-oriented digital twins rather than purely visual representations.

title: Double Closed-Loop Fractional Order Integral Sliding Mode Control of Voice Coil Motor for Direct Drive Valve
authors: Zihang Zhu, Hongtao Jin, Zhipeng Ma, Shuai Wu
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: Voice coil motor direct drive valve has become an important development direction of electro-hydraulic servo valve because of its simple structure and convenient control. Because the hydraulic load and external disturbance in the direct drive valve directly act on the valve core, the control performance of the system is affected. In order to improve the dynamic response of the system, a control scheme combining control structure and algorithm is proposed in this paper. The double closed-loop structure of the position loop and the current loop is adopted, and the fractional order integral sliding mode control is introduced in the position loop. Through system simulation and experimental verification, the results show that the rise time of the designed control strategy in the step response is only 4.5 ms, the steady-state error is significantly reduced, and it has better dynamic response ability, tracking accuracy and stability.

title: Leveraging Large Language Models to Investigate the Decoy Effect in Route Choice Behavior
authors: Mahbub Hassan, Sorawit Narupiti, Touhid Bhuiyan, Md Maruf Hassan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology and Engineering (i-COSTE)
abstract: Route choice behavior is a cornerstone of transport research, traditionally modeled under the assumption that travelers act as rational agents who maximize utility by trading off time, cost, and reliability. However, behavioral economics shows that real-world decision making often departs from rationality due to systematic biases. One such bias is the decoy effect, which occurs when the introduction of a dominated alternative increases the attractiveness of another option. While widely studied in consumer contexts, its role in transport decision making remains underexplored. This paper presents a novel methodology that leverages large language models (LLMs) to investigate the decoy effect in route choice. Using ChatGPT-4o mini, we generated textual framings of a dominated route alternative and employed the model as a synthetic respondent to simulate route selections. Four experimental conditions were tested: baseline (two routes), decoy with neutral framing, decoy with positive framing, and decoy with negative framing. Results demonstrate that the decoy increased the relative attractiveness of the premium route, particularly under positive framing, while negative framing attenuated the effect. Synthetic responses closely aligned with predictions from a multinomial logit model, confirming consistency with behavioral theory. The findings illustrate that LLMs can serve as both framing generators and behavioral simulators, offering a rapid testbed for prototyping hypotheses in transport research. This work establishes proof-of-concept evidence that LLMs can complement traditional behavioral models, opening pathways for future integration of artificial intelligence into the study of systematic biases in mobility decisions.

title: A Study on Improving Diffusion Flow Stability by Fusing Multi-Sensor Data With a Kalman Filter
authors: Dinghua Xu, Guifa Guo, Haiyun Zhang, Ruodong Wang, Liangliang Huang, Die Hu
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: Filter integrity testing are of great importance during aseptic production of pharmaceutical and microelectronics industries to ensure product quality and process safety. The stability of diffusion flow measurement is a critical performance parameter that fundamentally determines the accuracy and reliability of the test outcomes. To address the issue of single sensors being susceptible to interference from temperature, pressure, and airflow disturbances in complex working conditions-leading to accumulated measurement errors or even out-of-tolerance results - this paper proposes an improved solution based on multi-sensor fusion approach. The proposed approach involves the synergistic co-deployment of heterogeneous sensors-specifically, a high-precision pressure sensor (with an accuracy class of 0.1) and a thermal mass flow meter (with a maximum permissible error of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 0.8 \%$</tex> of reading). The Kalman filter uses this model to predict the system's state and then corrects the prediction with the actual sensor measurements, significantly improving the reliability and accuracy of the final pressure and flow data.The results conclusively demonstrate that the fusion system achieves a dramatic reduction in both diffusion flow fluctuation amplitude and Relative Standard Deviation (RSD). Specifically, the diffusion flow fluctuation amplitude is decreased from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 4.8 \%$</tex> (with the single-sensor system) to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 1.2 \%$</tex>, while the RSD is optimized from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2. 4 \%}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 6 \%}$</tex>. Moreover, the fusion model effectively suppresses measurement errors induced by temperature drift and pressure fluctuations - an improvement that directly translates to a substantial enhancement in the system's overall stability and anti-interference capability, adherence to the relevant requirements of USP <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1211$</tex>, and GMP regulations for the integrity testing of sterilizing filters.

title: Security Dilemma in Software Development: A Case Study on Understanding Developer Priorities and Practices in Stack Overflow
authors: Umar Albalawi, Mohammed Mustafa
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology and Engineering (i-COSTE)
abstract: Applications have advanced rapidly in recent years, taking software development to a whole new level. These advancements have led to a growth in the complexity of applications, cloud computing, and the Internet of Things (IoT). As a result of this improvement, software security has become a concern for developers, but it has traditionally been overlooked. But now, security issues are evolving day by day. In this paper, we explore developer priorities and practices in term of security in Stack Overflow website, whether they implement their software with security in mind or not. We utilize the Stack Exchange Data Dump to collect and compile a dataset of questions and their corresponding answers related to security vulnerabilities, specifically those that include user-submitted programming code snippets, our analysis concentrates on security-related topics. The experimental results indicate that Python emerged as the most commonly used language for security code snippets across these topics. From the expanded sample of code snippets, several vulnerabilities are flagged for security issues.

title: A Novel PWM Strategy with an Efficient Closed-Loop Control Technique for Grid-Connected Three-Level NPC Converter
authors: Niloy Das, Md. Sabbir Hasan Sohag, Ahmed Rayhan, M. Mahbubur Rahman, A B M Shawkat Ali
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology and Engineering (i-COSTE)
abstract: This paper embodies a novel Pulse Width Modulation (PWM) strategy with an advanced closed-loop control technique for grid-connected three-level Neutral Point Clamped (NPC) converters. The proposed method efficiently adjusts the power factor to meet grid demands, supporting unity, lagging, or leading power factors. It achieves significant reductions in voltage and current Total Harmonic Distortion (THD), with voltage THD at 20.92% and current THD at 1.64%, both complying with the Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE)-519 standard. These improvements are due to modifications in the modulating signal and carrier waveform. The method also demonstrates stable THD performance across varying switching frequencies and modulation indices. Simulation results validate the proposed approach, showing better THD performance than conventional PWM techniques such as Sinusoidal PWM (SPWM), Third Harmonic PWM (THPWM), Triangular PWM (TRPWM), and Bus-Clamping PWM (BCPWM). This work demonstrates the potential of the proposed method to enhance the performance of NPC converters in grid-connected systems, ensuring improved power quality and robust control.

title: A Multi-Task Deep Learning for Plant Disease Classification and Vegetation Index Regression Using Multi-Modal Data
authors: Kartikey Kumar Malkani, Ashita Tripathi, Arun Solanki
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: Plant diseases pose a significant threat to global food security, making early and accurate detection critical for timely intervention in precision agriculture. However, conventional approaches typically address either disease classification or vegetation index estimation in isolation, often relying on separate models or expensive multispectral sensors. This siloed design limits diagnostic robustness, scalability, and accessibility, particularly in resource constrained settings. To address these challenges, we propose a multi-task deep learning model that simultaneously performs plant disease classification and vegetation index regression from cost-effective RGB imagery. The architecture employs DenseNet121 as a shared backbone with task-specific heads, facilitating efficient feature sharing across tasks. Trained on a combined dataset of RGB images and sensor-derived vegetation indices, the model estimates key physiological metrics NDVI, PRI, MSI, and REP alongside disease categories. The proposed model achieves a classification accuracy of 99.57% across 12 plant disease categories and shows promising performance in index regression (MSE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.1632$</tex>, MAE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.1968$</tex>), especially for NDVI and REP. These results highlight the potential of multi-task learning for unified, low-cost, and scalable plant health monitoring, bridging the gap between visual symptoms and physiological stress assessment in real-world agricultural AI systems.

title: Orientation Optimization of Agrivoltaic Bifacial PV Farms in Morocco
authors: Mattia Parenti, Marco Fossa, Samuele Memme, Caterina Gibelli
publish: 2025 International Congress on Smart Agriculture and Sustainable Systems (SmartAgri&amp;SuSY)
abstract: Morocco aims to achieve 52% renewable energy by 2030, prioritizing photovoltaic over solar thermal technologies. Africa's solar potential is immense, with rapidly declining PV costs. Key challenges include grid integration, storage, and financing. Bifacial modules currently represent the most efficient option for utility-scale PV systems, providing increased solar energy output with only marginal additional costs. Agrivoltaics technology integrates solar panels with agriculture, optimizing land use and water efficiency. It enhances crop resilience to heat stress, supports rural electrification, and promotes sustainable development in arid regions. This paper helps in optimizing the productivity of agrivoltaic bifacial PV power plants in Morocco: Typical Meteorological Year data are retrieved from the PVGIS platform for 7 agricultural locations at different latitudes. A cumulative sky radiance is built for each location and an iterative search for the best tilt angle is performed to maximize the annual energy yield (on both front and back surfaces considering direct, diffuse and reflected energy) of south-facing modules, with both ground albedo and PV row spacing taken as parameters. The analysis is then repeated for vertical bifacial plants, finding the best azimuth, and it has been evidenced that the energy yield is on average <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$-22 {\%}$</tex> for vertical PV, respect to optimally oriented tilted PVs. Latitude is the primary influencing factor for optimal tilt angles, but the results indicate that the Ground Coverage Ratio is also a significant parameter, while albedo has only a minor effect. Finally, the irradiance distribution on the ground surrounding a PV installation has been determined for multiple configurations, quantifying the decreased shading under vertical PVs respect to tilted systems.

title: GrapeSense: Grape Leaf Disease Detection
authors: Angel Li, Rishit Agrawal, Sanskriti Farswal, Navid Shaghaghi
publish: 2025 International Congress on Smart Agriculture and Sustainable Systems (SmartAgri&amp;SuSY)
abstract: Grape crops are important for both food security amid climate devastation and the global wine-making industry. However, the prevalence of numerous plant diseases can devastate vineyards, making early detection of disease onset crucial. The GrapeSense project offers a sustainable and modern approach to viticulture, successfully developing models to classify ripeness stages and detect optimal harvesting time for large vineyards. This paper presents the project's expansion into detecting grape leaf diseases based on visual leaf condition observable in drone-captured imagery in real vineyard settings. While substantial research has been conducted on classifying single grape leaves under controlled laboratory conditions, the GrapeSense project presents a novel approach using a two-step pipeline to classify images captured directly at a vineyard with numerous leaves and complex environmental features. Vineyard drone-captured imagery were augmented with diseased leaves in various overlaying patterns and a two-step pipeline was developed by extracting leaves with the YOLOv8 object detection model, followed by classifying each leaf using ResNet-18's transfer learning model on the singled out leaf. The leaf detection model achieved a mAP50 of 85.0% and the disease classification model obtained an accuracy of 99.61 %, offering a successful deep learning approach to automate laborious agricultural practices and improve crop yield.

title: Energy Management in Agricultural Microgrids: A Comparative Study of Deterministic and Stochastic Model Predictive Control
authors: Yasmine Achour, Rachid El Bouayadi, Abderrahim Bajit, Hicham Labrim, Hamza Benzzine, Driss Zejli
publish: 2025 International Congress on Smart Agriculture and Sustainable Systems (SmartAgri&amp;SuSY)
abstract: This paper compares deterministic and stochastic Model Predictive Control (MPC) strategies for optimal energy management in an agricultural microgrid integrating renewable sources, battery storage, and controllable loads. The deterministic MPC assumes perfect knowledge of future disturbances, whereas the stochastic MPC accounts for uncertainties in weather and renewable generation through probabilistic forecasting. Both approaches are evaluated in terms of tracking performance, storage utilization, and grid interaction. The study highlights the value of uncertainty-aware control in enhancing the sustainability and resilience of agricultural microgrids under variable operating conditions.

title: NMPC for Vehicle Trajectory Tracking: A Study on Data Loss and Energy Efficiency
authors: Alessandra Elisa Sindi Morando, Roberto Sacile, Pedro Castillo
publish: 2025 International Congress on Smart Agriculture and Sustainable Systems (SmartAgri&amp;SuSY)
abstract: In this paper a Nonlinear Model Predictive Controller (NMPC) is proposed for a steering car's trajectory tracking. The aim is to analyze the robustness of NMPC algorithm to the data loss (loss of measurement of states in the control loop) and its energy efficiency in terms of total traveled distance. To have a full analysis, NMPC have been tested both in simulation and in a real environment by tracking different references with and without data loss. Moreover, to verify the performance of the NMPC algorithm a comparison with a classical controller is proposed. In all scenarios, better tracking performances are obtained with the NMPC strategy. Note that on the average the time required by one NMPC's iteration is almost equal to the sampling time. Regarding the energetic efficiency, although in simulation NMPC outperforms the classical controller notably in presence of losses, in the experiments the total traveled distance is almost the same for both algorithms.

title: Surpassing 16% Efficiency in Cd-Free CZTS Solar Cells via Si Back-Contact Engineering and IR Harvesting
authors: Lynda Metref, Essaid Mansouri, Mahfoud Abderrezek, Samira Sali
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy (AIESRE)
abstract: CZTS solar cell -We propose a fully Cd 1D, consisting of a ZnO:Al-SCAPS architecture simulated in Si -transparent contact, a ZnS buffer, a CZTS absorber, and a p Si layer -substrate that also acts as a secondary absorber. The <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p}$</tex> minimizes recombination losses at the back contact while infrared, thus improving -extending absorption into the near overall light harvesting.
