The results demonstrate that the integration of PCM significantly improves the thermal performance of the wall. Specifically, the heat flux through a PCM-filled brick reached a peak of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$22.4 \mathrm{W} / \mathrm{m}^{2}$</tex> after 600 s, then gradually stabilized around <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$20 \mathrm{W} / \mathrm{m}^{2}$</tex>, reflecting the latent heat absorption during the melting phase. This behavior confirms the role of PCM as a thermal buffer, delaying and reducing heat transmission to the interior. As a result, the system stabilized indoor temperatures around <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$25{ }^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex>, effectively enhancing thermal comfort while reducing dependence on active cooling systems.

title: RF PA Optimization Utilizing a Particle Swarm and Bayesian Collaborative Algorithm
authors: Benhao Jin, Zhiming Fan, Jialin Cai
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Artificial Intelligence
abstract: To improve power amplifier (PA) optimization efficiency and performance, a new enhanced particle swarm optimization (PSO) method combining the standard PSO algorithm with a Bayesian optimization algorithm is presented in this paper, referred to as the particle swarm Bayesian cooperative optimization (PSBCO) method. The basic theory and implementation procedure of the proposed method is described. Furthermore, the method has been applied to a single-ended multi-octave PA and an RF-input asymmetric load modulation balanced amplifier (ALMBA) optimization. The performance of both PAs optimized by the proposed method is presented, and compared with the initial design, the ADS built-in optimizer, the standard PSO method, and the simulated annealing PSO method. According to the results, the proposed method is capable of achieving superior performance for both PAs with a much higher optimization efficiency. A single-ended PA can achieve a power added efficiency (PAE) greater than 57.2%, an output power greater than 40.5 dBm, and a gain greater than 10.5 dB in the frequency range of 0.7GHz-3.2GHz. The RF-input ALMBA demonstrated saturation drain efficiency (DE) from 71.7% to 76.2%, 6dB back-off DE from 61.5% to 67.9%, and 10dB back-off DE from 58.7% to 62.4%, with a saturation output power from 42.6dBm to 42.9dBm in the frequency band of 1.8 GHz-2.1GHz. Furthermore, digital predistortion (DPD) is implemented in order to linearize the designed PAs with a 5G NR signal. After DPD, adjacent channel power ratios (ACPRs) can be greatly enhanced.

title: LeatherVision: Pre-Trained Transformer Models for Automated Leather Species Identification
authors: S. Sundar Rajan, Malathy Jawahar, A. Amalin Prince
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Precise identification of animal species in leather is pivotal in maintaining biological diversity and protecting consumers from buying illegal trade products. Conventional approaches concerning this research largely rely on leather specialists and physical assessment, often prone to ambiguous judgments. The practical applications of the vision transformer's (ViT) self-attention mechanism to capture global contextual relationships, enabling species-specific texture variations regardless of spatial distance, are explored in this research work. Accordingly, the research presents a comprehensive comparative study of three pre-trained ViT-based deep learning models (ViT-Base, ViTSmall, ViT-Tiny), fine-tuned via transfer learning on a diverse dataset of 10,000 region-specific leather images from four legally permissible animal species. Two data augmentation techniques, horizontal and vertical flipping, are employed during the training. The fine-tuned models demonstrate excellent performance on the challenging unseen dataset comprising 8800 leather images with ideal and non-ideal attributes. Among the three models, ViT-Base shows its superiority in species identification performance with an accuracy of 95.97%, and the other two variants, ViT-Small and ViT-Tiny, show comparable results with an accuracy of 91.78% and 82.61%, respectively. The results in this study highlight the proposed model's effectiveness in identifying the leather species. This, in turn, helps the leather specialists by developing precise species prediction methods.

title: AI Approach for Ultrasound Image Nerve Segmentation
authors: Sunitha. G. P, Prajwal Singh. G
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies in Electronics and Green Energy (ICETEG)
abstract: In the medicinal field, ultrasound nerve segmentation is essential in particular during nerve block and regional anesthetic operations. However, because of low contrast, noise and differences in human body structure, it can be challenging to detect accurately nerves in ultrasonic images. Conventional image processing methods can produce wide and incorrect results. In medical image segmentation, deep learning techniques such as UNet and its improved versions have recently shown remarkable results. Due to its encoder and decoder structure, which helps in learning both big and small attributes of images, U-Net is recommended. The proposed system automatically identifies and segment the nerves from ultrasound pictures using the U- Net, U-Net++, and YOLO models. The developed system is a unified Flask-based platform that integrates U-Net, U-Net++, and YOLOv8 for ultrasound nerve segmentation. Though YOLO locates nerves in real time, U-Net++ provides higher segmentation by using additional links across the layers. In this proposed system a dataset of ultrasound pictures with marked nerves to train the models is used. The images are preprocessed to improve the quality of the images before applying the models. The models performance is evaluated with performance metrics like accuracy, dice score, and intersection over union (IoU). U-Net++ achieved the highest Dice score 0.92, showing robustness to low-contrast images, while YOLOv8 scored 0.78, limited by image quality and its detection-oriented design.

title: Evaluating LLM-Induced Investment Biases via Persona Design and Prompt Analysis: A Quantitative Study of Post-Retirement Asset Depletion Risk Through Social Simulation
authors: Takamasa KIKUCHI, Hiroshi TAKAHASHI
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: This study quantitatively evaluates whether responses from large language models (LLMs) can reinforce individual investment biases, and how such reinforcement may alter medium- to long-term asset depletion risks, through a three-stage methodology involving persona design, prompt analysis, and social simulation. First, representative personas are constructed by clustering data from a large-scale survey, incorporating plausible individual profiles. For each persona, financial consultations are conducted with an LLM using multiple prompts that integrate varying risk preferences. The risk tolerance exhibited in the LLM responses is scored and statistically analyzed for significant differences via one-way ANOVA. Next, asset allocations derived from these risk preferences are input into Monte Carlo simulations-including exogenous macroeconomic variables-to estimate age-specific depletion rates. The results demonstrate that changes in the proportion of risky assets can affect the probability of future asset depletion. This approach is novel in that it empirically links the impact of prompt design on long-term household risk by path wise visualization of response bias stemming from the “sycophancy problem” of LLMs.

title: Automated Detection of Knee Osteoarthritis Using an Enhanced AlexNet with Attention and Grad-CAM Visualization
authors: Divyashree M S, Pradeep B M, Karthik Uttarkar Gangadhar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies in Electronics and Green Energy (ICETEG)
abstract: This paper proposes the Enhanced-AlexNet-ROI-Attention (EARA) framework for automated knee osteoarthritis (OA) detection. EARA integrates region-of-interest localization, attention mechanisms, and class-weighted optimization to improve sensitivity to subtle OA features. Evaluated on <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 3,000$</tex> knee radiographs from a multi-center dataset, our method achieved 87.4% accuracy, 0.84 macro <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F 1$</tex>-score, 0.81 weighted Cohen's kappa, and 0.92 AUC for binary OA detection (KellgrenLawrence <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\geq 2$</tex>), significantly outperforming AlexNet, VGG-16, and ResNet-50 (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$p<0.05$</tex>). Grad-CAM visualizations confirm the model focuses on clinically relevant regions like joint space narrowing. The results demonstrate EARA's potential as an interpretable, robust tool for automated OA assessment that could reduce diagnostic variability in clinical practice.

title: Adaptive and Fine-Grained Power Gating Techniques for Leakage-Aware VLSI Design
authors: Keerthi Kassan V, S. Joshua Kumaresan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: This Power gating (PG) has become a cornerstone technique for leakage reduction in sub-100 nm VLSI by disconnecting idle blocks using sleep (power-switch) transistors. This paper reviews recent advances in PG with emphasis on (i) sleep-transistor sizing and distributed networks, (ii) state-retention and multi-mode/retentive gating, and (iii) adaptive/fine-grained control (including ML/RL and hardware–software co-design) for modern multicore and accelerator chips. We summarise trade-offs—area, wake-up latency, IR drop and reliability (BTI/JT)—and highlight practical design algorithms and architectures that emerged recently. The survey synthesises key results from foundational MTCMOS work through contemporary adaptive and retention-aware methods, and identifies open challenges (scalability to NPUs/NPsUs, verification of selective retention, and variation-aware sizing). The review cites representative works (≤ 16 refs) and concludes with recommendations for integrating adaptive PG in nextgeneration low-power SoCs. In the proposed PG strategy, optimised sleep-transistor size, dispersed power-switch placement, and multi-mode state-retention minimise leakage and stable wake-up performance. The co-optimized architecture decreases leakage-energy by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.5 \times$</tex>, peak wake-up current by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3 \times$</tex>, and improves reliability under BTI/JT stress.

title: Anomaly Detection in Satellite Attitude and Orbit Control Via Multivariate LSTM Networks
authors: Eleonora Mariotti, Thomas Ciarfuglia, Marco Anania, Vito Trianni
publish: 2025 IEEE Space Computing Conference (SCC)
abstract: Autonomous anomaly detection is an increasingly critical requirement in the spacecraft (S/C) Attitude and Orbit Control Subsystem (AOCS). The current approach, based on hard-coded hierarchical thresholds, is effective for well-known failure cases but becomes inadequate when satellite dynamics grow more complex, often leading to exceptional cases that escape detection. Every missed anomaly forces the Ground Station into time-consuming investigations and the restoration of the satellite's nominal operation. As an improvement over current practices, this work proposes a novel Machine Learning (ML)-based approach, specifically leveraging multivariate Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs)-specifically, a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network-to enhance the efficiency and reliability of onboard failure detection systems for AOCS. A key contribution of this study is the development of a synthetic multivariate dataset, designed using a flight-proven attitude simulator, which includes all necessary signals to capture the satellite's complex attitude dynamics. This dataset encompasses multiple anomaly sources, accounting for inertia uncertainties, actuator deviations, flexible modes, and the effects of unpredictable exogenous phenomena and malfunctioning hardware-posing a significant challenge for AOCS anomaly detection systems. Finally, we provide a comprehensive taxonomy of anomalies, which has been used to ensure the coherence of the dataset design. Our experimental validation shows that our system promises to deliver better performance in terms of failure presence detection and faster identification with respect to the canonical approach.

title: The Effect of MCUs and Single-Word MBUs on the ECC Failures of a Radiation Hardened Microcontroller
authors: Stefania Esquer, Jesse K. Mee, Heather M. Quinn, Windy Slater, Ronald D. Schrimpf, Brian D. Sierawski
publish: 2025 IEEE Space Computing Conference (SCC)
abstract: This paper reports the results of heavy ion irradiation of the instruction memory of the VA41630 microcontroller fabricated with the radiation hardened 130 nm HARDSIL technology node. The objective of the experiment was to capture single event upsets (SEUs) with precision to accurately estimate the error correcting code (ECC) failure rates in space. The memory exhibited sensitivity to multiple-cell upsets (MCUs), but no single-word multiple-bit upsets (MBUs) during the experiment. Monte Carlo simulation demonstrates that the SEU cross-section can be used to bound the probability of independent upsets leading to ECC failure without consideration of MCUs. However, a geometric analysis concludes that single-word MBUs unobserved in ground testing, may still dominate the ECC failure rates.

title: OPC UA-Based Embedded Gateway for PV Inverter Interoperability in Smart Grids
authors: Dwi Mayasari, Suwito, Astria Nur Irfansyah
publish: 2026 International Seminar on Intelligent Business and Edge-Computing Research (ISIBER)
abstract: The integration of photovoltaic (PV) inverters into Smart Grids faces critical security and interoperability challenges, as industrial protocols such as Modbus TCP provide limited built-in security for energy management. This study develops and validates a cost-effective Open Platform Communications Unified Architecture (OPC UA)-based embedded gateway using an Arduino Opta microcontroller to convert Modbus TCP measurements into an OPC UA information model, enabling interoperability and security-ready interface for Smart Grid applications. The simulation-based evaluation under three operating profiles (Sine, Flat, and Random) shows a mean absolute error below 0.5% for voltage, current, and power, with a power-factor error below 0.001 and a median latency below 10 ms at 1 Hz sampling. During dynamic Random conditions, transient deviations occur (546 W in power and 2.4 A in current); however, interoperability and timestamp consistency are maintained. OPC UA security policies are disabled in this proof-of-concept isolated-LAN testbed to isolate baseline performance. The results validate a practical pathway for deploying security-ready, interoperable, and cost-effective solutions for PV inverter integration in Smart Grids.

title: A Survey of AI-Based Detection Techniques for Intracranial Aneurysms
authors: Kathrina T. Dacanay, Jeffrey S. Ingosan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Early detection of intracranial aneurysms (IA) is essential for preventing subarachnoid hemorrhage, a condition associated with high morbidity and mortality. Recent advances in artificial intelligence (AI), particularly deep learning, have shown promising results in assisting radiologists across different angiographic modalities. This survey synthesizes findings from 23 peer-reviewed studies spanning CTA, TOF-MRA, DSA, 3D-RA, and multiphase CTA. Across modalities, CTA-based models achieved some of the highest reported patient-level sensitivities, often reaching <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${95-100 \%}$</tex>, while multiphase CTA further improved sensitivity by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4-6 \%$</tex> compared to single-phase scanning. TOF-MRA studies reported sensitivities ranging from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$90-96 \%$</tex>, with segmentation models attaining Dice scores of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.80-0.83$</tex> and AUC values up to 0.96. DSA and 3D-RA systems demonstrated strong performance for larger aneurysms, achieving sensitivities of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$91-97 \%$</tex>, though performance dropped for aneurysms <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\leq 3$</tex> mm. Across all modalities, 3D CNNs and U-Net variants were the most frequently used architectures, with two-stage detection-segmentation pipelines consistently reducing false positives. Despite these advancements, challenges remain, particularly false-positive rates in CTA, reduced sensitivity for very small aneurysms, and limited cross-institutional generalization. This review consolidates current trends, highlights persistent gaps, and outlines future directions for developing clinically robust AI systems for IA detection.

title: An Exploration of Lightweight BERT-Based Models for Emotion Classification in Indonesian Social Media Texts
authors: Ageng Tawang Aryonindito, Aristo Yongka, Ghinaa Zain Nabiilah
publish: 2026 International Seminar on Intelligent Business and Edge-Computing Research (ISIBER)
abstract: In Indonesia, social media platforms have become the primary channels for expressing thoughts and emotions, fundamentally transforming how individuals share opinions and interact. With millions of posts published daily, these platforms capture concise snapshots of the real public sentiment of the Indonesian population. This could be beneficial for various applications such as early detection of mental health issues and crisis response. One of the ways to use these texts for socially beneficial applications is by using emotion classification in Natural Language Processing. However, current approaches typically rely on large-scale Transformer architectures like BERT, which can have high computational needs that make them impractical for some real-world problems. Addressing this gap, this study evaluates the efficacy of lightweight Transformer variants, specifically DistilBERT, ALBERT, and TinyBERT, to balance performance with efficiency. Using a dataset of 7080 Indonesian tweets, the research investigates whether these compressed architectures can adequately handle the linguistic nuances of the Indonesian language. The experimental analysis reveals that while model compression is vital for scalability, it often presents a trade-off in semantic capacity. Ultimately, DistilBERT emerged as the most optimal architecture, achieving a testing accuracy of 71 percent.

title: Voice and Text-Based AI Chatbot for Trauma Identification and Real-Time Doctor-Linkage
authors: Bharathvaj. J, Abirami
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: With the increasing impact of traumainduced psychological distress in society, timely and accessible mental health assistance has become crucial. This study introduces an intelligent chatbot designed to evaluate emotional well-being through both spoken communication and written messages, utilizing advanced sentiment analysis and speech emotion recognition. The system identifies critical terms and emergency language to assess users emotional states, delivering immediate self-help strategies, emotional support, and direct connections to medical practitioners during crises. By harnessing NLP and voice emotion detection, the chatbot gauges the severity of psychological distress and recommends suitable interventions. Comparative analysis with existing conversational agents and traditional mental health tools demonstrates the effectiveness of the proposed approach in recognizing urgent emotional states and enabling prompt support. The research aims to expand the reach of psychological care by creating a responsive, scalable conversational platform that can accurately detect and address trauma-related distress in real time

title: Enhanced Security and Efficiency in IoT Authentication: A PUF-Based Protocol with Dynamic Response Mechanisms and Lightweight Blockchain Integration
authors: Hyunseok Kim, Hyuntae Yoo
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: The pervasive deployment of Internet of Things (IoT) devices across diverse critical domains, from smart cities to industrial control systems and healthcare, necessitates robust and efficient authentication mechanisms. Traditional cryptographic protocols often struggle with the resource constraints of IoT devices, while remaining vulnerable to emerging threats such as quantum attacks and sophisticated physical cloning. This paper proposes a novel PUF (Physical Unclonable Function)-based authentication protocol that integrates dynamic response mechanisms and leverages lightweight blockchain for enhanced security and efficiency in resource-constrained IoT environments. We address the limitations of static PUF behaviors, susceptibility to machine learning attacks, and the scalability challenges of centralized authentication systems. Through a comprehensive analysis, including informal and formal security verifications using tools like AVISPA and the Real-or-Random (ROR) model, we demonstrate the protocol's resilience against various attacks, including impersonation, replay, Man-in-the-Middle (MITM), and physical cloning. Furthermore, comparative performance evaluations against state-of-the-art protocols illustrate significant reductions in computational and communication overhead, making our proposed solution highly suitable for latency-sensitive and resource-limited IoT applications.

title: Why is it Difficult to Fit CPD Tensor Model to 3GPP 38.901 MIMO OFDM Channels?
authors: Denis Shuvalov, Semyon Dorokhin
publish: 2026 28th International Conference on Digital Signal Processing and its Applications (DSPA)
abstract: In this paper, we investigate possible reasons why it is extremely difficult to find Canonical Polyadic Decomposition (CPD) of Multiple Input Multiple Output (MIMO) Orthogonal Frequency-Division Multiplexing (OFDM) channel tensors generated according to 3GPP 38.901 model. Channel estimation methods based on tensor decompositions are highly popular for 6 G research since they can potentially provide overhead reduction and improve the accuracy. The majority of tensorbased channel estimation methods is based on CPD model since it directly corresponds to geometry-based narrowband MIMO OFDM channel, with canonical rank being equal to the number of scattering objects. 3GPP 38.901 channel model also follows CPD, but computing this decomposition becomes an extremely difficult task. By modifying the number of scattering objects in 3GPP 38.901 model we study three possible effects that may be the reason of such problem. First, we show that limited angular domain and time-domain resolution decrease the canonical rank that is enough for perfect decomposition. Second, we show that for scattering objects with the same delay (subpaths in 3GPP 38.901) this rank can be reduced even futher. Finally, we show that when the number of scattering objects is larger than the smallest dimension of the channel tensor, it is difficult to obtain exact CPD even with rank overshooting. This effect gets more prominent when the number of scattering objects is larger than two dimensions of the tensor. Our experimental results can be used to improve the performance of CPD-based channel estimation algorithms applied to 3 GPP 38.901 channels.

title: Support System for the Recruitment and Selection Process Using the Sbert Model
authors: Daniel Esteban Huerta Arellanos, Angie Magdiel Morales Silverio, Jymmy Stuwart Dextre Alarcon
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: The growing adoption of artificial intelligence in the business sector has driven the development of intelligent systems aimed at optimizing critical processes such as recruitment and personnel selection. This research aims to develop a system based on automated data processing using SBERT, capable of accurately and quickly identifying the most suitable profiles, reducing human effort and biases in decisionmaking. The system development included the selection of tools, the construction of a structured test dataset based on real résumés, the development of a semantic similarity model, the implementation of a functional web application, and its subsequent validation using metrics. The results showed a significant improvement in accuracy and a reduction in selection time, decreasing from 50 to 20 minutes per position, representing a 60 % reduction. The average similarity exceeded 0.70, demonstrating the system's effectiveness in identifying compatible candidates quickly and objectively. It is concluded that artificial intelligence is an effective strategy to optimize the selection process in Peruvian organizations.

title: ML Forecasting of Tunisia's Regulatory Freedom Index
authors: Amani Driss, Yamina Mathlouthi
publish: 2025 IEEE Afro-Mediterranean Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AMCAI)
abstract: This article explains how machine learning techniques can be used to forecast the ‘Regulation’ component (Area 5) of the economic freedom index published by the Fraser Institute for the next five years (2023-2027) for Tunisia. This component examines the effect of government regulations on voluntary trading in the credit, laborbor and business markets. The study demonstrates Tunisia's institutional inertia through the Ridge regression, Random forest regression, and XGBoost regression methods. The results were compared to those of simple univariate forecasting methods such as the naive, average, and drift models used in previous studies. Institutional theories underpin the results, which highlight persistent challenges and suggest improvements to the regulatory framework.

title: PCB Defect Localization and Classification Using Deep Learning
authors: Ranjan Kumar H S, Shabana S, Chinmai Shetty, Kiran Puttegowda
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies in Electronics and Green Energy (ICETEG)
abstract: Production defects have been a constant problem with high-volume manufacturing of Printed Circuit Boards (PCBs) as the consumer electronics market has attracted increased demand in the last couple of decades. Such defects, such as excess copper, short circuits and open circuits, may cause waste of the material as well as making PCBs unusable. Manual inspection procedures that are normally conducted on a predetermined schedule no longer meet the production specifications to an extent that makes automated inspection techniques necessary. AVI is an emerging strategy of defect screening because it allows non-obstructive observation in the manufacturing process. This paper presents a U-NET modelbased method to localise and classify PCB defects. This model is used to conduct semantic segmentation to detect and categorise defects at the pixel level with the power to localise and label defect-types. Experimental verification on Deep PCB dataset shows that the proposed solution has a high precision of 97.3 per cent, a recall of 96.7 per cent, and F1-score of 96.0. To achieve greater efficiency, the model size is further trimmed using a lowweight pruning strategy, which roughly achieves a threefold reduction in size to 2.4 MB and maintains high accuracy. The outlined framework provides a flexible and powerful approach to real-time deficiency detection in PCB manufacturing processes in the contemporary manufacturing space.

title: A Decision Support Framework for Blockchain Pattern Selection Based on Soft Goals
authors: Eddy Kiomba Kambilo, Nicolas Herbaut, Irina Rychkova, Carine Souveyet
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Distributed Ledger Technologies (ICDLT)
abstract: Blockchain technology is gaining momentum across many sectors. Whereas blockchain solutions have important positive effects on the business domain, they also introduce constraints and may cause delayed or unforeseen negative effects, undermining business strategies. The diversity of blockchain patterns and lack of standardized frameworks linking business goals to technical design decisions make pattern selection a complex task for system architects. To address this challenge, we propose Blockchain–TechnologyAware Enterprise Modeling (BC-TEAEM), a decision support framework that combines ontologies of blockchain patterns and domain-independent soft goals with a multi-criteria decisionmaking approach. The framework focuses on the interplay between a domain expert and a technical expert to ensure alignment and traceability. By iteratively capturing and refining preferences, BC-TEAEM supports systematic selection of blockchain patterns. We develop a prototype decision support tool implementing our method and validate it through a case study of a pharmaceutical company's supply chain traceability system, demonstrating the framework's applicability.

title: A Hybrid Graph Neural Network and Multilingual BERT Approach for Content Virality Prediction on Twitter
authors: Jordan Rubin Kurnia, Antoni Wibowo
publish: 2026 International Seminar on Intelligent Business and Edge-Computing Research (ISIBER)
abstract: Predicting content virality on social media is a challenging task because it is influenced by both textual content and patterns of information spread within user networks. Approaches that rely only on text often fail to identify viral content specifically when data distributions are imbalanced and viral posts represent only a small portion of the dataset. This study proposes a hybrid model that combines Multilingual BERT as a text representation method and a Graph Neural Network based on GraphSAGE to capture structure information. Text features and user profile attributes are integrated at an early stage using an Early Fusion strategy and propagated through a semantic graph constructed from content similarity. To improve model sensitivity toward the minority class, training is performed using Focal Loss and class weighting. Experiments are conducted on Indonesian Twitter data by comparing three models, namely a standalone mBERT model, a standalone GNN model, and the proposed hybrid model. The results show the hybrid model get the highest performance with accuracy of 82.64 percent and an AUC of 0.8970. A notable improvement is observed in the viral class, where Recall increases to 52.31 percent and exceeds both baseline models.

title: A Time-Optimal Trajectory Planning Method Based on Improved Particle Swarm Algorithm
authors: Hong Zhang, Tingxue Li, Yuxin Yang, Wenjun Wang, Yanzhu Zhang
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Computing (SPIC)
abstract: The continual advancements in robotics have led to a growing prevalence of six-degree-of-freedom robotic arms in a variety of industries. The trajectory planning of the CR5 robotic arm is examined in this paper using the MATLAB platform. This paper suggests an enhanced particle swarm optimization algorithm that employs <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3-5-3$</tex> interpolation to simplify the robotic arm's time-optimal trajectory planning. Traditionally, Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) has been susceptible to local optimal solutions. This algorithm is designed to resolve this issue. The algorithm implements the Singer Map to optimize the initial distribution of the particle swarm. It dynamically modifies the inertia weights and learning factors to significantly reduce the computational complexity and minimize the operating time of the robotic arm. Simulation investigations are conducted in the MATLAB environment within the constraints of the robotic arm's kinematics. The results indicate that the enhanced algorithm reduces execution time by approximately 20.87%, decreases the final fitness value by 6.78%, and shortens the number of iterations by 58.33% in comparison to the traditional PSO, all while maintaining seamless trajectories. This illustrates that the proposed method not only enhances optimization efficiency and solution quality but also offers a practical and effective approach for time-optimal trajectory planning in industrial robotic arms, thereby contributing to increased operational efficiency and stability in applicable technical fields.

title: LSTM-Based Hourly Sales Forecasting in SME Food and Beverage Retail
authors: Moch Azis Munawar, Ivan Diryana Sudirman
publish: 2026 International Seminar on Intelligent Business and Edge-Computing Research (ISIBER)
abstract: Another important aspect of inventory management in food and beverage business based on retailing is accurate demand forecasting. This paper will attempt to forecast hourly ice cream products sales in an SME using the Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) model, and to compare the performance of the Long Short-Term Memory with cross-sectional approaches, i.e., Naive Forecast, 7-hour Moving Average (MA7), and Daily Seasonal Naive. The data on the transactions are processed by the Point-of-Sale (POS) system and arranged into hourly time units to record the daily demand dynamics. The preliminary examination shows that there is a robust seasonal trend of the day and that there is a high variation in sales during the working hours. On the test set, LSTM attains the best performance with MAE and RMSE, which is smaller than Naive and MA7. The LSTM model is also demonstrated to have the capability of reducing possible losses as a result of over or understocking compared to the two approaches through cost-based simulations.

title: Machine Learning-Driven EEG Signal Analysis for Neurological Diagnostics
authors: Vandna, Banita
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: Electroencephalography (EEG) is a common non-invasive way to keep an eye on brain activity. It can give important information about neurological disorders like epilepsy, Alzheimer's disease, and depression. Nonetheless, the complexity, non-stationarity, and susceptibility to noise of EEG signals present considerable obstacles for manual interpretation and prompt diagnosis. This study suggests a machine learning-based framework for the design and analysis of EEG signals for emotion detection, with the goal of improving diagnostic accuracy and automation. The study investigates supervised and deep learning models, including Random Forests, and Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), for feature extraction, classification, and pathology detection. It also combines multimodal data by looking at EEG signals and MRI datasets at the same time to get a better understanding of brain problems in space and time. The results show that machine learning can make EEG-based diagnostics more efficient and reliable, which shows that it has the potential to turn them into scalable, real-time clinical tools.

title: UPI Sentinel: Anomaly-Aware Fraud Detection Using Hybrid ML Models
authors: N Sowmiya, Maitra Varuna G, Nesana S, Kasthuri I
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: Digital payment systems such as the Unified Payment Interface (UPI) have greatly improved the convenience of transactions in India, but they are more prone to financial fraud. The conventional rule-based method of fraud detection does not pinpoint the emerging behavioral anomalies of financial transactions, causing economic losses and a lack of user confidence. This study proposes a novel method using a hybrid model for fraud detection by merging supervised and unsupervised machine learning techniques to pinpoint behavioral irregularities of financial transactions in real time. This algorithm uses Random Forest classification from supervised machine learning along with the capabilities of Isolation Forest and Autoencoders from unsupervised machine learning techniques. The system assesses primary behavioral attributes of financial transactions such as amount, time of transactions, location factors, and device usage data to classify the financial transactions as fraud or genuine. The use case example shows how the sudden change in the amount of expenditures or the time of transactions invokes a verification message for the UPI financial transactions before effecting the final payment. On receiving the confirmation from the user that it is a genuine financial transaction, the next stage is the authentication process of the user through the use of PIN or facial recognition before the financial transactions are authorized. The experimental result reveals better performance of the fraud detection model and a decrease in the amount of false positives when the hybrid model approach is used.

title: Comparing Genetic Algorithms for a Personalized Maternal Nutrition Recommendation System
authors: Richard Sugianto Laiman, Maria Susan Anggreainy
publish: 2026 International Seminar on Intelligent Business and Edge-Computing Research (ISIBER)
abstract: Stunting is a nutritional problem heavily influenced by maternal nutrient intake, making personalized nutritional intervention important as a prevention. This study aims to develop and compare nutrition recommendation systems using Genetic Algorithm (GA) and Adaptive Genetic Algorithm (AGA) to create personalized daily menus for pregnant women based on clinical profiles and medical conditions. The model was developed by using user profile data from NHANES (1999-2023), food composition data from the 2019 Indonesian Food Composition Table (TKPI), and the 2019 Indonesian Recommended Dietary Allowances (AKG) data. Both methods were implemented to optimize the fulfillment of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5}$</tex> daily nutrient targets and evaluated using Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE), Symmetric Mean Absolute Percentage Error (SMAPE), and Coefficient of Determination (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex>) metrics. The results show that the performance of the two models is similar, which was proven by a t-test, where GA achieved a MAPE of 8.08%, SMAPE of 8.89%, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> of 0.9106, while AGA achieved a MAPE of 8.04%, SMAPE of 8.86%, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex> of 0.9139. It is concluded that GA and AGA are equally effective approaches in terms of accuracy for this recommendation system with food data quality being important in model performance.

title: UAV Path Planning Based on Multi-Objective Grey Wolf Optimization
authors: Teng Chen, Yufei Wang, Xiangyu Liu, Yanhua Yin, Jiannan Zhao, Yunpeng Zhang
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Computing (SPIC)
abstract: For three-dimensional (3D) path planning of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) in complex environments, traditional methods struggle to balance multiple objectives such as path length, safety, energy consumption, and smoothness. To address this issue, this paper proposes a multi-objective gray wolf optimization (MOGWO) UAV path planning algorithm based on navigation variables. By introducing navigation variables to parameterize path nodes, the approach inherently embeds flight kinematic constraints and reduces search dimensionality. We construct a multi-objective evaluation framework covering distance, threat exposure, altitude stability, and turning smoothness, and employ Pareto dominance together with a gridbased crowding maintenance strategy to produce a welldistributed set of trade-off solutions. The simulation results of different scenarios show that this method has advantages in path quality, convergence speed and stability. This work provides a reusable and easily deployable methodological framework for multi-objective intelligent optimization and path planning in unmanned systems.

title: Pre-Processing Framework for Solar IOT Measurements Big-Data Using a Correction and Imputation Techniques
authors: Suzan Katamoura, Yusif A. Alghamdi, Hmood Al-Dossari, Mehmet Sabih Aksoy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: A national solar monitoring network has been created in order to support ambitious renewable energy goals in Saudi Arabia, which is comprised of high-resolution Internet of Things (IoT)-based irradiance measurement stations. Nevertheless, the quality of the solar radiation data is usually compromised by environmental conditions like dust deposition, intermittency of rainfall and operational disruptions which result in missed or biased observations. In order to solve these difficulties, this paper suggests a combined pre-processing model of solar IoT big data to improve the accuracy and stability of Global Horizontal Irradiance (GHI), Direct Normal Irradiance (DNI) and Diffuse Horizontal Irradiance (DHI) measurements under the extreme desert environment. The framework proposes a soiling correction model by use of weather classification matrix and Soiling Ratio (SR) which is calculated relative to clean benchmark periods. The suggested model was successful in alleviating soiling bias. To recover missing data, a hybrid imputation system is created which uses Observation Carried Forward/Backward Imputation (OCFBI) and a new multi-year system, Same Attribute Value from Different Years Imputation (SAVDYI) to exploit seasonal patterns of irradiance on the same calendar days/years. Experimental validation was done based on datasets of five representative monitoring sites and satellite-based irradiance products. The findings indicate that the suggested framework has an average Mean Bias Error (MBE) of less than 3 percent, a decrease in the root mean square error (RMSE) of up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5}$</tex> percent and a correlation coefficient (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{r}$</tex>) of more than <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 9 5}$</tex> in all the irradiance components. The augmented datasets are substantial in improving the accuracy of solar resource assessment, reliability in design of photovoltaic systems, and the Saudi Arabia energy diversification objectives of the vision 2030.

title: Bayesian Approach for Change-Point Detection in Z Control Charts
authors: Chensi Li, Jianjun Ma
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Computing (SPIC)
abstract: Aiming at the problem of locating the change point of single-valued Z-control chart when the out-of-control signal appears, a detection method based on Bayesian inference and Markov Chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) sampling-Bayesian Z-control chart change point detection (BCD) is proposed. The prior distribution for change point locations is modeled as a geometric distribution, effectively capturing the prior probability of their first occurrence. Through the MCMC algorithm based on Gibbs sampling, the posterior point estimation of the change point position are realized, posterior standard deviation as a quantitative measure of effective uncertainty Under varying mean shift magnitudes <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\theta=0.5$</tex> to 1.5), which demonstrates exceptional precision and robust reliability. The simulation results show that can accurately identify the real change point position and has good estimation accuracy and stability. Case analysis further verifies the practicability and effectiveness of the proposed model and method in industrial quality control scenarios.

title: Empirical Validation of Cyber Defence Effectiveness Using Statistical Analysis Across Attack Categories
authors: Rajesh Tanti, Smita Rai Agrawal
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies in Electronics and Green Energy (ICETEG)
abstract: This study presents a comprehensive empirical analysis of five major cybersecurity defence mechanisms (Firewall, Antivirus, Encryption, AI-based Detection, and VPN) across 3,000 cybersecurity incidents spanning 2015-2024. Using quantitative metrics including incident resolution time, financial impact, user protection, and success rates, we evaluate the relative effectiveness of each defence mechanism across six primary attack types: Phishing, Ransomware, Man-in-the- Middle, DDoS, SQL Injection, and Malware. Our findings reveal that Firewall systems demonstrate superior overall performance with the fastest average resolution time (35.71 hours) and lowest financial impact ($49.72M average), while defence effectiveness varies significantly by attack type. This research provides evidence-based recommendations for cybersecurity professionals in selecting optimal defence strategies. In this paper We also include a detailed architectural block diagram that illustrate the complete step by step strategy for analysis. The diagram shows the end-to-end workflow for our analysis. The all study provides the best defence mechanism/criteria to protect form the cyber-attack.

title: Secure and Scalable IoT-Based Smart Door Lock with Multi-Authentication Mechanisms
authors: S. Vignesh, Pavithraa S, Shalini S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: Conventional mechanical locks remain vulnerable to various security threats, including unauthorised duplication of keys, tampering, and the lack of access traceability. To address these concerns, this work introduces an IoT-enabled smart locking system that incorporates multiple layers of authentication, including mobile application access, numeric PIN verification, RFID/NFC credentials, and biometric fingerprint identification. The system is built around an ESP32 microcontroller controlling a motorised deadbolt, ensuring efficient processing with minimal power consumption. Encrypted cloud connectivity supports remote monitoring, event logging, and instant user notifications through customised mobile and web applications. Experimental validation demonstrates enhanced security, reduced response latency, and improved user convenience compared with traditional locking solutions. The proposed design provides a cost-effective, adaptable, and dependable security framework suitable for homes, businesses, and institutional environments.

title: Design and Implementation of a Smart Monitoring and Control System for Drying Machine Using IoT Technology
authors: Khakam Ma'ruf, Rizal Justian Setiawan, Yu-Tzu Chen, Desi Ayu Kumalasari, Darmono, Amna Rusmala
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Materials and Electrical Engineering (ISMEE)
abstract: Water hyacinth is widely used as a raw material for handicrafts, but its processing, particularly drying, remains largely manual and highly dependent on weather conditions. Efficient drying is essential to ensure product quality and support small and medium enterprises (SMEs) in the natural fiber craft sector. This research developed an integrated Internet of Things (IoT) monitoring and control system for a water hyacinth drying machine, incorporating a DS18B20 temperature sensor, an ESP32 microcontroller, and a solenoid valve. The system is designed to automate temperature regulation and optimize the drying process. The study employs a Research and Development (R&D) methodology with four stages, including identify IoT objectives, IoT component selection, implementation and prototyping, and integrate IoT system. Experimental results indicate that the DS18B20 sensor demonstrates high accuracy, with an average deviation of 0.1-0-2°C compared to a standard thermometer. The control system maintains the drying temperature within the optimal range of 70-85°C, with a maximum deviation of 1.7° C. The Blynk platform allows for remote monitoring and control via an intuitive user interface. Usability evaluation using the System Usability Scale (SUS) yielded an average score of 85.5, classified as “Very Good” (Grade A). These findings confirm that the IoT-enabled drying machine improves operational efficiency, energy use, and product quality, while supporting the economic growth of SMEs in the natural fiber craft industry.

title: AI-Driven Glaucoma Detection with Visual Explanations Using CNN and Grad-CAM
authors: Baranidharan A, Sivakumar V, Santhosh Kumar K, Jeyakrishnan M, Karthik V, Nishanth P
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: This research introduces an AI-based model for early glaucoma diagnosis from retinal fundus images. A Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) extracts discriminative visual features automatically without human-involving feature engineering and reaches higher diagnostic accuracy than traditional approaches. For better interpretability, Gradientweighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM) produces visual heatmaps focusing on the most relevant retinal areas that affect the model's decision, enhancing clinical trust and reliability. The system has been quantitatively assessed on publicly available retinal image datasets and exhibits stable diagnostic performance. It also accommodates a real-time scanning submodule that allows users to upload and examine newly acquired retinal images to detect glaucoma in real time. Through its superior performance, interpretability, and realtime availability, the proposed model is found to have strong potential as a clinically applicable decision-support system for efficient glaucoma screening and management.

title: Comparative Analysis of Transformer Based Models for Automated Utterance Classification in Motivational Interviewing
authors: Chandankumar J Patel, Cassandra Mae Patel, Joshua C Patel
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Motivational Interviewing (MI) is a collaborative, goal-oriented communication method used in medical education and practice to enhance patient motivation and support behavior change. Accurate classification of clinician utterances—such as Open Questions, Closed Questions, Scaled Questions, and Reflections—is critical for evaluating MI quality and providing automated training feedback. However, linguistic complexity and class imbalance present challenges for automated analysis. This study compares the performance of advanced Transformer-based language models ALBERT, RoBERTa, Deberta, Electra and T5, for utterance classification in real-world clinician–patient transcripts. Models were trained on labeled utterances and evaluated using Precision, Recall, and F1 metrics. Results show that RoBERTa as the most reliable and effective model for MI utterance classification, followed closely by ALBERT which offers an optimal balance between performance and efficiency. DeBERTa and ELECTRA are viable alternatives for moderateperformance needs, while T5 is unsuitable for this task without further architectural adaptation. These insights highlight the importance of selecting architectures aligned with the discriminative nature of behavioral health analytics.

title: Joint Task Allocation and Offloading Decision for Multi-UAV-Assisted MEC Oriented Towards Emergency Tasks
authors: Jiajun Ma, Ping Liu, Lijie Ren, Zhuotong Xu, Duo Li
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Computing (SPIC)
abstract: Mobile Edge Computing (MEC) represents a new paradigm, which proactively situates computational capabilities at the network's fringe to tackle the high-latency challenges posed by intensive services. Capitalizing on the advantages of flexible d eployment a nd w ide c overage f rom u nmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), UAV-assisted edge computing can further reduce service delays. Joint task allocation and offloading decisions thus becomes a core challenge in UAV-assisted MEC systems. However, existing research mainly focuses on task allocation and offloading decisions under static task arrival patterns. Consequently, these methods fail to address the high volatility and dynamic rescheduling challenges introduced by emergency tasks. Such tasks, however, are critical in disaster response scenarios. This paper addresses the joint task allocation and offloading decision problem within a multi-UAV-assisted MEC system, specifically focusing on the critical challenge posed by emergency tasks. This study defines the system timeliness model based on task priority and task processing delay. To this end, the joint optimization of task allocation and offloading decisions is modeled as a Markov Decision Process (MDP), with the objective of maximizing system timeliness. To handle the volatility of emergency tasks, this study proposes a Proximal Policy Optimization algorithm based on a two-stage mechanism and a multi-head attention structure (TAPPO).Simulation results under emergency task scenarios show that the proposed algorithm converges faster than three deep reinforcement learning baselines and consistently achieves higher system timeliness. Thus, it provides an effective and scalable solution for emergency task scheduling in multi-UAV-assisted MEC systems.

title: Ensemble Learning for Air Quality Forecasting: A Comparative Analysis of Random Forest, XGBoost, and LightGBM for PSI Prediction in Singapore
authors: Chelsea Willis, Cathrine Nadya Suyatno, Olivia Joanna Sanusi, Evander Banjarnahor
publish: 2026 International Seminar on Intelligent Business and Edge-Computing Research (ISIBER)
abstract: Accurate air-quality classification is essential for supporting public health protection and environmental risk mitigation, particularly in densely populated urban regions such as Singapore. This study evaluates the performance of three ensemble machine learning models Random Forest, XGBoost, and LightGBM in classifying Singapore's Pollutant Standards Index (PSI) using multivariate air-pollutant data. A total of 52,686 validated observations collected from official National Environment Agency records between 2016 and 2024 were utilized. Model performance was assessed using standard classification metrics, including accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score. Experimental results demonstrate that LightGBM consistently outperforms the comparative models, achieving classification accuracies ranging from 99.96% to 99.99%. Feature-importance analysis indicates that <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 4}$</tex>-hour <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{PM}_{2.5}$</tex> and PM10 concentrations are the most influential predictors of PSI categories, emphasizing the dominant role of particulate matter in urban air-quality conditions. However, classification performance for extreme PSI categories remains affected by data imbalance. Overall, the findings confirm the effectiveness of LightGBM for high-accuracy PSI classification and highlight its potential application in data-driven air-quality monitoring and early-warning systems.

title: Author Index
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Blockchain, Cloud Computing, and Data Analytics (ICoABCD)

title: Trustworthy Multimodal Fraud Detection with Federated Learning and Computer Vision
authors: Dendy K Pramudito, Jufriadif Na'am, Ferda Ernawan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Blockchain, Cloud Computing, and Data Analytics (ICoABCD)
abstract: Fraud detection in financial systems is still very difficult to do due to a vast class imbalance, how quickly fraudsters change their tactics and stringent privacy laws. This study interprets the XClever framework which is based on gradient fatigue and that embraces multi-modality and interpret-ability. These modifications include gradient boosting (XGBoost), attention-based gated recursive unit (GRU—Attention), cost sensitive thresholding, federated learning (FL) as well as computer vision (CV) extensions. This study employed a benchmark credit card dataset in this experiment. While GRU-Attention had a higher recall and better accuracy, XGBoost had the best precision and ROC—AUC (0.985). A late-fusion strategy can find a middle ground between these trade-offs, which could lead to the lowest predicted loss in costly calibration. The use of federate simulations shows that decentralized training with FedAvg preserves recall with only a slight decrease in ROC—AUC (0.010.02), while differential privacy further protects sensitive data. Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) trained on feature grids and recurrence plots attain a ROC—AUC of approximately 0.96, with visual representations elucidating supplementary structural anomalies. SHAP explanations identify V14, V12, and V4 as primary predictors, ensuring transparency and compliance with regulatory standards. The results indicate that an effective fraud detection system necessitates a comprehensive workflow that includes imbalance-aware learning, multimodal representation, privacy preserving collaboration, and explainability to ensure a reliable and scalable implementation within the financial services ecosystem.

title: Research and System Implementation of LoRa Technology for Intelligent Lighting in LED Street Lamp Power Control
authors: Yunfang Zhong, Shengyu Gan, Jinhui Wang, Huixiong Wang
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: Addressing the limitations of traditional LED streetlight control systems employing AC power line carrier communication—where signal transmission relies on AC cables and is susceptible to interference from external electrical equipment, resulting in poor anti-interference capabilities and low signal transmission accuracy—this paper proposes an optimised LED streetlight control solution based on LoRa communication technology. Firstly, an overall block diagram of the LoRa control system is established, comprising a LoRa gateway, terminal control module, and LED driver unit, clearly defining the functionalities and signal interaction logic of each module. Secondly, incorporating spread-spectrum communication theory, a LoRa signal transmission model is established to analyse its antiinterference mechanisms and long-range transmission characteristics. Based on this, an LED streetlight control circuit integrating EMI filtering, AC/DC conversion, constant-current control, and a LoRa communication chip is constructed. Finally, field testing validates the system's performance, comparing signal reception success rates and transmission stability under various operating conditions. Experimental results demonstrate that the designed LoRa-based LED streetlight power supply achieves 100% signal transmission accuracy at standard 33-metre streetlight intervals, with an effective transmission range spanning 2 kilometres. Compared to traditional power line carrier control, interference resistance improves by over 40%, significantly optimising the reliability and stability of LED streetlight control systems.

title: Adaptive Machine Learning for Energy-Efficient Resource Allocation in 6G Networks
authors: Rashmi P Sonar, Priyanka Abhijit Deshmukh, Kaveri A. Katake, Yogesh P. Khadse, Krunal S. Panpaliya, Vikramsingh R. Parihar
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Multimedia, Signal Processing and Communication Technologies (IMPACT)
abstract: The advent of sixth-generation (6G) wireless networks requires the use of smart and energy-aware systems that can help in the efficient management of communication and computational resources. In this paper, an Adaptive Machine Learning (AML) framework is suggested to allocate resources in dynamic 6G environments economically and with reduced energy consumption. The framework is a combination of multi-agent deep reinforcement learning (MADRL) and federated adaptive coordination, which is used to coordinate distributed base stations to jointly optimize power, bandwidth, and computation resources in real time. An energy-efficiency maximization problem is formulated, where transmit power constraints, bandwidth, and quality-of-service (QoS) requirements are taken into account. The AML algorithm is trained to learn near-optimal allocation policies in response to environmental interactions, retraining, and selftuning of parameter changes, so that the algorithm can be resilient under varying traffic loads and mobility patterns. The simulation results demonstrate that the proposed AML framework outperforms static and heuristic benchmarks, achieving up to 35% improvement in energy efficiency, faster convergence, and higher QoS satisfaction. The findings validate AML as a viable, scalable, and sustainable approach for intelligent 6 G resource management, enabling autonomous, energy-aware, and selfoptimizing communication systems.

title: Securing the Metaverse: Challenges and Solutions in Virtual Reality Cybersecurity
authors: Shaharyar Alam Ansari, Nikhil Sharma, Shashank Chaudhary
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: The metaverse will introduce unparalleled opportunities and cybersecurity challenges with its integration of VR, AR, and Internet technologies. Immersive environments introduce new attack surfaces through VR hardware and the collection of vast amounts of personal data, introducing concerns around privacy, identity theft, and digital asset protection. This academic review maps the current landscape of metaverse cybersecurity, identifies core gaps in user privacy awareness, mismatched AI/IoT security models, weak infrastructure security, inadequate authentication and digital identity, interoperability issues, inconsistent regulatory policies, visualization vulnerabilities-including inference attacks and deepfakes-and privacy trade-offs in blockchain configurations. We further propose cross-platform privacy protocols, Zero Trust Architecture models native to the metaverse, adaptive AI security, secure cross-world identity verification, privacy-preserving AR/VR rendering, and decentralized identity management. Addressing these gaps can help guide researchers, platform developers, and policymakers toward a safe and privacy-by-design metaverse.

title: Compact Meander-Line Rectenna Operating at 3.5 GHz for RF Energy Harvesting in IoT and Wearable Devices
authors: Atul Sharma, Vandana Vikas Thakare, P.K. Singhal
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical, Communication, and Computing Technologies (iCONECCT)
abstract: The proposed research work focuses on development of high-performance meander-line rectenna resonating at 3.5 GHz. The antenna is fabricated using FR-4 substrate. Initially the proposed antenna is simulated in CST software and the achieved return loss is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{- 1 8 ~ d B}$</tex>. In the later stage the antenna is fabricated and tested on Vector Analyser. The obtained results are more better with the values of return loss equal to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$-\mathbf{2 0. 5 0 ~ d B}$</tex> and a gain of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2. 5 2 ~ d B i}$</tex>. The rectifier full-wave circuit is developed using HSMS-2850 Schottky diode and a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pi$</tex> matching network on ADS Software with 80% RF-to-DC efficiency and 1.42 V DC across a 350Ω load.

title: Thompson-Lampard Theorem Based Cross-Capacitive Sensor for the Detection of Conductivity and Adulteration in Liquid Foods
authors: Mohammad Haris Bin Anwar, Mirza Mohammad Shadab, Anwar Ulla Khan
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: The quality and safety of liquid foods, such as milk, is a worldwide problem due to the common presence of adulterants. Moreover, it is difficult to solve this problem using common laboratory techniques, such as chromatography and spectroscopy, since this technique possesses a low rate of rapidity and is not applicable in the field. In this paper, the design, simulation, fabrication, and evaluation of a crosscapacitive alternating current (A.C.) sensor are discussed. The design of this sensor is based on the Thomson-Lampard theorem, in which it is possible to determine the conductivity of a solution with limited dependence on geometric calibration. At the same time, this sensor is compact and economical, and it is possible to read it with common multi-meter equipment. Moreover, it is possible to determine that the results of measuring the conductivity of ground-water, tap-water, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R O}$</tex>-water are consistent with theoretical expectations. In addition, it is possible to determine that this sensor is useful to detect changes in dielectric constant with the addition of water or urea to milk using a finite element simulation.

title: Research on the Use of Generative Adversarial Networks to SDN-Enabled Home Networks to Classify Traffic on the Internet with Encryption
authors: Rajesh Kumar, Deepak Sharma, Kavita
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: The exponential rise in encrypted network traffic presents significant challenges for traditional classification techniques, particularly within Software-Defined Networking (SDN)-based home networks where packet inspection is limited by privacy constraints. To address this, we derive a novel Generative Adversarial Network (GAN)-based framework that classifies encrypted traffic without requiring decryption, thus preserving user privacy while enhancing detection accuracy. The GAN leverages adversarial learning to model complex traffic distributions and differentiate between benign and malicious flows based on flow metadata and statistical features. Experimental evaluation using the ISCX VPN dataset demonstrates that the proposed model outperforms conventional classifiers-such as CNN, RNN, SVM, and Random Forestachieving an accuracy, precision, and recall of 98.8 %,an AUC of 0.995, and a low inference time of 2 ms. Additionally, the model maintains extremely low positive and negative rates of 0.006, confirming its robustness and reliability in dynamic, real- time environments. This research presents a scalable, efficient, and privacy-preserving solution for encrypted traffic classification in SDN-enabled home networks, paving the way for secure and intelligent network management.

title: Advancing Repurchase Prediction in E-Commerce: RFM-Driven Explainable Hybrid Machine Learning Paradigm
authors: Lovleen Kaur, Paurav Goel, Sonam Khattar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: The intense market competition brought about by the fast growth of e-commerce has resulted in the importance of customer retention and proper prediction of repurchase to business success. Well-functioning forecasting allows improving marketing, provides personal approach, and helps to build relationships with customers. Although recent hybrid and ensemble machine learning models have high predictive power, they cannot provide transparency and trust in business decisions due to being black-box models. Explainable Artificial Intelligence can overcome this issue, as it reveals such crucial elements of behavior that determine repurchase, like Recency, Frequency, and Monetary (RFM) variables. This is a systematic exploration of explainable and hybrid machine learning methods to predict repurchases, which focuses on methods, data, performance metrics, and explainability methods. The study will contribute to the improvement of explainable hybrid learning models by combining the RFM knowledge with the XAI techniques to achieve an effective balance between predictive accuracy and interpretability.

title: Copyright Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)

title: Edge-Based Clique Similar Formation Control for Second-Order Multi-Agent Systems
authors: Yupeng Mao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechatronics, Automation and Intelligent Control (MAIC)
abstract: This paper proposes a distributed similar formation control method for second-order multi-agent systems, relying solely on relative position and relative velocity information. By constructing a maxclique-based cost function incorporating rotation matrices and scaling factors, the designed control strategy achieves both geometric similarity maintenance and velocity consensus under dynamic constraints. Utilizing the edgebased clique addition topology, the method guarantees formation convergence to a globally consistent configuration without the need for a global coordinate frame. Stability analysis based on the Lyapunov method and LaSalle's Invariance Principle demonstrates that the closed-loop system globally asymptotically converges to the desired equilibrium set, wherein all agents attain unified rotation, scaling, and steady-state velocity. Simulation experiments in both two-dimensional and three-dimensional scenarios further verify the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed method.

title: Optimization of Fuzzy Control Strategy for Electric Vehicle Based on Cross Entropy Algorithm
authors: Jie Wang, Minyu Liu, Liu Yan, Chong Song, Zhenjun Liu, Te Mu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechatronics, Automation and Intelligent Control (MAIC)
abstract: In order to study the influence of energy management strategy on the performance of composite vehicles in hybrid electric vehicles, nickel-hydrogen batteries and super capacitors were selected as composite power sources, and the fuzzy control strategy was used to allocate the energy demand of vehicles. Based on the constraints of UDDS operating conditions, the vehicle power consumption and the braking energy recovery capability of supercapacitors in the composite power supply are optimized. Firstly, the basic framework of energy management strategy is obtained through the analysis of vehicle structure and working conditions. Secondly, the fuzzy controller based on the framework is built in MATLAB, and the cross entropy algorithm(CEA) is used to iteratively optimize the position of the membership function in the fuzzy controller. The parameters are compared with the NSGA-II, and the optimization ability of the two algorithms under the same conditions is studied. The research results show that the fuzzy controller can achieve the desired goal by the cross entropy algorithm. The supercapacitor energy recovery has gained 16.3%, and the vehicle power consumption has been reduced by 63.67%. At the same time, the vehicle performance has also been improved. The increase was <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$82 \%, 25 {\%}$</tex> and 12.77% in the nickel-hydrogen battery DOD, maximum grade and maximum speed. In summary, it is valid to use the cross entropy algorithm to optimize the fuzzy energy management strategy of the hybrid power supply.

title: Integration of Convolutional Neural Networks and Morphological Feature Analysis for Tomato Leaf Disease Diagnosis
authors: Siddharth R Shigehalli, Manish Bharat, Ritesh Dash, Bhabasis Mohapatra
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Tomato leaf disease classification is critical challenge in modern precision agriculture where nearly and accurate prediction can significantly enhance the crop health and yield. This steady presents a CNN based classification model designed to detect and categorize tomato leaf disease using an automated approach. The methodology involved systematic collection of modular data set, processing trough augmented procession and thereby validating the model with the testing data which is 20% of the classical data set considered for the analysis. 6layer architecture CNN model with leaky-ReLu activation function has been used. Different statistical parameters such as ROC, AUC, PR, Hyper parameter tuning has been adopted in order to validate the model under extremely adverse conditions. Python 6 with augmented base has been utilized for analysis.

title: Research on Electrochemical Mechanism Modeling and Steady-State Characteristic Simulation of Alkaline Water Electrolysis for Hydrogen Production
authors: Haiying Song, Changhao Guo, Zhifeng Li, Min Xie
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechatronics, Automation and Intelligent Control (MAIC)
abstract: Alkaline water electrolyzers are pivotal to water-electrolysis hydrogen production. In this work, an electrochemical model built in Matlab/Simulink was used to quantify how steady-state performance depends on operating temperature, electrolyte concentration and system pressure. Raising the temperature consistently lowers the cell voltage required at a given current density. As the KOH concentration is progressively increased, the cell voltage first declines to a minimum that coincides with the electrolyte's optimal conductivity and then increases again;consequently, faradaic efficiency peaks around 30 wt %, whereas overly dilute or concentrated solutions both incur penalties. Elevating pressure slightly increases the operating voltage yet markedly improves overall electrolysis efficiency. The simulations elucidate the mechanistic links between these parameters and both the polarization curve and energy efficiency, these findings were subsequently tested with an orthogonal experimental design that targeted the optimal ranges of the three variables; measured performance agreed closely with the model predictions, providing a theoretical basis for the optimal design and high-performance operation of alkaline water electrolyzers.

title: A Machine Learning-Based Approach to Real-Time Traffic Prediction using Large Language Models: Model Performance and Scalability Challenges
authors: Chandra Nikitha Shatdarsanam, Srinivasa Rao Kurakula, Satyam Sheshansh, Balaji Salem Balasundram, Riaz Ahmed Mohammed Sait, Sivakoteswararao Katta
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Semantic and accurate real-time contextdependent traffic prediction is essential to develop smart city intelligent transportation systems, which can be used to improve route plan, decrease traffic jams and rapid response of emergency. Despite the partial success of classical machine learning and conventional deep learning paradigms, they failed to accurately model the complex traffic data with spatial and temporal patterns. In this paper, we introduce an innovative machine learning-based framework that exploits sequence modeling of large language models (LLMs) including fine-tuned GPT and LLaMA models on real-time traffic prediction. The framework represents multi-source traffic features to tokenized sequences, which enables LLMs to exploit long-range temporal dependencies and cross-location traffic flow interactions. We perform extensive experiments on the benchmark datasets METR-LA, PEMS-BAY, and NGSIM, and compare the LLMbased method with classical models, such as SVM and LSTM, as well as the recently developed transformer-based architectures. We demonstrate that LLMs provide substantial improvement in predictive accuracy and out-of-sample consistency compared to baselines in terms of RMSE and MAE metrics. But we also emphasize considerable drawbacks, especially about latency, computational overhead, and scalability for the edge placement. The paper concludes with potential and future research directions on optimizing the models, tokenization methods, as well as largescale LLM architectures that can be tailored for intelligent traffic systems.

title: A Spatiotemporal CNN-LSTM Framework for Forecasting Crop Disease Progression
authors: Shashank Sahu, Sunny Teotia, Yash Jain, Ritwik Goswami, Santosh Kumar Upadhyay
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: The majority of deep learning methods used in plant pathology are based on the classification of the static images and do not consider the way diseases change with time. This limitation impairs premature diagnosis and accurate severity determination, in particular in the field, dynamic farm conditions. To overcome this, we suggest a spatiotemporal deep learning model that brings together a convolutional Neural Network (CNN) for image extraction with a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network on temporal sequence modeling. This hybrid CNN-LSTM architecture studies the consecutive images in order to capture the progression of disease symptoms. Real time crop data are available only in small datasets, and time-varying datasets do not involve real-time. With scarcity, we present a synthetic sequence generation approach that simulates the disease development out of still pictures with alpha blending. The individual frames are run in a pre-trained inceptionV3 model to generate strong feature vectors, which the LSTM then use to learn the temporal patterns. The proposed approach is demonstrated to work with the publicly available rice leaf disease dataset constantly achieves higher performance than a state-of-the-art static CNN baseline in terms of accuracy, precision, recall and f1-score. By incorporating temporal information, this framework offers a more credible and informative instrument of predictive disease surveillance in precision agriculture.
