title: Predicting Clinical Trial Site Performance using Machine Learning
authors: Sumanth Singh
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Success of clinical trials depends extensively on the operational performance of the participating site, yet early detection of underperforming sites remains an ongoing challenge. In this work, the authors are presenting a machine learning-based framework for predicting the performance of clinical trial sites from historical operational and enrollmentrelated indicators. For early risk identification and data-driven support for decision-making in a trial management team, several supervised learning models have been evaluated to classify site performance outcomes. Experimental results showed that ensemble-based models significantly outperform traditional baselines regarding prediction accuracy and robustness. The proposed approach emphasizes the potential of artificial intelligence to improve trial efficiency by mitigating operational risk and enabling proactive planning of clinical trials.

title: Design and Development of a Low-Cost Portable Potentiostat for Screen Printed Electrodes
authors: RajithKumar B K, Navya Singh, Sphatika S Jain, V Megha, Shreya Hanimanal, Priyal Khandelwal
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: This work presents the design and development of a low-cost, portable potentiostat to be used for flexible electrochemical sensing applications. The system is built using operational amplifiers for precise potential control and current measurement. It uses a Blue Pill (STM32F103C8T6) microcontroller, which provides 12-bit digital waveform generation while maintaining low power consumption and small size. The entire circuit was simulated and validated prior to integration. The potentiostat was evaluated using cyclic voltammetry (CV) in a standard redox electrolyte <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(5 \text{mM} \mathrm{K}_{4}\left[\text{Fe}(\text{CN})_{6}\right] in 0.1 M KCl\right)$</tex>, scanned from −0.6 V to +0.8 V at scan rates of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$25 - 100 \text{mV} / \mathrm{s}$</tex>. The device produced clear and well-defined redox peaks, demonstrating proper control of the working electrode potential and accurate current detection. The measured response showed an anodic peak at approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$+ 0.20 \mathrm{V}$</tex> and a cathodic peak at around <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$-0. 1 9 \text{Vvs}$</tex>. <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Ag}/\text{AgCl}$</tex> with a peak separation (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\Delta \text{Ep}$</tex>) of approximately 390 mV. Overall, the results confirm that the potentiostat achieves reasonable sensitivity, reproducibility and stable electrochemical performance, making it suitable for applications such as point-of-care diagnostics, environmental monitoring and biomolecule detection. This contributes to affordable and accessible analytical tools aligned with SDG 11 for sustainable communities.

title: Implementation of MPPT Algorithms Control for Grid-Connected PV Inverter
authors: Arpita Panda, Kumkum Mallick, Souvik Mondal, Riju Nandi, Arnab Ghosh
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: The increased need for sustainable energy sources around the world has led to a faster adoption of photovoltaic (PV) systems into the main power grid. This paper presents the development and simulation of a grid-tied photovoltaic (PV) system with maximum power point tracking (MPPT) capabilities, utilizing the MATLAB/Simulink platform. The model gives a full picture of important parts of the system, like the PV array, MPPT controller, Pulse Width Modulation (PWM) generator, and Phase-Locked Loop (PLL) mechanism. In this single-stage setup, the PV array goes straight to a DC-AC inverter. To accomplish real-time tracking of the maximum power under dynamic irradiance and temperature conditions, MPPT is applied by dynamically controlling the inverter's output current reference. By controlling the inverter, the DC power from the PV array may be regulated into high-quality electricity, with the output current phase-and frequency-synchronized with the grid voltage. PLL is essential in ensuring precise phase tracking, hence, improving grid compliance and power quality. The system represents the performance of an actual PV plant and proves the viability of smart control methods for enhancing efficiency and reliability in renewable energy systems.

title: An Embedded Vision-Based Haptic System to Assist the Visually Impaired Using Kinect Sensor and NVIDIA Jetson
authors: Karthikeyan G, Samuel Venkatesan, Mothesh K, Nakshathra N, Anania Maman M, Kaushikram M, Venkata Ramana Rao S, Mahiban Lindsay N
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: Millions of individuals suffer from restricted mobility and freedom due to visual impairment, making it a major global public health concern. Conventional assistive devices, such as the long cane, have limitations; they can only detect impediments at ground level within a short distance and do not provide protection from head-level threats or object classification. In order to provide complete ambient awareness through haptic feedback, this research suggests a revolutionary wearable assistive device that makes advantage of The Microsoft Kinect sensor. The recommended method uses a MS Kinect sensor model paired with a Nvidia Jetson Nano processing unit to capture and process the real time 3D data of the user's surroundings. It uses a prebuilt AI model which detects objects like vehicles, trees and humans. This integrated data, which determines both what an object is and how far away it is, is translated into distinct, pre-defined vibratory patterns. These patterns are transmitted to the user via a wearable bracelet fitted with an Adafruit DRV2605L haptic motor controller, delivering intuitive, non-visual cues about the environment.

title: Optimizing Climate Condition Prediction Using Q-Learning, Deep Q-Networks, and Policy Gradient Reinforcement Learning Methods
authors: Sivanesan N, Thankaraj A, Satheesh S, John Peter V, Satheesh Kumar J, Uma Devi M
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: Future climate condition predictions contribute essential functions to environmental supervision and agricultural resource planning and disaster response preparation. The study uses reinforcement learning methods with artificial intelligence progress to develop next-day climate event forecasting as a sequential decision problem. A predictive model receives rewards through accuracy metrics that rate its predictions of following-day rainfall based on historical temperature and humidity and rainfall data. The Indonesia Climate Dataset receives evaluations through implementation of Q-learning and Deep Q-Networks (DQN) and Policy Gradient method (REINFORCE). Q-learning implements a tabular method yet DQN makes action-value approximations using deep neural structures and REINFORCE optimizes the policy directly. The evaluation includes both classification metrics like accuracy, precision, recall and F1-score and reinforcement learning metrics that measure cumulative reward and average reward per episode. The experimental outcome reveals that DQN delivers optimal prediction results by accumulating more rewards than its competitors Q-learning and reinforce.

title: Closed Loop PI and Sliding Mode Control of PV-Wind Driven BBCCI-Quadratic Boost Converter for Motor Applications with Auxiliary Battery Charging
authors: D Vanitha, V. Malathi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical, Communication, and Computing Technologies (iCONECCT)
abstract: This paper presents a hybrid PV-wind energy system for dynamic motor loads and battery charging, utilizing a dual-stage converter: a Buck-Boost Converter with Coupled Inductor (BBCCI) cascaded with a Quadratic Boost Converter (QBC). This topology achieves high voltage gain and stable operation despite fluctuating renewable inputs. The closed-loop performance is comparatively evaluated using ProportionalIntegral (PI) and Sliding Mode (SM) controllers. The SM controller demonstrates superior robustness against disturbances and parameter variations. Simulation results confirm that the SM-controlled system achieves faster transient response, lower overshoot, and improved stability under dynamic loading than the PI-controlled system. Due to high component costs, closed-loop hardware validation was not conducted in this work, but is intended for future investigation; the closed-loop control comparison was confirmed solely through detailed simulation.

title: Intelligent Zonal Control and Energy Optimization of TRNSYS-Based Radiant Floor Heating Systems
authors: Haiying Song, Zhenyu Pan, Yingkai Sun, Wei Zheng, Tianqi Liu, Zekai Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechatronics, Automation and Intelligent Control (MAIC)
abstract: To address the high energy consumption and limited intelligence of conventional continuous radiant floor heating systems, this study develops a TRNSYS-based model that integrates smart room-by-room control with a solar-assisted airsource heat-pump floor-heating system. A two-bedroom, two-hall apartment is selected as the reference building and fully represented in TRNSYS, incorporating detailed modules for room geometry, envelope properties, solar collectors, air-source heat pump, radiant floor circuits and PID controllers. A sparrowsearch algorithm is employed to auto-tune the PID parameters of the heating loops. Annual simulations reveal that, relative to conventional uninterrupted heating, the proposed adaptive zoning strategy cuts total energy use by 29.5 %, raises the seasonal system COP to 3.26 and still maintains every room within the comfort band prescribed, thereby demonstrating that real-time adjustment of heating delivery to occupancy patterns can secure thermal comfort while markedly improving energy efficiency.

title: A Method for Product Rating Improvement Using NLP and Sentiment Analysis
authors: Prateek Gupta, Rajendra Kumar Gupta, Akhilesh Tiwari
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical, Communication, and Computing Technologies (iCONECCT)
abstract: The advent of web 2.0 and 3.0 has popularized online shopping applications. E-commerce application provides user generated contents to customers and business organizations. Star rating and review sentiment analysis are important integral component of every e-commerce application. They are used for product decision making process. Reliability of rating system reduces due to mismatch between user provided ratings & sentiment analysis of reviews. Platform specific ratings, rating biases & fake reviews are important issues that need to be solved. To address some of these issues, the presented work has introduced a method for product rating improvement using sentiment analysis of reviews based on natural language processing (NLP). Sentiments from text reviews are calculated by VADER and TextBlob and SentiWordNet. Then sentiment score of them are averaged to get improved sentiment score. The improved sentiment score are utilized to optimize rating. The proposed method produces a more reliable, consistent and trustworthy rating. After that there exists no need to look on individual sentiments. Data under research is collected from Flipkart and Amazon. The result of experimentation shows that combining sentiment adjustments in rating leads to more trusted and reliable ratings, thereby providing help to consumers making better purchase decisions.

title: Fuzzy Slack Enhanced Graph Random Vector Functional Link Based on Multi-View Learning
authors: Reetu Shrivastava, Bhagat Singh Raghuwanshi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical, Communication, and Computing Technologies (iCONECCT)
abstract: Machine learning models in real-world datasets tend to give biased predictions. Therefore, it is important to use evaluation metrics other than accuracy, such as precision, recall, F1-score, G-means, and AUC. Conventional backpropagationbased neural networks face problems such as slow convergence, stuckness at local minima, and learning rate sensitivity, while randomization based architectures such as Random Vector Functional Link (RVFL) provide fast training and universal approximation capability. Multi-View Learning (MVL) improves performance by using complementary data representations. Building on all of these, Graph Random Vector Functional Link based on Multi-View Learning (GRVFL-MV) gives strong results but degrades under noise, outliers, and imbalance. To address these limitations, this work proposes Fuzzy Slack-Enhanced GRVFLMV (FSE-GRVFL-MV), which integrates fuzzy membership weighting, leaky ReLU activation, Xavier initialization, adaptive regularization, and weighted ensemble prediction. Experimental validation on multiple imbalanced datasets shows that FSE-GRVFL-MV significantly outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods in various metrics, especially in scenarios with highclass imbalance, noise, and outliers, and is also computationally efficient, making it suitable for practical applications where robustly handling imbalanced data is essential.

title: Formal Modeling of Staking and Validator Creation in PoS System
authors: Raeesa Mukhtar, Nazir Ahmad Zafar, Hamra Afzaal
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Innovations in Information and Communication Technologies (IICT)
abstract: Blockchain Technology has presented a tamperproof and secure structure that supports the modern digital environment. Among the many consensus blockchain systems, the Proof of Stake (PoS) has emerged as an extensively used protocol due to its lower energy consumption and high security. However, the staking mechanism and creation of the validator process are the main parts of the PoS network, and any flaw in these processes can cause financial loss and breach the system's security. Therefore, we use formal verification methods for the verification and validation of the staking mechanism. In this study, we presented a formal model of staking processes in a PoS-based system, emphasizing the verification of accurate signer, stake allocation, creation of a validator with an NFT minted, and update timeline. The CSP\# language is used to specify these processes, and Linear Temporal Logics (LTL) are used to define the properties against these specifications. To verify that the system satisfies the critical properties, like verification of an authentic signer, secure stake registration, and deadlock-free validator creation using the Process Analysis Toolkit (PAT) model checker, and ensure the system's liveness and safety. Our results describe the importance of formal verification in enhancing the Polygon Blockchain's integrity by ensuring the staking mechanism in the stake manager contract is secure and reliable.

title: Simulation and Optimization of an Intelligent Sorting System for Industrial Robots Based on Digital Twin and Reinforcement Learning
authors: Yunshuo Gai
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Intelligent Manufacturing (MAIM)
abstract: To meet urgent demands for flexible sorting in intelligent manufacturing, this study explores an innovative solution combining digital twin technology with deep reinforcement learning (DRL). Using MATLAB/Simulink, we developed a high-fidelity virtual simulation accurately replicating dynamic behaviors of six-degree-of-freedom industrial robots, conveyor belts, and vision sensors. Unlike traditional path planning relying on pre-programmed trajectories or precise physical models, our approach employs the Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (DDPG) algorithm [1], enabling the control agent to autonomously learn optimal grasping strategies through environmental interaction. Simulation results demonstrate that the fully trained controller efficiently accomplishes dynamic target sorting tasks, achieving an average success rate exceeding 94%, while exhibiting satisfactory stability when handling uncertainties like conveyor belt speed fluctuations and sensor measurement noise. This method's key advantage is avoiding explicit solutions to complex robotic kinematic models, offering a promising technical pathway for constructing adaptive control systems integrating perception, decision-making, and execution.

title: An Investigation into the Influence of Head Movements on Fundamental Eye-Gaze Directions Using Mediapipe-Based Mesh of Intersection Point and Head Pose Estimation
authors: Abdul Rehman, Muhammad Syaiful Amri bin Suhaimi, Ooi Joo On, Sayed Ahmad Zikri Bin Sayed Aluwee, Pringgo Widyo Laksono
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Innovations in Information and Communication Technologies (IICT)
abstract: Eye-gaze tracking is one of the most vital parameters for understanding human behavior, cognitive processes, and visual perception in computer vision. It has wide applications in real-world scenarios for behavior analysis, particularly in tracking the real-time attentiveness of students in online learning. However, most existing research overlooks the impact of head movements on gaze accuracy, despite it being a frequent and unavoidable behavior during real-time interactions such as online learning. This gap highlights a critical limitation in current attentiveness monitoring systems, which often assume a static head position and fail to account for how head pose affects gaze direction. To explore this in this research we used Mesh of Intersection Point method for eye-gaze tracking, a featurebased method suitable for real-time analysis, offering reliable results in detecting and tracking eye using laptop's webcam to analyze the head movement behavior. The study is carried out in two phases. In the first phase, we analyze the accuracy drop in eye-gaze tracking caused by head movements across five reference direction points on a laptop screen. In the second phase, separate machine learning classifiers are trained for each gaze direction, for allowing horizontal and vertical head movement for each direction. This research investigates how head movements influence eye-gaze accuracy and uses machine learning to enable gaze direction classification even when head movements occur.

title: Comparative Evaluation of Intelligent Control Strategies for AVR Performance
authors: Muhammad Shehzore Hashmi, Muhammad Shahjahan Hashmi, Zahid Muhammad
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Innovations in Information and Communication Technologies (IICT)
abstract: This paper conducts a comprehensive comparative evaluation of Automatic Voltage Regulator (AVR) models for thermal and hydro power systems, focusing on the performance evaluation of conventional Proportional-Integral-Derivative (PID) and intelligent control strategies, namely Artificial Neural Network (ANN), Fuzzy Logic Controller (FLC), and Adaptive Neuro-Fuzzy Inference System (ANFIS). The AVR models are meticulously designed in MATLAB/SIMULINK, employing transfer functions to model critical power system components such as the amplifier, exciter, generator, and sensor. Each controller is integrated to regulate the terminal voltage under dynamic conditions. Key performance measures are the settling time, rise time, and overshoot, which are thoroughly examined to identify the effectiveness of the controller. The outputs show that FLC performs better with the least overshoot (0.0000038) and settling time (1.9421s), closely surpassed by ANFIS, where it has an overshoot of 0.00033 and a settling time of 2.2635s. Comparatively, the tuned PID controller and ANN have more overshoot and longer settling time. This paper highlights why intelligent controllers can be transformative in modern voltage regulation of complex power systems, which gives a solid and scalable theoretical framework to enable both industrial and academic research on the state-of-the-art advances in the area of advanced power system control.

title: Blockchain-Enabled Consent-Based Security for Multi-Face Digital Images Captured in Public Areas
authors: Vivek Kumar Sharma, Renuka Arora, Chandra Shekhar Yadav
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: Today, digital images are captured everywhere through mobile phones, CCTV cameras, and public surveillance systems. When someone takes a picture in a public place, many other people often appear in the background without knowing it. These bystanders may not want their faces to be captured or shared, but they have no control over where these images go. This creates serious privacy and safety concerns in real-life situations. For example, a stranger taking a photo in a mall or park may unknowingly capture several other people, and these images can be uploaded online without their permission. CCTV systems also record thousands of people daily, and these images may be accessed or misused without their consent. To solve this real life problem, this paper proposes a blockchain enabled, consent-based security system for digital images containing multiple faces. In our method, every face in an image is detected automatically and encrypted separately using a fast and secure ChaCha20 encryption algorithm. This means no one's face is visible unless they give permission. When someone wants to view another person's face in the image, a consent request is sent to that person. The person can verify their identity using a simple Aadhaar-style biometric and OTP check, and then approve or reject the request. To protect the faces of individuals in digital images, user-submitted approval and rejection requests are recorded on the blockchain, eliminating any possibility of tampering. Implementation results demonstrate that the proposed framework displays only the faces of individuals who have provided their permission, while the faces of all other individuals remain securely encrypted. During the experiments, it was found that face detection yielded results with 96.4% accuracy. The blockchain-enabled consent framework prevented unauthorized access with 100% effectiveness. The proposed solution effectively preserves the privacy of individuals in digital images captured in public spaces and protects individuals from misuse of technology in society.

title: Integrating Explainability, Fairness, and Privacy in AI Powered Clinical Decision Systems
authors: Anshul, Aleem Ali
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) is now part of the current healthcare systems, especially clinical decision support systems as well as diagnostic imaging. Despite the high diagnostic accuracy of deep learning models, their practical implementation is still restricted because of issues that are connected to interpretability, algorithmic bias, data privacy, and accountability. The paper explores how the principles of ethically oriented AI, i.e., explainability, fairness, and privacy can be applied to the AI-based clinical decision-making systems. Explainable AI methods are also used to increase the transparency of the model and allow clinicians to understand the diagnostic predictions with visual and featurebased explanations. Learning strategies that are fairnessconscious are integrated to prevent demographic bias and achieve equal diagnostic performance between different groups of patients. Besides that, a privacy preserving federated learning is also examined to allow collaborative model training without a central storage of information, enabling the preservation of sensitive patient data. The results of experimental assessment based on publicly available medical imaging datasets indicate that ethically enhanced AI models are competitive in the diagnostic accuracy, and they are also expected to significantly enhance their interpretability, fairness, and data security. The results indicate that to foster trustful, open, and clinically credible decision support systems within health facilities, the ethical concerns of AI systems need to be directly incorporated into the design of such systems.

title: Virangana: Advanced Women's Safety Application
authors: Maithili Kohale, Drishti Sharma, Ritesh Bobade, Shrushti Pohankar, Yashaswi Khobragade, Rutuja Chirwatkar
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: We introduce Virangana, an advanced, featurerich mobile application designed to enhance women's safety. It provides features like the panic/SOS button and location sharing, along with advanced features such as fake calling, voice-assisted SOS alerts, safe route suggestions and zone highlighting, and a proactive safety monitor on the dashboard. Our app uses rulebased machine learning on user context parameters to calculate safety scores for each zone on the map. These safety scores are used to indicate the safety level on the proactive monitor and to efficiently suggest safe routes using the A* search algorithm. The app is intuitive to use and has been developed using agile methodologies. It is implemented using Dart and Flutter to support cross-platform deployment. Through comparison with existing methods, we demonstrate that our app outperforms all existing methods and contributes to enhancing women's safety.

title: Control-Based Simulation of COPD Physiology using Adaptive Solvers for Digital Health Applications
authors: Anju Saini, Shazmani, Kalpana Chauhan, Sarita
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: In this study, a control-theoretic framework for simulating COPD respiratory physiology was proposed. The proposed framework utilizes adaptive numerical solvers for simulating the disease's effects on the body. It also has the potential for developing new digital health applications. A nonlinear pulmonary model was used for simulating the effects of airflow limitation in the lungs, which is a major characteristic of COPD. The dynamic control of alveolar ventilation was achieved by adjusting the inspiratory pressure based on the difference between the simulated oxygen saturation signal <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\Delta \text{SO}_{2}\right)$</tex> by a proportional controller. The default numerical solver for MATLAB's integration routine, ODE45, was used for ensuring the stability of the simulated results. For simulated desaturation episodes, the proposed method has shown a qualitatively consistent response for ventilatory adjustments. This is due to the numerical stability of the proposed method. It is a control-oriented method that has the potential for being a fundamental component for the development of digital twins for COPD management. It utilizes computer modeling and remote sensing for personalizing disease management.

title: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies and Interdisciplinary Innovation (ICAT2I)
authors: N/A
publish: N/A

title: Optimization of Machine Equipment Fault Prediction Model Based on Ensemble Learning
authors: Jiaquan Dai, Jianzhi Chen, Gongxing Wu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechatronics, Automation and Intelligent Control (MAIC)
abstract: This paper is based on the design and development of models using ensemble learning for fault prediction in mechanical equipment. It addresses the issues of imbalanced equipment datasets and the scarcity of data for faulty machines by oversampling the fault data to increase the amount of data for malfunctioning machines. By incorporating a cost function, the model introduces probability estimation and misclassification costs in the classification process, reclassifying the outcomes. Finally, the model is evaluated using ROC curves and confusion matrix metrics to reduce classification errors. The established model provides reliable support for the timeliness of machine maintenance.

title: Application of Ultra-Wideband to a Cut-to-Length Forest Harvester for Decimeter-Level Positioning Under Forest Canopies
authors: Zuoya Liu, Heikki Hyyti, Harri Kaartinen, Tamás Faitli, Matias Mäki-Leppilampi, Antero Kukko, Juha Hyyppä
publish: 2025 International Ubiquitous Positioning, Indoor Navigation and Location-Based Services Conference (UPINLBS)
abstract: In this paper, we present an ultra-wideband (UWB)based method for locating a cut-to-length forest harvester (CTL-FH) under forest canopies with decimeter-level accuracy. The proposed method involves placing and fixing several UWB beacons around the area of operation and one UWB receiver on the top of the harvester. This work is the first in the literature to integrate UWB positioning into a CTL-FH, which achieves decimeter-level accuracy, addresses the unavailability of Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) in positioning the forest harvester under forest canopies, and provides a real-time positioning solution for forest machines. The experimental results show that the proposed method can accurately and continuously locate the forest harvester under forest canopies with a root-mean-square error (RMSE) of 0.28 cm, providing a strong foundation and reference for the future applications of forest harvesters, such as individual tree-based harvest plan.

title: A Neural Algorithm Approach to Turbine Floor Noise Prediction Based on TabPFN
authors: Haitian Chen
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechatronics, Automation and Intelligent Control (MAIC)
abstract: Accurate prediction of noise signals is of great significance for evaluating the operation of hydroelectric generating units. This study conducted a comparative analysis using the Random Forest (RF) and Tabular Prior-data Fitted Network (TabPFN) algorithms, and established a turbine floor noise regression model based on rotational speed, guide vane opening, and active power. The results show that both models can describe the overall changing trend of noise. However, RF has problems such as response lag, overreaction and low stability of multiple operation results when facing rapidly changing working conditions. In contrast, TabPFN has demonstrated its advantages in terms of fitting accuracy (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{2}=0.994$</tex>), tracking ability, and stability in multiple modeling, showing its potential in the noise prediction task of hydroelectric generating units. In addition, sensitivity tests show that TabPFN has significantly stronger robustness against input disturbances. When the operating condition variables are subject to small random disturbances, its prediction results can still remain stable. However, as a neural model, TabPFN has insufficient interpretability. Future research may consider integrating physical principles or explainable learning methods into models to enhance their interpretability.

title: Deepfake Detection: A Hybrid Deep Learning Approach Using ResNext and LSTM Models
authors: Md Shohel Arman, Md Asaduzzaman, Touhid Bhuiyan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology and Engineering (i-COSTE)
abstract: Deepfake technology, leveraging advancements in deep learning, has become a significant threat to digital media authenticity, enabling the creation of hyper-realistic yet deceptive videos that challenge existing detection methods. This paper presents a hybrid approach combining ResNext Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) for frame-level feature extraction and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks for analyzing temporal dependencies to improve deepfake detection accuracy. The study utilized a balanced dataset comprising videos from FaceForensic++, Celeb-DF, and custom-crafted deepfakes, with preprocessing steps that included facial region cropping, frame standardization, and noise reduction. The proposed model achieved an accuracy of 94.87 %, outperforming existing methods by effectively capturing both static and dynamic video features. Key innovations include leveraging the complementary strengths of CNNs and LSTMs to address frame-level and sequential inconsistencies in fake media. This approach is validated through extensive experimentation, demonstrating robustness against evolving generative adversarial techniques. The results establish a strong foundation for scalable and real-time detection applications, with future work aiming to enhance detection for multi-modal data and improve computational efficiency for deployment in resource-constrained environments.

title: YOLO-RDFN: Steel Surface Defect Detection Model
authors: Baolong You, Li Min, Pengyang Song, Yadong Hao, Yuhan Xu, Xinna You, Shizhe Zheng
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: To address the issues of complex structure, large parameter count, difficulty in detecting multi-scale targets, and poor performance in dense scenarios found in current steel surface defect detection models, this paper proposes a lightweight detection model based on YOLOV11. Firstly, the backbone network of YOLOV11 is replaced with RepGhostNet to achieve lightweight design and improve detection speed. Secondly, an RDFPN model is designed and embedded with the multi-scale dense object enhancement attention mechanism, MdAM. Finally, a multi-scale perception loss function based on distance, DWLIOSS, is designed to enhance detection performance in dense scenes. To further balance model lightweighting and detection accuracy, the modified network model is pruned to trim redundant parameters and compress the model further. Experimental results demonstrate that compared to the original YOLOV11 model, the improved model achieves a 9.89% increase in MAP, a 25.88% reduction in parameters, and a 17.68% improvement in detection speed.

title: Navigating Artificial Intelligence Adoption Decisions in Entrepreneurial Ventures: Insights from the Extended Technology-Organization-Environment Framework
authors: K.K. Shihab, Arif Hasan, Shubhangi Vitthal Gaikwad, Mini Agrawal, Sonal Sharma, Sandeep Raghuwanshi
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: This study investigates factors that influence how entrepreneurs in India are adopting artificial intelligence. 462 entrepreneurs belonging to different industries such as service, trade and manufacturing sectors were approached. Exploratory factor analysis-(EFA), confirmatory factor analysis-(CFA) along with structural equation modeling-(SEM) techniques were used for data analysis through SPSS (v22) and AMOS (v24). The results show that the organizational preparedness, technological commitment and external pressure play a major role in driving AI adoption whereas, sub-factors perceived ease of use and usefulness have minimal influence. These findings suggest that entrepreneurs need to pay more attention to organization and external market dynamics, rather than simple technological interfaces. This study also contributes to the challenges faced in AI adoption, especially by extending and validating the TOE-TAM framework within an entrepreneurial set-up. These insights can really help policymakers concerning challenges entrepreneurs faced with AI.

title: Enhancing FANET Security with Elliptic Curve Cryptography: Protocol Evaluation and Analysis
authors: Sandeep Rai, Anoj Kumar, J.Sathish Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) operating in Flying Ad-hoc Networks (FANETs) are becoming essential for applications like surveillance, agricultural monitoring, disaster relief, and military missions due to their fast deployment and autonomous functionality. However, FANETs' dynamic nature, constant movement, and open wireless communications introduce critical security vulnerabilities, making them targets for threats such as data interception, identity spoofing, message alteration, and routing attacks. This study proposes a secure communication approach using Elliptic Curve Cryptography (ECC), a resource-efficient public-key encryption technique ideal for UAVs with limited processing capabilities. The ECCbased security protocol is incorporated into three common routing algorithms—4Ad-hoc On-demand Distance Vector (AODV), Optimized Link State Routing (OLSR), and Destination-Sequenced Distance Vector (DSDV)—which represent different routing paradigms: reactive, proactive, and table-driven. Simulation tests under realistic FANET scenarios, including varying node densities and mobility patterns as well as active security attacks like black hole, Sybil, and man-in-themiddle, assessed metrics such as packet delivery rate, latency, throughput, and control overhead. ECC-enhanced AODV offers the optimum balance between security and performance, according to research showing that incorporating ECC greatly improves security while maintaining network efficiency. This study shows that integrating cryptographic techniques at the routing layer offers a workable and scalable way to address FANET security issues. In order to improve intrusion detection and trust frameworks, future research will examine practical implementation, scalability for bigger UAV groups, and integrating ECC with cutting-edge technologies like blockchain and machine intelligence.

title: The Design and Research of Dynamic Calibration Device for Torque-Speed Sensor
authors: Xu Zhou, Fangjian Yuan, Qiliang Dai, Hang Xiu, Lei Wang, Yu Li, YuanPeng Lv
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: Torque-speed sensors are widely used in the output torque and speed detection of rotating power equipment such as motors and generators, and their accuracy and reliability are critical to the healthy operation of power equipment. In this paper, a vertical single-axis torsional pendulum dynamic calibration device and its type selection are proposed. The device adopts servo motor to provide dynamic excitation, connects the sensor to be calibrated through flexible coupling, and eliminates friction interference with air float bearing; The rear end is connected with the self-designed replaceable weight standard inertia module, which combines the circular grating to monitor the angular displacement, angular velocity and angular acceleration in real time. A new dynamic calibration device for torque-speed sensor is provided, and the design of standard inertia module is optimized to improve the calibration accuracy.

title: Quantum Encryption Scheme Based on Taylor Series and Fourier Transform
authors: Hari Sudharsan G, Nithin S, Amritavarshini B, Devadarshini M, S. Manimaran
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: This paper proposes a novel quantum encryption scheme that integrates the Quantum Fourier Transform (QFT), Taylor Series expansion, and quantum gates to establish a secure and efficient encryption framework. By blending classical mathematical principles with quantum computational techniques along with No-cloning theorem. QFT is used to transform the data domain, while encoding with Taylor-derived functions forms a robust private-key mechanism, setting foundation for security. Quantum gates act as encryption layers, and a signal derived from secret Taylor terms is added to obfuscate the data during public key generation. This approach upon further development will be particularly suited for privacy-preserving applications such as federated learning and secure machine learning. The results demonstrate reliable state retrieval after encryption and decryption, validating the scheme's practicality. This work sets a foundation for scalable, quantum-cloud-based secure computation and opens avenues for further advancements in quantum cryptography and multi-party secure protocols.

title: BPEC-EKNN Based Consistency Detection for Energy Storage Systems
authors: Nan Wang, Zhen Li, Xichao Zhou
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: With the increasing penetration of renewable energy sources such as photovoltaic and wind power in the power grid, the problems of grid security and stability caused by their randomness and volatility are becoming more and more prominent. As a cushion for the volatility of renewable energy, the proportion and importance of energy storage in the power grid is gradually increasing. Recently, problems such as explosions and fires caused by overcharging and over-discharging of energy storage systems have occurred from time to time, seriously affecting the safe and stable operation of energy storage systems and energy systems. The main reason for overcharging and discharging of energy storage systems is the inconsistency of the state of the electric core in the charging and discharging process, which not only affects the safety of the electric core, but also influences the overall charging and discharging capacity of the energy storage system. To address the inconsistency of energy storage cores, this paper proposes an energy storage consistency monitoring method under the framework of clustering-classification, which adopts the BPEC and EKNN classification algorithm to realize the detection and localization of energy storage core consistency anomalies under the consideration of time-series data.

title: Research on Full-Condition Model Construction and Operation Strategy for Multi-Energy Complementary Integrated Energy Systems Based on Source-Loadstorage Coordination
authors: Hongyi Li, Yucheng Jiang, Weijun Zhong, Lipeng Wang, Wei Xue
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: To address the coordination issue of the fluctuation of renewable energy and the spatiotemporal coupling of load demand in multi-energy complementary integrated energy systems, a full-condition operation optimization strategy based on “source-load-storage synergy” is established. By dynamically matching photovoltaic (PV) systems, heat pumps, energy storage devices, and other equipment, and leveraging time-of-use (TOU) electricity price incentives, the balance between energy supply and multi-energy load is coordinated. Refined mathematical models of PV power generation, air-source/geothermal heat pumps, and heat/electricity storage devices are constructed. By integrating the time-of-use electricity pricing policy in northwest China, a phased operation strategy of “peak discharging and off-peak charging” is proposed to achieve load shifting and efficient utilization of renewable energy. Based on hourly simulation throughout the year, the system's performance is verified from three dimensions: thermal performance, environmental benefits, and economic viability. The case study indicates that the system's primary energy utilization rate reaches 78.51 %, which is a 29.04 % increase compared to traditional separate supply systems. It also achieves an annual <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CO}_{2}$</tex> emission reduction of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1,905.19$</tex> tons. The energy storage arbitrage profit amounts to 254,400 yuan, and the investment payback period is shortened to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 2. 0 9}$</tex> years, with a PV energy consumption rate exceeding 85%.

title: Improving the Quality of Monitoring Facilities
authors: Oleg N. Bodin, Oksana E. Bezborodova, Vasily M. Zhigachev, Kristina D. Mishina, Dmitry V. Martinov, Alina O. Kholudeneva
publish: 2025 International Conference on Quality Management, Transport and Information Security, Information Technologies (QM&amp;TIS&amp;IT)
abstract: The article substantiates the need to develop and apply automated monitoring systems for objects of various origins. Existing systems reviewed. It is noted that they do not allow real-time adjustment of the object parameters. Adjustment is possible, at best, in the next monitoring cycle, and more often after one or more cycles. Disclosed is a method of monitoring the state of an object based on a set of parameters, which improves the quality of monitoring objects. Distinctive features of proposed method are separation of monitoring cycle into several time intervals, measurement of monitored parameter value at the end of each monitoring interval and its comparison with model value calculated using mathematical model of object. Analysis of mismatch of actual parameter value with expected parameter value makes it possible to generate and implement control action depending on value of mismatch of actual parameter value with expected parameter value. Examples of the method for monitoring various objects are given.

title: Mathematical Model for Information Security Risk Assessment in Electronic Document Management Systems
authors: Irina R. Shekhovtsova
publish: 2025 International Conference on Quality Management, Transport and Information Security, Information Technologies (QM&amp;TIS&amp;IT)
abstract: The transition to electronic document management systems (EDMS) has evolved into a missioncritical digital transformation initiative. Ineffective implementation of EDMS and its associated business processes can result in operational disruptions, financial losses, missed deadlines, and reputational damage. This situation necessitates the integration of manageable information flows within business processes into the sphere of information security management procedures. A structured approach is proposed to analyze existing business processes with attention to conducting quantitative risk assessment through modeling. Analysis is performed within the developed decision support system to optimize business processes in the EDMS while considering both system quality and information security requirements.

title: Optimal Charging Schedule for an Electric Metrobus Fleet Under PV-Grid Integration
authors: Tuna Aykut, Sıtkı Güner, Ümmühan Başaran Filik
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Driving and Smart Transportation (IDST)
abstract: The electrification of public transit fleets plays a vital role in achieving low-carbon urban mobility. This study presents a Mixed-Integer Linear Programming (MILP) model to optimize the daily charging schedule of a 100-vehicle electric metrobus (EM) fleet operating on Istanbul's Avcılar-Zincirlikuyu line. The proposed framework integrates photovoltaic (PV) generation and grid interaction to minimize total energy cost while improving grid load factor and reducing carbon emissions. Two scenarios are analyzed: a base case with unmanaged overnight charging and an optimized case with PV-supported scheduling and grid power constraints. The findings highlight the effectiveness of integrated <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{P V}$</tex>-grid energy management in enhancing both the economic and environmental sustainability of urban transportation systems.

title: Based on XGBoost and SHAP: A Predictive Maintenance Framework for Manufacturing Equipment in CAD/CAM Systems
authors: Muyu Teng, Meifa Huang, Zhemin Tang, Yunkun Jiao
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: This paper proposes a machine learning-based predictive maintenance framework for manufacturing equipment integrated with computer-aided design and manufacturing (CAD/CAM) systems. Utilizing XGBoost for multi-class failure prediction on sensor data, the model achieves high accuracy in forecasting equipment faults, thereby optimizing design parameters such as tool wear and reducing downtime. The framework incorporates SHAP for interpretability, ensuring transparent decision-making in critical sectors. Experimental results on a synthetic dataset demonstrate an accuracy of 98.65%, weighted F1-score of 98.34%, and AUC of 0.8804, outperforming baselines like Random Forest. Ablation studies confirm the importance of features like tool wear, with performance drops when removed. Visualizations, including confusion matrices, feature importance plots, SHAP summaries, ROC and PR curves, violin plots, and dependence plots, highlight model robustness and key insights into fault mechanisms. This approach aligns with Industry 4.0 principles, promoting sustainable manufacturing through proactive maintenance. Future extensions could incorporate real-time data streams and deep learning for enhanced temporal analysis. The framework's efficiency, with training under 1 second, makes it suitable for real-world CAD/CAM integration.

title: Design of a Model Predictive Control Strategy for Adaptive Cruise Control in Vehicles
authors: Safoin Rahal, Dingli Yu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Driving and Smart Transportation (IDST)
abstract: This report presents the design and simulation of a Model Predictive Control (MPC) strategy for Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) in vehicles, along with a comparative analysis of this approach against a conventional Proportional-Integral-Derivative (PID) control method. The study seeks to assess the effectiveness of MPC in maintaining safe following distances while ensuring ride comfort across various dynamic traffic scenarios. A linear state-space model that captures the longitudinal dynamics of an ego vehicle has been developed using MATLAB/Simulink, with both controllers implemented to manage acceleration in response to the behaviour of the lead vehicle. Four distinct simulation scenarios are explored: highway cruising, a lead vehicle exceeding the speed limit, highway traffic conditions, and instances of sudden braking. Performance is assessed using various metrics, including speed tracking, inter-vehicle distance, tracking error, acceleration, jerk, control effort, energy consumption, and ride comfort. The simulation results demonstrate that MPC yields smoother control actions and exhibits better adherence to safety constraints, particularly in rapidly changing conditions. The findings support the potential of MPC as a more robust alternative to PID for real-world ACC systems.

title: Research on Control of a Two-Stage LED Driver Based on LLC Resonant Converter and Power Factor Correction Circuit
authors: Min Yao, Muqin Tian, Junzhao Zhang
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: This paper presents the design and simulation of a two-stage LED driver circuit, which consists of a front-stage power factor correction (PFC) circuit and a rear-stage LLC resonant converter. The front stage employs a Boost PFC structure based on voltage and current dual-loop control, with feedforward control introduced to improve dynamic performance, achieving a power factor (PF) greater than 0.99. The rear stage utilizes a pulse frequency modulation (PFM) controlled LLC resonant converter to provide constant current output to the LEDs.

title: Design Optimisation and Analysis of Nanosheet EET Devices
authors: Km Nitu Gupta, Dhandapani Vaithiyanathan, Baljit Kaur
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: Progress in semiconductor technology has been driven the electronics sector to the center stage. The nanosheet (NS) device is an example of gate-all-around (GAA) structure, suggest better gate electrostatics, mitigating the adverse effect of short-channel effects (SCEs) and allowing scaling lower than sub-7nm technology node. This article imparts a thorough design examination of a two-sheet NSFET, exploring its geometric configurations. Major digital and analog or RF figures are analysed and compared considering the length of gate variations, variations of sheet thickness, and variations of the sheet width. The work presents simulation of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 n m}$</tex> nanosheet FET (NS-FET) structures using gate-all-around and fully depleted silicon-on-insulator technologies. The paper presents crucial insights into NSFET design optimizations for sub- 7 nm and advanced technology node applications. The nanosheet (NS) device, which is gate-all-around based, gives better gate control, reducing short channel effects (SCEs) and allowing more scaling lower than the sub-7nm technology node. This analysis explaining a detailed design analysis of a two-sheet NSFET and examines the effect of a range of geometric parameters on the device.

title: Forecasting Air Quality Index in a Himalayan City: A Comparative Analysis of Statistical and Machine Learning Methods
authors: Ishaan Dawar, Poorvi Singh, Vasudev Pant, Anil Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: Himalayan cities have specific problems with air quality forecasting because of the complicated terrain, specific atmospheric patterns, and seasonal changes. This paper discusses the necessity of precise Air Quality Index (AQI) prediction through comparison of conventional statistical techniques with the latest machine learning (ML) techniques. The four methodologies included Seasonal Autoregressive Integrated Moving Average with Exogenous variables (SARIMAX), Vector Autoregression (VAR), CatBoost Regression, and XGBoost Regression (XGBR). The training and testing of models were performed with the help of historical data on air quality, going through rigorous cross-validation processes. As can be seen, the ML algorithms performed much better than the statistical ones, with XGBR having a much higher predictive accuracy, as the following values demonstrate: MAE: 0.0176, MSE: 0.0006, RMSE: 0.0254, and R 2 score: 0.9903. This exceptional performance highlights the importance of capturing complex non-linear relationships between temporal variables and pollutant concentrations in high-altitude environments. The research contributes valuable insights for environmental monitoring in mountainous regions. It offers a methodological framework for developing localized, highprecision AQI forecasting tools applicable to other Himalayan urban centers, potentially enhancing public health interventions and environmental policy decisions.

title: Operational Maintenance Priorities and Critical Technologies for Converter Transformers in Controllable Line-Commutated Converter Systems
authors: Mingjia Zhou, Hongyu Duan, Hongbo Ji, Long Chen, Wenbin Zhao, Chenxi Li
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: Controllable Line-Commutated Converter (CLCC), as a novel converter technology, effectively resolves commutation failure issues. It was officially commissioned at Shanghai Nanqiao Converter Station in 2021. Due to the integration of IGBT auxiliary branches for forced commutation, CLCC's commutation process involves rapid injection of high-frequency voltage pulses. These steep voltage wavefronts propagate through converter transformer windings, where the coupling effect between distributed capacitance and inductance parameters excites multiconductor transmission line effects. This results in complex superimposed phenomena of standing waves and traveling waves within the winding interlayer, turn-to-turn, and ground insulation structures, thereby exacerbating core vibration and noise and posing long-term operational threats to converter transformers. At Shanghai Nanqiao Converter Station, synchronous measurements of physical quantities such as transformer neutralpoint current and vibration were conducted. The operational status of the converter transformer under CLCC conditions was monitored and compared with conventional LCC operation. This study identifies general patterns of neutral-point current and vibration in CLCC-operated converter transformers and highlights key maintenance priorities for subsequent converter transformer operation.

title: The Effect of Gameplay on Student Stress: A Statistical and Psychophysiological Analysis of Influencing Factors
authors: Pratishtha Agarwal, Mayukh Chakraborty, Sakshi, Jyoti Yadav, Nikita
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: In this paper, the immediate impact of stress levels of students has been analyzed with the help of Electrocardiography (ECG). Additionally, factors responsible for stress and changes in immediate stress levels before and after playing the game are identified using paired t-tests and chisquare tests. The setup for experiment was created with 54 participants, being asked to play a horror game on a standard mobile device. Physiological stress levels have been calculated by recording ECG before and after playing the game under consistent laboratory conditions and psychological stress have been assessed using self-reported answers on the Depression Anxiety and Stress Scale (DASS-42). Additionally, demographic information of participants has been recorded. The stress levels have reduced after playing the game since p>0.05. Psychological stress is significantly associated with family income category (p=0.0096) and place of residence (hostel or day scholar) where p=0.029. The immediate change in physiological stress is significantly related to gender (p = 0.0026), sleep duration (p=0.027), and water intake (p=0.03).

title: Pose Estimation for Real-Time Worker Activity Recognition in the Oil and Gas Industry Using Mediapipe
authors: Ibnu Zakaria Arrazi, Ahmad Bukhari Aujih
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Sensors and Nanotechnology (SENNANO)
abstract: This study evaluates two AI approaches for realtime worker activity detection in the oil and gas industry. The first approach is based on object detection using YOLOv8. The second approach is a new proposed method that uses pose estimation with a classification model, built using MediaPipe library. Both approaches showed similar accuracy in identifying key activities like climbing and lifting. However, pose estimation methods were proven to be more efficient, using less computational power and processing frames faster, making it better suited for real-time monitoring on resourcelimited devices. By analyzing human body posture instead of just detecting objects, the pose estimation approach offers more precise recognition of unsafe worker behaviors. These findings highlight that the proposed approach has a strong potential to improve safety monitoring, offering faster and more re-source-friendly solutions to reduce accidents in highrisk industrial settings.

title: LoRa-based Environmental Monitoring System
authors: Muhammad Syahmi Abdul Kadir, Saeed S. Ba Hashwan, Illani Mohd Nawi, Mohd Haris Md Khir
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Sensors and Nanotechnology (SENNANO)
abstract: The rapid advancement of Internet of Things (IoT) technology has significantly enhanced environmental monitoring, particularly in weather observation systems. This project focuses on the design and implementation of a LoRabased autonomous environment monitoring system that enables real-time environmental data collection and transmission over long distances with minimal power consumption. The system is designed to overcome key limitations of existing monitoring solutions, such as reliance on Wi-Fi connectivity, limited mobility, and high energy consumption. The proposed system consists of an autonomous solar powered sensor node equipped with multiple environmental sensors, including temperature, humidity, pressure, and gas level detectors. These sensors collect environmental data, which is then transmitted via the LoRa (Long Range) protocol to a master node for storage and analysis. The autonomous mobility feature allows the system to cover a wider geographic area, ensuring comprehensive data collection.

title: Smart System for Project Allocation and Mapping with Faculty Recommendation and Transformer-Based Report Summarization: A Proposed Approach
authors: Sankalp Goel, Anshika Singh, Kanika Singla, Sushant Jhingran
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Academic project management in the universities is usually an ineffective and opaque exercise leading to wrong mapping of the faculty and students, asymmetrical evaluation, and delays in project execution. Systems that exist today either consider project allocation or faculty recommendation or report summarization, but they certainly do not offer the entire integrated package. This paper proposes and designs a system that automates the entire academic project life cycle, including AI-driven modules for domain-based faculty recommendation, student auto-grouping, evaluation panel allocation, and transformer-based project report summarization. In opposition to earlier attempts, ours is the only approach that combined more than one AI technique-semantic similarity matching, clustering, and abstractive summarization. By leveraging institutional datasets of academic projects combined with the faculty's research data from Google Scholar and ORCID, the system guarantees fair allocation, reduces faculty workload, and shortens evaluation time. This integration creates transparency, optimizes mentor matching, and shortens project assessment time drastically, providing a scalable framework for automating academic processes.

title: Spray Cooling Optimization for IVR-ERVC Applications
authors: Vitor Fernandes de Almeida, Jingqi Zhao, Lining Liu, Feng Xie
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Electronics, Electrical and Green Energy (AEEGE)
abstract: Severe nuclear accidents impose extreme thermal loads on the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), where localized focusing effects can produce dangerous heat flux intensification and threaten vessel integrity. Ensuring effective In-Vessel Retention through External Reactor Vessel Cooling (IVR-ERVC) requires enhanced strategies capable of mitigating these hotspots. This study investigates multi-nozzle spray cooling as a promising method to reinforce IVR-ERVC. Numerical simulations were performed at nozzle outlet pressures ranging from 1.0 to 3.0 bar to analyze droplet impingement, liquid film formation, and wall temperature response under high heat flux conditions. Results show that increasing the nozzle pressure improves the liquid film thickness and uniformity, thereby reducing temperature gradients and accelerating surface cooling. Optimal performance occurs near 2.5 bar, where film coverage becomes highly stable and wall temperatures are minimized, while further pressure increases yield only marginal benefits. These findings confirm that spray cooling can effectively suppress focusing effects, increase critical heat flux margins, and strengthen the safety of advanced nuclear reactor designs.

title: Copyright Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)

title: Review on Adversarial Attack Defense Mechanism in Machine Learning Models
authors: Vibhakti Bagade, Sargam Kamble, Samiksha Sadanshiv, Kundan Patle, Deep Gaoner, Durgeshnandini Meshram
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: Based on the opposition with small input perturbation, with such well crafted attacks, predictions may often be steered strongly in the wrong direction and even erratic; therefore, machine learning models are highly susceptible to adversarial attacks. Some of these techniques have trade-offs, such as less model accuracy on clean data, increased computational cost or vulnerability to novel attack techniques. It might not however be possible to implement a strong and effective countermeasure to such attacks in the real world application. To solve the above-mentioned issues, the following research aims at creating a strong adversarial defense mechanism by combining various techniques such as adversarial training, feature smoothing, and ensemble learning. A proposed technique was a combination of these techniques to boost the resilience of the models to all the varieties of adversarial attacks with the quality maintained in terms of accuracy and computational efficiency. Develop defense mechanisms to make machine learning models more resilient to adversarial attacks without making a substantial impact on them.

title: Variance-Entropy Guided SWT Fusion with P-Sharpened Softmax for Enhanced Medical Imaging
authors: Mohammed Rafiq, Ankur Maurya, Prabhishek Singh, Manoj Diwakar
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: The primary goal of medical image fusion (MIF) is to combine important medical information from different medical modalities, including computed tomography (CT) and magnetic resonance imaging (MRI), into a single image. This research paper proposes a novel MIF method that integrates Stationary Wavelet Transform (SWT) with content-aware saliency and soft decision rules. SWT breaks down the image into low-frequency (LF), and high-frequency (HF) domain. The LF components are fused using Variance-Entropy (VE) saliency measure. A temperature-controlled softmax function generates smooth fusion weights, ensuring robust approximation component integration. The HF components are fused using psharpened softmax strategy. The fused image is reconstructed using inverse SWT that has high visual quality with less time complexity. The comprehensive experimental evaluation is done using metrics like (Qab/f, Qy, Qcb, and Qs). This shows the superiority of the proposed method compared to other MIF models. The proposed method achieves an effective balance between interpretability, novelty, and clinical applicability.

title: AI Driven Early Warning System for Brain Stroke Risk
authors: Sarabjeet Kaur, Shiraz Khurana, Vidushi Sharma
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Stroke is considered a significant medical condition since it is closely linked to a high level of disability and mortality. Stroke is usually categorized into two main classes: ischemic and stroke caused by hemorrhage. Ischemic happen when there is a blockage in the brain's blood vessels, while hemorrhagic is characterized by bleeding in the brain. Stroke's early detection is considered a vital aspect in assisting medical practitioners in administering various treatment measures, such as thrombolytic and anticoagulant drugs. Stroke's early symptoms and warning signs are important in mitigating the severity of a stroke's consequences. This study's primary goal is to apply different artificial intelligence models in creating a predictive model that can assist in assessing a patient's early risks of having a stroke. Various classification models were utilized in this research, Specifically, K-nearest-neighbours (KNN), a standard Random Forest (RF), Adaptive Boosting (AdaBoost), Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost), the conventional Decision Tree Classifier, and naive Bayes. The models' performance was assessed using various metrics, such as Accuracy, Precision, Recall, and F1-Score. Among all methods under evaluation, the performance of the XGBoost classifier was found to be superior with an accuracy of 88.9 % and precision of 94.9% compared to all other machine learning methods under evaluation for this study.

title: Multimachine Hydro Turbine Governor Controller for Small Signal Stability Enhancement of Renewable Penetrated Power System
authors: Samarjeet Satapathy, Rajeswari Behera, Satya Narayan Barik, Sanghamitra Behera, Jyoti Ranjan Sahoo, Sangram Keshari Sahoo, Narayan Nahak, Akshaya Kumar Patra
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: For multimachine power system, maintaining stability is an important challenge brought by intermittency in renewable sources integration. This work presents an efficient damping torque implementation by hydro governors to improve stability of power system. The perturbation taken here is step and discrete integration of solar & wind sources. Solar increased by 0.3 pu and wind increased by 0.5 pu step raised and the settling time decreased by 75 %. The case study consists up of two areas four machine system with four hydro governors and 39-bus system with multiple hydro governors imparting simultaneously damping torque to oscillations brought by solar and wind source penetrations. To optimize the governor gains, a multi objective sand-cat swarm optimization (SCO) has been implemented. The SCO is less complex and more efficient because it employs only pre optimizing parameter. The SSO has been contrasted with PSO and DE algorithms. The case studies reveal that, the hydro governor can be employed efficiently to handle oscillation in power system for stability improvement, if their gains are tuned efficiently like SCO algorithm.

title: Triple-Band Ultrawideband Antenna for Multi-Standard Wireless Applications
authors: Vidushi Sharma, Neeraj Sharma
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical, Communication, and Computing Technologies (iCONECCT)
abstract: This work describes the design and assessment of a novel triple-band ultrawideband (UWB) antenna that can cover communications in S-band, C-band, and X-band, including 4G/5G, Wi-Fi, and radar systems. Aside from the 2-3.7 GHz band, the antenna can operate between 5.2 and 6.4 GHz and 7.75 and 10.8 GHz. Across all bands, the proposed antenna achieves maximum gains of 4 dBi at 3.6 GHz, 3.5 dBi at 5.7 GHz, and 5.7 dBi at 9.6 GHz, indicative of strong performance. At 2.45 GHz, the antenna reaches its maximum radiation efficiency of 98%, and it maintains efficiencies above 75% across all bands it operates in. Utilizing multi-resonant structures, the design attains ultrawideband qualities across all bands. These bands have fractional bandwidths of 59.65%, 20.69 %, and 32.88 %, respectively. Incorporating modern wireless communication systems, Internet of Things devices, and satellites-this antenna's compact size, strong gain, and exceptional radiation efficiency make it ideal.

title: Comparative Analysis of Various Deep Learning Models for Image Deblurring
authors: Rabbuni Rajakumari P, Ashish Gupta
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical, Communication, and Computing Technologies (iCONECCT)
abstract: Image deblurring is a crucial part of computer vision. It restores the latent, sharp original images from the blurred or distorted images. Recently deep learning algorithms have gained popularity among researchers compared to conventional deblurring techniques. However, for real-world outdoor images, the quality of the deblurred image is one of the major concerns in these deep learning algorithms. This work investigates the performance of all state-of-the-art deep learning models on real-world outdoor scenes. we consider four deep learning–based models, such as U-Net, Res-UNet, MPRNet, and DeblurGAN in this work and evaluate their performance using two widely utilized measures: Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR) and Structural Similarity Index Measure (SSIM). Among all these models, Res-UNet achieved the best results with a PSNR of 29.40 and an SSIM of 0.92. all these models were tested on the Make3D dataset, which contains real-world outdoor scenes, to demonstrate the model's effectiveness in deblurring tasks.

title: Transformer-Based Multimodal Fall Detection Using VARISHTA-MM15 Dataset
authors: Abha Tewari, Dhananjay Kalbande
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: With the world population aging so rapidly, fall detection and activity monitoring have become increasingly important areas for research. Multimodal Human Activity Recognition (HAR) approaches may provide a potential solution by integrating wearable inertial sensor monitoring with visual signals to achieve the integration of motion and spatial information processing. We propose a fully implemented and benchmarked implementation of multimodal anomaly detection using the newly developed VARISHTA-MM15 dataset. The data set consists of synchronized inertial and video recordings from 15 older people (aged 60 and older), conducting a diverse selection of Activities of Daily Living (ADLs) and simulated fall events under controlled conditions. This data set includes synchronized data preprocessing, modality alignment, windowed feature extraction, and early fusion to create a single flow. We evaluated three transformerbased baselines: (i) an Unsupervised Multimodal Autoencoder (UMMAE), (ii) a Perceiver IO model for heterogeneous sen-sor-video fusion, and (iii) a Cross-modal Autoencoder (Cross-AE) for robust representation under missing or occluded modality environments. A subject-wise evaluation protocol is used, enabling generalization to unseen participants. The data results show that Cross-AE significantly exceeds unimodal baselines, leading to the best ROC-AUC and F1 scores of all the datasets tested. In addition, convergence to multimodal fusion-based methods for training and validation loss curves is significantly faster than unimodal methods. The VARISHTA-MM15 dataset, along with opensource code and alignment protocols, provides a reproducible basis for advanced deep anomaly detection research in the elderly health monitoring space.

title: Comparative Analysis of Single, Dual, and Triple Phase-Shift Control Strategies for Dual Active Bridge DC-DC Converters
authors: Jajjalya Bikash Kashyap, E S N Raju P
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: The dual active bridge (DAB) DC-DC converter has emerged as a key topology for bidirectional power conversion in solid-state transformers, renewable energy interfaces, and electric vehicle charging systems. Owing to its symmetrical structure, galvanic isolation, and high-frequency operation, it is well suited for medium- and high-power applications. The converter's performance is strongly influenced by the modulation technique, which governs power transfer capability, current stress, and soft-switching behavior. This paper presents a comprehensive comparative analysis of three phase-shift control strategies such as single phase shift (SPS), dual phase shift (DPS), and triple phase shift (TPS). Analytical models have been developed for each control method to characterize their switching dynamics and power transfer mechanisms. Simulation studies have been conducted under identical operating conditions to evaluate variations in zero voltage switching (ZVS) range, current stress, and conversion efficiency. The results demonstrate that while SPS offers simplicity, DPS enhances soft-switching and current optimization, and TPS achieves superior efficiency and control flexibility. The presented comparison provides valuable insight into selecting an appropriate phase-shift control strategy for high-performance DAB converters in advanced power conversion systems.

title: Copyright Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)

title: Modelling Coating Disbondment Rates Using Acoustic Emission Technique: A Pseudo Kinetic Perspective
authors: Noor A'in A. Rahman, Zazilah May, Lukman Ab Rahim
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Sensors and Nanotechnology (SENNANO)
abstract: Coating disbondment is a serious concern for the long-term durability of metallic structures, especially when these structures are exposed to high voltages in harsh environments. In this study, a pseudo kinetic modelling approach was used to quantify how fast disbondment occurs under different voltage levels. To do this, the acoustic emission (AE) parameters were used to indicate the interfacial damage. AE data were acquired during cathodic potential on the coated test specimen at a variety of voltage ranging from 3 to 6 Volt. After applying a cathodic potential to the coating substrate, the disbonded area was measured according to the test standard ASTM G8. The pseudo kinetic constant (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$k$</tex>), representing normalized AE activity over time, was derived for each condition and modelled using seven different fitting approaches: linear, exponential, power-law, logarithmic, polynomial, sigmoid, and saturation growth models. The results suggest that the stages of coating disbondment is dissolved resulting in the delamination of coating around the initial defect, which related to the increasing of the cathodic potential strength. The results of the pseudo kinetic modelling indicate that Average Signal Level (ASL) provides the most accurate and consistent fit among all tested AE parameters when correlating with the disbondment process under varied voltage stimuli. This is evidenced by its highest coefficient of determination (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}} \boldsymbol{=} \mathbf{0. 7 3}$</tex>) and RMSE value <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.07$</tex> given by the Quadratic model, suggesting that ASL captures the voltage-dependent evolution of disbondment more effectively than other eight (8) AE metrics. These results suggest that ASL could serve as a reliable, realtime diagnostic tool for detecting and monitoring coating failures.
