Overall, this study offers a fresh, data-driven perspective on using AE technology to model coating degradation and supports future efforts in predictive maintenance using AE signal analysis in future.

title: Vertically Integrated Moisture Flux Convergence Associated with the Rainfall Event Over the West Coast of India
authors: Anshu Choudhary, Shashi Kant, Vivek Kumar
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: In the Western Coast of India, the coastal fisheries, port infrastructure and several plantations are the major contributors to the regional economy. The Ghats on the west contain extensive forests and are recognized as a biodiversity hotspot. These western Ghats are a steep and continuous mountain chain running parallel to the coastline and hence have a major impact on the climate of the area. The proximity to the Arabian Sea and presence of the Western Ghats create one of the most efficient orographic rainfall zones in the monsoon. June 2025 delivered a notably intense rainfall along the western coast. During 12<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</sup> – 16<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</sup> June 2025, the western coast received a prolonged heavy spell of rainfall that broke the historical records. The moisture transport plays a crucial role in shaping the rainfall over a region. This paper aims to study and examine this prolonged heavy spell of rainfall from 12<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</sup> – 16<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</sup> June 2025 using Vertically Integrated Moisture Flux, along with some other dynamical and thermodynamic diagnoses. This paper utilises various sources to gather data and apply computing to visualize and understand this episode. The visualization through computing showed that relative humidity remained (850-500 hPa) high during the study period. The negative omega revealed the rising motion of air, and the divergence at 200hPa supports the convection and rainfall occurrence. The findings of this study provide valuable insights into disaster management and forecasting, as well as improved management in various sectors, including infrastructure and agriculture, in the western region of India.

title: Calibration Machine Implementation for Fine Phase Control Adjustment in Phase Interpolator Using a Discrete Current Modulation Profile
authors: Erick J. Arenas Mendoza, Iurhitsi Elias Valdez, Ernesto Conde Almada, Rodrigo Jaramillo Ramirez
publish: 2025 IEEE Latin American Electron Devices Conference (LAEDC)
abstract: This paper presents a digital Calibration Machine (CM) architecture designed to improve the linearity of a 127step phase interpolator with coarse and fine control topology by the means of the adjustment of biasing current per code in the Discrete Modulation Profile (DMP). The digital design flow covers from coarse/fine control technique until CM layout implementation. The CM process has two different operations, which are calibration and characterization. In calibration mode, the CM saves the data for fine-control adjustment through the DMP. The second operation, characterization, is used to determine the order in which the codes obtained in calibration are turned on. The digital control was designed in Verilog and implemented in 130 nm SkyWater technology using the OpenROAD workflow from RTL-to-GDSII. Input stimuli are provided by a Time-toDigital Converter (TDC), which measures phase offsets between ideal timing references and the interpolated output, forming a closed-loop feedback mechanism for calibration.

title: Unbalance Fault Detection in an IoT-Connected Motor Network Using Artificial Neural Networks
authors: Massine GANA, Zakia CHELLI, Rafik SADDAOUI, Hakim ACHOUR, Mourad LAGHROUCHE
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy (AIESRE)
abstract: This paper presents the design and implementation of a low-cost integrated system for the preventive detection of imbalance faults in a network of interconnected induction motors using Internet of Things (IoT) technologies. The system monitors two key parameters in real time (vibration and temperature) via non-invasive sensors. These signals are collected and transmitted to an ESP32 microcontroller, which forwards the data to the ThingSpeak cloud platform for centralized monitoring and communication between machines. A graphical user interface (GUI) enables remote visualization of motor status, and alerts are generated automatically when predefined thresholds are exceeded. A lightweight artificial neural network (ANN), implemented on the ESP32 board, performs real-time diagnosis of motor health every 15 seconds. To enhance the quality of vibration data and reduce the impact of sensor noise, a Weighted Moving Average (WMA) filter is applied. This filter offers a computationally efficient and adaptive smoothing method, well suited for embedded systems, by emphasizing recent measurements while attenuating fluctuations. The proposed system ensures high motor availability by enabling timely, accurate fault detection and early intervention. Experimental validation demonstrates the system's effectiveness in distinguishing faulty motors from healthy ones, making it applicable to a broad range of industrial contexts, including hydraulic pumps and wind turbines.

title: Artificial Intelligence-Based Optimisation Techniques for Energy Management of Hybrid PV/Wind/Fuel Cell Microgrids
authors: Imane Benkhadir, Badre Bossoufi, Mohammed Amine Hoummadi, Chakib EL Bekkali
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies and Interdisciplinary Innovation (ICAT2I)
abstract: Over the past few decades, the growing cost of fossil fuels combined with the urgent need to decarbonize the energy sector has intensified the global shift toward renewable energy sources (RES). Achieving a sustainable future largely depends on the widespread generation of clean energy, in which renewable technologies play a decisive role. The integration of renewable sources such as photovoltaic (PV) panels, wind turbines, and fuel cells (FCs) into microgrids provides a sustainable and efficient pathway for electricity production. The reliable operation of such systems hinges on sophisticated Energy Management Systems (EMS) supported by effective optimization strategies. While conventional optimization methods offer fundamental solutions for scheduling, dispatch, and control, they often struggle to maintain performance under the intermittency and uncertainty inherent to RES. To address these limitations, Artificial Intelligence (AI)-based optimization techniques have recently emerged as powerful tools to enhance EMS adaptability and efficiency. Accordingly, this review delivers a comprehensive overview of hybrid microgrid configurations, EMS types, conventional optimization frameworks, and AI-driven approaches, emphasizing their objectives, methodologies, and major contributions.

title: Solar Inverter Performance Prediction Using Double Exponential Curve Fitting
authors: Lyazid Kaci, Amar Hadj Arab, Rachid Zirmi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy (AIESRE)
abstract: In this paper, a performance model is developed and mainly evaluated for the SMA/ Sunny Boy 3000TL grid-connected photovoltaic inverters based on field data from the power plant of the Renewable Energy Development Center (CDER) located in Algiers. The model is based on double exponential curve fitting method. The inverter performance is expressed by the model as a function of the inverter AC output power. The accuracy of the developed double exponential model is compared to the SANDIA National laboratories for the SMA single phase inverter. Throughout system operation the performance model of the grid-connected photovoltaic inverter can be used to continuously monitor the inverter performance

title: Sustainability of AI Use in Higher Education: The Role of Perceived Usefulness and Self-Efficacy
authors: Samah AL Hassan, Noura Yassine
publish: 2025 International Conference on Recent Advances in Mathematics and Informatics (ICRAMI)
abstract: The pandemic, along with the technological revolution, has shifted the world toward fundamental digital transformation in different fields, particularly in education. To address the digital needs of higher education, several institutions have integrated digital learning into their academic approaches. The rapid integration of technology in education highlights the importance of exploring the drivers behind AI adoption in the academic setting within a sustainable framework. This research examines the interplay between AI features such as system updates, memorability, and non-language barriers and their sustainable usage in educational contexts. The roles of perceived usefulness and self-efficacy in this relationship are also assessed. Data were collected from 203 university students. Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modeling (PLS-SEM) using SmartPLS 4.1 was employed to assess the associations within the research model. The findings indicate a positive correlation between the intelligent capabilities of AI and students' sustainable use of AI in education. Moreover, the results reveal that this relationship is mediated by perceived usefulness and self-efficacy. In addition, contextual variables such as the type of university were found to exert a moderating effect on this relationship.

title: Sensor Placement for Agricultural Field Coverage
authors: Ilir Shinko, Erison Ballasheni, Lutis Picorri, Vladi Kolici, Dritan Nace, Alban Zyle
publish: 2025 International Congress on Smart Agriculture and Sustainable Systems (SmartAgri&amp;SuSY)
abstract: This paper addresses the sensor placement problem in agricultural LoRaWAN networks under sensor failure scenarios. We extend proportional fairness (PF) optimization from network controller placement to agricultural sensor deployment, where coverage is defined as the ability of deployed sensors to successfully monitor target field locations within their sensing range. In addition, we integrate machine learning approaches for coverage prediction with spatial clustering techniques to enforce diversity. Experimental validation demonstrates that the new approach outperforms baseline methods across varying sensor failure intensities.

title: Smart Power Management in PV Systems with Battery–Supercapacitor Hybrid Storage for Electric Vehicle
authors: Dounia Touahria, Amel Bouchemha, Sami Kahla
publish: 2025 International Conference on Recent Advances in Mathematics and Informatics (ICRAMI)
abstract: This study suggests a system of hybrid energy storage (HESS) for use alone photovoltaic (PV) applications. The system integrates a lithium-ion battery and a supercapacitor (SC) to ensure both long-term energy supply and fast dynamic response to power fluctuations. While the battery provides sustained energy delivery, its lifetime is negatively affected by frequent high-current demands. To mitigate this issue, the SC is introduced to absorb rapid transient loads, thereby reducing stress on the battery and extending its service life. The PV system charges both storage elements when solar irradiance is sufficient and ensures energy availability during low irradiance conditions. To highlight the benefit of the SC, a baseline configuration using PV with battery-only storage is also simulated. Power distribution across the storage devices is coordinated using a Genetic Algorithm (GA)-optimized energy management method based on proportional-integral (PI) controllers. MATLAB/Simulink results under varying irradiance and load profiles demonstrate that, compared to the baseline, the proposed HESS reduces battery SOC fluctuation, improves DC-bus voltage stability, and enhances overall energy utilization efficiency.

title: Unveiling Volatility Drivers: Machine Learning and SHAP Across Weak and Strong Institutions
authors: Mohamed Malek Belhoula, Ichrak Dridi
publish: 2025 IEEE Afro-Mediterranean Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AMCAI)
abstract: Leveraging eleven machine learning models in a cross-country analysis, this study investigates the drivers of stock market volatility, emphasizing institutional quality's (IQ) pivotal role across high- and low-quality regimes in emerging markets. Employing regularization techniques (Lasso, Ridge, Elastic Net), tree-based models (Decision Tree, Extra Tree, Random Forest, LightGBM, XGBoost), and deep learning approaches (LSTM, ANN, GRU), alongside volatility models (EGARCH, HAR-GARCH, GARCH-MIDAS), we analyze 16 emerging countries with stable policy frameworks, over 1995Q1–2024Q1. Both mean SHAP feature importance and SHAP interaction techniques reveals distinct volatility dynamics: in high institutional quality environments, volatility is shaped by long-term market fundamentals and stable global financial linkages. In contrast, in low institutional quality settings, volatility is driven by immediate economic fragilities, speculative trading, and external uncertainties. Robust across tranquil and crisis periods, these findings affirm institutional quality's moderating role as a critical determinant of financial market stability. These insights offer significant implications for risk management and policy formulation in emerging economies.

title: Making Zero-Days Hard: A Deployment-Oriented Review of Preventive Defences
authors: Praneeth Kasiraju, Harshak Golusu, Balasubramanian Prabhu Kavin
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Intelligence, Security, and Artificial Intelligence (IntelliSecAI)
abstract: Exploitation of previously unjammed vulnerabilities in functioning environments is known as zero-day exploitation and is a constant challenge in the cybersecurity arena. This review focuses on the various preventative approaches implemented from March 2022 up to July 2025, divided in eight different categories at the eight different levels that range from design-time, runtime and operational level. Case studies are taken from across the board of Android, Chrome, Windows, Linux, the Apple platform, the financial sector, and how the changes in the use of memory safe languages, compiler based and hardware based protections, architectural isolation, proactive bug discovery, supply chain controls and operational mitigations are changing the nature of the defensive posture. The analysis also presents persistent challenges, such as the use of mitigations in runtime, issues with reproducibility and lack of automation for enforcement of SBOM/VEX. Overall, the review sets a general trend: prevention is no longer an add-on, but rather it is a basic principle of secure system design. While issues of memory safety are decreasing, logical loopholes, supply chain gaps and phishing attacks are becoming more common impacting ways to implement adaptable, proactive defenses. This review provides a deployment facing synthesis of preventive measures using a lifecycle taxonomy to systematically provide a organization of recent platform rolls, adoption barriers, and measurable impacts. It further highlights open challenges which require further advancement in order to effectively address the zero day exploitation.

title: What Remains of the Human in the Age of AI and Other Disruptive Technologies?
authors: Jason Idriss Sparkes
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Intelligence, Security, and Artificial Intelligence (IntelliSecAI)
abstract: Many experts and members of the public fear that AI and other disruptive technologies pose an existential threat to humanity. This paper argues that this threat is rooted not primarily in technical concerns but in a deeper crisis of human intention and purpose, which can only be addressed by returning to first principles. Writing from a transdisciplinary humanities perspective and engaging substantially with religious studies and Islamic studies, this intervention examines the foundations of that crisis. As we outsource core human functions to machines, we risk forgetting who we are. Rather than begin with technical fixes, we must revisit fundamental questions: What is a human? What is our purpose? What is our place in the cosmos? Recentring the human is urgent and requires cross-civilizational dialogue. Drawing on classical philosophy, Abrahamic thought, and the transdisciplinary humanities, this analysis demonstrates that intelligence and knowledge cannot be severed from intention, presence, virtue, and ethical purpose. When machines are built without clear human ends in mind, they risk reshaping us into beings we no longer recognize. Education becomes procedural and technology a mirror of our confusion. In response, we must recentre the human as a moral and existential agent, not merely a productive or computational one. Tools must serve our higher aspirations, and education must rekindle awe, contemplation, and responsibility. This is not a call for nostalgic retreat but for principled renewal. Only by clarifying our intention can we design technologies that support, rather than undermine, our flourishing in body, heart, mind, and spirit.

title: Adversarially Robust and Explainable AI Systems for High-Risk Clinical Decision Support with Iot-Integrated Health Monitoring
authors: Md Ariful Islam Rana, Zubaida Iftekhar, Md Akhtaruzzaman, Salman Qavi, Maruf Ahmad, Md Mohiuddin
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Intelligence, Security, and Artificial Intelligence (IntelliSecAI)
abstract: High-risk medical settings are mainly about making decisions, and the implementation of artificial-intelligence (AI) solutions can significantly enhance clinical outcomes. However, the effectiveness of AI is often posed by adversarial attacks that alter the outputs of the models and might have disastrous effects. In order to alleviate this problem, we present an adversarially resistant AI system that can be used to assist high-risk clinical decision support in Internet of Things integrated health-monitoring infrastructures. Our system applies stringent AI techniques enabling the system to be resistant to adversarial perturbations to guarantee reliability of the predictions even in dynamically hostile and risky surroundings. Furthermore, we incorporate explainable-AI (XAI) techniques to maximize transparency and establish trust in professionals working in the healthcare sector making the system more palatable in clinical practice. We test the system with the help of a full healthcare dataset and show its performance in adversarial scenarios or in the real-time decision making problem. The findings indicate that our solution yields a robustness to the AI models against adversarial attacks, in addition to enhancing interpretability, thus leading to an excellent clinical decision-making and patient safety.

title: Investigating the Impact of Medical Knowledge on Self-Medication Practices Among Non-Medical Students
authors: Saumendra Ku. Mohanty, Raghu N, Ansh Kataria, V.J.K. Kishore Sonti, Kunal Meher, Varalakshmi Dandu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Intelligence, Security, and Artificial Intelligence (IntelliSecAI)
abstract: Self-medication among non-medical students has become a prevalent global issue, often influenced by varying levels of medical knowledge. Understanding the relationship between knowledge and self-medication behaviours is essential for minimizing associated health risks. Existing studies primarily rely on descriptive surveys, which fail to capture nuanced risk patterns and knowledge-behaviour associations. To address these limitations, this study introduces the Knowledge-Behavior Impact Model (KBIM), integrating KMeans Clustering for grouping students by knowledge-practice profiles and Chi-Square Analysis for testing statistical associations in risk classification. The proposed model enables the accurate identification of high-risk self-medication behaviors based on objective knowledge levels and behavioral indicators. Findings reveal that students with lower medical knowledge scores exhibit significantly riskier self-medication practices <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 5 \%}$</tex> for antibiotics, while those with higher knowledge demonstrate safer and more informed behaviours. The KBIM framework thus provides a robust analytical basis for designing targeted educational interventions in nonmedical academic settings.

title: HOPF-Bogdanov-Takens Bifurcation in a Delayed Hopfield Network Model with Bidirectional Connection
authors: Houssem Achouri, Chaouki Aouiti
publish: 2025 International Conference on Recent Advances in Mathematics and Informatics (ICRAMI)
abstract: In this study, we examine the dynamical behavior of a three-neuron Hopfield network that includes bidirectional connections as well as time-delay effects. Our primary goal is to identify and analyze a higher-order singularity of codimension three, specifically a Hopf-Bogdanov-Takens (H-B-T) point, arising in this framework. Such a degeneracy appears when a double zero eigenvalue occurs simultaneously with a pair of purely imaginary eigenvalues, giving rise to a variety of local phenomena that blend the dynamics typical of Hopf and Bogdanov-Takens bifurcations. By employing center manifold reduction together with normal form computations, inspired by the methodology presented in [1], [2], we rigorously establish the normal form corresponding to the H-B-T degeneracy. These analytical developments are then applied to the delayed Hopfield system, where the synaptic weights and the delay parameter act as unfolding parameters. A thorough bifurcation analysis enables us to construct diagrams capturing the intricate behavior of the model near the singularity, revealing multiple possible local scenarios. To complement and validate the theoretical predictions, we also carry out numerical experiments that illustrate the rich dynamical patterns exhibited by the network.

title: Attacker-Aware Contrastive Learning for Robust Malicious URL Classification
authors: Sara Afzal, Abraham Gebrehiwot, Farrukh Aslam Khan, Filippo Maria Lauria
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Intelligence, Security, and Artificial Intelligence (IntelliSecAI)
abstract: Uniform Resource Locators (URLs) are frequently exploited as entry points for cyberattacks such as phishing, malware distribution, and website defacement. Detecting malicious URLs directly from their lexical structure, without page retrieval, is essential for low-latency defense but remains challenging due to adversarial obfuscations (e.g., homoglyphs, zero-width insertions, subdomain stuffing) and severe class imbalance in real-world corpora. To address these issues, we propose an attacker-aware lexical classification pipeline that combines structure-preserving tokenization, a library of attacker-style augmentations, supervised contrastive (SupCon) pretraining, and class-weighted focal loss fine-tuning. The Transformer encoder serves as the feature extraction model, while a lightweight MLP head performs classification. Empirical evaluations performed on the large Kaggle malicious phishing dataset show that the proposed methodology achieves the accuracy of 99.57% and the macro-F1 score of 99.38% on a hold-out test set, and thus consistently outperforms previously established state-of-the-art baselines. Notably, minority classes, which includes malware, achieves an accuracy and macro-G1 score greater than 99% in all aspects of measurement, highlighting the resentment of the method to adversarial string perturbations. The framework developed is computationally efficient, content agnostic and capable of deployment in a real-time operational scenario that provides a pragmatic defence mechanism against modern cyber threats.

title: Edge-Cloud Intelligence: Adaptive Circuit Topology Perception System*
authors: Yongzhuo Yu, Changju Wu, Chuanyi Wang, Weiwei Yue
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: This paper presents an intelligent circuit topology perception system based on a collaborative edgecloud architecture, focusing on algorithmic and model optimization to address challenges in wire connection detection under non-uniform pin layouts and overlapping scenarios. An enhanced YOLOv8n-seg model is deployed at the edge, incorporating a novel dual-stage perspective mapping mechanism that achieves 0.53 mm positioning accuracy. A wire feature enhancement module maintains a 90.4% recognition rate under varying illumination and wire overlaps. Coupled with a dynamic load balancing strategy, the system attains 93.1% wire recognition accuracy and 202 ms end-to-end latency, significantly improving accuracy and efficiency in electronic experiment teaching.

title: A Novel Flexible Job Shop Scheduling Method via Modified Particle Swarm Optimization
authors: Zhixuan Zhang, Pengpei Gao, Chunhe Song
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: The flexible job shop scheduling problem is a typical complex optimization challenge in intelligent manufacturing and production systems. To overcome the shortcomings of standard particle swarm optimization (PSO), which often falls into local optima and struggles with large-scale instances, this paper proposes an improved discrete PSO integrating simulated annealing and variable neighborhood search. The method employs a nonlinearly decreasing inertia weight to balance exploration and exploitation, while simulated annealing and multi-neighborhood strategies enhance local search. In addition, a dynamic rescheduling framework is developed to handle disturbances such as machine breakdowns and urgent orders. Experimental results on the Kacem and MK benchmarks show that the proposed algorithm outperforms existing methods and demonstrates superior robustness and adaptability in dynamic environments.

title: Sustainable Public Lighting: A Stakeholder Survey on Addressing Ecological, Economic, and Social Complexities
authors: Johannes Weninger, Maximilian Dick, Martina Ascher
publish: 2025 IEEE Sustainable Smart Lighting World Conference &amp; Expo (LS25)
abstract: Urban public lighting faces growing ecological, economic, and social challenges, as higher efficiency technologies have not yielded proportional environmental benefits and have instead contributed to higher overall urban illuminance levels. This study investigates how current and emerging lighting technologies can address these challenges while reflecting diverse stakeholder perspectives. An online survey of 239 participants, including municipal authorities, lighting professionals, manufacturers, and standardization bodies, assessed perceived responsibilities, trust, technology potential, and implementation barriers. Results highlight human health and biodiversity as primary objectives, with federal institutions assigned key responsibility. Advanced technologies were recognized as environmentally and economically beneficial, yet adoption is constrained by regulatory gaps, integration challenges, maintenance demands, and privacy concerns. The findings indicate that sustainable public lighting requires integrated governance context-sensitive technology deployment to achieve sustainable outcomes.

title: Effect of Electrosprayed Aluminum Nitride (AlN) Coatings on Thermal Performance of Multi-Chip Gan Devices
authors: Ibrahim Saadouni, Oumaima Douass, Bousselham Samoudi, Adel Asselman, Laurent Canale
publish: 2025 IEEE Sustainable Smart Lighting World Conference &amp; Expo (LS25)
abstract: Electrosprayed Aluminum Nitride (AlN) coatings are promising for thermal management applications due to their high thermal conductivity and electrical insulation properties. This paper numerically investigates their effect on the thermal performance of a multi-chip GaN LED mounted on a microchannel heat sink with seven pin fins. A 40 W device is analyzed under natural and forced convection using a validated CFD model. AlN layers with thicknesses ranging from 0.01 to 2 mm are applied to the heat sink base and fins. The numerical model is validated against literature data with a maximum deviation of about 2%. The results show that AlN coatings improve heat spreading and reduce thermal resistance by approximately 6% under natural convection and up to 25% under forced convection, demonstrating their effectiveness as a passive cooling solution for high-power GaN LED devices.

title: A Heuristic Optimization Approach for Wind Turbine Dimensions to Enhance Energy Capture and Reduce Costs
authors: Mourad Naidji, Mohamed Ilyas Rahal, Alla Eddine Toubal Maamar, Aicha Aissa-Bokhtache, Maamar Latroch, Radu-Florin Porumb
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy (AIESRE)
abstract: Emerging as a vital component in the worldwide shift towards environmentally friendly power generation is wind energy. Maximizing energy output and reducing cost depend on well-designed wind farms, particularly considering the growing demand for renewable energy. This work expands on several ideal wind turbine configurations suggested in previous work and chooses them as basis for more thorough investigation and enhancement. While most previous studies have concentrated mostly on lowering the cost per kilowatt of generated power, this work takes a more all-encompassing view aiming to improve general wind farm efficiency through strategic optimization of turbine tower heights and rotor diameters, so simultaneously lowering total costs. This kind of approach might produce more flexible and efficient wind farms. A Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) method is used to find the best arrangement of rotor diameters and tower heights over the wind farm in order to reach these targets.

title: Ml and Sentinel-2 Data for Crop Prediction: RF and KNN Performances
authors: Salah Benmahmoud, Olfa Charfi, Raef Maali, Yasmine Zghal
publish: 2025 IEEE International Multi-Conference on Smart Systems &amp; Green Process (IMC-SSGP)
abstract: This study evaluates the temporal dynamics and the apparent change in Land Cover (LC), focusing on the region of El Houareb, in the governorate of Kairouan located in center of Tunisia country where arid climate prevails. By using the June 2019 dataset, we predicted the LC properties and highlight their changes by using two Machine Learning (ML) methods Random Forest (RF) and the K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), for the dates of: November 2023, February 2024 and May 2024. To achieve this goal, we used the TCI (True Color Image) Sentinel2A satellite images obtained from the Copernicus hub, coupled with onsite ground truth data of June 2019 which representing the diverse vegetation and LC of the terrain: Arboriculture, Bare soil, Olives, Fallow, Cereals and Market Garden. These ML classifiers showcase a results with accurate and a visually appealing classification reaching an overall accuracy values more than 0.72 %. However, this study highlights the distinctive disparity between the two ML algorithms in LC.

title: Evaluating Code Readability in Java-to-Kotlin Migration Using Large Language Models and Static Analysis
authors: Nirel Marie M. Ibarra
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: This study investigates the effectiveness of large language models (LLMs) as quantitative evaluators of code readability in industrial-scale Java-to-Kotlin migrations. Focusing on a production Android MVVM repository, the research deploys a cross-validated methodological framework using ChatGPT-5 and Gemini-2.5 Pro, triangulated against established static analysis metrics. By systematically comparing legacy Java and refactored Kotlin code through multi-run LLM scoring and structural complexity measures, the study demonstrates that Kotlin migration yields substantial improvements in cognitive readability. The framework also achieves strong reliability through agreement between models and statistical controls. Practical implications include using LLM-driven analytics in continuous integration pipelines to monitor maintainability, guide refactoring, and reduce technical debt. The findings support an objective and scalable approach to assessing code quality and readability, providing a blueprint for modern software analytics during language and architectural transitions.

title: Performance Analysis of OFDM, OTFS, and AFDM Modulation Over Doubly Selective Channels
authors: Amani Bouguila, Abdelhakim Khlifi, Mohamed Yahia
publish: 2025 IEEE International Multi-Conference on Smart Systems &amp; Green Process (IMC-SSGP)
abstract: Orthogonal Time Frequency Space (OTFS) modulation is a novel technique designed to address the challenges of communication over doubly selective channels, where both time and frequency variations occur due to high mobility. Affine Frequency-Time Modulation (AFDM) extends the concept of time-frequency processing by applying an affine transformation to ensure orthogonality in both domains, making it suitable for combating time and frequency selectivity. In contrast, Orthogonal Frequency Division Multiplexing (OFDM) modulates data onto orthogonal subcarriers in the frequency domain. This work compares OTFS, AFDM, and OFDM in terms of their structure, performance, and resilience under doubly selective channels, with a focus on bit error rate (BER) performance across varying signal-to-noise ratios (SNRs). This paper aims to offer valuable insights into the performance of modern wireless systems under various real-world conditions, providing practical recommendations for designing robust communication systems that can adapt to diverse channel environments.

title: Novel Fault Indicator for Voltage Imbalance Detection and Localization in Asymmetrical Dual Three-Phase Induction Motor
authors: M. W. Chaouch, Y. Gritli, M. Benrejeb
publish: 2025 IEEE International Multi-Conference on Smart Systems &amp; Green Process (IMC-SSGP)
abstract: Multiphase machines are gaining increasing interest in a wide range of modern industry applications, such as aerospace, or automotive applications, because of their diverse advantages over three-phase machines' counterparts. Voltage imbalance is a common fault affecting three-phase machines. However, with a higher number of phases, multiphase machines are more subjected to this type of fault, which has an impact on their performance. Thus, the diagnosis of voltage imbalance is crucial to ensure the required performance level. This paper presents a technique for detecting and localizing voltage imbalance in asymmetrical six-phase induction motors. The developed approach is based on the combined use of current positive and negative sequences for synthetizing a diagnosis fault indicator. The proposed fault indicator is adopted for an effective detection and localization of voltage imbalance. The validity of the proposed strategy is validated by numerical simulations.

title:
High-Performance Activated Carbon Derived from Palm Petiole Using Microwave-Assisted H
                    <sub>3</sub>
                    P O
                    <sub>4</sub>
                    Activation for Methylene Blue Adsorption
authors: Nesrine Abderrahim, Amira Hrichi, Mariem Ahbil, Hedi Ben Amor
publish: 2025 IEEE International Multi-Conference on Smart Systems &amp; Green Process (IMC-SSGP)
abstract: Activated carbon (AC) was synthesized from palm petiole (PP), an abundant agricultural waste, using phosphoric acid activation followed by microwave-assisted impregnation and carbonization. The material was applied for the adsorption of Methylene Blue (MB) from water-based solutions. Important operational factors, such as pH solution, contact time, initial dye concentration and adsorbent dosage, were systematically exanimated. Adsorption was optimal at neutral pH and an adsorbent dose of 0.24 g/L. The equilibrium data were best represented by the Langmuir isotherm, whereas the adsorption kinetics were described by a pseudo-second-order model with a strong correlation coefficient (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>). With a specific surface area of 895 m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>/g and a maximum adsorption capacity of 352.6 mg/g, the produced AC showed promise as an effective and long-lasting dye removal adsorbent.

title: DLR: A Unified Framework for Semi-Supervised Learning with Large Language Models
authors: Imran Ahmad, Khalid N. Alhayyan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: This paper introduces Distill, Label, and Refine (DLR), a teacher-student framework for semi-supervised learning (SSL) designed to address label scarcity in specialized domains by leveraging the capabilities of large language models (LLMs). In this framework, an LLM teacher first generates weak labels and explanatory rationales for a large corpus of unlabeled data. A compact student model then learns from these signals using robust SSL techniques, including confidence thresholding and distribution alignment. To mitigate the effects of noisy labels and confirmation bias, the framework incorporates a refinement stage in which pseudo-labels are audited and filtered through retrievalconsistency checks, critic-LLM reviews, and privileged feedback. By synthesizing state-of-the-art methods such as task-adaptive pre-training (TAPT) and retrieval-validated self-training, DLR provides a cohesive and practical methodology for developing specialized models under significant data and compute constraints. We demonstrate DLR's effectiveness on medical text classification and present preliminary cross-domain evidence on general-domain benchmarks, with comprehensive multi-domain evaluation reserved for future work.

title: Transforming MRI Data Into Latent Space Embeddings Using Multi-Head Learning to Understand Alzheimer's Progression
authors: Manasvini Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: Alzheimer's disease (AD) is a fatal neurodegenerative disorder that affects millions, with one in nine people over 65 years of age diagnosed, and AD-related care costs projected to reach one trillion dollars by 2050. This research focuses on visualizing the progression of AD by leveraging latent space embeddings to depict a continuous trajectory of disease stages, transitioning from cognitively normal (CN) to AD with smooth bridges connecting intermediate states. MRI data from the Alzheimer's Disease Neuroimaging Initiative (ADNI) were preprocessed using FreeSurfer for brain segmentation, and thresholding was applied to reduce input size while preserving key structural features. Several models were evaluated, with ResNet101 achieving the highest F1 score of 0.39. A multi-modal feedforward neural network (FNN) was then introduced to aggregate slice-level predictions, improving the F 1 score to 0.42. A multi-head ResNet101 variant integrating patient metadata (age, sex, and APOE genotype) yielded more distinct and interpretable latent space visualizations. While class overlap and misclassification remained challenges, the multi-head model revealed enhanced class separability and provided a clearer visual continuum of disease progression. Overall, this work presents a data-driven framework that transforms high-dimensional MRI scans into low-dimensional latent space embeddings, enabling visual exploration of Alzheimer's progression through interpretable maps that reveal the continuous transition from healthy to diseased states, offering a visual analytics perspective on neurodegenerative change.

title: Agro E-Commerce Platform with AI-Based Disease Outbreak Prediction
authors: K. Vanitha, K.B. Jayanthi, Harish V, Kavyaa S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: Modern agriculture faces persistent challenges such as soil nutrient degradation, crop disease outbreaks, and inefficient utilization of agricultural resources, which collectively reduce productivity and increase operational costs. To address these challenges, this paper presents an IoT-enabled agro e-commerce platform integrated with an AI-driven disease outbreak prediction framework. The system continuously monitors critical soil parameters, including moisture, pH, and nutrient levels, through distributed IoT sensors, while enabling real-time assessment of crop health. A machine learning-based multi-modal prediction model analyzes plant images, environmental sensor data, and weather information to forecast potential disease occurrences at an early stage, facilitating timely preventive interventions. The platform further incorporates an intelligent chatbot interface that delivers context-aware recommendations for fertilizer application, crop management, and disease mitigation. To enhance practical usability, an integrated e-commerce module allows farmers to directly procure recommended seeds, fertilizers, and agroinputs. Experimental results demonstrate a disease prediction accuracy of 92% and a statistically significant crop yield improvement of approximately 15 %, along with improved efficiency in soil resource utilization. The proposed platform provides a secure, scalable, and data-driven solution that supports sustainable and resilient agricultural practices.

title: Probabilistic Virtual Sensor Modeling for Building Digital Twins Using Generative Inverse Models
authors: Dagimawi D. Eneyew, Girma T. Bitsuamlak, Miriam A.M. Capretz
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Building digital twins can reduce energy consumption and emissions by virtually replicating buildings' static and dynamic characteristics, enabling proactive decision-making to optimize operations. A key feature of building digital twins is their continuous evolution with the physical building, enabled by real-time sensor data. However, buildings often have sensing limitations, including difficulty measuring certain variables, insufficient redundancy, and absent or malfunctioning sensors. This study proposes a Probabilistic Virtual Sensor (PVS) modeling framework to address such limitations. Specifically, Conditional Variational Autoencoders (CVAE) and Conditional Invertible Neural Networks (cINN) were employed as generative inverse models for multi-variable sensing with uncertainty quantification. These models achieved less than 20% Mean Absolute Percentage Error in predicting zone-level lighting and less than 15% for plug loads. In uncertainty quantification, the cINN-based model reduced calibration error by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$93.3 \%-99.9 \%$</tex>, outperforming the CVAE-based model. These results showed that PVS models address physical-sensing limitations while ensuring reliable decision-making.

title: Volumetric Dam Seepage Modelling Using Soil Moisture Data from Sentinel Satellite Imagery
authors: Alvin Allen Manuel, Jessie Mae Allyson Co, Christabel Jane Rubio, Ryan Ramirez
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Seepage control measures are essential in preventing damage, which is a leading cause of dam failures. These measures require constant monitoring for any indication of degradation, which typically manifests as seepage weirs. An underutilized alternative to established seepage monitoring platforms is the use of remotely sensed data, despite the variety of detection principles and platforms available. This study investigates the application of a paired PolSAR remote sensing technique using the Sentinel satellite platform to quantify seepagesoil moisture relationships for dam seepage monitoring. Specifically, with the synergistic use of Sentinel-1 and Sentinel-2 for soil moisture estimation with the Oh-Water Cloud Model in measuring the volumetric seepage of the Pantabangan Dam. A well-correlated season was isolated and analyzed as the basis of the derived model. A good agreement was found during the dry season analysis <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(R^{2}=0.71\right)$</tex>. However, precipitation during the wet season led to a failure of isolating soil moisture due to seepage alone (maximum 5-day antecedent rainfall <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=187 \text{mm}$</tex>). Alternatively, remotely sensed soil moisture was modeled in conjunction with the reservoir water level, yielding comparable results to other coupled models (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}} \boldsymbol{=} \mathbf{0. 7 1}, \mathbf{R M S E} \boldsymbol{=} \mathbf{2. 1 2} \frac{\mathrm{l}}{\mathrm{s}}$</tex>) during the dry season. These results offer the viability of remotely sensed data in monitoring seepage.

title: 3D Body Silhouette Modeling System for Morphological Analysis: Proof of Concept
authors: Cordero-Fernández Gabriela, Santiesteban-Torres Ernesto, Gastelum-Strozzi Alfonso, Sánchez-Pérez Celia
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Accurate, accessible, and non-invasive anthropometric assessment is essential for monitoring healthrelated morphological changes in clinical and wellness applications. This work presents a 3D vision system based on the Intel RealSense D455 camera, capable of capturing depth maps to obtain digital measurements of neck circumference. This measurement is used as an input variable for two computational approaches: (i) predictive models trained on a public database to estimate body fat percentage and body weight, and (ii) a volumetric analysis method that quantifies excess volume based on a body silhouette tracing model that compares a volumegained silhouette with an initial reference silhouette. These models are evaluated through a proof-of-concept study to demonstrate their usefulness as quantitative monitoring tools. The system achieved cervical circumference measurements with relative errors below 1%. The regression models exhibited adequate performance in weight prediction (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}=0.84$</tex>) and provided a consistent estimation of body fat. In addition, the volumetric analysis detected small volume increases with relative errors below 6%. The proposed system offers a low-cost, noninvasive alternative for digital anthropometric measurement, estimation of body-related indicators, and detection of volumetric changes, supporting morphometric analysis applications in health and wellness contexts.

title: Sharp RéNyi-Entropic Isoperimetric Inequalities with Applications to RéNyi-Entropy Power Inequalities
authors: Hao Wu, Lei Yu
publish: 2025 Asia Pacific Workshop on Data Science and Information Theory (APWDSIT)
abstract: A generalized version of Fisher information, which we term the Rényi-Fisher information, is defined as a half of the time-derivative of Rényi differential entropy along heat flow. Based on this Rényi-Fisher information, we establish sharp Rényi-entropic isoperimetric inequalities, which generalize the classic entropic isoperimetric inequality to the Rényi setting. Utilizing these isoperimetric inequalities, we demonstrate that, unlike in the Shannon entropy case, the classic entropy power inequality does not admit a direct extension to Rényi entropy without introducing additional exponents or scaling factors. Furthermore, we establish a sharp Rényi entropy power inequality involving a scaling factor under the assumption that one of two independent random vectors is Gaussian.

title: Compensation for Residual Artifacts in Image Restoration for Robust Face Recognition
authors: Bordiuzha Viktor, Sergei Umnyashkin
publish: 2026 28th International Conference on Digital Signal Processing and its Applications (DSPA)
abstract: The Lucy-Richardson algorithm is widely used for image restoration under blur degradation but is highly sensitive to errors in point spread function estimation, often producing ringing and over-sharpening artifacts. We propose a recognition-oriented approach for compensating residual artifacts after Lucy-Richardson deconvolution. Instead of modifying the iterative restoration process, artifact suppression is performed at the stage of face alignment and normalization. A lightweight ConvNeXt-UNet model is employed to attenuate restoration artifacts while preserving identity-relevant facial structures. Experimental results on real and synthetic face images demonstrate that the proposed method improves biometric image quality while maintaining identity consistency, making it suitable for practical face recognition systems.

title: Topological Data Analysis for Breast Pathology Classification
authors: Aleksei Pakhomov, Anatoly Khamukhin
publish: 2026 28th International Conference on Digital Signal Processing and its Applications (DSPA)
abstract: Thermographic images of the mammary gland exhibit highly variable temperature distributions and low pathological contrast, complicating informative feature extraction. Conventional texture features rely on local information often dominated by task-irrelevant details. This paper investigates the use of topological data analysis (persistent homology) to capture global image structure. Although widely adopted, topological data analysis is mainly applied to homogeneous, well-formalized datasets; its effectiveness for heterogeneous thermographic data remains underexplored. The proposed method employs weighted persistence entropy for feature representation and gradient boosting for classification, and introduces a visualization technique to identify image regions contributing most to the decision process.

title: Domain-Adaptive and Multifactor Invariant Diagnosis of Inter-Turn Short Circuits in Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motors
authors: Alexander L. Shestakov, Dmitrii V. Galyshev, Victoria A. Eremeeva, Olga L. Ibryaeva
publish: 2026 28th International Conference on Digital Signal Processing and its Applications (DSPA)
abstract: This paper proposes an adaptive method for diagnosing inter-turn short circuits in permanent magnet synchronous motors (PMSMs) that ensures robustness against key factors impairing the performance of traditional machine learning models. The developed approach addresses three main challenges: uncertainty in defect phase localization, load variations, and supply frequency fluctuations. To achieve this, a combination of technical solutions is employed: pairwise differences of phase currents to eliminate load dependency, a multi-stage selection of invariant features, and the Margin Disparity Discrepancy (MDD) method for model adaptation between motors of different power ratings. Experimental validation on 1.5 kW and 3 kW motors demonstrated that diagnostic accuracy on the target motor improved from 60 % to 81 % after adaptation while maintaining 98% accuracy on the source motor. The results obtained confirm the practical feasibility of implementing diagnostic systems on new equipment without the time-consuming collection and labeling of fault data. This feature is of particular value for industrial applications, especially in industrial robot drives, where PMSM serve as critical actuators. Signal processing techniques applied to phase current waveforms enable reliable fault detection under varying operational conditions.

title: XGBoost-Based Web Application for Optimizing Credit Evaluation in Peruvian Loan Fintechs
authors: Rodrigo Salvador Gonzales Ysla, Kenny Alexander Ayme Cuba, Jymmy Stuwart Dextre Alarcon
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: The rapid growth of the fintech sector in Peru has created an urgent need for more accurate and automated credit evaluation systems, as traditional approaches often fail to consider external or alternative data sources. This paper proposes the development of a web application based on the XGBoost algorithm to optimize credit risk prediction in Peruvian fintech companies offering personal loans. The study followed four stages: data collection and preparation, model training, web application construction, and validation. A dataset of 59,682 historical loan applications provided by a Peruvian fintech company was used, after applying data cleaning, normalization, and categorical encoding techniques. The XGBoost model was compared against Random Forest and Logistic Regression using classification metrics such as accuracy, recall, F1-score, and AUC-ROC. Results show that XGBoost achieved an AUC-ROC of 0.965 and an F1score of 0.977, offering a balanced trade-off between accuracy and computational efficiency. When integrated into the web platform, the model reduced the average credit evaluation time by over 60 percent and increased accuracy by 13.2 percentage points compared to the manual process. Overall, the proposed system demonstrates that using XGBoost in a web-based environment significantly improves the automation, consistency, and speed of credit evaluation processes, contributing to more reliable financial decision-making in Peruvian fintechs.

title: A Deep Federated Learning Approach for Secure Malware Detection in Cloud Computing
authors: B. Chermadurai, S. Singaravelan, P. Gopalsamy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: In this paper, a privacy-preserving malware detection framework for cloud infrastructures is presented using federated learning and deep neural networks. The proposed system is designed to address the growing challenges of data privacy, distributed cloud environments, and evolving malware threats. Federated learning is employed to enable collaborative model training across multiple cloud clients while ensuring that sensitive data remain on local devices and are never centrally shared. Advanced deep learning (DL) architectures, namely Transformer-based and GRU-enhanced models, are integrated to capture both long-range contextual dependencies and temporal behavioral patterns in cloud network traffic. An ensemble learning strategy is adopted to combine the strengths of both models, resulting in improved detection accuracy and robustness. The system is evaluated using a synthetic dataset that simulates real-world cloud traffic scenarios, and performance is assessed through standard metrics including accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, AUC, and confusion matrix analysis. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed approach achieves high detection performance while maintaining strong privacy guarantees. The findings confirm that the integration of federated learning with hybrid DL models provides a scalable and effective solution for secure malware detection in cloud environments, making the framework suitable for deployment in privacy-sensitive and large-scale distributed systems.

title: Edge Embedded Deep Learning Intelligent Electronic Nose for Real-Time Gas Detection
authors: Fethi Farhat, Ridha El Hamdi, Hassen Dahman
publish: 2025 IEEE International Multi-Conference on Smart Systems &amp; Green Process (IMC-SSGP)
abstract: Electronic noses (e-noses) serve as innovative sensor platforms that emulate human olfactory capabilities to identify and categorize volatile organic compounds (VOCs) in real time. Conventional e-nose designs often depend on cloud-based computation, which can lead to delays and reliance on network stability. This research introduces a sophisticated e-nose system incorporating a deep learning algorithm deployed on an edge computing device, leveraging an enriched dataset and an intricate neural network design. Utilizing TensorFlow, the system is implemented on an ESP32 microcontroller, offering two operational modes: Mode 1 transmits sensor data to a remote server for analysis, while Mode 2 employs an optimized TensorFlow Lite model for on-device processing. The prototype achieves a 96% accuracy rate on validation datasets for detecting gases such as carbon monoxide (CO), methane (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CH}_{4}$</tex>), and liquefied petroleum gas (LPG). Notable contributions include the integration of edge-based AI, advanced model training techniques, and a versatile dual-mode prototype. Future efforts will target enhanced sensor calibration and broader gas detection capabilities.

title: Analysis of Few-Shot Transfer Learning for Image Classification on Spacecraft
authors: Eileen Wang, Alan George
publish: 2025 IEEE Space Computing Conference (SCC)
abstract: There are many important applications for realtime, autonomous classification of satellite images in the field of remote sensing (RS). Convolutional neural networks (CNN) have been shown to yield the highest classification accuracies in this field. However, typical CNN-based methods require large amounts of labeled training data to perform well, which is time-consuming and laborious to obtain. Few-shot learning is explored as a practical solution to enable fast adaptation to novel classes with few labeled samples. Two few-shot algorithms, namely Prototypical Networks and LaplacianShot, were explored in this work. However, the performance of these methods depends on pre-training on an abundance of related data, which may not necessarily be available in RS. To address this requirement, this research contributes insights into a more realistic satellite image classification scenario where novel class samples are scarce and have different spatial resolutions than existing labeled data. In this work, the aforementioned few-shot learning methods were compared to traditional supervised learning methods, specifically SVM and CNN fine-tuning. Ablation experiments were conducted to examine performance across varying amounts of training samples and the effect of pre-training the backbone on different publicly available RS datasets. Results show that few-shot methods fine-tuned on domain-relevant data demonstrate improved accuracies by up to 18 % for novel tasks in a 5 -way 1-shot scenario compared to using an out-of-domain backbone. In addition, LaplacianShot outperforms traditional training methods by 4.0 % in 5-way 1-shot tasks even without fine-tuning on domain-specific data, demonstrating viability for few-shot algorithms in extremely data-constrained situations.

title: LSTM Networks for Thermal Management in Power Converters of Electric Vehicles
authors: Prabakaran Kasinathan, R. Vijayan, K. Sindhuja, J. Kavitha, T. Yuvaraj, S Murugan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: Effective thermal control of power converters is essential for the efficiency, reliability, and durability of electric vehicles (EVs). This work introduces a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM)-based methodology for accurate temperature forecasting and anticipatory thermal regulation in EV power converters. The proposed model utilizes an extensive dataset including temperature readings, load currents, and switching frequencies to encapsulate the complex nonlinear thermal dynamics under diverse operating situations. Experimental findings indicate that the LSTM model attains a mean absolute error (MAE) of 0.9°C and a root mean square error (RMSE) of 1.3° C using previously unexamined test data, surpassing conventional machine learning methods by more than 30%. Integrating LSTM forecasts into the thermal management system reduces peak operating temperatures by 20%, hence boosting converter efficiency and minimizing thermally caused failures. Furthermore, the model demonstrates strong performance under fast load variations, rendering it appropriate for real-time, integrated heat management applications. The research underscores the capability of LSTM networks to enhance thermal management techniques in EVs, therefore augmenting the reliability and safety of power electronics and promoting the economy of electric transportation.

title: Real-Time Multi-Modal Risk Assessment in Public Spaces Using Vision
authors: Dhanaaravindhan S, N. Jeenath Shafana, Elkana Vilvin Easter
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: Common public places like schools, hospitals, shopping centers, airports, and transport hubs often have situations where emotional distress going unnoticed or sudden changes in human behavior can result in safety risks. Conventional CCTV systems require continuous human monitoring, which is limited by operator fatigue and attention span. The paper introduces an upgraded version of VisionSentinel, a real-time, multi-face monitoring framework that integrates facial emotion recognition, simple face-tracking, and machine-learning-based risk estimation. The system is performing live webcam or CCTV feeds, locating people with lightweight Haar Cascade face detection, DeepFace CNN-based emotion model per person for affective cues and RandomForest-based classifier for risk levels trained on behavioral indicators to assign risk levels. Interpretation layer in the system generates the interpretable summaries for the detected people. The prototype is efficient in performance on consumer hardware with the issues of explainability, modularity, and privacy-aware design being addressed. The experimental results show that even with minimal inputs, the combined emotion analysis and lightweight risk modeling are better in terms of responsiveness than basic video monitoring systems, thus, they provide a practical way for the implementation of scalable, ethical behavioral monitoring in public spaces.

title: Prediction Comparison in Educational Data using Hybrid Time Series SARIMA-XGBoost Model
authors: Gede Bramanta Pandya Wisesa, Muhammad Zarlis
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Blockchain, Cloud Computing, and Data Analytics (ICoABCD)
abstract: Accurate student enrollment forecasting is a cornerstone of institutional budgeting and resource allocation. The present study proposes and assesses a hybrid time-series model for forecasting monthly new-student enrollments at the Bali Institute of Design and Business. The methodology utilizes a two-stage, residual-learning framework. Initially, a Seasonal Autoregressive Integrated Moving Average (SARIMA) model is fitted to capture the primary linear trends and seasonality in the data. Secondly, an Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) model is trained on the resulting residuals to learn the remaining non-linear patterns, reconciling statistical structure with flexible non-linear function approximation. Two distinct sets of engineered features were tested to optimize the XGBoost component, incorporating multiple lag variables and rolling mean windows to capture short, medium, and long term dynamics. The model's performance was evaluated using Mean Absolute Error (MAE), Root Mean Square Error (RMSE), and Symmetric Mean Absolute Percentage Error (sMAPE). The model was trained using data from 2021–2024 and tested using data from 2025. The findings substantiate that the hybrid model, particularly when endowed with a comprehensive feature set, exhibits a marked superiority in performance over the standalone SARIMA model, achieving an 18% reduction in MAE, thereby furnishing a more precise forecasting instrument for institutional planning.

title: Hybrid Blockchain-Based Presidential Election System to Increase Vote Efficiency and Security
authors: Inayatulloh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Blockchain, Cloud Computing, and Data Analytics (ICoABCD)
abstract: The presidential election is a process of voting by the people to elect the president and vice president of a country. This process involves citizens actively participating in determining the national leader who will lead the government for a given period. The purpose of the presidential election is to realize democracy and ensure that state power comes from the sovereignty of the people by electing leaders through majority votes. Several countries have used information technology to support presidential elections, making the process easier. The problems arising from conventional information technologybased election systems include data manipulation and nontransparent vote-counting processes, which cast doubt on the presidential election system. Several studies offer solutions using blockchain technology. Blockchain technology offers several advantages, such as peer-to-peer validation, which ensures transparency in the vote-counting process, and distributed storage, which provides security for vote-counting data. However, research on applying existing blockchain technology is limited to conceptual models that are difficult to understand and implement. Therefore, this research aims to build a prototype of a presidential election using Ganache. Selecting a hybrid blockchain for research enables the government-owned presidential election system to be used for blockchain-based presidential elections. Research uses a qualitative approach through literature review to study the weaknesses of conventional information technology-based and blockchain technology-based presidential election systems. The latest research is a prototype of a presidential election using a hybrid blockchain and simulation with Ganache.

title: LockEase- Smart Door Security System
authors: Pallavi Nayak, Gunnikaa P Gupta, Vipul Raj, Manishka Nayaka, Sushma Shree H S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: The Smart locking system is of advanced technology for an access control system implemented for residential and commercial security, where the traditional locking systems are based on physical keys, whereas RFID tags and password authentication access, which are prone to loss, duplication or unauthorized access which has an impact on the security. To overcome these limitations, this idea revolves around a smart locking system that combines biometrics, mobile app-based unlocking, biometric verification, remote access control, and live surveillance using the ESP32, and integrates radio frequency identification for access control. Every attempt is recorded and timestamped, and the activity log also enhances real-time communication by enabling remote control, allowing users to see and verify, after which they can grant access to the protected system safely. The system can also be accessed and monitored through a web application with instant notifications, which is reliable and scalable. The system can be installed and used as a security system in any environment, like houses, apartments (commercial and private), wardrobes, safe boxes, gates and small businesses. Compared to a traditional locking system, the smart door locking is customizable to an extent, making it versatile in use and easily implemented.

title: EDGChain-E: A Decentralized Git-Based Framework for Versioning Encrypted Energy Data
authors: Alper Alimoglu, Kamil Erdayandi, Mustafa A. Mustafa, Ümit Cali
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Distributed Ledger Technologies (ICDLT)
abstract: This paper proposes a new decentralized framework, named EDGChain-E (Encrypted-Data-Git Chain for Energy), designed to manage version-controlled, encrypted energy data using blockchain and the InterPlanetary File System. The framework incorporates a Decentralized Autonomous Organization (DAO) to orchestrate collaborative data governance across the lifecycle of energy research and operations, such as smart grid monitoring, demand forecasting, and peer-to-peer energy trading. In EDGChain-E, initial commits capture the full encrypted datasets—such as smart meter readings or grid telemetry—while subsequent updates are tracked as encrypted Git patches, ensuring integrity, traceability, and privacy. This versioning mechanism supports secure collaboration across multiple stakeholders (e.g., utilities, researchers, regulators) without compromising sensitive or regulated information. We highlight the framework's capability to maintain FAIR-compliant (Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, Reusable) provenance of encrypted data. By embedding hash-based content identifiers in Merkle trees, the system enables transparent, auditable, and immutable tracking of data changes, thereby supporting reproducibility and trust in decentralized energy applications.

title: Blockchain Technologies for Traceability and Efficiency in Service-Sector Supply Chains
authors: Joel Curado, Ana L Martins, Joao C. Ferreira
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Distributed Ledger Technologies (ICDLT)
abstract: Liquids-focused service-sector supply chains (healthcare cold chain, maritime documentation, luxury spirits) face concurrent challenges in traceability, privacy, and throughput. We present a modular blockchain architecture that combines a permissioned core with zeroknowledge proofs (zk-SNARKs) for selective disclosure, Layer-2 batching for scale, and IoT edge ingestion for realtime monitoring. Using Design Science Research, we design, prototype, and simulate representative workloads. Under WAN latencies typical of port operations, L2 batching reduces end-to-end verification latency relative to a Fabriconly baseline; in cold-chain scenarios, proof cadence tuning maintains privacy with bounded overhead. We discuss oracle and interoperability risks and provide a cost model. Results and artifacts are intended to guide practitioners balancing privacy, performance, and compliance in regulated logistics.

title: A Smart Electricity Meter Using IoT for Real-Time Monitoring and Remote Control
authors: Linga Reddy Gari Kavyanjali, K. Amaresh Srujan Gupta, Jonnavaram Gireesh Reddy, Markapuram Lokesh Kumar, Kavyashree I Pattan, Nandini K
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies in Electronics and Green Energy (ICETEG)
abstract: Traditional electricity meters leave consumers with nothing but information on their consumption, giving them extremely little real-time feedback on the usage of energy. Thus, consumers cannot manage electricity utilization effectively, and unscheduled energy utilization and surprise bills are experienced. With growing needs for electricity, there is an urgent requirement for smart energy management systems that are consumer-friendly and utility-friendly as well. In this paper, an IOT based smart electricity meter system design, with a focus on real-time monitoring, enhanced accuracy of bill estimation, and better control over the utilization of energy by the users, has been described. The system has been implemented on an ESP8266 micro controller with a voltage and a current sensor and is webenabled with a web interface, where electricity statistics of a user can be checked on any device connected to the internet. The most prominent features are real-time units of usage, history of usage, and prediction, which assist the user in identifying which appliance is drawing more electricity. Users can set personal consumption levels and are notified when they reach near these levels in order to utilize energy in the most optimum manner. All these enhancements lead to improved utilization of energy distribution. Remote monitoring and control of energy eliminate the necessity for manual readings of meters and therefore maximize operations and minimize inefficiencies. With the use of data analytic and IOT, such a smart metering solution is a costsaving, scalable solution for electricity management in the present day. The suggestion is that such a system's operation can instill increased efficiency in household electricity utilization, cut costs, and head towards a greener and smarter grid.

title: ML-Based Analysis of Crop Recommended System
authors: Rahul Bhandari, Suresh Kaswan, Daulat Sihag, Reshma Khan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies in Electronics and Green Energy (ICETEG)
abstract: This study is interested in assisting farmers to select the right crop on their fields using the latest methods to enhance crop management system. The current study uses a Kaggle dataset of 2,200 inputs and outputs: eight inputs (see Figure 3), i.e., N, P, K, rainfall, pH, temperature, humidity, and labels of 22 crops to fit and test predictive models. Five classification algorithms, i.e., Decision Tree (DT), Logistic Regression (LR), Support Vector Machine (SVM), Random Forest (RF), and K-Nearest Neighbour (KNN), were used and performance evaluated by Precision, Recall, F1-score and Mean Squared error. The findings suggest that the Random Forest algorithm is both the most accurate and predictive algorithm, whereas Logistic Regression performs the worst.

title: Improving Sentiment Classification of Product Reviews in Bahasa Indonesia Using Kernel-Based SVM
authors: Tuga Mauritsius, Aldi Rifaldi Saputra, Amelia Ludia Kafiar, Gabriella Gayle Monica, Astari Retnowardhani, Eka Miranda
publish: 2026 International Seminar on Intelligent Business and Edge-Computing Research (ISIBER)
abstract: Sentiment analysis of user-generated reviews has become an important tool for understanding consumer opinions in e-commerce platforms. However, sentiment datasets in online marketplaces are often highly imbalanced, and limited research has examined how kernel selection in Support Vector Machine (SVM) models interacts with imbalance handling strategies in Indonesian-language text classification. This study presents a comparative analysis of Support Vector Machine (SVM) kernel functions for sentiment classification of Indonesian-language product reviews. A dataset comprising 5,495 reviews of Kahf skincare products was collected from Tokopedia and Shopee platforms and manually labeled as positive, neutral, or negative. Text preprocessing included normalization, tokenization, stopword removal, and TF-IDF vectorization, enabling unstructured text to be systematically transformed into structured numerical features for analytical modeling. Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique (SMOTE) was employed on the numerical feature representations (TF-IDF vectors) of the training data to mitigate class imbalance, while preserving the integrity of the test set. Three SVM kernelsLinear, Radial Basis Function (RBF), and Polynomial-were evaluated both with and without SMOTE balancing. Performance was assessed using accuracy, F1-score, and macroaveraged AUC. Results indicate that the Linear SVM with SMOTE achieved the most balanced and robust performance, attaining an AUC of 0.84 and outperforming the non-linear kernels. The findings demonstrate that a linear decision boundary, combined with oversampling, is well suited for sentiment analysis of high-dimensional Indonesian-language text in imbalanced scenarios.

title: Heart Disease Prediction Using Scaled Conjugate Gradient and Resilient Backpropagation Neural Network Models
authors: Yessi Jusman, Aban Athaya Nurza, Masayu Alya Nur Aini
publish: 2026 International Seminar on Intelligent Business and Edge-Computing Research (ISIBER)
abstract: Cardiovascular disease (CVD) remains the leading cause of mortality worldwide, underscoring the urgent need for early and accurate prediction to reduce associated risks. Traditional diagnostic methods often struggle to capture the complex interactions among multiple risk factors, prompting the adoption of computational intelligence approaches to address this challenge. In this study, artificial neural networks (ANN) were employed to predict heart disease using two gradient-based training algorithms: Scaled Conjugate Gradient (SCG) and Resilient Backpropagation (RProp). A dataset of patient health records was deployed to train and validate the models through a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0}$</tex>-fold cross-validation scheme, ensuring robust performance evaluation. Model effectiveness was assessed using key metrics, including classification accuracy and Receiver Operating Characteristic (ROC) curves. The experimental results demonstrated that both SCG and RProp achieved high accuracy in predicting heart disease, with stable performance across training, validation, and testing sets. Notably, the SCG model with 5 hidden nodes yielded the highest accuracies of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$92.3 \%, 93.6 \%$</tex>, and 92.3% for training, validation, and testing, respectively. Overall, the findings exhibited that SCG and RProp models could serve as reliable tools for predicting heart disease, providing valuable decision-support capabilities for clinicians in early diagnosis and risk management.

title: Design and Construction of ELCB Practical Equipment as Touch Voltage Protection
authors: Sunarto, Toto Tohir, Yudi Pranahikmat, Sudrajat
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Materials and Electrical Engineering (ISMEE)
abstract: The tools and practicums required in the Low Voltage Protection course of the D3 Electrical Engineering Study Program, Department of Electrical Engineering, Politeknik Negeri Bandung include a practicum tool for testing leakage current protection components using Earth Leakage Circuit Breaker (ELCB). The availability of these practicum tools is essential to increase the practicum facilities for the Low Voltage Protection course. This practicum tool can be used for ELCB testing as protection against direct and indirect touch voltage disturbances. Direct touch voltage occurs when humans directly touch the phase part. At the same time, indirect touch voltage occurs when there is insulation damage so that there is a leakage current to the equipment body, which can cause a touch voltage hazard. Based on these problems, this research was conducted to improve the performance and services of the Electrical Installation Laboratory of Politeknik Negeri Bandung as the main facility for learning and research. In low-voltage systems, the ELCB protection component is a very important protection component to prevent the occurrence of touch voltage hazards. As touch voltage protection, MCB has a very important role in safety in the world of electrical system work. The method used in this research is the engineering research method. The result of this research is a single-unit laboratory tool, specifically an ELCB testing tool for touch voltage protection. The ELCB component used in the testing is a single-phase 30 mA ELCB.

title: AI Enhanced Intrusion Detection with Facial Biometrics
authors: Josephine Selle Jeyanathan, V. Subashini, Lakshmi Sreenivasulu Boginipeta, Rahul Raj S, Kavya Sree Garladinna
