publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: The face recognition smart home system provides secure, reliable access control that seamlessly integrates deep learning (CNN/embeddings), hardware for the Internet of Things (IoT), and an adaptable Android app. The backend, written in Python, uses Firebase (Storage, Authentication, Firestore, Realtime Database) to provide scalable functionality across multiple devices while also asserting intuitive data management. The system has a unique multi-source registration process (ESP32-CAM, in-app captures, gallery) that is designed to be efficient with respect to users' face datasets, but also provide realistic constraints in data augmentation to improve the robustness of face embedding in real-world scenarios experiencing occlusion and/or poor-lighting conditions. It relies on deep learning technology (Facenet, MTCNN) for embedding extraction and was trained on a simple SVM classifier, to allow easy reimplementation of new classifiers. The events are logged in real-time, and the notifications and the access control are achieved using the ESP8266 and powered by Firebase, resulting in a secure, user-friendly, and privacy-informed solution.

title: Underwater Image Stitching Based on Image Enhancement Methods
authors: Wei Zhang, Xiang Li, Jun Liu
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Computing (SPIC)
abstract: Underwater image stitching and fusion belong to an important topic in marine exploration, underwater archaeological activities and resource development. The underwater environment's characteristics cause issues like color distortion, low contrast, and detail blur due to the absorption and scattering of light in water, leading to challenges in image stitching and fusion. To solve these problems, the paper puts forward an underwater-image-stitching-fusion algorithm with image enhancement technology, so as to enhance underwater images and realize the smooth stitching of underwater images. The algorithm first improves underwater pictures by doing better color correction and noise reduction. It uses a feature point based registration that makes sure feature matching is better than with usual methods, so images match up more strongly. finally, we use a multi-resolution fusion method via laplacian pyramind division to blend the images, which are well-aligned, to remove the visible stitches, producing high-quality panoramic photos. To check the effectiveness of the algorithm, an algorithm is tested on a few real underwater scenes systematically. Underwater panorama images processed by this method have significant advantages in terms of color naturalness, detail retention, and stitching trace removal. Experimental results show that compared to traditional underwater image stitching algorithms, this algorithm can increase the PSNR of the stitched panoramas by an average of 8.3dB, improve the SSIM value by 0.15, and reduce the matching error of the feature points to less than 0.5 pixels.

title: Coal-Gangue Recognition Technology Based on Bimodal Data Fusion
authors: Yaohui Wang, Xiaoli Han, Xiaohu Sun, Yihang Hua, Bo Pan, Haoran Zhang, Zheng Wang, Hua Li, Lingbo Li, Qing Yang
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Computing (SPIC)
abstract: Accurate online recognition of coal and gangue during top-coal caving is a key technology for improving coal quality, enhancing recovery efficiency, and realizing intelligent mining. This paper proposes a deep learning-based recognition method founded on bimodal signal-image fusion, in which sound and vibration signals are innovatively integrated into a unified composite image representation after standardizing the dimensions of the single-modality images from both sources. Experimental results show that using only acoustic signals yields an accuracy of 68.52%, while using only vibration signals achieves 74.4%. When both modalities are combined, the recognition accuracy on the test set increases by more than 10% compared to any single-modality model, reaching 83.7%. This study not only verifies the great potential of multisource information fusion in coal-gangue recognition but also provides a new technological pathway for intelligent separation in complex industrial environments. Furthermore, it offers a simple and effective approach for multimodal fusion of physical quantities such as vibration and sound in other application fields.

title: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Blockchain, Cloud Computing, and Data Analytics (ICoABCD)
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Blockchain, Cloud Computing, and Data Analytics (ICoABCD)

title: Multi-Stage Framework for Robust Drone Face Detection and Recognition
authors: Mostafa Farwiz, Mohamed Hamed
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Advancements in drone technology, combined with recent progress in computer vision and deep learning, have enabled new possibilities in surveillance and security applications. This paper proposes a novel multi-stage framework for drone-based face detection, gender classification, and recognition optimized for robust real-time aerial performance. The YOLOv8 model, fine-tuned on the WIDER FACE dataset, is employed to achieve reliable face detection under challenging aerial conditions, including varying poses, scales, and lighting. Following detection, a convolutional neural network (CNN) performs gender classification, enhancing contextual demographic awareness before the recognition stage. For recognition, detected faces are processed using deep embedding generation and cosine similarity to ensure lightweight and accurate identity matching. The proposed framework effectively addresses challenges associated with aerial imagery and resource-limited drone platforms, achieving high accuracy and efficiency. These contributions highlight the framework's novelty for drone-based surveillance systems supporting security, search and rescue, and public safety applications.

title: Standardized Communication Protocols for Digital Twin Integration in Discrete Manufacturing: From Machine to Plant
authors: Mohsin Raza, Naeem Ayoub, Arne Bilberg, Ali Ahmad Malik, Anis Assad Neto
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Bringing together simulation models and real manufacturing systems is central to creating effective digital twins (DTs) and cyber-physical production systems. This study emphasis on the communication standards that make such integration possible, showing how information can move smoothly from a single machine to an entire production cell and, ultimately, the whole plant. The analysis is based on academic sources and global standards. We look at the structure, semantic modeling, time performance, and how well standardized communication protocols work for system integration. The research demonstrates that these standards collectively facilitate a seamless exchange of information between digital and physical realms. Drawing from existing literature, it formulates an integrated framework for communication spanning from the machine level to the cell and plant levels, grounded in these standards. It concludes that standardized communication is what makes the ISO 23247 digital twin framework operate and is what makes it possible to have scalable, secure, and interoperable manufacturing environments.

title: Edge-Efficient Crop Health Monitoring using Selfsupervised Shufflenet and Attention-Based Multimodal Fusion
authors: P. Vanathi, R. Nithees Kumar, K. Gokul, S. Giritharan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: A major concern of sustainable agriculture is to ensure crop health, especially with rising climate variability and low natural resources. The majority of approaches to crop monitoring are based on the traditional approach or deep neural networks that necessitate a lot of manual inspections and are computationally complex, which is incompatible with real-time monitoring of large fields. The following paper will suggest an efficient and smart crop health monitoring system, which will be implemented as a lightweight ShuffleNet-driven deep neural network, with heavy emphasis on high-precision and low-computational-overhead. Self-supervised learning is used to perform initial training on a large number of unlabeled crop images to extract robust and domain-invariant features and avoid the need to annotate a large number of Paris crop images manually. In order to further improve stress detection performance, the visual data is fused with the measurements of the environmental sensors, such as soil moisture, temperature, humidity, and nutrient levels, using an attention-based multimodal fusion mechanism that adaptively focuses on the most effective biotic and abiotic stress indicator. Also, an edge cloud collaborative inference architecture is presented with lightweight ShuffleNet models running real-time analysis at the edge and temporal modeling on the cloud assisting in making early predictive decisions before crops progressively lose their health. Experimental findings also indicate that the suggested framework is more precise and generalizes better and incurs less inference latency than the traditional approaches based on supervised convolutional neural networks. In general, the system is scalable, energy efficient and suitable to be applied to next generation precision agriculture uses.

title: Design and Implementation of Multi-Characteristic Overcurrent Relay for Protection Systems Using DFRduino Microcontroller
authors: Dini Hariani Fitri Lubis, Annisa Izaty, Sunarto, Mutia Nur Khasanah, M Adrian M, Euis Hindasah
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Materials and Electrical Engineering (ISMEE)
abstract: An overcurrent relay is a protective device that interrupts an electrical circuit when the current exceeds its preset limit. Overcurrent relays are generally classified into three types based on their operating time: Instantaneous, Definite Time, and Inverse. This paper presents the design and implementation of a multi-characteristic overcurrent relay for power system protection using a DFRduino microcontroller. The proposed system integrates a current sensor (ACS712), a DFRduino Uno R3 microcontroller, an LCD I2C 20×4, a 5 V relay, and indicator lamps. The design aims to provide an affordable and flexible relay capable of operating under different protection characteristics. Performance evaluation was conducted by comparing the current values displayed on the relay LCD with clamp meter measurements, as well as by validating relay operating times against IEC 60255 standard curves. Experimental results demonstrate that the developed relay achieves current measurement accuracy above 95% and shows a responsive tripping performance consistent with theoretical protection characteristics. This work highlights the potential of microcontroller-based relays as an effective, low-cost solution for protection systems in small to medium-scale electrical networks.

title: Performance Evaluation of a Solar Panel Under Different Types of Surface Contamination
authors: Ferry R A Bukit, Naemah Mubarakah, Soeharwinto, Riswan Dinzi, Alwi Dwi Cahyo
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Materials and Electrical Engineering (ISMEE)
abstract: The transition toward renewable energy, particularly solar power, continues to accelerate due to its abundance and environmental benefits. However, photovoltaic performance is highly affected by external factors, especially surface contamination. This study examines the impact of dust, clay, and sand on the performance of a monocrystalline solar panel. The experiment used one panel tilted at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5}^{\boldsymbol{\circ}}$</tex> over nine days, with each contaminant (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 ~ g}$</tex>) applied for three consecutive days. Voltage, current, power, temperature, and efficiency were measured hourly from 08:00 to 16:00. Results showed that dust contamination caused the most severe performance drop, reducing average power from 31.68 W to 20.14 W and efficiency from 13.01% to 8.27%, with current falling from 1.54 A to 0.99 A, while voltage declined slightly from 24.58 V to 24.40 V; dust also raised panel temperature from 39.68° C to 42.04° C. For clay, power fell from 48.95 W to 35.78 W and efficiency from 14.15% to 10.28%, while temperature dropped from 44.81° C to 37.70° C due to its moisture content. Sand contamination reduced power from 42.62 W to 33.90 W and efficiency from 14.10% to 11.18%, while increasing temperature from 42.57° C to 44.63° C. Overall, the findings confirm that each contaminant influences solar panel performance differently depending on particle size and physical properties.

title: Big Data of TikTok Shop Consumer Reviews for Mapping Service Quality Dissatisfaction Aspect Using ABSA Method
authors: Nanda Ardi Pradita, Gunawan Wang
publish: 2026 International Seminar on Intelligent Business and Edge-Computing Research (ISIBER)
abstract: The rapid expansion of social commerce, particularly through TikTok Shop, has generated a massive volume of unstructured consumer feedback that meets Big Data characteristics. Although such data contain valuable insights into service quality, traditional sentiment analysis provides only document-level polarity and fails to identify the specific factors driving user dissatisfaction. This study aims to automatically map and rank the most influential service quality aspects contributing to negative sentiment by applying Aspect-Based Sentiment Analysis (ABSA). More than 29,000 consumer reviews were collected and processed through rigorous text preprocessing, including normalization, text cleaning, and stopword removal, before being vectorized using TF-IDF. Support Vector Machine (SVM) and Logistic Regression were employed for sentiment and aspect classification, achieving strong performance with an overall sentiment accuracy of 83%. Using ABSA, the study successfully identified the key aspects shaping consumer dissatisfaction. Results indicate that the delivery aspect represents the most dominant source of negative feedback, accounting for 76.63% of all negative reviews. These findings offer actionable, data-driven insights that can assist TikTok Shop management in prioritizing strategic service improvements and enhancing customer retention in a highly competitive e-commerce environment.

title: ARDUINO Based Smart Water Quality Monitoring System
authors: Sunaina Tiwari, Sapna Kumari, Arpita Ghosh, Sauraj Sen
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies in Electronics and Green Energy (ICETEG)
abstract: This work proposes a Smart Water Quality Monitoring System implemented utilizing pH, Total Dissolved Solids (TDS) sensor, temperature sensor and ATmega328P microcontroller. The system provides real-time data acquisition and analysis, interfaced with an Arduino UNO microcontroller for data processing and transmission to a central monitoring unit. The implementation includes sensor calibration, data processing algorithms, and a user-friendly interface. Prototype testing shows reliable and accurate measurements, offering a cost-effective solution for maintaining water quality standards and ensuring public health.

title: Anti-Cheating Online Exam Through Hand Gesture Answering Using Webrtc
authors: Rafli Zaki Rabbani, Siscka Elvyanti, Nurul Fahmi Arief Hakim
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Materials and Electrical Engineering (ISMEE)
abstract: Shorter exam formats, such as Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs), which are preferable for online exams has amplified vulnerabilities for exam dishonesty. Online exams easily exploited through search engines or current state of AIdriven platforms such as ChatGPT. Existing proctoring approaches, ranging from authentication, facial monitoring to browser lockdowns, remain limited by requirements and evolving cheating strategies. This research proposes a system designed to prevent remote cheating on online exams, particularly in MCQ exams, by requiring no additional software installation and hardware beyond a built-in camera, enforcing gesture-only answering. A dual-detection algorithm combined hand orientation validation with angle features classification, supported by delay buffers to reduce false inputs, with real-time video streaming and processing was facilitated through WebRTC (Web Real-Time Communication). Experimental evaluation demonstrated that the system achieved robust performance and low latency, which maintained approximately 15 FPS (Frames Per Second). Offering a reliable and practical complement to conventional proctoring methods, enhancing the integrity of online MCQ examinations.

title: Advanced IOT-Based Real-Time Fire Detection and Smart Home Environmental Monitoring System
authors: Khakam Ma'ruf, Rizal Justian Setiawan, Desi Ayu Kumalasari, Nur Azizah, Darmono, Nur Evirda Khosyiati
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Materials and Electrical Engineering (ISMEE)
abstract: The implementation of an early fire warning system is essential to mitigate substantial losses and provide individuals with sufficient time to evacuate. This study develops an Internet of Things (IoT)-based fire detection system designed to effectively monitor environmental conditions in residential settings. The system integrates an infrared sensor, MQ-2 gas sensor, and DHT11 humidity sensor to detect potential fire hazards in realtime. Utilizing the ESP32 microcontroller, the system processes sensor data and transmits notifications via the Blynk application to users' smartphones. Experimental results indicate that the infrared sensor achieves 73.3% accuracy in detecting flames, while the MQ-2 sensor and DHT11 sensor record 86.67% accuracy in detecting hazardous gas concentrations and humidity levels that could trigger fires, respectively. This study underscores the importance of continuous environmental monitoring to mitigate fire risks. By employing this system, users receive early warnings, enabling swift action in emergency situations. The IoTbased fire detection system proposed in this research is expected to enhance home safety and can be adapted for industrial and smart building applications.

title: An Improved MPPT Performance Using Horse Herd Optimization Algorithm for PV-Battery Charge Controller Application
authors: Rizky Ajie Aprilianto, Subiyanto, Mario Norman Syah, Dwi Bagas Nugroho
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Materials and Electrical Engineering (ISMEE)
abstract: The PV-battery charge controller has integrated maximum power point tracking (MPPT) to optimize the power extraction result. Unfortunately, oscillation existing in steady-state conditions makes power extraction less efficient. This work proposed an improved MPPT performance using a horse herd optimization (HHO) algorithm for PV-battery charge controller application. The proposed system comprises 2 kW PV, MPPT with interleaved buck converter (IBC), and a charge controller dedicated to the lead acid battery type using the constant-current constant-voltage (CC-CV) method. MATLAB/Simulink is used to verify this work by comparing several MPPT algorithms, consisting of perturb and observe (P&O), incremental conductance (INC), and genetic algorithm (GA), with a fair comparison. It includes power extraction capability, dynamic testing performance, ripple cancellation result, and battery charging performance. The findings show that the proposed system with the HHO algorithm achieves the highest average PV power extraction at standard test condition (STC), 1993 W result in 99.53 % efficiency and better stability for dynamic testing conditions. Also, by applying this MPPT method, ripple cancellation is achieved. The proposed system results in a ripple of only 11.56 % from the 35 % inductor ripple design, smaller than adopting other algorithms. It is beneficial because a low current ripple can avoid increasing gasification, power fluctuations, battery temperature, and overheating. Moreover, the proposed system shows the best battery charging performance testing result, outperforming other algorithms.

title: Optimizing Block Size and Memory Usage for Image Processing on GPU Using CUDA
authors: Mekhriddin Rakhimov, Mannon Ochilov, Shakhzod Javliev, Azizbek Khojamurotov
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: In this article, the Canny algorithm was used to accelerate the computational processes by parallel processing in image processing. In this case, all stages of the Canny algorithm were implemented in the computer's graphics processor using Compute Unified Device Architecture technology, and in order to accelerate the computational process, the image data was optimally used from the graphics processor's global, shared memory, and texture memories. During the study, images of different sizes were tested in the global and shared memory of the graphics processor using the Compute Unified Device Architecture model to optimize the use of graphics processor memory, with block sizes of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$8 \times 8,16 \times 16$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$32 \times 32$</tex>. For comparison, the program execution was measured on the central processor using the Open Computer Vision Library. Based on the results obtained, the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 x 8}$</tex> blocks of the graphics processor were considered the most optimal for small-sized images, and the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 2} \boldsymbol{\times} \mathbf{3 2}$</tex> blocks were considered the most optimal for large-sized images. The <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$16 \times 16$</tex> block configuration showed the highest efficiency for all images. Shared memory and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 6 x 1 6}$</tex> block configuration showed <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 81$</tex> times faster performance compared to CPU on a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4096 \times 4096$</tex> image and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{\sim} \mathbf{4 9 \%}$</tex> faster performance compared to global memory.

title: Brain Tumor MRI Interpretation and Classification using Swin Transformer Framework with Multi-Method Explainable AI
authors: Ch. Rajendra Prasad, Ramu Moola
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: The accurate and interpretable classification of brain tumors in magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) is crucial for supporting clinical diagnosis and improving patient outcomes. The given paper introduces an end-to-end deep learning pipeline with the help of Swin Transformer architectures to classify tumors by their types using an upscaled brain MRI dataset. Class imbalance is managed via more advanced training algorithms and built in explainability modules with attention rollout, Grad-CAM, Integrated Gradients, SHAP, LIME, and occlusion sensitivity extend the concept of interpretability of predictions of a machine with a multi-perspective view. Experimental evidence on a community standard indicates the high classification precision, as well as detailed visual explanations, which enhance transparency of the model and clinical confidence. This paper indicates the importance of multi-channel XAI to contemporary vision transformers in medical imaging processes.

title: Command-Based Speech Recognition with LSTM for Visually Impaired Users
authors: Gabryelle Ninna Deffanya Siahaan, Annisa Fadhillah Pulungan, Rossy Nurhasanah
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Materials and Electrical Engineering (ISMEE)
abstract: Visually impaired individuals face significant barriers in accessing text-based information, which impacts their independence. To address this, this research presents the implementation of a voice command system for a mobile application designed to assist blind users in reading physical documents. A custom dataset consisting of seven specific Indonesian voice commands was collected from 53 speakers and was augmented to ensure model robustness. MelFrequency Cepstral Coefficients (MFCCs) were extracted as features from the preprocessed audio signals. The LSTM architecture was optimized through a series of experiments with different hyperparameters and layer combinations to achieve the best performance. The evaluation of the final model demonstrates high efficacy, achieving a classification accuracy of 96.6% and a macro-averaged F1-Score of 0.966. Furthermore, the system exhibited excellent real-time processing capabilities, with a Real-Time Factor (RTF) of 0.0314. The results confirm that a simple LSTM architecture is highly effective for recognizing a limited set of voice commands in the Indonesian language. This work provides a foundational component for an assistive mobile application aimed at enhancing the independence of visually impaired users, contributing to the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) by reducing inequalities.

title: Emotion Recognition in Programming Learning Contexts Using Physiological Data
authors: Myelinda A. Baldelovar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: This study examines the application of Galvanic Skin Response (GSR) as a physiological indicator for monitoring emotional states in the context of computer programming. The undergraduate students of the Computer Science program were assigned a set of structured Python-based coding exercises designed precisely to reflect distinct degrees of cognitive demand. To collect both physiological and self-reported emotional responses, the subjective data on emotional experiences of students before and after each task were gathered using a modified Geneva Emotion Wheel (GEW), while fingertip GSR sensors recorded real-time electrodermal activity. To ensure analytical consistency, the GSR readings underwent preprocessing procedures including baseline correction, low-pass filtering, z-score normalization, and artifact removal. Emotional arousal was subsequently categorized into low, moderate, or high levels based on the standardized GSR and was cross-referenced with the corresponding emotional label from the GEW. The findings of the study revealed a strong indication that task complexity positively correlated with physiological arousal. Highload programming tasks were associated with greater skin conductance and were predominantly linked to negatively influenced emotions like fear and irritation. In contrast, lower arousal, which was typically observed during exercises with less demand of cognitive load, coincided with positive emotional states like curiosity and contentment. Markedly, statistical analysis further revealed a negative correlation between GSR arousal levels and programming performance (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$r=-0.35, p=0.009$</tex>), which indicates that heightened emotional arousal may interfere with the efficiency of performing the coding exercise.

title: Robust UAV Deployment and Task Offloading Based on Road Traffic Flow in Low-Altitude Wireless Networks
authors: Weifeng Zhong, Jiahai Xiao, Xumin Huang, Jiawen Kang, Shengli Xie, Chau Yuen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: This paper studies long-term decision-making for unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) deployment and task offloading in a UAV-enabled low-altitude wireless network (LAWN), which offers computing service to moving vehicles on a road segment. A key challenge in such high-mobility scenarios is that real-time decision-making demands frequent updates, leading to considerable implementation costs. Hence, our objective is to derive steadfast decisions that remain valid throughout system operation and robust against vehicle mobility under certain traffic flow, thereby eliminating the need for repeated decision updates. We formulate a data-driven joint UAV deployment and task offloading optimization model, where incorporating richer historical vehicle data yields more robust decisions. The optimization aims to minimize deployed UAVs' energy consumption, resulting in a nonconvex mixed-integer problem. To solve the problem efficiently, we develop a tailored heuristic that integrates the alternating direction method (ADM) and analytical target cascading (ATC). Results demonstrate that the proposed heuristic can adapt UAV placement and offloading strategies to specific traffic conditions and produce decisions that are robust to on-road vehicle dynamics.

title: High-Resolution Fast Neutron Imaging using Layered CsI:Tl/Polymer Scintillators
authors: Nicholas Anastasi, Charles Sosa, Bipin Singh, Lakshmi S. Pandian, Vivek V. Nagarkar
publish: IEEE Transactions on Nuclear Science
abstract: Fast neutron radiography uniquely provides sensitivity to low-Z materials while maintaining the ability to penetrate dense structures; however, achieving high spatial resolution without sacrificing detection efficiency remains a central challenge. In this work, a layered scintillator architecture for fast neutron imaging is investigated, consisting of a 350 μm-thick vapor-deposited microcolumnar thallium-doped cesium iodide (CsI:Tl) film coupled to a hydrogen-rich polymer conversion layer. Fast neutrons undergo elastic scattering within the polymer, generating recoil protons that deposit energy in the CsI:Tl layer, enabling indirect neutron detection with reduced lateral light spread. Radiographic measurements were performed under both 14.1 MeV deuterium–tritium (D–T) and 2.5 MeV deuterium–deuterium (D–D) neutron irradiation using two readout configurations: a lens-coupled electron-multiplying charge-coupled device (EMCCD) camera and a directly coupled amorphous silicon flat panel detector. Sub-millimeter spatial resolution was demonstrated in both configurations, with contrast transfer function (CTF) values of 34.2% at 550 μm line spacing under D–T irradiation and 25.4% at 500 μm under D–D irradiation. Monte Carlo simulations based on Geant4 were used to analyze neutron–proton scattering, proton transport, and geometric detection probability as a function of neutron energy and converter thickness. Simulated detection probabilities were on the order of 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−3</sup> for optimized configurations, consistent with other layered fast neutron conversion approaches. These results demonstrate that layered CsI:Tl/polymer scintillators provide a scalable and manufacturable platform for high-resolution fast neutron imaging under both generator-based and accelerator-based irradiation conditions, with potential applications in non-destructive testing, security screening, and nuclear materials characterization.

title: Analysis of Surface Coverage Loss Caused by Sensor Misplacement in 3D Triangulation Systems
authors: Jan Maslowski, Tomáš Kot, Jakub Chlebek, Zdenko Bobovský
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper presents a quantitative analysis of the impact of inaccurate positioning (deviations in position and orientation) of a 3D triangulation on surface coverage. Using simulated scanning of 50 heterogeneous models, the ideal sensor position maximizing surface coverage was first identified for each object. Subsequently, the influence of spatial deviation and angular inaccuracy was systematically examined in this optimal configuration. The results show high sensitivity of the system, where even a small deviation in position (25 mm) leads to an average loss of coverage of 7.99 %, and in the case of geometrically complex objects with a high degree of self-occlusion, the degradation of data is statistically significantly higher, which was confirmed by statistical analysis using paired t-tests. Orientation analysis revealed that tilts around axes perpendicular to the viewing direction have a significantly more destructive impact than rotation around the viewing axis. The simulation results were validated by real measurements using a triangulation-based 3D scanner mounted on a robotic workstation, showing good agreement between simulated and real data. The proposed methodology and the obtained data provide a quantitative framework for defining scanner positioning tolerances in real-world industrial applications and for the design of robotic inspection cells.

title: Gaze Authentication: Factors Influencing Authentication Performance
authors: Dillon J. Lohr, Michael J. Proulx, Mehedi Hasan Raju, Oleg V. Komogortsev
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper examines the key factors that influence the performance of state-of-the-art gaze-based authentication. Experiments were conducted on a large-scale, in-house dataset comprising 8,849 subjects collected with Meta Quest Pro equivalent hardware running a video oculography-driven gaze estimation pipeline at 72 Hz. State of the neural network architecture was employed to study the influence of the following factors on authentication performance: eye tracking signal quality, various aspects of eye tracking calibration, and simple filtering on estimated raw gaze. This report provides performance results and their analysis.

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cybernetics

title: Adaptive MPC-based Path Following Control for Autonomous Vehicles with Random Time Delays
authors: Chao Yang, Ruixin Zhang, Weida Wang, Yuhang Zhang, Qi Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: Autonomous vehicles are further improved with the development of in-vehicle networks and X-by-wire technologies. Due to the increasing integration of vehicle systems and the growing complexity of control links, time delays in information transmission and execution have become increasingly prominent. Because of late responses to control inputs, autonomous vehicles have deterioration in terms of path following accuracy and control stability, which poses a threat to driving safety. To solve this problem, an adaptive model predictive path following control strategy is presented in this paper. Firstly, a real-time delay estimator is designed based on historical feedback information processing and the gradient descent algorithm. Secondly, based on the estimated delay, a delay-augmented model with variable dimension is proposed for adaptive control to mitigate the impact of time-varying delay. Meanwhile, a tube-based model predictive control method is modified for the proposed model to deal with uncertainties generated by time-varying delays. Finally, the proposed control strategy is verified by simulations and experiments in multiple cases. The results show that the proposed strategy achieved a smaller lateral offset than that in the ordinary model predictive control method, leading to a 52.86% improvement in accuracy under the existence of time-varying delays.

title: Design and Control of an Electromechanical Human-Robotic Manipulator’s Interface for Upper-Limb Rehabilitation
authors: André Gonçalves, Hélio Mendonça, Manuel F. Silva, Cláudia D. Rocha
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Stroke affects over 100 million people worldwide, and over two-thirds of survivors experience lasting upper-limb impairments, which significantly impact their quality of life. The global shortage of rehabilitation providers, which cannot attend to all patients who need it, creates an urgent, not yet answered, need for reliable and accessible rehabilitation innovations. Robotic rehabilitation has been emerging as an effective alternative to traditional physical therapy. This paper presents the development and evaluation of 2 degree-of-freedom exoskeleton, coupled to a collaborative robotic manipulator, which performs upperlimb rehabilitation. The system targets elbow flexion/extension and forearm pronation/supination, using two direct current brushless actuators. To accommodate a wide range of users, the mechanical design is modular and adjustable, allowing the rehabilitation of a broad range of arm lengths, while mechanical barriers prevent unsafe joint motions. Furthermore, limit switches ensure the movements are performed within safe values and an emergency button is also available for emergency stop. Safety assessment confirmed the actuators’ performance and the integrity of the physical barriers. Three different rehabilitation modes were implemented: passive assist, active assist and active resist. Passive assistance tests achieved consistent trajectory tracking with a root mean square error of 4.85º for pronation/supination and 0.87º for elbow flexion/extension, while maintaining smooth motion profiles with spectral arc length values of -1.603 and -1.56, respectively. Active resistance generated stable bidirectional torque across the full range of motion, reaching up to 1 Nm for forearm pronation/supination and 7 Nm for elbow flexion/extension. The adaptive active assistance strategy modified the assistance torque in real time according to the detected user performance. These findings establish a foundation for future clinical evaluation and real-world applications, with the system’s modular design and multiple therapy modes showing potential to support diverse rehabilitation needs.

title: A Data-Driven Optimal Control Architecture for Grid-Connected Power Converters
authors: Ruohan Leng, Linbin Huang, Huanhai Xin, Ping Ju, Xiongfei Wang, Eduardo Prieto-Araujo, Florian Dörfler
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Systems
abstract: Grid-connected power converters are ubiquitous in modern power systems, acting as grid interfaces of renewable energy sources, energy storage systems, electric vehicles, high-voltage DC systems, etc. Conventionally, power converters use multiple PID regulators to achieve different control objectives such as grid synchronization and voltage/power regulation, where the PID parameters are usually tuned based on a presumed (and often overly-simplified) power grid model. However, this may lead to inferior performance or even instabilities in practice, as the real power grid is highly complex, variable, and generally unknown. To tackle this problem, we employ a data-enabled predictive control (DeePC) to perform data-driven, optimal, robust, and adaptive control for power converters. We call the converters that are operated in this way DeePConverters. A DeePConverter can implicitly perceive the characteristics of the power grid from measured data and adjust its control strategy to achieve optimal, robust, and adaptive performance. We present the modular configurations, generalized structure, control behavior specification, inherent robustness, detailed implementation, computational aspects, and online adaptation of DeePConverters. High-fidelity simulations and hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) tests are provided to validate the effectiveness of DeePConverters.

title: A 129FPS Full HD Real-Time Accelerator for 3D Gaussian Splatting
authors: Fang-Chi Chang, Tian-Sheuan Chang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Rendering large-scale, unbounded scenes on AR/VR-class devices is constrained by the computation, bandwidth, and storage cost of 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS). We propose a low-power, low-cost 3DGS hardware accelerator that renders full-HD images in real time, together with a hardware-friendly compression pipeline that combines iterative Gaussian pruning and fine-tuning, progressive spherical harmonics (SH) degree reduction, and vector quantization of all SH coefficients and colors. The scheme achieves a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$51.6\times$</tex-math></inline-formula> model-size reduction with a 0.743 dB PSNR loss. The accelerator uses a frame-level pipeline that integrates point-based culling and projection with tile-based sorting and rasterization, skips zero-Jacobian matrix multiplications (reducing processing elements by 63% and computation by 53%), and adopts comparison-free tile-based sorting with deterministic latency. Implemented in a TSMC 28-nm process at 800 MHz, the design occupies <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$0.66~\rm {mm}^{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula> with 1.1438 M gates and 120 kB SRAM, consumes 0.219 W, and delivers 1219 Mpixels/J at 267.5 Mpixels/s, enabling 1080p at 129 FPS. Overall, it is <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$5.98\times$</tex-math></inline-formula> smaller in area, <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$5.94\times$</tex-math></inline-formula> higher throughput, and delivers <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$7.5\times$</tex-math></inline-formula> higher energy efficiency than prior 3DGS accelerators.

title: Combining Lightweight and Post-Quantum Cryptography with Physically Unclonable Functions for Secure In-Vehicle Communications
authors: Arcangelo Castiglione, Teresa Elia, Christian Esposito, Franco Cirillo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Consumer Electronics
abstract: Modern vehicles are complex cyber-physical systems that rely on In-Vehicle networks to coordinate the operation of multiple Electronic Control Units (ECUs). Among the various communication protocols used in this domain, the Controller Area Network (CAN) remains the most widely adopted due to its reliability and performance. However, it is inherently insecure, making vehicles increasingly vulnerable to cyber threats, especially in the context of growing connectivity and emerging post-quantum risks. This paper proposes a protocol to enhance the security of CAN networks, combining Physically Unclonable Functions (PUFs) for efficient ECU authentication, Lightweight AEAD (Authenticated Encryption with Associated Data), and Post-Quantum Cryptography for the confidentiality of communications. The protocol stands out for its low computational overhead, requiring only 2n messages to authenticate all n nodes in the network, and for its compatibility with the standard 8-byte CAN frame. This aspect makes our protocol a powerful, robust, high-performance, and scalable security solution that can be integrated into current and next-generation in-vehicle networks, making it suitable for the safety-critical automotive context.

title: RSMA-Enhanced Secure Transmission Scheme for Active STAR-RIS-Aided Networks
authors: Xinying Guo, Rui Yuan, Jiankang Zhang, Yi Song, Sheng Chen
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications Letters
abstract: In this letter, a secure transmission scheme is proposed for active simultaneously transmitting and reflecting reconfigurable intelligent surface (STAR-RIS)-aided networks based on rate-splitting multiple access (RSMA). Specifically, we formulate a joint optimization problem which maximizes the minimum secrecy rate by simultaneously designing the base station beamforming vectors and the active STAR-RIS coefficient matrices. To tackle this challenging non-convexity joint optimization, we design an efficient alternating optimization algorithm, which leverages the weighted minimum mean square error method to reformulate legitimate user rates and applies successive convex approximation to handle eavesdropper rates. Simulation study shows that our proposed active STAR-RIS achieves superior security performance compared to its passive counterpart, while our RSMA strategy provides higher secrecy rates than both space-division multiple access and non-orthogonal multiple access schemes under the considered system setting.

title: Disentangled Generative Graph Representation Learning
authors: Xinyue Hu, Zhibin Duan, Xinyang Liu, Yuxin Li, Bo Chen, Chaojie Wang, Yilin He, Hongwei Liu, Xuefei Cao, Mingyuan Zhou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems
abstract: Generative graph models have recently emerged as a powerful paradigm in self-supervised learning (SSL) for graph representation tasks, demonstrating remarkable potential in capturing complex structural and feature information. Despite their promise, most existing generative graph representation learning (GRL) methods treat the entire graph as a monolithic entity, employing random masking strategies across the entire structure. This approach often neglects the intricate interplay between different substructures of the graph, leading to entangled representations. Such entanglement can reduce the robustness of learned models and limit their ability to produce reliable outputs. To address these limitations, this article presents disentangled generative graph representation learning (DiGGR), a novel SSL framework designed to disentangle latent factors in graph representations. DiGGR introduces a disentanglement-driven approach to guide graph mask modeling, enabling the framework to learn distinct latent factors that better capture the underlying structure of graphs. By incorporating these latent factors into an end-to-end joint learning process, DiGGR enhances the clarity and structural alignment of the learned representations, while also improving their robustness and generalization across tasks. The effectiveness of DiGGR is validated through extensive experiments on 15 public datasets spanning three distinct graph learning tasks. The results consistently demonstrate that DiGGR outperforms a wide range of previous self-supervised methods, highlighting its superior ability to learn meaningful, disentangled representations. These findings underscore the potential of DiGGR to advance the state of the art in generative GRL research.

title: IEEE Computer Society Call for Articles
authors: N/A
publish: Computing in Science & Engineering

title: Dynamic Uncertainty-aware Multimodal Fusion for Outdoor Health Monitoring
authors: Zihan Fang, Zheng Lin, Senkang Hu, Yihang Tao, Yiqin Deng, Xianhao Chen, Yuguang Fang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing
abstract: Outdoor health monitoring is essential to detect early abnormal health status for safeguarding human health and safety. Conventional outdoor monitoring relies on static multimodal deep learning frameworks, which requires extensive data training from scratch and fails to capture subtle health status changes. Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) emerge as a promising alternative, utilizing only small datasets to fine-tune pre-trained information-rich models for enabling powerful health status monitoring. Unfortunately, MLLM-based outdoor health monitoring also faces significant challenges: i) sensor data contains input noise stemming from sensor data acquisition and fluctuation noise caused by sudden changes in physiological signals due to dynamic outdoor environments, thus degrading training performance; ii) current transformer-based MLLMs struggle to achieve robust multimodal fusion, as they lack a design of fusing noisy modalities; iii) modalities with varying noise levels hinder accurate recovery of missing data from fluctuating distributions. To combat these challenges, we propose an uncertainty-aware multimodal fusion framework, named DUAL-Health, for outdoor health monitoring in dynamic and noisy environments. First, to assess the impact of noise, we accurately quantify modality uncertainty caused by input and fluctuation noise with current and temporal features. Second, to empower efficient muitimodal fusion with low-quality modalities, we customize the fusion weight for each modality based on quantified and calibrated uncertainty. Third, to enhance data recovery from fluctuating noisy modalities, we align modality distributions within a common semantic space. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our DUAL-Health outperforms state-ofthe- art baselines in detection accuracy and robustness.

title: Environmentally Responsive Ag/BaTiO/TiO Nanocomposite for Selective Gas Sensing and Advanced Piezo-Photocatalysis_supp1-3678665.docx
authors: Deepshikha Rathore
publish: N/A
abstract: This article reports the environmental sensitivity including piezophotocatalytic activity and gas sensitivity of the Ag/BaTiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>/TiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> nanocomposite toward ethanol, toluene, and isopropanol. The structural properties and crystalline morphology of Ag/BaTiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>/TiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> nanocomposite were determined by analysing peak positions of XRD pattern, high-resolution TEM images including 2D faceted, alongside Moiré fringes, and SAED patterns. In gas sensing trials, it showcased n-type semiconductor behaviour, with rapid response times of 3-5 s and recovery times of 3-7 s toward each test gas, showing enhanced sensitivity with increasing flow rate and temperature with best response for ethanol. Further, 70% degradation of ARS dye was achieved in photocatalytic activity, which was enhanced to 90% via piezo-photocatalytic activity with Ag/BaTiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>/TiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> nanocomposite as catalyst. Consequently, these results present a promising new avenue for the development of advanced, efficient gas sensors and catalysts.

title: Energy-Balanced Routing for Hybrid Optical-Wireless Networks Using Enhanced Ant Colony Optimization
authors: Jin Ren, Jingwen Zou, Weiting Zhang, Xi Han
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Hybrid Optical-Wireless Sensor Networks (HOWSN) combine wireless flexibility with optical high-speed communication, yet face energy management challenges in resource-limited settings. A critical issue is the "hot node" problem, where certain nodes deplete energy rapidly due to uneven traffic load, severely curtailing network lifetime. This paper proposes an energy-efficient routing optimization scheme using an improved ant colony algorithm that incorporates three key mechanisms: an energy-aware path selection factor, a dynamic communication mode switching mechanism, and an adaptive parameter adjustment strategy. These mechanisms work collectively to balance energy consumption across the network. Simulations in a 100-node network show the proposed algorithm reduces energy consumption variance to 0.024531 (a 47.6% improvement over traditional methods), thereby improving energy distribution uniformity and extending network lifetime by approximately 40%. The approach demonstrates strong potential for applications in military, environmental, and urban monitoring systems.

title: Air-Friction Loss Characteristics and Thermal Analysis of MW-Class Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor with Active Magnetic Bearings
authors: Wei Li, Changsheng Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Transportation Electrification
abstract: For MW-class high-speed machines, rotor airfriction loss constitutes one of the primary components of total loss. The complex air gap structure inherent in the non-contact support provided by active magnetic bearings (AMBs) makes investigating these air-friction loss characteristics particularly critical. Utilizing high-fidelity computational fluid dynamics (CFD) simulation, the distribution of air-friction losses across the rotor surface was obtained by a full-scale air domain model. Further analysis was conducted to elucidate the characteristics and patterns of these losses. Based on fine flow field results by CFD combined with empirical formulas, accurate convective heat transfer coefficients were derived. An enhanced equivalent air gap thermal model based on CFD inputs was established. Based on the finite element model, this enabled the rapid calculation of rotor temperatures. Finally, MW-class motor drag and temperature rise experiments were performed in a 1 MW, 15000 r/min permanent magnet synchronous motor (PMSM) with AMBs. The experimental results were compared and validated against the simulation results. This research provides a significant reference for rotor air-friction loss analysis and rapid prediction of rotor temperature.

title: ADiP: Adaptive-Precision Systolic Array for Matrix Multiplication Acceleration
authors: Ahmed J. Abdelmaksoud, Cristian Sestito, Shiwei Wang, Themis Prodromakis
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Solid-State Circuits Society
abstract: Transformers are at the core of modern AI nowadays. They rely heavily on matrix multiplication and require efficient acceleration due to their substantial memory and computational requirements. Quantization plays a vital role in reducing memory usage, and can be exploited for computations by designing reconfigurable architectures that enhance matrix multiplication by dynamically adjusting the precision. This paper proposes ADiP, a novel adaptive-precision systolic array architecture designed for efficient matrix multiplication acceleration. The proposed architecture consists of N × N reconfigurable processing elements (PEs), along with shared shifters and accumulators. ADiP supports multiple computation modes, including symmetric single-matrix multiplication as well as asymmetric multi-matrix multiplication with a shared input matrix, thereby improving data-reuse and PE utilization. By adapting to different precisions, ADiP achieves up to 4× higher throughput and up to 4× higher memory efficiency. Analytical models are developed for ADiP architecture, including latency and throughput for different architecture configurations. A comprehensive hardware design space exploration is demonstrated using commercial 22nm technology. Furthermore, ADiP is evaluated on different Transformer-based workloads from GPT-2 medium, BERT large, and BitNet-1.58B models, delivering total latency improvement up to 53.6%, and total energy improvement up to 24.4% for attention workloads in BitNet-1.58B model. At a 64×64 size with 4,096 reconfigurable PEs, ADiP achieves a peak throughput of 8.192 TOPS, 16.384 TOPS, and 32.768 TOPS for 8bit×8bit, 8bit×4bit, and 8bit×2bit operations, respectively.

title: SSPF-Net: Learning Organ Shape-aware and Self-calibration Prototypes for Few-shot Medical Image Segmentation
authors: Shihao Zheng, Huisi Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: Few-shot medical image segmentation is a promising solution for scarce data scenarios, especially for medical classes with limited labeled data. However, there are still several flaws in the current few-shot medical image segmentation methods. The diversity of the base classes in the training stage is insufficient, so it is still difficult to train a model with strong generalization through a large number of images and classes like natural images. Besides, due to the discrepancy between the query and support images, relying solely on representative prototypes from support features fails to handle intra-class variability. In this paper, we propose a novel network with an organ shape-aware module (OSA) to optimize the prototype-based network and enhance the model’s generalization ability. Specifically, the OSA is designed to obtain a variable number of prototypes that contain the shape and edge information of the organ. Compared with general models using fixed-shape prototypes, the prototypes obtained by our OSA are more precise and informative. In addition, we propose a new inferring prototype self-calibration (IPSC) module with a mechanism to mine the query potential information. Such a scheme can extract foreground and background inferring prototypes from query features to enhance support prototypes, reduce intra-class differences, and eliminate disturbing information around the target class. Furthermore, the self-calibration operation in IPSC can select reliable foreground inferring vectors to generate foreground inferring prototypes. Extensive experiments on two public datasets demonstrate the superiority of our method over state-of-the-art methods.

title: Hybrid Machine Learning & Time-Series Approaches for Hourly GHI Forecasting—Dehradun (NSRDB 2000-2014)
authors: Aniket Kumar Rawat, Rakhi Bhammarkar, Mamta Dahiya, Manish Kumar Lakhiwal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: Accurate short-term Estimation of solar radiation levels (especially Global Horizontal Irradiance, GHI) is critical for grid integration of solar power. This study uses a 15-year NSRDB dataset (2000-2014) for Dehradun (India) to develop and compare several forecasting models. After creating a continuous datetime index and extracting temporal features (hour, day, month, weekday) and a one-hour lag of GHI, we trained various ML models like Random Forest (RF), feedforward neural network (MLP) and deep learning models like LSTM, CNN+LSTM hybrid alongside a classical ARIMA time-series model. Assessment of performance was done using MAE, RMSE, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^2$</tex> on a hold-out test set. Results show that the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R F$</tex> model achieved the lowest error (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$M A E \approx 9.05, R^2 \approx 0.994$</tex>), far outperforming ARIMA (MAE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 231.6$</tex>, RMSE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 262.6, R^2 \approx 0.480$</tex>). The LSTM and CNN-LSTM hybrids also gave low RMSE (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 54.5$</tex> and 56.3, respectively) with strong accuracy. These findings demonstrate the advantage of nonlinear ML approaches (especially ensemble and deep learning) for GHI forecasting in this region. A performance comparison table is provided. This study's unique contribution is the integration of temporal feature engineering with hybrid models, specifically optimized for the Dehradun site, leading to improved performance over previous studies. Future research will investigate a broader range of weather inputs and enhanced hybrid ensemble techniques.

title: Wavelet-Alienation Based Diagnosis of Broken Rotor Bar Faults in Induction Motor
authors: Ravi Soni, Nandkishor Gupta, Bhuvnesh Rathore
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: This paper presents an intelligent and robust protection scheme for the fault detection and diagnosis of broken rotor bar faults in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3-\phi$</tex> squirrel cage induction motors (SCIM). The proposed method employs Wavelet Transform for decomposing the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3-\phi$</tex> current signals in the stator and extract the approximate coefficients that capture essential fault-related features. Subsequently, alienation coefficients are computed from correlation analysis among the phase components, serving as reliable fault indices. Fault conditions are identified by comparing these indices against a predefined threshold. Experimental validation demonstrates that the proposed algorithm offers high accuracy, computational efficiency and robustness in identifying rotor bar faults under diverse operating conditions.

title: Conference Proceedings
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)

title: Wireless Integration of Fiber Optic Vibration Sensor
authors: Maxim S. Akatov, Aleksander L. Tuv, Dmitry I. Godyno, Arseny D. Beglov, Alina A. Opekunova
publish: 2025 International Conference on Quality Management, Transport and Information Security, Information Technologies (QM&amp;TIS&amp;IT)
abstract: The article discusses approaches to integrating Internet of Things (IoT) technologies into a fiber-optic vibration sensor (FOVS). The paper examines modern technologies for wireless data transmission that allow expanding the possibilities of using such a sensor. The benefits of wireless connectivity are analyzed, including efficient, flexible and scalable monitoring, which improves production processes and increases the reliability of equipment operation. The paper discusses the connection scheme, the algorithm for processing the information received from the sensor, as well as the protocols for transmitting and processing data.

title: Robust VI Controller for a 80-kW Three-Level Active Front End Supporting Bidirectional EV Charging and V2G Integration
authors: Nicola Toscani, Marco Mauri, Francesco Castelli-Dezza
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Driving and Smart Transportation (IDST)
abstract: Power management algorithms necessary for voltage and frequency regulations and coordination logic among power converters are currently hot research topics, being fundamental for AC grid stability. However, inner controllers driving voltages and currents are essential for safe and reliable interfaces between AC and DC systems, since they actuate effective P, Q trackings. Indeed, converters may suffer from stability issues even at unit level, being them inherently nonlinear switching devices and connected to power grids with time-varying configurations. Due to deep penetration of power electronics, renewable sources and storage systems interfaced into AC grids, converters need often to work bidirectionally, further increasing challenges for stability. Thus, this paper shows robust control syntheses for voltage and current loops, trying to bridge the gaps and clarify open points left by the existing literature, as well as comparing the behaviors of tuned control schemes.

title: Optimization of Bidirectional Operating Performance of Micro Pumped Storage Units Based on Genetic Algorithm
authors: Yan Li, Yaju Zuo, Yan Chen, Jianlin Zhao, Di Zhu, Gaoming Qin, Wensheng Ma, Ran Tao
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: The bidirectional high-efficiency and stable operation of micro pumped storage units has, to some extent, realized the energy storage and utilization of agricultural machine wells, fulfilling functions such as farmland irrigation and surplus energy recovery. Therefore, this paper presents a multi-objective optimization design system suitable for micro pumped storage units. Taking the blade inlet/outlet angles and wrap angles as optimization variables, parameterization is conducted. The optimization objectives include the unit efficiency at the rated operating point of the pump, the unit efficiency at the rated operating point of the turbine, and the cavitation performance of the pump. By applying the multi-objective genetic algorithm to the parameterized unit, an optimized runner is obtained. Results show that the weighted efficiency of the optimized unit is increased by 2.05 %; under the pump operating condition, the streamline distribution at the impeller inlet is improved, and the inlet pressure is increased, indicating a significant optimization effect. This study provides certain reference value for addressing issues such as energy conservation, emission reduction, and cascaded utilization of energy in agricultural fields like agricultural machine wells.

title: Ethical Challenges of Applying Artificial Intelligence in Education: Issues in Developing Standards
authors: Victor V. Timchenko, Petr V. Matveyev, Olga A. Yeliseeva
publish: 2025 International Conference on Quality Management, Transport and Information Security, Information Technologies (QM&amp;TIS&amp;IT)
abstract: The integration of artificial intelligence (AI) in education has the potential to transform teaching and learning processes, enhancing personalization, efficiency, and access to knowledge. However, its widespread adoption raises significant ethical challenges that demand careful consideration. This article explores the key ethical issues related to the use of AI in education, including transparency, bias, data privacy, accountability, and the potential for exacerbating existing inequities in educational systems. It also examines the ongoing difficulty of developing comprehensive ethical standards that balance innovation with social responsibility. By analyzing current frameworks and the gaps they present, the article underscores the need for a shared ethical approach to guide AI implementation in education, ensuring that technological advancements prioritize equity, fairness, and the well-being of learners.

title: Integration of Smart Agriculture and Energy for Empowering Rural India
authors: Pranjal Jog, Suwarna Shete, R.K. Kumawat, Ritu Tak, Pravin Sonwane, Gaurav Jain
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: This paper explores the transformative potential of integrating smart agriculture and renewable energy to empower rural India. The agricultural sector faces critical challenges, including low productivity and environmental degradation, while rural areas suffer from energy poverty. This paper argues that a symbiotic relationship, where smart farming technologies are powered by decentralized renewable energy systems, can create a sustainable and economically viable ecosystem. We detail key technologies, analyze socio-economic impacts, and propose a roadmap for large-scale adoption, contributing to rural revitalization and national food security.

title: Federated Meta-Learning for Lifelong Adaptation in Smart Building Portfolios: Learning to Learn without Forgetting
authors: Adam Bouras, Ali Benbrahim, Mohamed Wassim Fekih
publish: 2025 IEEE Sustainable Smart Lighting World Conference &amp; Expo (LS25)
abstract: Smart building control systems face a critical limitation: their static intelligence degrades as building usage evolves, requiring costly manual recommissioning. This paper presents a novel framework combining federated learning with metalearning to enable building portfolios to collaboratively develop adaptive intelligence while preserving occupant privacy. Our architecture allows buildings to share learned adaptation strategies, not raw sensor data, enabling rapid response to concept drift through learning-to-learn mechanisms. Each building maintains a local multi-agent system with dual-memory continual learning, while participating in privacy-preserving federated meta-learning across a building portfolio. Through simulation studies on a 20-building portfolio expe-riencing diverse drift scenarios, we demonstrate that federated meta-learning achieves 87% faster adaptation to new patterns (2.3 days vs. 18 days to reach <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 0 \%}$</tex> optimal performance), maintains 89% knowledge retention across task transitions (vs. 34% for standard retraining), and reduces commissioning time for new buildings from 8-12 weeks to 3-5 days. The system achieves 31% energy savings compared to static control while maintaining <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\epsilon=2.4; \delta=10^{-5})$</tex> differential privacy guarantees

title: Research on the Application of Interval Bidding Mechanism in Market Power Risk Prevention
authors: Yuhan Zhang, Yanjun Jiang, Fangbo He, Yuan Pei, Feihu Hu, Xinzhuang Diao
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: As the generation side of China's electricity market is usually highly concentrated, abuse of market power may occur in the centralized bidding market. Meanwhile, in order to adapt to the needs of market users, the relevant mechanism of interval declaration is proposed and studied. In this paper, various types of market indicators are screened, and a market power risk evaluation system based on hierarchical analysis and gray relation coefficient is constructed, which can effectively identify individuals with high market power risk. Subsequently, this paper investigates the performance of different modes of interval bidding in the market and the impact on market power based on experiments. The result shows that high-low matching with interval generally mitigates market power abuse, while the interval volume prioritization mode significantly facilitates trading while not promoting market power explicitly.

title: Analysis of Flight Fare Data and Fare Prediction Using Machine Learning
authors: Rama Kant, Ankit Goyal, Sanjiv Kumar Singh, Sanjiv Agrawal, Richa Mishra
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: Competitive market dynamics and growing consumer expectations in the aviation sector have recently led the spike in demand for effective pricing methods. Both airlines and passengers now depend a lot on the ability to analyze the previous flight rent data and make accurate predictions about future fare. Many variables like the length of the flight, the number of days up to the departure, the arrival and departure, etc., affect the cost of an airline ticket. When they need to develop their market and when it is more difficult to get tickets, airline companies can reduce the price. They could increase the price to maximum. Many things can affect pricing. To determine the value depending on each component, separate -owned ownership procedures and algorithms are used. It is now possible to reduce these principles and follow the thanks to recent developments in Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML). In this study, we used absolute error, Route Meen Clored Err, Meaning of Route Meen Clored Err, Meaning of Nirate Percentage Error, Meaning Filter, Route Meen Squad Log Err and R2 score.

title: Hierarchical Active Safety Control Strategy for Steering Collision Avoidance on High-Curvature Roads
authors: Chengcheng Yang, Shunan Liang
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: This paper addresses the challenges of inaccurate risk assessment and delayed trajectory tracking in steering collision avoidance maneuvers for intelligent vehicles on high-curvature roads. To tackle these problems, an active safety strategy integrating trajectory prediction and composite control is proposed. Firstly, the ego vehicle's trajectory is accurately predicted using the Unscented Kalman Filter (UKF) combined with the Constant Turn Rate and Acceleration (CTRA) model, and a robust safe distance model is constructed using a projection method. Secondly, a dynamic quintic polynomial planner is designed to adaptively generate low-intensity collision avoidance trajectories by incorporating the Separating Axis Theorem (SAT). Finally, a Feedforward-Predictive-PID Linear Quadratic Regulator (FF-Pred-PID LQR) composite controller is introduced, which integrates curvature feedforward, state prediction, and heading angle PID compensation to achieve precise tracking across the full speed range. Simulations demonstrate that this strategy reduces the maximum position error by 68.34% and the heading angle error by 71.87% in a 300m radius curve scenario, significantly enhancing decision accuracy and control response speed.

title: Real-Time Indian Sign Language Recognition Using Mediapipe Holistic and Lstm
authors: Samiksha Bijwe, Ketaki Chalke, Srushti Bhirud, Paras Kadam, Atharva Karle, Rohan Suryavanshi
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: Indian Sign Language (ISL) is the primary means of communication for more than 2.78 million hearing-impaired individuals in India, yet widespread communication barriers remain because less than 1% of the general population is ISL-literate. Existing recognition systems often rely on costly external hardware or fail to operate in real-time on mobile devices, limiting their accessibility. To address this gap, we present MudraAI, a mobile-based real-time ISL recognition system that integrates MediaPipe Holistic for landmark extraction with a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network for temporal gesture classification. The system recognizes single-handed ISL gestures using only a smartphone camera, eliminating the need for gloves or depth sensors, and provides gesture-to-text and text-to-speech conversion. Experimental evaluation on a dataset of 12 common ISL gestures achieved 96.8% accuracy at 30 FPS on standard Android devices. Furthermore, a user study with 25 deaf-mute participants reported a 94.2% communication success rate and high satisfaction scores, validating the system's usability in real-world scenarios. By combining accuracy, portability, and real-time performance, MudraAI demonstrates significant potential as a practical assistive technology and establishes a foundation for future work on larger vocabularies, two-handed gestures, and NLP-based sentence-level translation.

title: Smart Lighting and 5G Connectivity: Toward a Unified Infrastructure for Smart Campus Applications
authors: Rahim Kacimi, Laurent Canale, François Thiébolt, Yves Le Henaff, Georges Zissis
publish: 2025 IEEE Sustainable Smart Lighting World Conference &amp; Expo (LS25)
abstract: Lighting infrastructures are evolving from static systems to dynamic, connected ecosystems that host sensing, communication, and computing functions. This paper presents the design and deployment of a 5G Standalone (SA) private network integrated with a smart lighting infrastructure on the Toulouse University campus. The objective is to build a multi-purpose, energy-efficient, and secure platform enabling experimentation in smart mobility, environmental monitoring, and digital twin applications. Smart lamp posts act as nodes combining LED lighting, IoT sensors, 5G and C-V2X radio units, and edge computing. Preliminary results demonstrate the feasibility of using lighting networks as communication and sensing backbones for advanced campus services, providing lowlatency and high-bandwidth connectivity to support AI-based control and real-time data collection.

title: Copyright Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 IEEE Sustainable Smart Lighting World Conference &amp; Expo (LS25)

title: Equivalent Circuits as Limitless Solution for OLED Modeling and Simulation
authors: Rimeh Sassi, Ghada Mbarki, Slimane Oussalah, Houssein El Housseiny, Cedric Renaud, David Buso, Skandar Douss, Mohamed Khalfaoui
publish: 2025 IEEE Sustainable Smart Lighting World Conference &amp; Expo (LS25)
abstract: Compact electrical models are essential for OLED driver design, as physical fabrication is costly and TCAD simulations (e.g., SILVACO ATLAS) are computationally intensive. Buso's equivalent circuit provides a simple framework but assumes fixed capacitance and uses an exponential I-V law inadequate for multilayer OLEDs. This paper develops an improved LTSpice model for a specific multilayer structure (ITO/NPD/Alq3/BCP/Ca), calibrated against ATLAS reference data. Key enhancements include: a behavioral current source <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(I(V)=V / a+b \exp (c * V))$</tex> fitted to I-V characteristics, and voltage-dependent charge <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\mathrm{Q}(\mathrm{V})=\mathrm{C}_{0} \sum \mathrm{a}_{\mathrm{n}} \text{Vn}\right)(0-4 \mathrm{V}$</tex> range) yielding <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathrm{C}(\mathrm{V})=\text{dQ} / \text{dV})$</tex>. Results demonstrate superior performance: I-V error reduced to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<5 \%$</tex> vs. 15% for Buso model, with accurate reproduction of capacitance peak <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\sim 5.5 \mathrm{V})$</tex> and drop. This enhanced model facilitates rapid system-level simulation of OLED drivers, with potential extensions to thermal/aging effects.

title: PotatoGANs: Utilizing Generative Adversarial Networks, Instance Segmentation, and Explainable AI for Enhanced Potato Disease Identification and Classification
authors: Fatema Tuj Johora Faria, Mukaffi Bin Moin, Mohammad Shafiul Alam, Ahmed Al Wase, Md. Rabius Sani, Khan Md Hasib
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology and Engineering (i-COSTE)
abstract: Numerous applications have resulted from the automation of agricultural disease segmentation using deep learning techniques. However, when applied to new conditions, these applications frequently face the difficulty of overfitting, resulting in lower segmentation performance. Traditional data augmentation approaches, such as rotation, flip, and translation, have limitations and frequently fail to provide strong generalization results. To address these issues, our research employs a novel approach termed as PotatoGANs. In this hybrid data augmentation approach, two types of Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) are utilized to generate synthetic potato disease images from healthy potato images. This approach not only expands the dataset but also adds variety, which helps to enhance model generalization. The CycleGAN model outperforms the Pix2Pix GAN model in terms of image quality, as evidenced by its higher IS scores CycleGAN achieves higher Inception scores (IS) of 1.2001 and 1.0900 for black scurf and common scab, respectively.
