To address these challenges, we propose the Supply Chain Twin Security Framework (SCTSF), which integrates Digital Twin Modeling to create virtual replicas of supply chain processes with the Isolation Forest Algorithm for advanced anomaly detection. This framework enhances system transparency, contextual monitoring, and predictive risk management. The proposed SCTSF framework is applied for detecting disruptions, fraud, and cyber anomalies across logistics, manufacturing, and distribution nodes, enabling proactive decision-making and rapid response. Findings demonstrate that SCTSF significantly improves detection accuracy, reduces false positives, and enhances resilience by providing real-time insights into vulnerabilities and threats. Thus, it establishes a reliable, adaptive, and intelligent approach for securing Industry 4.0 supply chains. The proposed method reduces response time by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 1 \%}$</tex>, enhances resilience by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 5 \%}$</tex>, and improves scalability by 89.2%.

title: BotonyBuddy: AI-Enabled Precision Plant Nursery Management for Multicrop Disease Classification and Phenology-Aware Age Estimation
authors: Pandula Pallewatta, Samantha Mathara Arachchi, Kasun Karunanayaka, Trapp Kayuni, Tinmei Aleksandr, Aditi Bhattarai, Thilina Halloluwa, Kelvin A Nicodemas, Williams Kyei
publish: 3rd 2025 IEEE International Conference on Agrosystem Engineering, Technology &amp; Applications (AGRETA)
abstract: Plant nurseries face a critical challenge, as manual inspection of plant health and growth is labour-intensive, inconsistent, and unable to scale with rising demand. Early disease detection and accurate age estimation are crucial for nursery quality assurance; however, traditional methods remain subjective and prone to error. To address this gap, this paper presents BotonyBuddy. Smart plant nurseries can automatically benefit from AI to monitor plant health and growth. This AI-driven smart nursery management system unifies disease diagnosis and age prediction for four tropical crops (Mango, Bell Pepper, Durian, and Anthurium) using real nursery and greenhouse imagery. The system leverages deep learning (DL) models (VGG19, ResNet50, EfficientNet-B7, and DeiT Vision Transformer) for leaf disease classification on <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 7,500$</tex> images and integrates a vision-based age estimation pipeline trained on <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 1,200$</tex> labelled samples. Two modelling strategies are explored: (i) Direct age regression/classification with Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and transfer learning, and (ii) an object-detection approach (YOLOv8) to count leaves and branches as inputs to an age prediction model. EfficientNet-B7 achieved the highest disease classification accuracies, 95.08% (Bell Pepper), 95.94% (Durian), 94.36% (Mango), and 92.02% (Anthurium), with DeiT close behind <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{(} \boldsymbol{\sim} \mathbf{8 9. 8} \boldsymbol{-} \mathbf{9 3. 7 \%} \boldsymbol{)}$</tex>, while ResNet50/VGG19 performed between <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 85-92 {\%}$</tex>. For age estimation, the hybrid CNN and leaf-count approach achieved a mean absolute error (MAE) of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 - 1 5}$</tex> days for young plants and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 2. 5}$</tex> days overall, with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 8 \%}$</tex> classification accuracy across age groups, with per-crop MAE of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim \mathbf{4}$</tex> days (Bell Pepper), <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 18$</tex> days (Mango), <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 21-22$</tex> days (Durian), and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 15-16$</tex> days (Anthurium). A prototype web system enables QR-code-based plant identification, customer photo uploads, and personalized care recommendations. Results confirm that BotonyBuddy delivers reliable, real-time insights into plant health and growth, offering a scalable, AI-driven plant nursery tool for precision and smart agriculture. Future directions include expanding disease coverage, adopting multi-view imaging, and incorporating IoT data for higher-fidelity predictions.

title: Recent Advances in the Application of TinyML and Edge Devices in Photovoltaic Systems: Bridging the Gap Between Laboratory Research and Industry
authors: Adel Mellit, Marco Zennaro
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy (AIESRE)
abstract: With advancements in Artificial Intelligence (AI) techniques and embedded systems, various platforms have been developed to design and deploy Tiny Machine Learning (TinyML) on edge devices for real-time applications. TinyML enables ML models to run efficiently on resource-constrained devices, making it particularly useful in photovoltaic (PV) systems. This paper briefly overviews potential TinyML applications in PV systems, including solar energy forecasting, fault detection and diagnosis, predictive maintenance, and more. Recent applications are discussed regarding the edge devices used, platforms, accuracy and real-time deployment. Most research in this topic is still conducted at the laboratory scale; therefore, bridging the gap between laboratory research and industry is crucial for transitioning from theory to real-world applications. The paper also discusses the challenges and outlines future directions for implementing embedded ML on edge devices in real-world PV system applications.

title: Post-Vaccination COVID-19 Side Effects in Annaba, Algeria: Construction, Analysis, and Clustering of a Comprehensive Database
authors: Soumia Layachi, Amel Slim, Ahlem Melouah, Abdeldjalil Halisse
publish: 2025 International Conference on Recent Advances in Mathematics and Informatics (ICRAMI)
abstract: In the effort to control the pandemic, it is important to understand the side effects of COVID19 vaccines for the sake of public health. This study presents a new and thorough database specifically focused on the side effects of COVID-19 vaccines. This is an area of research that has been lacking, especially in the Arab region. The data was carefully gathered from EPSP Annaba of Annaba city in Algeria and processed to ensure accuracy. Through the use of clustering method, relevant information was extracted from the database, revealing specific patterns and connections among side effects. This innovative work provides valuable insights for healthcare professionals and policymakers, contributing to a more detailed understanding of the safety of COVID-19 vaccines.

title: Enhancing Spectral Efficiency and Resilience in SAGIN-ITS: An IRS-Assisted Semantic Offloading Framework
authors: Ali Mehrban, Hajar Moudoud, Bouziane Brik, Zakaria Abou El Houda
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Magazine
abstract: In dense Vehicle-to-Everything (V2X) scenarios within Space-Air-Ground Integrated Networks (SAGIN), connected autonomous cars (CAVs) generate a large volume of sensor data, which leads to significant bandwidth congestion. There is a risk to safety because of the poor Packet Success Rate (PSR) and dangerous transmission delays caused by this congestion. To address this issue, we introduce SemantIRS, a framework that integrates semantic offloading with Intelligent Reflecting Surface (IRS)-assisted links to achieve high network resilience, when compute resources permit and lower latency. SemantIRS deploys lightweight Large Language Models (LLMs) as encoders to compress massive sensor data at the edge of the SAGIN network before transmission through IRS-assisted links. Intent-Based Networking (IBN) and Federated Learning (FL) are also integrated to automate resilience policies and preserve distributed offloading privacy throughout SAGIN layers, respectively. We conduct our experiments using a 12-state hardware benchmark of three LLM encoders (Phi-2, Llama-2-7B-Chat, and Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2) on four compute tiers (TPU v6e-1, A100, L4, and Intel Xeon CPU). The measured inference latencies are then fed into an IEEE 802.11p airtime simulator. Our obtained results show that, in congested network conditions, SemantIRS improves the packet success rate from 1.7% to 66.4% while reducing transmission energy by 90%.

title: The Log-∝μ Fading Model
authors: Godfred K. Tenkorang, Michel D. Yacoub, André A. Dos Anjos, Elvio J. Leonardo, Fábio C. Martins
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: The paper introduces the Log-∝μ fading model, a nonlinear envelope framework that generalizes the classical ∝–μ distribution with a logarithmic transform to capture composite propagation effects with controlled complexity. Two distinct formulations (Implicit and Explicit), leading to the same particular cases, yield closed-form first-order statistics - probability density function (PDF) and cumulative distribution function (CDF) - and second-order statistics - level crossing rate (LCR) and average fading duration (AFD) - while subsuming a broad family of canonical and new channels as special cases. Using line-of-sight (LoS) and non-line-of-sight (nLoS) measurements at 1.8 GHz, parameter estimation via nonlinear least squares reveals that Log-∝μ achieves superior fits to empirical envelopes compared to established models, accurately reproducing deep-fade behavior in the LCR while preserving analytical tractability. Moreover, the special case Log-μ—a three-parameter fading model in its normalized form—and its six reduced-complexity variants, identified as Type I, II, III, IV, V, VI, further validate the framework: the two-parameter variant model, with complexity comparable to the ∝–μ model, achieves similarly strong fitting performance, and the one-parameter variant also delivers competitive results when applied to the measured data. Interestingly, one of these variants is the Lomax distribution, which is related to the Pareto, Beta Prime, and other distributions, for which higher-order statistics are presented for the first time. Overall, the proposed framework strikes a balance between accuracy and analytical complexity, providing a unified tool for channel characterization and performance analysis.

title: Cybersecurity Compliance for Operational Technology using Large Language Models
authors: Regan Bolton, Mohammadreza Sheikhfathollahi, Simon Parkinson, Dan Basher, Howard Parkinson
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Computer Society
abstract: Operational Technology Cybersecurity (OTCS) continues to be a dominant challenge for critical infrastructure such as rail. As these systems are vulnerable to malicious attacks, effective documentation and development processes are essential to perform compliance assessments and proactively protect systems. In addition to maintaining compliance, it is also necessary to periodically evaluate the design documentation to account for the evolution of cybersecurity standards and threats. In this paper, a system is proposed that uses Large Language Models (LLMs) and multi-stage retrieval to enhance the compliance verification process. Initially, a basic architecture named baseline compliance architecture (BCA) is evaluated to answer OTCS compliance queries. After this, the extended approach is defined, which is called parallel compliance architecture (PCA). The performance of OpenAI-gpt-4o and Claude-3.5-haiku models is compared when used in the pipeline against the relative performance of the BCA. A discussion of their responses is provided and, through empirical evaluation, it is observed that PCA can score 0.1 points higher in correctness and 0.55 points higher in reasoning than BCA.

title: Balanced K-Means with One-Swap for User Grouping in NOMA
authors: Cheng bin Xu, Zhen gang Shi
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Computing (SPIC)
abstract: In non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) satellite communication systems, user grouping strategies play a crucial role in determining system performance. This paper proposes Balanced K-means with One-Swap (BK-OS) for grouping with subchannel access. Unlike machine-learning-based clustering, BK-OS is training-free and does not rely on learned representations. It explicitly enforces group-size balance at initialization and performs a deterministic one-swap local search that directly maximizes sum-throughput, while reducing per-user throughput dispersion. We further integrate a subchannel allocation mechanism and evaluate BK-OS under subchannel access scenarios. Simulation results show that, upon introducing the subchannel access mechanism, BK-OS achieves approximately a 4% increase in average throughput and about a 30% reduction in the standard deviation of per-user throughput relative to the throughput-driven greedy grouping (TDGG) method, demonstrating superior performance and practical potential. The key innovations lie in (i) balance-constrained K-means to avoid over-concentrating strong-channel users, and (ii) a deterministic one-swap search that optimizes the true throughput objective without training. This work delivers a training-free, near-linear per-beam user-grouping module for multi-beam NOMA with subchannel access, advancing practical and scalable resource scheduling for satellite wireless networks.

title: Rice Plant Disease Identification Using YOLOv8s with C2f-CBAM Backbone and Multi-Scale Attention
authors: Nadin Nabil Hafizh Ayyasy, Riyanarto Sarno, Dalila Anwar Dini Rasyidah, Laurensia Simanihuruk, Alya Kamilah, Kelly Rossa Sungkono, Fadlilatul Taufany, Arif Abdullah Sagran
publish: 2026 International Seminar on Intelligent Business and Edge-Computing Research (ISIBER)
abstract: Rice leaf diseases, such as Blast, Bacterial Leaf Blight, and Brown Spot, pose a significant threat to agricultural productivity, often evading early detection due to visual similarities and small lesion sizes. Current object detection methods, such as YOLOv5s [5], still exhibit limitations in capturing fine-grained features against complex natural backgrounds. This study proposes an enhanced YOLOv8s based model that integrates the Convolutional Block Attention Module (CBAM) into the backbone and Multi-Scale Attention (MA) into the detection head. This strategic integration aims to sharpen spatial-channel focus and improve feature representation for small lesions. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed model (YOLOv8s+C2f + CBAM + MA) outperforms baseline architectures and single attention configurations [4], [6]. The model achieved superior performance with a Precision of 1.00 at a 0.747 threshold, a Recall of 0.93, and an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex>-Score of 0.79. These findings indicate that the synergistic combination of attention mechanisms significantly enhances detection consistency and classification accuracy for rice leaf diseases under real-world conditions.

title: Dual-Threshold Association Strategy for Enhancing UAV-Based Object Tracking with OC-SORT: A Lightweight Approach for Real-Time UAV Multi-Object Tracking
authors: Yang Zhao, Xiannan Wang, Xiuyan Zhou, Zhibo Liu, Jian Zhang
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Computing (SPIC)
abstract: Unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) are increasingly deployed in surveillance, traffic monitoring, and defense missions, where reliable multi-object tracking (MOT) plays a crucial role in situational awareness and decision-making. However, UAV video streams often contain dense small targets, frequent occlusions, and abrupt camera motion, while onboard platforms are constrained by limited computing resources. These challenges make UAV-based MOT particularly demanding. To address them, we propose a lightweight tracking framework built upon the Observation-Centric SORT (OC-SORT) paradigm, introducing two key enhancements: a dual-threshold association mechanism and a health-aware gating strategy. The dualthreshold design separates detections into high- and lowconfidence pools, ensuring robust matching while avoiding false track initialization, whereas the health-aware gating integrates trajectory reliability with distance-based filtering to suppress unreliable associations. Experiments on the VisDrone2019-MOT benchmark demonstrate that our method achieves consistent improvements over OC-SORT, with IDF1 and HOTA gains of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$+4.8 \%$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$+3.0 \%$</tex>, respectively, and over 25% reduction in identity switches and fragmentation. Importantly, the proposed approach maintains near real-time performance at 28.5 FPS, striking a favorable balance between accuracy and efficiency. These results highlight the technical contributions of this work to UAV-based computer vision, intelligent signal processing, and resource-efficient tracking, aligning closely with the conference scope.

title: Artificial Intelligence in Omani Public Schools: Challenges and Strategies for Effective Educational Administration
authors: Shaima Adil Al Juma, Yasser Al-Mahdy
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Blockchain, Cloud Computing, and Data Analytics (ICoABCD)
abstract: This study aims to identify the challenges facing the administration of artificial intelligence (AI) implementation in public schools in the Sultanate of Oman and to propose developmental procedures to enhance its effectiveness in alignment with the national digital transformation agenda. A mixed-methods research design was adopted. The quantitative phase employed a questionnaire administered to school principals, deputy principals, and lead teachers across selected Omani governorates to examine the current state of AI implementation management and the major obstacles encountered. The qualitative phase involved semi-structured interviews with a purposive sub-sample from the same population to gain deeper insights into the identified challenges and to explore effective developmental procedures. The findings revealed that limited technical and administrative competencies among school leaders, along with the absence of clear policies for AI implementation, constitute key barriers to effective administration. In addition, insufficient specialized training and inadequate digital infrastructure were identified as significant constraints. Based on these findings, the study proposes two main developmental procedures: (1) establishing a national strategic framework for AI administration in education, and (2) implementing sustainable professional development programs aimed at strengthening digital leadership and smart transformation management. Overall, the findings emphasize the need for innovative administrative approaches to transform Omani schools into intelligent learning environments capable of leveraging AI to enhance educational quality and institutional performance.

title: Multilevel Confirmatory Factor Analysis for Modelling the Determinants of Malnutrition Among Under-Five Children in Indonesia
authors: Kamaluddin Simamora, Bambang Widjanarko Otok, Setiawan Setiawan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Blockchain, Cloud Computing, and Data Analytics (ICoABCD)
abstract: Malnutrition, including stunting, wasting, and underweight, remains a serious public health challenge in Indonesia. Various factors contribute to malnutrition, ranging from individual- and household-level to environmental, socioeconomic, and health policy factors. The use of data analysis as a foundation for policy formulation has been widely adopted in many fields, including healthcare. Most studies have analyzed the determinants of nutritional status using conventional approaches, without accounting for the hierarchical structure of the data or the presence of unobserved (latent) variables. This study aims to examine the factors influencing malnutrition in Indonesia using a Multilevel Confirmatory Factor Analysis (MCFA) approach. This approach allows modeling the causal relationships among latent variables at two distinct levels of analysis, the district (level one) and the province (level two) simultaneously. Based on the Intraclass Correlation test, all malnutrition indicators have values greater than 0.05, indicating variability at the group (province) level. In conclusion, most indicators of Parenting, Health and Environmental Services, and Malnutrition are significant, with Parenting and Malnutrition showing a strong impact. Socioeconomic factors at the district level show limited influence, while provincial-level factors are more significant for wasting and underweight. Both district and pro-vincial-level factors contribute to stunting, with district-level factors having a greater impact.

title: Message from the Chairman
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)

title: Foreword
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 International Seminar on Intelligent Business and Edge-Computing Research (ISIBER)

title: Blockchain Technology in Digital Governance: A Comprehensive Framework for Decentralized Public Administration
authors: Garima Singh, Chandra Shekhar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Distributed Ledger Technologies (ICDLT)
abstract: As public services become increasingly digitized, governments worldwide are exploring innovative technologies to enhance operational efficiency, transparency, and citizen engagement. Blockchain technology, particularly through smart contracts, digital identity systems, and decentralized autonomous organizations (DAOs), presents unprecedented opportunities for creating more transparent, accountable, and participatory governance systems. This paper provides a comprehensive analysis of blockchain applications in digital governance, examining real-world implementations, regulatory challenges, and legal frameworks. We propose an adaptive four-layer framework that enables governments to systematically integrate blockchain technologies while addressing privacy, security, and compliance requirements. Through case studies from Estonia, India, Dubai, and emerging DAO implementations, this research demonstrates both the transformative potential and practical challenges of blockchain-based governance systems.

title: Performance Evaluation of Electric Vehicles Using Single-Speed and Continuously Variable Transmissions
authors: Le Thanh Quang, Dat Ly Vinh, Phuc Le Thanh, Ngo Quang Thanh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Two drivetrain options for electric vehicles are compared in this study: a continuously variable transmission (CVT) and a single-speed gearbox. A Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor (PMSM) controlled by a Field-Oriented Control (FOC) technique was included in the MATLAB/Simulink simulation model. The Federal Test Procedure 75 (FTP-75) driving cycle was used to test the drivetrains in order to evaluate vehicle dynamics, torque adaptability, and energy efficiency. According to simulation data, the CVT-equipped drivetrain reduced total state-of-charge (SOC) consumption from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 1 \%}$</tex> to 5.6 % while maintaining higher torque responsiveness (up to 230 Nm), virtually double the energy efficiency of the single-speed system. These results validate the potential of CVT systems to improve overall performance and increase driving range for electric vehicles in actual urban driving situations.

title: Identifying Key Determinants of Stunting Prevalence in East Nusa Tenggara Using Multiview Fuzzy C-Means Clustering
authors: Alya Fitri Syalsabilla, Nur Iriawan, Muhammad Mashuri
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Blockchain, Cloud Computing, and Data Analytics (ICoABCD)
abstract: Stunting remains a pressing public health issue in East Nusa Tenggara (NTT), Indonesia, where malnutrition severely affects child development. This study investigates the multidimensional determinants of stunting using secondary data from the Ministry of Home Affairs (2024), covering health, sanitation, education, and economic indicators across districts. Data preprocessing involved imputation, normalization, and variable selection, after which the Multiview Attribute Fuzzy C-Means (MVFCM) clustering method was applied. Cluster validity was assessed using the silhouette index, while the significance of indicators was evaluated through fuzzy membership degrees. The results reveal four key determinants are women's education level (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mu_{\text {max }}$</tex> =0.99), clean water facilities (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mu_{\max }=0.98$</tex>), health infrastructure <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\mu_{\max }=0.96\right)$</tex>, and livelihood (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mu_{\max }=0.73$</tex>). Cluster analysis identified three distinct groups of regencies, with Cluster 2 showing consistently high prevalence (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$18.8-20 \%$</tex>) requiring urgent intervention. The silhouette index of 0.72 indicates strong clustering validity, confirming the reliability of the analysis. Policy recommendations emphasize improving women's education through expanded access and community programs, strengthening sanitation via clean water infrastructure, and enhancing health services in remote areas. Livelihood improvement through sustainable local economies and social protection is also advised. In conclusion, targeted, evidence-based strategies integrating education, sanitation, health, and economic empowerment are essential to reduce stunting prevalence and achieve equitable child health outcomes in NTT.

title: LSTM-Enhanced MADDPG Approach for Adversarial Agents in Security Simulation Training
authors: Mo Su, Mingfan Qiu, Yuming Shen
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Computing (SPIC)
abstract: To make adversarial scenes in security drills more dynamic and uncertain, we embed Long Short-Term Memory inside Multi-Agent Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient. Standard MADDPG breaks state information into snapshots and ignores history when observations are partial, so agents struggle to coordinate. By placing LSTM cells in both Actor and Critic, each agent keeps a memory of past states and uncovers temporal patterns, sharpening its picture of the changing scene and the quality of its decisions. Tests in coordinated attack scenarios show the new model converges roughly 40 % faster and produces reward sequences whose standard deviation drops by about 28.5 %, confirming its value for building rich, evolving security exercises.

title: Gamified Code Clash Platform for C Language
authors: Angammal R, Divya P, Sangeetha M
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: Learning C programming often feels like an uphill battle due to dry theory and complex syntax, but CodeClash changes this by turning the curriculum into a competitive, retro-style adventure. By replacing traditional exercises with narrative “quests” and interactive “Learning Paths,” the platform transforms technical challenges into an engaging journey where students collect coding tools in a personal “Arsenal” and climb the “Hall of Heroes” leaderboard. With a built-in sandbox for real-time code execution and a robust backend for managing content, CodeClash bridges the gap between abstract logic and practical application, making the process of becoming a proficient programmer feel less like a chore and more like a rewarding game.

title: AI Based Face Emotion Recognition and Mood Mapping Interface
authors: K. Vignesh, B. Baranitharan, K. Yuvaraj, D. Indra Sena Reddy, R. Manoj Kumar Reddy, M. Vineeth Kumar Reddy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: The capability of systems to interpret human emotions and respond to them is shaping the human-computer interaction. Conventional ways of communication do not provide as much emotional context that decreases the system adaptability as well as user engagement. The AI-Based Face Emotion Recognition and Mood Mapping Interface is a user interface that is based on deep learning models, computer vision, and real-time processing of facial expression and emotions including happiness, sadness, anger, surprise, and neutrality. The system combines the use of Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), the pre-trained face detection models, and lightweight structures to deploy the system and achieve the desired accuracy and speed in recognition. Moreover, mood mapping modules have customized feedback and can be used to offer adaptive human-computer interfaces, monitoring wellness, and intelligent decision making. They comprise Open CV in detection of faces, Tensor Flow in emotion detection and a mood inference engine that provides meaningful insights to be used in healthcare, education and user experience design. This extensible and extensible system provides a solid platform to affective computing, improves the human-machine interaction, and provides adaptive behaviors to real-time environments.

title: Blockchain Interoperability in Supply Chains: A Systematic Literature Review of Benefits, Challenges, and Key Success Factors
authors: Fatemeh Ali Mohammadi, André Vasconcelos
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Distributed Ledger Technologies (ICDLT)
abstract: Global commerce requires efficient supply chain management (SCM), yet current supply chains face persistent challenges, including limited traceability, data security concerns, poor collaboration, lack of transparency, and inconsistent standards. While blockchain technology (BCT) offers improvements in traceability, security, and transparency, fragmented ecosystems reveal the critical need for blockchain interoperability (BI) to facilitate seamless integration across diverse platforms. This paper presents a systematic literature review (SLR) of peer-reviewed studies to examine the potential of BI to enhance supply chain efficiency and transparency. The review identifies key benefits such as end-to-end visibility, enhanced traceability, improved security, and operational efficiency. It also highlights the technical and organizational factors essential for successful BI adoption and maps prevailing solutions against the main barriers to implementation. Overall, this study provides a structured analysis of BI in overcoming SCM limitations, offering a foundation for future research and highlighting critical gaps in the practical deployment of interoperable blockchain (BC) solutions across supply chain ecosystems.

title: Anticipating Crypto Success: An XAI Framework for Early-Stage Token Viability Using Deployment Features
authors: Alexy Bounsavath, Csaba Kiss, Tamás Savci, Gábor Hellner, Roland Molontay
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Distributed Ledger Technologies (ICDLT)
abstract: The exponential growth of blockchain-based tokens has heightened the need for reliable methods to assess their longterm viability at deployment, a stage where historical market data is absent and risks such as scams and project failures are prevalent. This study introduces an explainable machine learning framework to predict token viability using static features available at launch, including smart contract properties (e.g., mintability, centralization), deployment details (e.g., network), and metadata (e.g., presence of an icon). We collected 100,000 ERC-20 tokens from Ethereum, Binance Smart Chain, and Polygon and analyzed their characteristics available at deployment and derived features. We labeled them as live or failed based on post-deployment scores derived from liquidity, transfer frequency, and holder distribution. Among the models evaluated, XGBoost with class-weight adjustment excelled, creating an enriched token set that contained, on average, 11 times more live tokens than the original dataset, surpassing other classification models in identifying viable tokens. SHAP analysis highlighted key predictors: tokens with icons, complex yet high-quality code, and deployment on Ethereum were more likely to succeed, while Polygon deployments correlated with higher risk. Though effective as an early filter, the framework's modest standalone precision underscores its role as part of a broader strategy integrating post-launch data. This approach advances early-stage token evaluation, enhancing investor decision-making and risk assessment in decentralized finance.

title: IMFM-GNN: Interpretable Multimodel GNN Via Fourier-Markov Dynamics for Community Detection in Sustainable Energy Networks
authors: Yanfei Ma, Daozheng Qu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Green energy networks, such as smart grids and distributed renewable systems, exhibit complex topologies with multi-scale interactions and stochastic behaviors. This paper proposes a Fourier-Markov Multimodel Graph Neural Network (FM-GNN) framework for community detection and efficiency optimization in green energy systems. The model integrates Fourier spectral embeddings to capture global topological frequencies and Markovian transition modeling to characterize dynamic energy exchanges between communities. A multimodel learning mechanism is employed to align local GNN-based node embeddings with global spectral-probabilistic representations, enhancing stability and interpretability. Experimental results on benchmark smart grid and renewable energy datasets demonstrate that FM-GNN achieves superior modularity, energy flow balance, and predictive resilience compared to existing spectral or GNN-based baselines. The proposed approach provides a promising pathway toward interpretable, scalable, and uncertaintyaware graph intelligence for sustainable energy infrastructures.

title: Federated Learning for Multi Institutional Pancreatic Tumor Segmentation with Robust Domain Generalization
authors: V. Srinivasan, Gunaseelan Namperumal, Lavanya Shanmugam, Ranjeet Kumar, Manish Tomar, Bhargav Kumar Konidena
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: Pancreatic tumor segmentation is extremely challenging due to low lesion contrast and high anatomical variability, which is further exacerbated by large domain shifts across different multi center CT datasets. Centralized learning obviously violates privacy regulations, whereas the standard FL methods often fail under heterogeneous non IID client distributions. This work introduces a Federated Learning approach that leverages a Heterogeneity Aware Federated Aggregation strategy and a Domain Generalization module for improved robustness and cross institution generalizability of the models. In this work, HAFA integrates uncertainty guided weighting, domain similarity scoring, and adaptive learning rate correction, while DG Mix generates pseudo domains to strengthen generalization. Experiments on MSD, NIH, and TCIA datasets have demonstrated that the proposed HAFA FL has achieved a Dice score of 0.87 and Jaccard 0.77, outperforming FedAvg (Dice 0.85), FedProx (Dice 0.84), and FedBN (Dice 0.85). Its performance on an unseen hospital has been improved to Dice 0.82, yielding an 8–12% gain over baselines. These results will provide a highly privacy preserving, scalable, and clinically reliable solution to multi center pancreas tumor segmentation.

title: Cross-Domain Detection of AI-Generated Songs: Benchmarking Encoder Models on Bahasa Indonesia
authors: Jenson Christopher Halim, Henry Lucky
publish: 2026 International Seminar on Intelligent Business and Edge-Computing Research (ISIBER)
abstract: In recent years, advances in deep learning have significantly improved our ability to detect highly realistic AIgenerated song content. This progress has raised concerns about copyright protection, cultural authenticity, and the spread of misleading content. Despite growing global attention, there are still very few research studies related to this domain, especially in low-resource languages such as Bahasa Indonesia, due to the lack of appropriate datasets. To address this gap, we compiled and gathered a dataset of Indonesian songs collected from publicly available sources on social media, consisting of both real performances and AI-generated modifications, reflecting real situations in which AI-generated content is usually shared. We investigate our detection strategies by comparing several detection techniques using established audio encoders such as AST, Wave2Vec2, and WavLM. These models are then evaluated on unseen and different domain datasets. This is a crucial challenge given the rapid growth of AI music generation technologies. This experiment highlights both techniques and offers new insights into cross-generator detection, as well as dataset design and model robustness, which are important for protecting musical integrity in the era of generative AI.

title: HSCKBase: A Robust Hierarchical Federated Learning Framework for Semantic Communication Knowledge Base in Regionalized Service Scenarios
authors: Liang Li, Nan Ma, Yuhang Zhu, Jinyi Zhao, Xiaoxuan Qi, Ping Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing
abstract: Multi-user, real-time semantic communication is the key to applying next-generation mobile communication technologies to realize complex, multimedia interactive applications in regionalized service scenarios. However, two factors challenge existing model-structure-based semantic knowledge base (SKB) updating and aggregation approaches, including the stochastic nature of mobile devices' service participation and the gradient-based update of multimedia multidimensional attributes. Therefore, this paper proposes a hierarchical federated learning (HFL)- based service framework for SKB updating and aggregation. Specifically, we first propose a synchronized waiting mechanism and a dynamic batch-updating aggregation approach for HFL SKB updating to overcome the efficiency and accuracy challenges caused by uncertainty in the distribution of computational resources. Secondly, the HSCKBase framework proposes a forced-wait mechanism for sub-network gradient updates to address the unreliability of gradient computation during sub-network aggregation. Finally, the HSCKBase framework proposes a weighted-average algorithm to mitigate the impact of uneven sub-network distribution, caused by the randomness of mobile device service participation, on the global SKB convergence efficiency and accuracy. Experiments demonstrate that HSCKBase achieves higher global updating and aggregation accuracy, lower communication latency, and greater robustness than existing HFL-based SKB updating and aggregation approaches, while ensuring the availability of the source semantic coding.

title: Graph Percolation as Decision Threshold for Risk Management in Cross-Country Thermal Soaring
authors: John J. Bird
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: Long range flight by fixed-wing aircraft without propulsion systems can be accomplished by “soaring” – exploiting randomly located updrafts to gain altitude which is expended in gliding flight. As the location of updrafts is uncertain and cannot be determined except through in situ observation, aircraft exploiting this energy source are at risk of failing to find a subsequent updraft. Determining when an updraft must be exploited to continue flight is essential to managing risk and optimizing speed. Graph percolation offers a theoretical explanation for this risk, and a framework for evaluating it using information available to the operator of a soaring aircraft in flight. The utility of graph percolation as a risk measure is examined by analyzing flight logs from human soaring pilots. This analysis indicates that in sport soaring pilots rarely operate in a condition which does not satisfy graph percolation, identifies an apparent desired minimum node degree, and shows that pilots accept reduced climb rates in order to maintain percolation.

title: DPSCS-Net: Dynamic Path Selection and Compressed Sensing Unfolding Network for ISAR Imaging
authors: Shuaihua Tian, Jianwen Guo, Hongyin Shi, Ting Yang, Tianchi Zhao, Hui Liu, Fang Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: Traditional Inverse Synthetic Aperture Radar (ISAR) imaging algorithms frequently struggle with low resolution and poor noise suppression when operating under sparse aperture (SA) conditions, primarily due to the inherent non-uniformity and incompleteness of the collected data. In recent years, unfolding networks have emerged as a promising solution, establishing a systematic connection between conventional iterative algorithms and deep learning to achieve fast and efficient ISAR imaging. However, a common limitation of these unfolding networks is their reliance on a fixed number of layers for parameter optimization, which inherently limits their adaptability across diverse scenarios. To address these limitations, we propose an ISAR imaging unfolding network, termed DPSCS-Net, which is based on dynamic path selection and compressed sensing (CS). Specifically, DPSCS-Net unfolds a CS optimization algorithm into a deep network while simultaneously integrating phase error estimation within the CS framework. This integration significantly enhances cross-range resolution. To overcome the fixed-layer constraint of traditional unfolding networks, DPSCS-Net innovatively incorporates a dynamic path selector. This selector generates a one-hot vector based on the input data, where elements (0 or 1) dictate the selection of a fast and appropriate processing path. This dynamic execution mechanism effectively mitigates the unnecessary computational burden associated with conventional deep unfolding networks (DUNs), where all sampled data must traverse every processing stage. Both simulated and measured experimental results demonstrate that our DPSCS-Net exhibits notable flexibility and delivers superior performance with dynamic adaptability. This allows for an optimal balance that meets the primary demands of practical ISAR imaging applications. The network consistently shows strong robustness across various scenarios, coupled with superior performance and focusing accuracy.

title: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems Publication Information
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems

title: Interpretable Deep Learning in Dermoscopy: An XAI-Driven Ensemble for Skin Lesion Diagnosis
authors: Bhagyashri S. Sonune, R Udaykumar, Shon G. Nemane, Dhiraj P. Tulaskar, Manish Bhaiyya, Hossam Haick
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The problem of accurate and interpretable automated classification of dermoscopic skin lesions is difficult because publicly available benchmark datasets are imbalanced, diagnostically heterogeneous, and prone to image-level confounding effects. While individual techniques such as pre-trained convolutional neural networks (CNNs), ensemble learning, and explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) are established, their combined effectiveness is reported without controlled comparison to determine whether ensemble consensus improves robustness and explanation quality in a unified framework. In this paper, we report a controlled comparison of six pre-trained CNN architectures, namely ResNet50, Xception, EfficientNetB3, MobileNetV2, DenseNet201, and InceptionV3, on the publicly available HAM10000 dataset, as well as their soft voting ensemble. The contribution of this paper is not in proposing a new deep learning architecture but rather in presenting a harmonized evaluation framework to determine whether diversity in architectures can be exploited to improve robustness on minority classes and qualitatively better explanations in terms of spatial coherence in seven-class classification of dermoscopic images of skin lesions. To this end, we used Grad-CAM, LIME, and occlusion sensitivity methods to evaluate the interpretability of each individual CNN as well as the ensemble. The soft voting ensemble outperformed all individual architectures by achieving a macro-average ROC curve of 0.985 and accuracy of 89.37%, while simultaneously providing qualitatively better spatially coherent explanations that are lesion-centric, suggesting better trustworthiness in transparent XAI-based automated screening in dermatological studies.

title: Systematic Evidence of Progressive Workflow Discontinuities in CubeSat Microstrip Antenna Design: From Electromagnetic Optimization to System Integration
authors: Segundo Francisco Segura Altamirano, Carlos Leonardo Oblitas Vera, Víctor Paúl Palacios Paredes, Lucia Isabel Chaman Cabrera, Leo Danny Camizan Cabrera, Carlos Enrique Santos Tavara, Diana M. Castro Cárdenas
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This systematic review of 79 CubeSat antenna studies reveals a progressive "workflow attrition cascade" (68%→70%→87%→92%) wherein electromagnetic design sophistication systematically decouples from deployment-ready system integration. While CubeSats demand reliable communication under severe constraints, our PRISMA 2020-compliant synthesis demonstrates that current research workflows rarely translate theoretical optimization into mission readiness. Specifically, 68% of studies omit critical simulation documentation, 70% rely on single-domain experimental validation inadequate for space qualification, 87% fail to achieve full thermal-mechanical integration of feeding elements, and 92% lack environmental qualification during fabrication. Rather than reflecting technical inability, this attrition highlights systemic gaps between component-level design and system-level engineering, exacerbated by academic incentives and resource constraints. Addressing these discontinuities requires a paradigm shift from isolated electromagnetic optimization toward concurrent multiphysics integration, establishing validated workflows essential for transitioning CubeSat payloads from experimental demonstrations to operational multi-year constellations.

title: The Sliding Truncation Matched Filter for Periodic Pulse Interference Mitigation in Spaceborne SAR
authors: Jieshuang Li, Mingliang Tao, Lei Cui, Yujuan Li, Yanyang Liu, Ling Wang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: In practical applications, periodic pulse radio frequency interference (PRFI) is often detected in spaceborne SAR echoes. The current suppression methods for such interference mostly adopt a three-step processing flow: “time-domain detection, notch filtering, and missing data repair”. However, most of these methods focus on improving time-domain detection performance while ignoring the impact of the interference-to-signal pulse repetition frequency ratio (ISPRFR) on the effectiveness of data repair algorithms. To this end, this paper proposes a sliding truncation matched filter (STMF) that dynamically constructs a range-dependent azimuth reference function by adaptively truncating the matched filter according to the range-variant Doppler centroid. This enables coherent azimuth compression while suppressing ghost targets induced by periodic signal loss. The effectiveness of the STMF is validated through simulations and real-measured data experiments.

title:
CGM
                    <sup>2</sup>
                    Net: Cloud-Guided Mamba-Based Multitask Network for Multimodal Remote Sensing Semantic Segmentation and Cloud Removal
authors: Jianhao Li, Jue Wang, Hao Shi, Shan Dong, Wenchao Liu, Liang Chen, Wei Li
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Semantic segmentation and cloud removal via optical-Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) fusion are crucial tasks in Earth observation. Existing works have proposed multitask architectures to simultaneously address segmentation and cloud removal, but they lack targeted modeling of under-cloud features, leading to blurred ground-object reconstruction and poor fine-grained recognition performance in thick-cloud regions. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a Cloud-Guided Mamba-based Multitask Network (CGM<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>Net) for end-to-end multimodal segmentation and cloud removal. Specifically, CGM<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>Net utilizes a dual-stream encoder and integrates three key modules at each stage: cloud-guided feature restoration (CGFR) module, which, under cloud-mask-guided gating, exploits spatial- and channel- wise cross-modal correlations to reconstruct sub-cloud optical features and suppress SAR speckle; cloud-guided semantic interaction (CGSI) module, which performs mask-aware state-space modulation to enable selective, region-aware cross-modal semantic exchange; and Mamba-Based Fusion (MF) module, which adaptively fuses enhanced multimodal features to fully exploit modal complementary information. Two task-specific decoders synergistically optimize segmentation and cloud removal, thereby promoting mutual enhancement between the semantic prior and visual restoration. Experiments on M3M-CR and LuojiaSET-OSFCR demonstrate that CGM<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>Net achieves state-of-the-art performance on both tasks. Ablation studies and feature visualizations further validate the complementary roles and effectiveness of the proposed modules. The source code will be available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/1lee1338/CGM2Net</uri>.

title: Low Loss and EMI Noise Split-Gate Trench IGBT with Self-Powered Voltage Source
authors: Lian Gan, Jinping Zhang, Zhengyu Lang, Bo Zhang
publish: IEEE Electron Device Letters
abstract: This letter presents simulations on a low loss and electromagnetic interference (EMI) noise split-gate (SG) trench IGBT with self-powered voltage source (SPSG-TIGBT). Combined with the self-powered circuit composed of the integrated polysilicon diode and external capacitor, the floating P-body (FP) in the dummy region anticipated to work as an integrated voltage source to charge the SG during turn-off process. This reduces the on-state voltage drop (V<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ceon</sub>), while maintaining the advantage of SG structure on the gate capacitance and charge. Numerical simulation results show that compared with the Con. TIGBT and Con. SG-TIGBT, the proposed device is expected to offer better trade-off between turn-off loss (E<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">off</sub>) and V<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ceon</sub>. At the same V<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ceon</sub> of 1.18 V, the E<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">off</sub> of the SPSG-TIGBT is 29.90% and 55.58% lower than that of the Con. TIGBT and Con. SG-TIGBT, respectively. Moreover, its high SG potential significantly reduces the turn-on loss (E<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on</sub>) and enables a better trade-off between E<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on</sub> and surge current (I<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">surge</sub>) of the device, thereby effectively mitigating EMI noise.

title: IEEE Computer Society Grants for Emerging Technology Activities
authors: N/A
publish: Computing in Science & Engineering

title: From a Glance to a Boundary: Uncertainty-Aware Distillation for Glance-Supervised Video Moment Localization
authors: Yupeng Hu, Hao Liu, Kun Wang, Ruping Cao, Yinwei Wei, Liqiang Nie
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: Glance-supervised video moment localization, which requires only single-frame annotations within target moments, offers a promising balance between annotation efficiency and localization precision. However, existing methods suffer from a fundamental limitation: the inherent uncertainty regarding precise moment boundaries when given only glance supervision. Current approaches either assume deterministic relationships between glance annotations and boundaries or employ predefined soft-label distributions, both failing to capture the complexity of real-world boundary uncertainty. We introduce UDist, an uncertainty-aware distillation network that explicitly models boundary prediction uncertainty as learnable probability distributions. Our framework consists of two key components: 1) an Uncertainty-aware Offset Distribution (UOD) mechanism, transforming implicit boundary uncertainty into explicit probability distributions over discretized offset bins; and 2) a dual-branch distillation framework that transfers uncertainty knowledge from a teacher to a student branch through a competitive distillation process. This “certainty to uncertainty to refined certainty” paradigm converts glance annotation certainty into uncertainty representations, then distills them into precise boundary predictions. Experiments on Charades-STA, ActivityNet Captions and TACoS show UDist improves localization accuracy while maintaining computational efficiency.

title: Reconfigurable DAB Converter With Variable Transformer Turns for Wide Voltage Range Using Hybrid EPS Modulation
authors: Harikesh V. Kurup, Shelas Sathyan, Praveen Kumar K. S.
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: In this article, a variable transformer-based modified dual-active bridge (DAB) converter is proposed that has wide voltage range performance. The conventional DAB delivers maximum efficiency and performance only at unity voltage ratio and also fails to deliver wide voltage range operation. To overcome these limitations, this work proposes a modified DAB with reconfigurable secondary that can handle a wide output voltage range using transformer turns ratio. The variable secondary of the transformer provides three turns-ratio choices, and the reconfigurable bridge provides three operating modes for each. In effect, the converter operates in nine different modes, delivering higher performance over a voltage gain range of 0.3 to 4. In order to minimize the current stress across the switches and reduce losses, a hybrid extended phase shift modulation is proposed, which enhances the overall performance of the converter. A 500 W prototype is built for the proposed converter configuration, and the effectiveness is experimentally verified.

title: A 95.3% Efficiency APT/AET/SPT Multimode Multiband CMOS/GaN Envelope Tracking for 6G-Oriented Systems
authors: Chun-Yuan Chen, Yi-Chun Huang, Ke-Horng Chen, Ying-Hsi Lin, Shian-Ru Lin, Tsung-Yen Tsai
publish: IEEE Journal of Solid-State Circuits
abstract: This article proposes an envelope-tracking (ET) supply modulator (SM) that is scalable for sixth-generation (6G) communication systems. The design leverages a cost-efficient CMOS process for the power converters and a high-performance GaN process for the high-frequency power amplifier (PA) and the depletion-mode-only GaN-based amplifier. The proposed ET supply modulator (ETSM) supports average power tracking (APT), Analog ET (AET), and symbol power tracking (SPT) operation modes, achieving 400 MHz ET bandwidth and up to 95.3% efficiency, while also enabling fast mode transitions. Parasitic-induced voltage ringing in SPT mode is effectively mitigated by activating the depletion-mode-only GaN-based class-AB amplifier as an active snubber, maintaining performance despite parasitic variations. This solution overcomes the limitations of existing techniques and provides robust performance across multimode-multiband (MMMB) operation, spanning 2G to 5G FR2 and beyond. Experimental results demonstrate high efficiency, rapid mode transitions, and substantial mitigation of parasitic voltage ringing.

title: Performance Analysis of Space Satellite Communication System Based on Single-Photon Detectors
authors: Camellia S. Mouhammad, Ziyuan Shi, Mohamed-Slim Alouini
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: Free-space optical (FSO) satellite communication systems face distinct challenges depending on link topology, as horizontal or inter-satellite links (ISLs) operate above the atmosphere, uplink (UL) channels experience atmospheric turbulence near the transmitter, and downlink (DL) channels suffer primarily from geometric losses. This paper presents a comprehensive analysis of low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite communication links employing single-photon detectors (SPDs) to address photon-starved conditions under various FSO channel impairments. We develop comprehensive channel models for DL, ISL, and UL that capture the combined effects of atmospheric turbulence, attenuation, pointing errors, angle-of-arrival (AoA) blockage, and geometric spreading. To further enhance performance in high-data-rate scenarios, we investigate multi-pixel SPD arrays and derive analytical expressions for error-rate performance that include detector dead time and dark counts. Our theoretical models are validated through extensive Monte Carlo simulations, showing strong agreement with predicted results. The findings highlight the benefits of spatial diversity in multi-pixel SPDs, demonstrating notable gains over single-pixel configurations. Finally, we provide key insights into optimizing link parameters: receiver aperture, link distance, detection efficiency, modulation order, and beam divergence for robust, high-sensitivity photoncounting FSO satellite communication.

title: Intra-Group Individual Item Fairness-aware Recommendation
authors: Ruijia Ma, Lin Zhang, Rongbo Qi, Chunyao Song, Tingjian Ge
publish: IEEE Transactions on Knowledge and Data Engineering
abstract: The recommendation system, as a widely used and effective tool to alleviate information overload, has been receiving increasing attention regarding its issues of bias and fairness. Many studies have focused on addressing fairness on the item side, targeting item fairness by minimizing exposure discrepancies of items among similar individuals. However, in real-world recommendation scenarios, many relevant items requiring similar exposure to users may exhibit certain dissimilarities while existing methods could not solve the problem. To address this, we define a broader item fairness recommendation issue aimed at improving fairness within specified groups of related individual items, which we term as “intra-group item fairness”. To solve this issue, we propose a Group-oriented Individual Fairness recommendation model called GIFRec. First, we introduce a global exposure balance module to mitigate exposure imbalances at a global level, with the help of multimodal information contained in each item. Then, at the group level, we propose a group fusion embedding representation method, allowing individual items within the same group to adaptively share group information. Additionally, as unfair training opportunities may arise for different items during model training, we propose a general fair intra-group optimization method to reduce individual training biases within the same group. Extensive experiments conducted on four real-world datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach with an average improvement of 11.27% in accuracy and 24.91% in fair ness compared to eight SOTA methods. Our implementation and some details can be found at https://github.com/xderui/GIFRec.

title: PoTable: Towards Systematic Thinking via Plan-then-Execute Stage Reasoning on Tables
authors: Qingyang Mao, Qi Liu, Zhi Li, Mingyue Cheng, Zheng Zhang, Rui Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Knowledge and Data Engineering
abstract: In recent years, table reasoning has garnered substantial research interest, particularly regarding its integration with Large Language Models (LLMs), which have revolutionized natural language applications. Existing LLM-based studies typically achieve step-by-step thinking for table reasoning guided by task semantics. While these approaches emphasize autonomous exploration and enhance fine-grained table understanding, they often overlook systematic thinking in the reasoning process. This oversight can lead to omitted steps, disorganized logic and misleading results, especially in complex scenarios. In this paper, we propose <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">PoTable</small>, a novel stage-oriented plan-then-execute approach that incorporates systematic thinking into table reasoning. Specifically, <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">PoTable</small> involves several distinct analytical stages with clear objectives to provide adequate guidance. To accomplish stage-specific goals, <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">PoTable</small> employs a plan-then-execute mechanism: it first plans the operation chain based on the stage objective, and then executes operations sequentially through code generation, real-time running and feedback processing. Consequently, <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">PoTable</small> produces reliable table reasoning results with highly accurate, step-wise commented and completely executable programs. It mirrors the workflow of a professional data analyst, offering advantages in both accuracy and explainability. Finally, we conduct extensive experiments on four datasets from the WikiTQ and TabFact benchmarks, where the results demonstrate the effectiveness, efficiency and explainability of <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">PoTable</small>. Our code is available at: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/Double680/PoTable</uri>.

title: Editorial Special Issue on ICWOC 2025
authors: Xiong Deng, Chen Chen, Xiping Wu, Chao Li
publish: IEEE Photonics Technology Letters

title: Reliability Analysis of IGZO Thin Film Transistor for Wearable Biosensing Applications
authors: Anchal Thakur, Samriti Sharma, Jaya Madan, Rahul Pandey
publish: IEEE Transactions on Device and Materials Reliability
abstract: This article reported the impact of mechanical bending on Indium gallium zinc oxide (IGZO) thin film transistor (TFT) with single gate electrode for wearable electrics devices for its reliability analysis. The deformation caused by mechanical bending caused a strain in the active layer of IGZO in TFTs. This strain is compressive and tensile in the parallel and perpendicular direction of current conduction. Mechanical bending is induced by a change in the radius of curvature (R= 1 μm to 10 μm). For smaller radii (0 > R > 2 μm), the strain is higher for IGZO TFTs. The radius of curvature also influences the electrical performance matrices. In this paper, the charge carrier mobility, electron concentration, drain current, and threshold voltage shift are also evaluated with variations in the radius of curvature. Furthermore, the compressive strain degrades mobility, and tensile strain enhances mobility depending on the radii of curvature. Thus, the radii less than 3 μm, the degraded reliability for IGZO TFTs has been observed in terms of threshold voltage shift and I<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ON</sub>/I<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">OFF</sub> ratio (ΔV<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</sub>=-116.7% and I<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ON</sub>/I<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">OFF</sub> =-132.9%), respectively.

title: DAUNet: A Lightweight UNet Variant with Deformable Convolutions and Parameter-Free Attention for Medical Image Segmentation
authors: Adnan Munir, Muhammad Shahid Jabbar, Shujaat Khan
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Medical image segmentation plays a pivotal role in automated diagnostic and treatment planning systems. In this work, we present DAUNet, a novel lightweight UNet variant that integrates Deformable V2 Convolutions and Parameter-Free Attention (SimAM) to improve spatial adaptability and context-aware feature fusion without increasing model complexity. DAUNet's bottleneck employs dynamic deformable kernels to handle geometric variations, while the decoder and skip pathways are enhanced using SimAM attention modules for saliency-aware refinement. Extensive evaluations on two challenging datasets, FH-PS-AoP (fetal head and pubic symphysis ultrasound) and FUMPE (CT-based pulmonary embolism detection), demonstrate that DAUNet outperforms state-of-the-art models in Dice score, HD95, and ASD, while maintaining superior parameter efficiency. Ablation studies highlight the individual contributions of deformable convolutions and SimAM attention. DAUNet's robustness to missing context and low-contrast regions establishes its suitability for deployment in real-time and resource-constrained clinical environments.

title: Charge Redistribution Loss Suppression and Phase Current Balancing Control for 48-V Two-Stage Multiphase Switched-Capacitor Converter
authors: Rui Xu, Linjun Han, Yenan Chen, Dehong Xu
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: This work focuses on minimizing charge redistribution loss and maintaining phase current balance for a two-stage 48-V hybrid switched-capacitor multiphase converter. The converter integrates a 3-to-1 Dickson converter with two flying capacitors as the intermediate bus converter, and two parallel four-phase series-capacitor buck converters for regulation and high output current. To minimize charge redistribution loss, the Dickson stage operates at an optimal duty cycle. Furthermore, the inter-stage decoupling capacitor is designed to be smaller than the flying capacitors to further reduce loss. However, this leads to increased intermediate bus voltage ripple, which induces an unequal volt-second distribution across the output phase inductors. A current balancing control strategy is proposed, eliminating the need for active current sensing. The operational principles of both the loss mitigation method and the current balancing control are presented. Experimental validation using a 48-V to 1-V/460-A two-stage, 8-phase prototype confirms the effectiveness of the proposed approach.

title: QUBO Simplification by Singular Value Decomposition and Coefficient Elimination for Ising Machines
authors: Shinnosuke Inaba, Takeru Ota, Nozomu Togawa
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Ising machines, including quantum annealing machines, can efficiently solve combinatorial optimization problems by formulating them as Quadratic Unconstrained Binary Optimization (QUBO). However, it is known that the solving performance of Ising machines deteriorates as the energy landscape represented by the QUBObecomes more complex. Simplifying the coefficients of QUBOmatrix can improve the performance of solving combinatorial optimization problems using an Ising machine. In this paper, we propose QUBO simplification methods for Ising machines using singular value decomposition (SVD) and coefficient elimination based on their absolute value (ABS). The proposed SVD method simplifies QUBO by capturing the global structure of the QUBO matrix. QUBO is further simplified by combining it with the simple ABS method. The simplified QUBO is expected to have a less complex energy landscape, while maintaining the global structure of the original, enabling the Ising machine to efficiently find the (semi-)ground state.We demonstrate the numerical experiments using an Ising machine to evaluate the effectiveness of the SVD method alone, the ABS method alone, and the two methods combined.

title: An Accurate Sea Ice Identification Method for Chinese FY-3F MERSI-III Based on Fuzzy C-Means Clustering
authors: Han Wang, Fuqiang Zheng, Yong Hu, Cailan Gong
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Arctic sea ice variability is a key indicator of global climate change and is closely related to navigation safety. In this study, we propose a sea ice identification method based on fuzzy c-means (FCM) clustering, combined with a two-stage cloud detection strategy, and quantitatively evaluate its performance using bias and root mean square error (RMSE). The cloud detection first performs an initial screening, then refines the cloud mask through scene-based spectral analysis of cloud-free pixels, effectively reducing cloud interference. This method enables accurate discrimination between sea ice and water surfaces, including water in leads and broken ice areas, and is applied to medium-resolution optical observations from the Medium Resolution Spectral Imager III (MERSI-III) onboard the Fengyun-3F (FY-3F) satellite. Validation of the MERSI-derived sea ice concentration (SIC) using Landsat 8/9 imagery at 30 m spatial resolution shows an average bias of approximately 5.5% and an RMSE of about 8.5%. Comparisons with MODIS sea ice products (MOD29/MYD29) further demonstrate reduced cloud-ice misclassification and improved representation of fine-scale sea ice features.

title: Deep Learning Based Bus Occupancy Forecasting for Intelligent Public Transport Management
authors: Galwin V R, Desrinath B, Poornima M
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: Proper predictions of bus occupancy are the key to streamlining operations in the public transport and improving the experience of passengers. The classical forecasting techniques usually do not reflect complicated temporal patterns and external influencing factors. The current paper introduces an extended deep learning architecture of bus occupancy prediction that considers various data streams, such as the historical occu- pancy data, weather, time specific data, as well as the route data. This paper suggests and compares three deep learning models, including Long Short Term Memory (LSTM), Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU), and Transformer based models. The structure includes a multimodal data fusion technique in order to address the temporal relationships as well as external contextual variables. The experimental testing on the real life data gathered in the Chennai Metropolitan Transport Corporation proves that the proposed models provide noticeable gains compared to the basic strategies. The most successful model has an MAE of 2.87 passengers and RMSE of 4.21 passengers that are 34.2 percent lower than the historical ARIMA models. The system helps the transport authorities to streamline the scheduling, resource distribution as well as passenger information services on demand.

title: Joint Detection and Motion Estimation Via Machine Learning in Single-Antenna FMCW Radar
authors: Andrey Dergachev, Alexander Osinsky, Roman Bychkov, Andrey Ivanov
publish: 2026 28th International Conference on Digital Signal Processing and its Applications (DSPA)
abstract: In this paper, we present a multitask learning approach for joint detection, ranging, and velocity estimation in a single-antenna frequency-modulated continuous wave (FMCW) radar. Such radars are widely used for object detection thanks to their low cost. They calculate the range-Doppler map (RDM) to increase the detection range for small objects. This map requires the calculation of the 2D fast Fourier transform (FFT) of the time-chirp matrix to find spectral peaks. We propose a novel ResNet-based architecture that employs blockwise RDMs to implement both detection and motion estimation. It outperforms an analytical approach that is based on a geometrical kinematic principle.

title: Pre-Harvest Prediction of Fruits with Deep Learning Techniques
authors: T.M. Saravanan, R. Akshya, B.K. Dharani, M. Sukitha, Moureeshvar Sa
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: Precise forecast of pre-harvest fruit ripeness is of extreme significance in farming, processing, and supply chain management. It will help to provide quality, shelf life, and reduced post-harvest loss. Traditional tests for fruit readiness are highly reliant on visual observation, which is arbitrary, subjective, and time-consuming. It is highly reliant on human experience and prone to environmental factors like light, orientation, and environment. To fix such issues, in this present work, it is proposed to use a pre-harvest fruit ripeness prediction model using MobileNetV2 with transfer learning. The model is of such a type that it predicts seven fruits, i.e., mango, pineapple, strawberry, banana, orange, grapes, and pomegranate, to be either ripe or not based on images. Approximately 3,000 images were deeply preprocessed, normalized, and augmented to ensure the images are valid. It made use of Keras and TensorFlow in its construction. MobileNetV2 model achieved a mean accuracy of 92% and even surpassed the traditional models like SVM and k-NN. Its lightness aspect makes it perfectly suitable for mobile and IoT devices, having real-time prediction ability. It shows the promise of deep learning to bring precision agriculture to a new level and offer data-driven and sustainable agriculture.

title: Real Time Deepfake Detection System Using MobileNetV1
authors: Evangeline D, Pranshu Agarwal, Mohit R, Mohammed Faizan, Hammad Khan, Parkavi A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies in Electronics and Green Energy (ICETEG)
abstract: The advancement of artificial intelligence has led to the creation of deepfakes-hyper-realistic manipulated videos generated using neural networks such as GANs (Generative Adversarial Networks). These videos can convincingly mimic real human appearances and expressions, posing serious risks such as misinformation, identity theft, political manipulation, and digital fraud. The increasing prevalence of deepfakes in social media, virtual meetings, and online broadcasts necessitates the development of a robust and real-time detection mechanism. This paper presents the design and implementation of a Real- Time Deepfake Detection System using a combination of liveness detection techniques and lightweight deep learning models. Our system processes live video streams, detects faces using MediaPipe/OpenCV, and classifies frames as real or fake using a MobileNetV1-based classifier trained on curated datasets such as FaceForensics++, CelebDF, and DFDC. To achieve low latency and high inference speed (; 100 ms), the model was optimized using TensorFlow Lite and integrated into a web-based dashboard built with React.js. The dashboard provides real-time detection results along with a visual confidence score for each prediction. This approach ensures practical deployment in edge and cloud environments (e.g., Jetson Nano or AWS). The system was tested across various real-world scenarios and achieved a balanced trade-off between speed and accuracy. The result demonstrates a promising step towards scalable deepfake mitigation, and future improvements can include multimodal analysis, speaker verification, and transformer-based models to further enhance detection accuracy

title: Intelligent Sensors and IoT-Driven Approach for Automated Water Quality Analysis & Segregation
authors: Sonali Nandish Manoli, Yusra Suroor Khan, Prema R, Arshiya Taskeen, Darshan O
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies in Electronics and Green Energy (ICETEG)
abstract: Ensuring the safe supply of drinking water requires continuous real-time monitoring of water quality. This research presents the design and development of a low-cost IoTbased system for real-time water quality analysis and segregation. The system integrates multiple sensors to measure essential physical and chemical parameters such as temperature, turbidity, conductivity and dissolved oxygen. These sensor readings are processed by a core controller and transmitted to a cloud-based platform for remote monitoring. Traditional water quality testing methods involve manual sampling and laboratory analysis, which are time-consuming and inefficient. Our proposed IoT-driven approach enables continuous water quality assessment, improving efficiency and ensuring a timely response to contamination issues. Furthermore, the system facilitates water segregation for different purposes, distinguishing between potable water and non-drinking water. Future enhancements could include automated billing based on water consumption and the integration of AI-based predictive analytics for contamination detection.

title: Classification of Manufacturing Companies' Financial Report Performance Using Machine Learning
authors: Salda Pasyia Islamiah, Tora Fahrudin, Renny Sukawati
publish: 2026 International Seminar on Intelligent Business and Edge-Computing Research (ISIBER)
abstract: Evaluating the financial performance of manufacturing companies is essential to support managerial and investment decision-making. However, conventional ratiobased financial analysis has limitations in capturing the complex relationships between financial indicators. This study aims to classify the financial performance of manufacturing companies using a machine learning approach.
