publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical, Communication, and Computing Technologies (iCONECCT)
abstract: The exponential increase in the population of old age people has created an immediate demand for a dependable, realtime health monitoring devices that have the capability of monitoring vital health constraints and giving update on one of the most dangerous health issues, i.e. fall detection of old age people. This article will be presenting an smart wearable health detecting solution which is built using Arduino Nano 33 IoT development board and also uses various sensors like MPU6050 as motion detection module, an AD8232 sensor used for ECG monitoring of elder people and an analog based heart rate monitoring sensor. The system is designed to continuously collect the acceleration data and various other physiological data for identification of fall occurrence, and measures ECG signals and heart rate of the person. It also uses a 16*2 LCD display module so as to provide the real-time visualization of the data of person. Further, a buzzer is used to provide immediate alerts whenever any abnormal activity is detected. For improved accuracy of the system, moving average filters are also applied to the acceleration data of the person and heart rate values are also computed with a 10 seconds gap in between two values. It also uses a cooldown mechanism for reducing false positives during the repeated movements. Since it is a low cost and compact design, it will be a promising solution for the elder people. Future enhancements will include GPS location, cloud-based connectivity and machinelearning approaches for advanced anomaly detection.

title: Chairs' Welcome
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 IEEE Afro-Mediterranean Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AMCAI)
abstract: It is with great pleasure and excitement that we extend a deepest welcome to you all for the 2nd IEEE Afro-Mediterranean Conference on Artificial Intelligence - IEEE AMCAI 2025, taking place in the beautiful city of Valenciennes, France, from October 14th to 16th, 2025. IEEE AMCAI 2025 is organized by Polytechnic University in Hauts-de-France, Valenciennes, France and ATIA (Association Tunisienne pour l'Intelligence Artificielle) with the Financial Co-Sponsorship of IEEE France Section, and the Technical Co-Sponsorship of IEEE Computer Society France Chapter, IEEE Africa Council, IEEE Tunisia Section, IEEE SPS Tunisia Chapter, IEEE CIS Tunisia Chapter and IEEE SMC Tunisia Chapter.

title: Brain Tumor Detection Through Deep Neural Network
authors: Aryan Tyagi, Aman Chaudhary, Abdul Aleem
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: Brain Tumor Detection has always been one of the most difficult task in the medical history due to its manual classification, the risk of inaccuracy in prediction is high. The amount of data stored is too voluminous, which further increases the chances of error. Brain tumor exhibit diverse pattern but due to similarity between tumor tissues & normal tissues, accurate detection becomes critical. A new technique of identifying and detecting type of brain tumor with 2D magnetic resonance brain image has been presented, which utilizes convolutional neural network (CNN). During the analysis of tumor in concurrent time, it is observed that tumors have difference in shape, size and are present in different locations. The proposed CNN method has been successful in detecting all types of tumors. When compared with different algorithms like support Vector Machine, K-Nearest Neighbors, Random forest, VGG16, Logistic Regression, and Light GBM, the proposed model gave best result with the accuracy of 97.87%.

title: Smart Warehouse Management for SMEs: A Low-Code Digital Transformation Approach
authors: Mahaning Indrawaty Wijaya, Ali Gunawan, Richard Wiputra
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies and Interdisciplinary Innovation (ICAT2I)
abstract: Small and medium enterprises currently contribute to around 60 % of GDP in Indonesia. However, many of them still do not leverage the benefit of information technology to support their activity yet. Meanwhile, rapid technology development has been transforming the business in many industries, and also increasing the competition. Therefore, SMEs need to be aware and start to apply available technology to increase competitiveness. In this paper, a warehouse management system is developed using power apps to support warehouse management activity in one small medium enterprise that focuses on distributing fresh fruit. The application helps to eliminate manual and redundant activities such as manual entry, re-copying reports, and other activities that are very time-consuming. Besides digitizing the process, the system also can provide recommendations about which products have near expiry dates or have low stock levels. Thus, the company could use its time and resources to achieve more strategic goals rather than the operational routine that has low value-added.

title: AI-Driven Real-Time Synchronization (ASC-6): Optimizing Logistics Resilience and Financial Performance
authors: Lahcen Kandsi, Arif Jabir, Fouad Jawab
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies and Interdisciplinary Innovation (ICAT2I)
abstract: In the era of Supply Chain 4.0--named after an adjectival portrayal of technologies like IoT, AI and DLT that adds up to a “4.0”-the days when logistics was about sequential batch processing are over, replaced by new real-time event driven networks moving in its place. In an age where the name of the game is real-time distribution, this paper will address the essential difficulties of systems engineering in complex cyber-physical environments, and specifically talk about a big hole: no full set frame system to make end-to-end real-time synchronization possible across vital shipping interfaces (Warehouse Management Systems (WMS), Transportation Management Systems TMS).) and hardware. To this end, we present the Architecture for Synchronization in Six Layers (ASC-6), which is a new and secure framework that forms a blueprint for high-fidelity logistics operations. ASC-6 coordinates and directs the data through a series of: •Acquisition (IoT/CDC): Persistent telemetry by IoT and CDC from core systems. •Communications (MQTT/Edge) Low latency data transfer with MQTT-based edge connection. •Harmonization (Kafka): Data harmonising across organisations, resulting in Unified Data Model for Semantic coherence among various partners. •Predictive Intelligence (LSTM): Using LSTM Neural Network for predicting supply chain deviations and scoring prescriptive actions. •(Event-Driven Orchestration (BRM): Automatically trigger remediation through a Business Rule Engine (BRM) for immediate operational response. •Governance (Blockchain) Write all sync events DLT for transparency, immutability and decentralized trust. The method involves a bibliometric analysis of 500 peer-reviewed papers (2000-2025) to validate the architectural design by identifying deficiencies in existing research regarding integrated frameworks.) Shortly, we will present a digital twin architecture able to calibrate and validate the ASC-6 based on an Agent-Based Model (ABM) in order to test it under different breaking disruption scenarios. The simulation results show that the prescriptive capability of ASC-6 dramatically decreased DL by 45 % on average (from minutes to milliseconds) compared with classical systems. The framework also results in a 69 % recovery improvement in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R T$</tex> after a major disruption, which causes significant measurable reduction of CNS. Such system design, integration and validation is a crucial cornerstone for realizing resilient, proactive and genuinely agile global networks the researchers and practitioners in Advanced Technologies for Supply Chain Management (ATSCM) are hoping to create.

title: Abnormal Action Detection for Surveillance Robot Based on Deep Ensemble Learning
authors: Souhila Kahlouche, El-Fani Roufaida, Tazekritt Malika
publish: 2025 International Conference on Recent Advances in Mathematics and Informatics (ICRAMI)
abstract: Mobile robotic systems are increasingly deployed to ensure human safety, particularly in environments where early detection of abnormal or risky behaviors is essential. Identifying suspicious human actions from video streams, however, remains a complex task, especially when real-time inference is required. This study introduces a real-time framework that enables a surveillance robot to recognize abnormal human activities from visual data. Three distinct deep learning models are trained to capture both spatial and temporal cues of human motion. Their outputs are later fused using an ensemble decision mechanism, which significantly enhances recognition reliability compared to using any individual model alone. To train and evaluate the system, a dedicated dataset was built by merging samples from three public human-action datasets containing both normal and abnormal behaviors. Experimental tests conducted in real environments confirm that the proposed framework performs effectively and can be integrated into intelligent surveillance platforms.

title: DL-LoEM: Improving Data Locality and Load Balancing for MapReduce-Based Entity Matching
authors: Khadidja Midoun, Walid-khaled Hidouci, Malik Loudini, Nadjah Chergui
publish: 2025 International Conference on Recent Advances in Mathematics and Informatics (ICRAMI)
abstract: The entity resolution process consists to identify the duplicate entities, which is an important issue in data integration and data cleaning efforts. This process is very expensive in terms of the number of entity matching operations and the running time of each operation. To minimize this cost, MapReduce parallel programming model is widely used for blocking-based entity matching. However, the absence of skew handling mechanisms and the ignoring data locality factor during the distribution of intermediate keys through the Reducers can lead to a significant performance degradation in MapReduce job processing. To address these issues, we propose an enhancement to an existing approach for load balancing in MapReduce, referred to as the LoEM approach. In this paper, we introduce the DL-LoEM (Data Locality and Load Balancing for MapReduce-based Entity Matching) approach, which aims to improve both load balancing and data locality for blocking-based entity resolution in MapReduce. Our experimental results demonstrate that DL-LoEM improves the load balancing up to 52.3% and 14.7% compared to Hadoop and the LoEM approache, respectively. Additionally, DL-LoEM achieves improvements of up to 26.5% and 25.3% in data locality during the reduce phase compared to the Hadoop and LoEM approach, respectively.

title: Towards an Assistive Eye-Controlled Interface
authors: Mohamed Amine Yakoubi, Arslane Aouissi, Issameddine Amaidia, Walid Hariri, Sara Rekkal, Ahlem Melouah
publish: 2025 International Conference on Recent Advances in Mathematics and Informatics (ICRAMI)
abstract: Eye tracking is a technique used for capturing and interpreting eye movements to control systems without physical contact. This project leverages this technology to interact solely through gaze directions, enabling hands-free navigation in PDF documents. Using a convolutional neural network (CNN) based on MobileNetV2, a lightweight and efficient architecture was employed to develop a model capable of classifying eye movements. This model is integrated into a graphical user interface (GUI) built with Tkinter, providing an intuitive interaction experience with various types of documents, regardless of their size, while maintaining high precision and fluidity. Experimental results show an accuracy of 96%, demonstrating the effectiveness of this approach in terms of digital accessibility.

title: Study on Dynamic Optimization and Multi-Objective Decision-Making of Supply Chain Carbon Footprint Based on GA-LSTM Hybrid Model
authors: Wenyu Wang
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Logistics Engineering, Supply Chain and Digital Transformation (LESCDT)
abstract: In the context of dynamic optimization and multi-objective decision-making for the carbon footprint of global supply chains, this study aims to develop and validate a GA-LSTM hybrid model to enhance the accuracy, robustness, and practicality of carbon footprint prediction, addressing issues of insufficient prediction accuracy and low decision-making efficiency caused by endogenous and exogenous uncertainties. Methodologically, this paper optimizes LSTM hyperparameters (such as the number of hidden layer neurons, dropout rate, and learning rate) through genetic algorithms, constructs a two-layer LSTM structure to enhance the ability to capture long-term dependency features, and integrates a transfer learning mechanism to synergistically handle endogenous and exogenous uncertainties. Important results show that validation based on global datasets (EXIOBASE and World Bank) indicates a RMSE of 0.045, MAE of 0.032, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> of 0.963 for model performance testing; under noise interference, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\triangle$</tex> RMSE increases by only 8.2%, and △ RMSE is 12.5% when data is missing; in actual testing, the carbon footprint reduction rate reaches 18.7%, and the cost savings rate reaches 15.3%. The conclusion indicates that this model significantly optimizes the multi-objective decision-making efficiency of supply chain carbon footprint.

title: Hybrid Deep Learning Ensemble Models for Enhanced Financial Volatility Forecasting
authors: Boutheina Zouaoui Drira, Haykel Hamdi, Ines Ben Jaafar
publish: 2025 IEEE Afro-Mediterranean Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AMCAI)
abstract: this paper presents a novel ensemble methodology that integrates deep learning models to enhance the accuracy and robustness of financial volatility forecasts. By combining Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and GRU networks, the proposed approach captures both spatial and temporal patterns in financial time series data. Empirical results demonstrate the superiority of this ensemble model over traditional forecasting methods in various financial markets.

title: Radio Frequency Energy Harvesting for Smart Farming Applications: Opportunities and Challenges
authors: Zaheer Abbas Balouch, Jahariah Sampe, Huda Abdullah, Zeti Akma Rhazali, Noor Hidayah Yunus
publish: 3rd 2025 IEEE International Conference on Agrosystem Engineering, Technology &amp; Applications (AGRETA)
abstract: The surge of the Internet of Things (IoT) in smart farming has facilitated the mass adoption of sensor networks for monitoring soil, crops, livestock, and environmental parameters. A notable limitation of this paradigm is fundamentally constrained by its dependence on batteries, introducing significant challenges related to maintenance, cost, and environmental sustainability, specifically in the large or remote agricultural areas. Radio Frequency Energy Harvesting (RFEH) has emerged as a convincing strategy to achieve sustainable, battery-independent or battery-augmented operation of low-power agricultural sensors. This study presents a systematic investigation of a microstrip patch antenna (MPA) designed for integration into radio frequency energy harvesting (RFEH) systems targeting smart farming applications. The proposed antenna operates within the 2.4 GHz ISM band and achieves a notable gain of 2.78 dBi. Furthermore, it achieves efficient impedance matching, characterized by a low reflection coefficient below <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$- 10\text{dB}$</tex>. This study highlights persistent operational difficulties, such as low energy densities achievable through RFEH, signal propagation matching in rural landscapes, and the design constraints imposed by the pursuit of energy independence in sensor networks. This study outlines the opportunities and challenges of RFEH in agriculture, with the objective of informing ongoing research and enhancing resilient and environmentally sustainable smart farming systems.

title: Ambient Frictions: Place-conscious Frictions to Foster Intentional Technology Use
authors: Solomon Musa Pitluck, Anup Sathya
publish: Proceedings of the Twentieth International Conference on Tangible, Embedded, and Embodied Interaction
abstract: None

title: How Secure is a High-Performance RISC-V Core? A Spectre V1 Case Study on XiangShan Open-Source CPU
authors: Rey Paraula, Roberto Petenzi, Alessandro Bertani, Davide Conficconi
publish: Proceedings of the 19th European Workshop on Systems Security
abstract: None

title: Enhancing Ethereum Smart Contract Security: A Novel Slither-Based Static Analysis Framework
authors: Nasrin Nemati, Nasr Abosata, Usman Butt
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Distributed Ledger Technologies (ICDLT)
abstract: Blockchain technology is evolving rapidly, bringing significant opportunities as well as complex challenges, particularly in the area of smart contract security This paper addresses these challenges by presenting an enhanced version of Slither, a static analysis framework, specifically designed to improve vulnerability detection in Ethereum-based smart contracts. Our contributions target critical vulnerabilities of reentrancy, unchecked call return values, and access control issues, which pose serious threat to decentralized apps (dApps). The enhanced Slither tool incorporates advanced detection methodologies, including refined control-flow analysis, sophisticated pattern recognition, and comprehensive visibility checks. Experimental evaluation demonstrates that this proposed tool significantly improves accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score across various metrics, while also effectively reducing false positives. This advanced model not only strengthens the security posture of smart contracts but also contributes to fostering a more secure and reliable blockchain ecosystem.

title: Design and Development of Dynamic Pricing Based Smart Parking System for Electric Vehicles
authors: Prajwal K T, Tejas M, Sai Aman, Aditya K, Prajwal S, Karthik Kumar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies in Electronics and Green Energy (ICETEG)
abstract: In this article, the authors introduce the design and implementation of a dynamic pricing-based smart parking system in particular for electric vehicles (EVs). The system combines renewable solar power, wireless charging technology, and dynamic pricing algorithms to fulfil increasing demand for eco-friendly urban transportation solutions. The proposed system employs real- time slot detection based on IR sensors and wireless power transmission to provide user convenience, energy efficiency, and enhanced resource utilization. A working prototype is designed and tested, with successful slot detection, dynamic pricing management, and wireless charging integration. This solution presents a promising addition to smart city infrastructure, paving the way for environmentally conscious urban mobility.

title: Development of Interactive Learning Media Based Training Kit on Basic Electronics Subject for Industrial Electronics Engineering
authors: Khakam Ma'ruf, Rizal Justian Setiawan, Darmono, Nur Azizah, Nur Evirda Khosyiati, Apry Aditya Saputra
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Materials and Electrical Engineering (ISMEE)
abstract: Basic Electronics is a foundational subject in the industrial electronics engineering major at vocational school in Indonesia that plays a crucial role in shaping students' understanding of electronic components and circuit operations. However, traditional methods of teaching this subject often rely heavily on theoretical explanations, which can result in low student engagement and limited practical competence. To address this challenge, this study aims to develop an interactive learning media in the form of a training kit that enhances student participation through hands-on experience and real-time feedback. This study aims to develop an interactive learning media-based training kit tailored to the Basic Electronics subject for vocational high school students. The development followed the ADDIE (Analysis, Design, Development, Implementation, and Evaluation) model. Validation involved assessments from material and media experts. The material expert gave an average score of 95%, indicating a very valid level of media. Meanwhile, the media expert assigned a score of 92.6%, also categorized as very valid. The developed media was also tested on 30 students in a limited trial, achieving a student response score of 88.4%, which supports its usability and effectiveness. In addition, students' learning outcomes also increased from the average score of 72 to 95. These results indicate that the training kit is highly suitable as a learning medium for basic electronics instruction.

title: Detecting Online Gambling Promotion in Youtube Comments Using Transformer-Based Models
authors: Howan Anderson, Valina Evelyn Pranoto, Vanechka Radja Winata, Henry Lucky
publish: 2026 International Seminar on Intelligent Business and Edge-Computing Research (ISIBER)
abstract: YouTube comments section is often filled with online gambling promotional comments that are completely irrelevant to the video content. These comments typically share repetitive or templated patterns, making YouTube a major medium for online gambling promotion that negatively impacts social, economic, and psychological aspects of society. Transformer-based language models, particularly BERT, have shown strong performance in understanding contextual and semantic patterns in text. This study introduces the IndoGambling-Comments Dataset, a new Indonesian-language dataset containing labeled YouTube comments related to online gambling. Using this dataset, we compare the performance of several transformer models: IndoBERT, IndoBERTweet, RoBERTa, and XLM-RoBERTa. Experimental results show that IndoBERT achieves the best overall performance with an F1-score of 0.9850 when font preprocessing is applied. The inclusion of preprocessing consistently improves performance. Overall, the results demonstrate that transformer-based models are highly effective for online gambling comment detection, particularly when preprocessing strategies are aligned with model pretraining characteristics.

title: Transforming Information Retrieval with AI: Semantic, Neural, and Multimodal Approaches
authors: Anusha S, Sankar T. C, Geetha Priya J, R. Vinoth, Anju O. R, N. Ramshankar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: The concept of Intelligent Information Retrieval (IR) has evolved to more advanced systems that can comprehend semantics, user intent and multimodal content as opposed to traditional search that uses key words. This survey gives a detailed analysis of advanced IR methods that are motivated by artificial intelligence, machine learning, deep learning, and natural language processing. It brings out how IR has evolved to be less Boolean and less of a vector space model and more of a semantic retrieval, neural ranking, knowledge based search and personalized and context sensitive systems. The paper discusses such important elements as learning-torank models, transformer-based, knowledge graphs, multimodal retrieval, and federated search strategies. Other challenges explored by the survey include interpretability, bias, scalability, the issue of privacy and multilingual retrieval. Lastly, there are also emerging trends such as retrievalaugmented generation (RAG), neuro-symbolic IR, quantumenhanced retrieval and energy-efficient systems, which are pointed out as promising directions of future research.

title: Instant-Incidental-Close-Loop Modeling to Optimize Battery Life on Autonomous Electric Boat
authors: Yopi Sopian, Faizal Arya Samman, Muhammad Niswar
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Materials and Electrical Engineering (ISMEE)
abstract: This paper presents a simple new idea for modeling the duty cycle regulation method used by the instantaneous incidental closed loop (IICL) scheme to regulate the current flow in a circuit that serves as a power consuming load, facilitating a fast and smooth transition from open loop to closed loop power supply for a Mini Autonomous Electric Boat (MAEB). The algorithm embedded in the microcontroller that regulates the duty cycle transition regulation on wireless sensors in a closed loop circuit using batteries is named the “Switching Time Controlled to Get End Point and Average Duty Cycle” (STEADY) algorithm. The advantages of the STEADY algorithm are low memory usage, which is 4 Kbyte, or 12.5% of the 32 Kbyte available memory slot, simplicity, and easy implementation on a microcontroller device. Final testing of the IICL modeling using the STEADY algorithm on a wireless DC voltage sensor shows a battery life of 44.78 times longer and 54.31 times more efficient compared to the continuous active mode in the closed loop.

title: The Dependence of the Reynolds Number on Mixing Index Enhancement for Lipid Nanoparticle Production
authors: Rima Febria Rizkyta, Wira Hadi Kusuma, Roer Eka Pawinanto, Jumril Yunas, Fitri Khoerunnisa, Budi Mulyanti
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Materials and Electrical Engineering (ISMEE)
abstract: This study examines the effect of Reynolds number on mixing performance in a T-shaped Snowflake Fractal Micromixer (TSFM) with a 30° orientation. Simulations are performed using COMSOL Multiphysics with water and ethanol as working fluids relevant to lipid nanoparticle (LNP) production conditions. A concentration ratio <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1: 5$</tex> is used at the inlet, with Reynolds number variations from 0.1 to 150. The mixing index value measures the degree of fluid homogenization at the outlet. The results showed that at very low <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\operatorname{Re}(0.1-1)$</tex>, mixing is dominated by molecular diffusion with a relatively constant mixing index of approximately 74.6%. The mixing index increases sharply, reaching its maximum efficiency of 76.86% at Re 50 due to microvortices forming and flow stretching by fractal resistance. In contrast, at high <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\operatorname{Re}(100-150)$</tex>, the mixing index decreases gradually with a minimum value of 73.59% at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\operatorname{Re} 150$</tex> because the flow becomes predominantly axial, reducing lateral mixing. This finding indicates that TSFM operation at low to medium <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\operatorname{Re}(5 \leq \operatorname{Re} \leq 25)$</tex> provides the most stable conditions for LNP production, offering homogeneous mixing with less risk of process inconsistency compared to the highly efficient but less stable high-Re regime 10, thus potentially producing nanoparticles with a narrower size distribution.

title: A Multimodel Approach to Brain Stroke Risk Analysis Using Medical Imaging
authors: V Shalini, Shantini K, E Ahila Devi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: Cerebral stroke is a top morbidity and mortality cause, demanding quick and precise evaluation of lesion existence and nature for proper risk stratification. Current deep learning approaches tend to suffer from robust lesion segmentation on variegated MRI data and effective feature extraction for classification on intricate whole-brain images. This work introduces a multi-modal deep learning architecture to improve the precision and specificity of cerebral stroke risk assessment. The approach uses a strong two-stage pipeline: nnU-Net is initially used to perform automated and accurate lesion segmentation from T2/FLAIR MRI scans, giving a pivotal mask of the diseased region. This segmented lesion area is then input to a pre-trained Vision Transformer (ViT), which has to learn overall features and classify to determine the stroke risk. The model's last output is a probability score (0 100) of the likelihood of a brain stroke. The combination of nnU-Net for accurate localization and ViT for high-context classification provides a high-accuracy, efficient solution to automated stroke risk analysis. This framework is likely to become a useful diagnostic tool, greatly enhancing the objectivity and speed of medical imaging interpretation towards early clinical intervention.

title: An Intelligent ESP32-Driven Underground Cable Fault Detection and Self-Isolating Power Distribution Framework
authors: Jegadeesan S, Kanagaraj A, Madhan M, Harish K, Kavinraj S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: The detection, location and isolation of faults in the underground power distribution system is the main factor that determines the reliability of the systems. Nevertheless, traditional fault detection methods of underground cables are commonly labor intensive, time consuming and inappropriate as far as real time monitoring is concerned. This paper proposes an intelligent underground cable fault detection and selfisolating power distribution system using the ESP32 microcontroller and an Internet of Things (IoT) to solve these drawbacks. The suggested system would be able to detect abnormal operating conditions by constantly measuring the changes in voltage and current along the underground distribution lines with the help of embedded sensors. According to predefined threshold logic, the system is capable of categorizing common fault accurately like line to ground, line to line and double line faults. When the fault is detected, the faulty section is automatically isolated by the switching units that are controlled by relays, thus limiting the spread of faults and enhancing the safety of the system. A GPS module is fitted to improve effectiveness in the maintenance by locating an exact geographical position of the fault. All fault-related data, electrical parameters, fault category, isolation status, and GPS coordinates are sent to a cloud-based IoT dashboard to visualize them and examine them in the past. Experimental findings prove that the suggested system has a significant impact on the reduction of the response time of faults and the outage period and can be implemented in the smart underground power distribution.

title: Propeller Selection in Electric Propulsion using Multi-Criteria Sugeno Fuzzy Inference System
authors: Erwan Eko Prasetiyo, Erwhin Irmawan, Gaguk Marausna, Hendriana Helda Pratama, Rian Adrian, Aulia Dea Fadzila
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Blockchain, Cloud Computing, and Data Analytics (ICoABCD)
abstract: The selection of an appropriate propeller is a critical factor in ensuring the efficiency, endurance, and safety of Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs). Conventional selection methods often rely on single metrics such as thrust or efficiency, neglecting essential trade-offs involving electrical loading and aerodynamic constraints. This study proposes a systematic simulation-based framework for propeller selection using a Sugeno-type fuzzy inference system (FIS). The methodology integrates three main stages: (1) identification of Brushless DC (BLDC) motor parameters through a validated simulation model, (2) evaluation of four key propeller performance criteria thrust margin, efficiency, current ratio, and tip Mach number, and (3) implementation of a 32-rule Sugeno FIS to generate a unified suitability index (Fuzzy Score). All analyses were conducted entirely through simulation using empirically derived motor parameters and published datasets, ensuring consistent and reproducible evaluation without physical testing. The system successfully produced a clear ranking of fourteen commercial propellers, highlighting configurations that balance thrust capability, electrical safety, and aerodynamic efficiency. Results demonstrate the potential of the proposed fuzzy-based approach as a computationally efficient decision-support tool for UAV designers. The framework provides a practical basis for extending data-driven propeller selection toward real-time or embedded UAV applications.

title: Research on VMD Optimized Signal Processing Method Based on Tornado Optimization Algorithm
authors: Wenxuan Mo, Xiangyu Li, Chunhua Yuan
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Computing (SPIC)
abstract: The signal contains rich information about operating conditions, but direct analysis is highly challenging due to its nonstationarity and non-linear characteristics. Variational Mode Decomposition (VMD), as an adaptive signal decomposition method, is highly dependent on parameter selection. This study proposes a new method for optimizing VMD parameters based on the Tornado Optimization Algorithm with Coriolis force (TOC) for signal decomposition. The TOC algorithm adaptively determines the decomposition layer number K and the penalty factor <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\alpha$</tex> of VMD, thereby overcoming the limitations of traditional empirical selection. Experimental results indicate that the VMD method optimized by TOC can effectively extract characteristic components from electrical power signals, significantly improving the accuracy of operating condition recognition.

title: Motion Controllers vs. Hand Tracking: A Comparative Study on Usability and Performance in VR-Based Korean Vocabulary Learning
authors: Azka Isnandaru Fajrina Mulya, Ahmad Nasikun, Adhistya Erna Permanasari, Achmad Rio Dessiar
publish: 2026 International Seminar on Intelligent Business and Edge-Computing Research (ISIBER)
abstract: Virtual Reality (VR) has been increasingly used in Computer-Assisted Language Learning (CALL) to provide immersive and contextually grounded learning experiences. However, the best interaction method for novice learners remains debated, especially between standard handheld devices and natural gesture interfaces. This study offers a comparative analysis of Motion Controllers versus optical Hand Tracking in a VR-based Korean vocabulary learning environment. Participants performed interactive vocabulary tasks within an immersive setting, such as choosing items in a virtual kitchen or placing objects in a living room, which involved direct hand-based manipulation of virtual objects. A within-subject experiment was conducted with 30 university students (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$N=30$</tex>) who completed a pick-and-place vocabulary task in a virtual traditional Korean house. The evaluation focused on four main metrics: Performance (Task Completion Time), Usability (SUS), User Experience (UEQ-S), and Comfort (VRSQ). The results showed a clear trade-off between efficiency and naturalness. Motion Controllers resulted in significantly faster task completion times (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p}<0.001$</tex>) and better overall user experience <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(p=0.037)$</tex>, due to the precision of haptic feedback. On the other hand, Hand Tracking, though slower, achieved scores statistically similar to those of controllers in Usability and physiological comfort. These findings suggest that while Motion Controllers are better for time-sensitive tasks, Hand Tracking is a practical, hardware-minimized option for casual, self-paced vocabulary learning.

title: Effectiveness of a Multi-Sensor System for Methane and Air Quality Monitoring in Supit Urang Landfill
authors: Siti Sendari, Mukaromah, Tibyani, Vita Dewi, DanangArengga Wibowo, Fadila Claudia Pramesti, Qurrotul A'ini, Aan Anjar Setyowati, Farhan Nafis Dermawan
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Materials and Electrical Engineering (ISMEE)
abstract: Methane <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\text{CH}_{4}$</tex>) is a highly flammable and potent greenhouse gas, wich has produced in landfills through the anaerobic decomposition of organic waste. Supit Urang Landfill in Malang receives approximately 500-700 tons of waste per day. Studies indicate that such daily waste input may result in methane emissions ranging from 8 to 12 tons per day, depending on the organic fraction and environmental conditions. This contributing to significant <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CH}_{4}$</tex> emissions that pose risks of fire, air pollution, and climate impact. Although a fixed gas capture system exists, emissions in uncovered areas and limitations in static monitoring systems hinder reliable detection and response. This study proposes a mobile multi-sensor system for real-time methane and air quality monitoring using a grid-based measurement approach. The system integrates gas and environmental sensors including MQ-4, MQ-135, TGS2611, MH-Z19B, PMS5003, HTU21D, and GPS mounted on a mobile robot platform. The robot navigates predefined paths, collects data at each node, and transmits readings to a cloud server while keeping local backups. Experimental results show that improved spatial coverage, consistency, and operational safety compared to static systems. The method offers a scalable and cost-effective solution for landfill monitoring, supporting early warning systems and more informed waste management decisions.

title: Performance Analysis of Time Series Models to Predict Sales Activity of SMEs
authors: Justine Christian Susanto, Maureen Letitia Wiratama, Patrick Christian Nathaniel, Ivan Diryana Sudirman
publish: 2026 International Seminar on Intelligent Business and Edge-Computing Research (ISIBER)
abstract: Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) are a key pillar of the Indonesian economy. Many small businesses implement pre-order systems to minimize inventory risk and high initial capital requirements. However, pre-order systems also pose unique challenges, particularly in terms of uncertain production costs and potential delays in fulfilling orders. Daily sales in these systems tend to be unstable, with periods of no transactions and sporadic spikes in demand. The purpose of this study is to develop and evaluate a forecasting model for MSME data operating with a pre-order system and to test whether machine learning tools such as XGBoost can produce reliable performance. This pattern results in a high frequency of zero values, making accurate predictions difficult. To predict such sales patterns, this study tests a Predictive Regression Model using the XGBoost technique and compares it with a baseline method. The data used consists of daily sales from 2023 to 2025. The data is then augmented with lag features and rolling window-based statistical features.

title: Development of DeepLabV3+ Model with ResNet-101 Backbone for Crack Segmentation
authors: Yesri Elva, Iskandar Fitri, Syafri Arlis
publish: 2026 International Seminar on Intelligent Business and Edge-Computing Research (ISIBER)
abstract: Ensuring public safety and long-term structural sustainability requires efficient and accurate monitoring of infrastructure problems. The first signs of structural deterioration are cracks in the ground or in the building itself. Traditional visual inspection techniques, however, are still flawed in that they are subjective, labor-intensive, and might endanger field personnel. These constraints highlight the need of a quantifiable, objective, and automated method for fracture identification, especially in high-resolution photogrammetric images acquired from drone or ground-based aerial surveys. By including the ResNet-101 backbone and using Conditional Random Fields (CRF) as a post-processing step to modify segmentation boundaries, this work suggests improving the DeepLabV3+ semantic segmentation model. With ResNet-101, we can extract more hierarchical features, and with CRF, we can fix local inconsistencies and smooth out crack borders on the probability map of our predictions. Images of ground cracks from both lab experiments and field observations are part of the Landslide Crack Dataset, which was used to create a binary ground-truth mask. All of the data in the dataset was personally annotated. In order to enhance pixel segmentation accuracy, boundary sharpness, and model robustness in detecting thin cracks in complex and heterogeneous photogrammetric environments, this research primarily focuses on integrating the DeepLabV3+ framework with a deeper backbone and CRFbased refinement. The suggested model outperforms the DeepLabV3+ baseline model experimentally, with scores of 0.845 for Dice Score, 0.158 for Dice Loss, and an average Intersection over Union (IoU) value of 0.272. These results show that the suggested method can back up monitoring systems for mining environments and large-scale infrastructure with a trustworthy and unbiased solution.

title: Ensemble Geospatial Forecasting: A Hybrid GBDT—LSTM for Segmenting Indonesian MSME Product Region Markets
authors: Erni Widiastuti, Jani Kusanti, Asri Agustiwi, Susilowardani, Nurul Aini, Dafa Tri Putra Prabowo
publish: 2026 International Seminar on Intelligent Business and Edge-Computing Research (ISIBER)
abstract: Predicting the demand of Indonesian MSMEs is challenging due to sparsely populated transactions, strong seasonality (Ramadan and public holidays), volatile promotional effects, and spatial heterogeneity across districts and cities. The problem in this study is to build accurate models and predict sales while mapping product region segments with different demand behaviors. Our contribution proposes a geospatial hybrid ensemble that combines Gradient Boosting Decision Trees (GBDT) and Long Short Term Memory (LSTM). The method used by GBDT models exogenous signals of price, discount, stock, weather, mobility, and holiday indicators to capture nonlinear responses and provide feature attribution, while LSTM captures medium- to long-term temporal dependencies of daily/weekly aggregates. Spatial structures are injected through geospatial embedding (latitude-longitude and proximity between districts), followed by unattended grouping in a shared feature embedding space. We evaluate with rolling-origin splits and RMSE, MAE, MAPE, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex>. On real MSME data, standalone GBDT scores RMSE 123,109; MAE 98,598; MAPE 3.63%; <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}} \mathbf{0. 6 1 3 8}$</tex>. A univariate LSTM is weaker (RMSE 183,757; MAE 153,775; MAPE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5.89 \%; \mathrm{R}^{2} 0.1414$</tex>). A weighted ensemble (w_gbdt=0.85, w_lstm=0.15) is best with RMSE 119,806; MAE 94,871; MAPE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3. 5 2 \%}$</tex>; <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}} \mathbf{0. 6 3 5 0}$</tex>, improving over GBDT by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx \mathbf{2. 7 \%}$</tex> RMSE and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 3.8 \%$</tex> MAE. SHAP (GBDT) and temporal saliency (LSTM) highlight promotion intensity, holiday effects, and regional accessibility as the key drivers. This framework consistently outperforms a single outline, generates stable segments to guide stock allocation and promotion scheduling, and can be used as a decision support for the MSME ecosystem.

title: Discovering Service Quality from High-Rated Hospitality Reviews in Jakarta Using LDA
authors: Tegar Mandiri, Safitri Juanita
publish: 2026 International Seminar on Intelligent Business and Edge-Computing Research (ISIBER)
abstract: Customer reviews on digital platforms contain valuable insights into guest experiences, but their unstructured nature makes large-scale manual analysis impractical. This study aims to identify service-quality themes expressed in guest feedback for highly rated hotels in Jakarta by applying a topic modelling framework based on Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA). The dataset consists of 12,798 Indonesian-language reviews of highly rated properties, collected primarily from Google Maps and its partner platforms. The text was processed through cleaning, normalization, stemming, and stopword removal, after which a coherence-based evaluation was used to select the optimal model. <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$A$</tex> seven-topic configuration (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$C_{-} v=0.6065$</tex>) provided the most coherent representation of customer feedback. The resulting topics reveal two dominant patterns: positive reviews consistently highlight staff friendliness and breakfast quality as key drivers of guest satisfaction, while negative reviews frequently refer to late check-ins, administrative issues, and miscommunication. These findings produce detailed, data-driven indicators of service performance and offer practical guidance for hotel operators seeking to prioritize operational improvements. Overall, the results demonstrate the effectiveness of LDA in extracting actionable service-quality insights in the Jakarta hospitality sector.

title: Viral Content Prediction on Twitter Using a Hybrid XLNet and RoBERTa Approach
authors: Albert, Antoni Wibowo
publish: 2026 International Seminar on Intelligent Business and Edge-Computing Research (ISIBER)
abstract: The rapid spread of, information on Twitter, creates both of opportunities, for the effective communication, and also risks related to misinformation. This study addresses virality prediction of multiclass tweet by proposing a multimodal deep learning approach based on transformer architectures. Collecting dataset using Apache Solr and 4,953 Indonesian-language tweets was collected and labeled into three virality levels. low, medium, and high, based on retweet counts. Data augmentation techniques were also applied to mitigate severe class imbalance exclusively to the training set. Textual representations were learned using pretrained XLNet and RoBERTa models while to enrich contextual understanding, numerical metadata features related to user engagement were incorporated. Both models were fine-tuned independently and then combined using a soft voting ensemble strategy. Results of the experiment of the test set shows that the hybrid model achieves stable predictive performance with an accuracy of approximately 95 % and improved its robustness in identifying minority viral classes supported by macro-averaged metrics and strong ROC AUC values. These findings indicate that integrating complementary transformer architectures enhances generalization capability for virality prediction and the proposed approach contributes to the development of reliable early detection systems for viral content on social media platforms.

title: Tokenized Credit System for Edible Food Waste Redistribution and Fiscal Incentives
authors: Gilbert Christian Caesario Siburian, Herry Irawan, Andry Alamsyah
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Blockchain, Cloud Computing, and Data Analytics (ICoABCD)
abstract: Efficient management of edible food waste (EFW) requires systems that are transparent and auditable to safeguard social and fiscal accountability while supporting food security and sustainable development goals. Yet current practice remains fragmented and reliant on manual reporting, which hampers traceability, sustains risks of miscalculation and double-counting, and leaves non-refundable tax credits illiquid when liabilities fall below the incentive value. This study designs and validates a blockchain-based EFW tokenization model that automates policy-compliant issuance, circulation, and retirement of credits with verifiable audit trails, while measuring transaction confirmation performance as an operational benchmark. The method combines business process mapping, an integrative literature review, stakeholder interviews, and expert validation, followed by model design and proof-of-concept evaluation in a local EVM environment to measure transaction stability. Results from 200 sequential mint transactions show an average confirmation time of 4.1906 seconds <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\sigma=0.0171 ~\mathrm{s})$</tex> without degradation, validating performance consistency under load. In parallel, FIFO allocation and on-chain event logging yield a deterministic audit trail that prevents double-counting. These findings provide an operational basis for tax credit reconciliation and accountable redistribution governance, indicate readiness for deployment in low-cost EVM ecosystems such as Polygon, and offer practical guidance for policy design and broader field trials.

title: Comparison of Supervised Learning Performance and Graph-Based Methods in Detecting Fake Reviews on E-Commerce
authors: Aqiela Putriana Shabira, Putu Harry Gunawan, Fitriyani
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Blockchain, Cloud Computing, and Data Analytics (ICoABCD)
abstract: This study presents a comparative analysis between traditional machine learning and graph based approaches for fake review detection. Using a curated Amazon 2018 product review dataset from prior research, we evaluated the performance of the Support Vector Machine (SVM) and Graph Convolutional Network (GCN) models, with TF-IDF as the feature extraction method. Experimental results show that SVM slightly outperforms GCN, achieving an accuracy and F1-score of 0.854, compared to 0.839 for GCN. Both models also achieved high AUC scores above 0.90, indicating strong classification capability. These findings suggest that for text-based review detection tasks, traditional machine learning methods such as SVM can remain competitive with more complex deep learning architectures, particularly when the dataset lacks relational structures between entities. This research provides practical insights into model selection for fake review detection systems in realworld e-commerce applications.

title: A Comparative Analysis of the Block Placement Policies on Hadoop Distributed File System: A Comprehensive Study
authors: Swati Dongre, David Williams
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Apache Hadoop is a framework for processing large, distributed data sets. Its storage system, HDFS, ensures reliability by replicating data blocks, with replica placement key to performance. This paper presents a comprehensive study and comparative analysis of Apache Hadoop 3.4.1 HDFS Block Placement Policies (BPP), namely BPP-Default, BPP-RackFaultTolerant, and BPPWithNodeGroup. The main purpose of this analytical research is to comprehend the mechanism of block placement policies for HDFS and thereafter finding out the key features provided by these policies. Critical theoretical analysis is done to draw well-founded conclusions and gain insights. The findings enabled a more comprehensive understanding the placements of replica, highlighting the strengths and limitations of each policy and the salient features provided by these policies.

title: Advancements in Drought Prediction: A Comparative Study of SARIMA, SARIMAX, Multivariate LSTM and STAT-LSTM
authors: B. Shamreen Ahamed, Gomathi Sankari S, Hamsini Neelakantan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: This study investigates the comparative performance of conventional statistical models-namely Seasonal Autoregressive Integrated Moving Average (SARIMA) and its exogenous variant (SARIMAX)-and advanced deep learning approaches, including Multivariate Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) and a hybrid Spatio-Temporal Attention LSTM (STAT-LSTM), for drought index prediction. While statistical techniques effectively capture seasonal temporal structures and incorporate external variables, deep learning models are better suited for learning complex non-linear dependencies. Model predictions for the Standardized Precipitation Index (SPI) and the Standardized Precipitation Evapotranspiration Index (SPEI) are evaluated against observed data. Results indicate that statistical models offer stable baseline performance, achieving <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{2}$</tex> values in the range of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.498-0.499$</tex> and RMSE values between 0.290 and 0.291. The optimized Multivariate LSTM significantly improves prediction accuracy, attaining an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> of 0.702 after 152 hyperparameter tuning iterations. The proposed STAT-LSTM delivers the best performance, achieving an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{2}$</tex> of 0.833 after 193 trials. This corresponds to an improvement of 18.7 % over the standard LSTM and 68 % over statistical baselines, underscoring the effectiveness of attention mechanisms, weighted loss strategies, and hybrid spatio-temporal architectures in modeling drought dynamics more accurately than traditional or standalone deep learning methods

title: Leaf Care CNN Framework for Automated Plant Pathology
authors: Janani P, Jovitha R, Kanimozhi P, Saratha M
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: The general challenges in the management of crop leaf diseases stress the need for early detection to avoid crop loss and sustain good yields. However, present-day manual inspections are mainly time-consuming and prone to human error; they also require specialized skills. These shortages allow an information gap so that may lead to late diagnosis and the spread of crop loss. In worldwide, plant diseases have been estimated to contribute about <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$20-30 \%$</tex> of annual agricultural losses, henceforth reducing farmers' livelihoods and food security. It has been relevant in such contexts to design the Leaf Disease Detection System, a machine learning-powered intelligent system that can auto-detect and classify crop leaf diseases from photographs. Computer vision and a deep learning algorithm will analyze pictures of leaves and make accurate, real-time predictions of disease likelihood. By taking a photo through their mobile or web interface, models detect the type of disease and suggest possible cures. The back-end uses cloud storage of data, which enables continuous learning and optimization in performance. This process arms farmers and agricultural experts with a reliable tool for early disease identification, reduces the manual anxiety, and increases efficiency in crop management.

title: Anomaly Detection in Network Traffic Using Machine Learning and Blockchain Technology
authors: Madhushree, Jyothi Shetty
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies in Electronics and Green Energy (ICETEG)
abstract: In today's interconnected digital environment, safeguarding sensitive information and ensuring secure operations require advanced anomaly detection techniques. Traditional centralized methods that rely on static rules are often insufficient against evolving cyber threats and zero-day attacks. This paper proposes a hybrid anomaly detection framework that integrates machine learning and blockchain technologies. Machine learning algorithms, such as LSTM Autoencoder, Isolation Forest, One-Class SVM, and Local Outlier Factor, are employed to analyze network traffic and detect abnormal behaviour in real-time. Blockchain technology ensures tamper-proof, transparent, and decentralized logging of anomalies, thereby eliminating single points of failure and enhancing data integrity. Experimental evaluation using the NSL-KDD dataset shows that the proposed system achieves high detection accuracy, with the LSTM Autoencoder attaining 92.4%. This research demonstrates that combining machine learning and blockchain can significantly improve the accuracy, resilience, and auditability of network intrusion detection systems, paving the way for scalable and robust cybersecurity solutions.

title: Digital Twin-Enabled Intelligent Battery Health Management System for Electric Vehicles
authors: Esther Abraham, Dharani M, S Sridharan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: The proposed paper introduces a Digital TwinPowered Intelligent Battery Health Management System of Electric Vehicles (EVs). The real-time monitoring, predictive analytics, and intelligent control are used to enhance the safety, reliability, and lifespan of the batteries. The central controller is an Arduino Uno (ATmega328P), which is linked to temperature, voltage, current, and vibration sensors to capture data on key performance. The Digital Twin(DT) that simulates the behavior of the battery receives wireless connectivity through the NodeMCU ESP8266 module to a cloud platform. This model enables the estimation of the state-of-health, identification of anomalies, as well as prediction of life, which helps in the mainstream of proactive maintenance. Inbuilt safety devices such as relays and buzzers can be used to avert hazardous conditions such as overcurrent and overheating. The suggested design combines embedded intelligence and IoT connectivity in contrast to the conventional Battery Management Systems (BMS). Such a combination offers remote access, enhanced energy utilisation, and reduced maintenance expenses. The system can provide an efficient and scalable answer to the sustainable use of electric mobility and renewable energy storage.

title: Performance Modelling and Simulation of Cobot for Pick and Place Application Using RoboDK
authors: Rachayya R Arakerimath, Aditya H. Badve, Divyani P. Deshmukh, M. N. Chandan, Avinash Badadhe
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies in Electronics and Green Energy (ICETEG)
abstract: This paper presents the performance modelling and simulation of a collaborative robot (cobot) for pick-and-place applications using RoboDK. A parametric regression-based model is proposed to evaluate the Techman TM5-900 cobot, integrated with a vision system, under realistic industrial conditions with components of plastic, aluminum, and mild steel. The study employs the Techman TM5-900 cobot, known for its integrated vision system and suitability for high- precision industrial applications. Components are positioned on a conveyor system and picked and placed onto predefined pallets, simulating real- world manufacturing conditions. Using RoboDK simulation software, the influence of three input parameters robot speed (x1), component placement variation (x2), and trajectory angle (x3) on three output performance metrics task completion time (01), <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$x$</tex>-direction placement deviation (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$02 x$</tex>), and y-direction placement deviation (03y) is analysed through multiple regression analysis. The results show that robot speed is inversely proportional to task completion time and plays a significant role in improving operational efficiency, while component alignment variation significantly affects placement accuracy, particularly in the y-direction, indicating its criticality in maintaining precision. Trajectory angle, however, exhibits negligible influence across all materials. The study demonstrates that material type affects the degree of sensitivity to input variations, with mild steel showing the highest model accuracy (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{2} \approx 98\%)$</tex> for placement deviation. Overall, the research highlights the adaptability, precision, and reliability of the TM5-900 cobot and provides actionable insights for optimizing collaborative robotic systems based on material properties and workspace variability in industrial automation.

title: Experimental Study of 2.4 GHz and 5 GHz Wireless Using OpenWrt on Raspberry Pi
authors: Toha Ardi Nugraha, Zubair Adam, Dafid Firmansyah, Widyasmoro
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Materials and Electrical Engineering (ISMEE)
abstract: Stable and low-cost network access is still a major challenge, especially in rural areas with limited infrastructure. Rural areas often lack reliable internet connections, and setting up standard network equipment is expensive and difficult. This paper presents the design and implementation of a local access point using a Raspberry Pi 3B+ and the OpenWrt operating system to support wireless communication over the 2.4 GHz and 5 GHz frequency bands. The OpenWrt platform provides an open-source framework for custom network configurations. Experimental results demonstrate that the Raspberry Pi 3B+ using OpenWrt enables stable and reliable local network performance. Based on the measurement results under both line-of-sight (LOS) and non-line-of-sight (NLOS) conditions, the 2.4 GHz band offers a stable transfer rate with lower bandwidth and no packet loss, while the 5 GHz band provides higher bandwidth and transfer rates of up to 86.6 MBytes at short distances from the transmitter to the receiver, also without any drops.

title: Techniques for Optimal Placement of EV Charging Stations in Microgrids: Methodological Insights
authors: Raghavendra. M, Jagadisha N, Sandeep Kumar K J, Sachin C S, Mahesh. M, Archana N V
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies in Electronics and Green Energy (ICETEG)
abstract: There has been a heightened use of electric vehicles which requires the improvement of charging systems within micro grids. This will protect grid stability, reduce energy wastage and make it easier for the users. This paper presents a comparative study of recent techniques used in deciding the position of charging stations for electric vehicles in micro grids, looking at the optimization algorithms, machine learning models, hybrid systems, and event driven models. Some techniques like Genetic Algorithms (GA) and Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) are quite efficient in static grid arrangement, but lack adaptability to movement. Static grids, on the other hand, do adapt easily. Learning machines, however, make accurate estimates for demand and real-time adjustments but need endless data sets and powerful machines. These methods are, however, very adaptable to huge systems, which are mostly AI based, but they consume a lot of processing power. In addition, they tend to focus on communication infrastructures for robust control. This technique makes it possible to control the grid efficiently without making so many changes to the system. This technique encapsulates all the power equitably within the grid and ensures that the grid is well supplied in terms of power. The writer focuses on the benefits and hurdles of those techniques, points out specific research lacks, and recommend next steps towards dealing with the charging infrastructure problem in micro grids.

title: Teachers' Perceptions and Challenges in Utilizing ChatGPT Basis for Developing a Training Program
authors: Kennedy P. Kabiling, Jeffrey S. Ingosan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: This study investigates teachers' perceptions, utilization patterns, and challenges in adopting ChatGPT, aiming to develop an evidence-based training program for responsible and effective AI integration in basic education. Using a mixed methods design, data were gathered from eighty public elementary school teachers in the Philippines to explore frequency of use, perceived usefulness, and contextual barriers. Results show universal awareness and widespread application of ChatGPT, particularly for assessment generation, lesson preparation, and professional tasks. Teachers reported strong perceived usefulness, yet expressed concerns regarding academic integrity, privacy, prompt construction, accuracy verification, and curriculum alignment. Inferential analysis revealed significant differences in acceptance and utilization across teaching experience groups, indicating varying levels of digital confidence. Qualitative themes further underscored issues of ethical use and technical constraints. Guided by the findings, a structured training program was developed to enhance teachers' digital competence, strengthen ethical and pedagogical practices, and support responsible AI adoption in educational settings.

title: Lightweight Interpretable CNN for Four-Class Lung Lesion Classification on CT
authors: Periasamy S, Venkata Karthika V, Vama Anjanikumar, Yarrapirammagari Vaseema, Vuturu Madhusudhan Reddy, Balamanikandan A
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: This research proposes a lightweight interpretable deep-learning pipeline for the classification of four-class lung lesions, namely Adenocarcinoma, Large Cell Carcinoma, Squamous Cell Carcinoma, and Normal from chest CT scans. Input images are standardized to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$256 \times 256$</tex> grayscale, and an extensive augmentation strategy has been followed to enhance generalization under a limited data condition. The proposed CNN consists of compact convolutional blocks with batch normalization and ReLU activation along with progressive channel expansion. These are followed by a densely connected classifier with strong dropout regularization. Explainability is provided through Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping that localizes discriminative regions which drive model decisions. The method achieved an overall accuracy of 93.06%, a precision of 95.53%, and an F1-score of 93.84% on a held-out test set while maintaining a low parameter count necessary for fast training and deployment. Qualitative results from Grad-CAM point toward an agreement between model attention and clinically relevant pathology - a testament to the suitability of the method in resource-constrained clinical settings.

title: CNNs, Transformers, and Beyond: AI Models for Crop Disease Detection - A Review
authors: Deepak Kumar Pathak, Lavika Goel, Aishwarya Mishra
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies in Electronics and Green Energy (ICETEG)
abstract: Crop disease detection plays a pivotal role in precision agriculture, influencing both food security and farmer productivity. This survey reviews recent advancements in artificial intelligence (AI) models-particularly Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), Transformer-based architectures, and hybrid approaches-applied to image-based disease identification. Through a comparative analysis of over 20 studies from 2021 to 2025, we report classification accuracies ranging from 84 % to 99.8 % across real-world datasets including PlantVillage, RARI, and various crop-specific image collections. CNNs such as ResNet50 and MobileNetV2 have shown reliable performance, achieving over <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 8 \%}$</tex> accuracy in controlled datasets. Meanwhile, Transformer-based models and attention mechanisms are increasingly favored for their scalability and ability to capture long-range dependencies, especially in complex or noisy field data. Hybrid models combining CNNs with nature-inspired optimization or attention layers further enhance detection accuracy and model robustness. However, practical challenges remain, including dataset imbalance, model generalization to different geographies, explainability, and edge-device deployment. This review not only synthesizes the current state of the art but also identifies critical research gaps and future directions, including the need for lightweight, explainable, and scalable solutions that can operate effectively in real agricultural environments.

title: Design of an Overcurrent Protection System Using a Mini PLC for Three-Phase Induction Motors in Process Machines
authors: A Fadhiil Apriansyah Z, M Bachtiar Nappu, Salama Manjang
publish: 2026 International Seminar on Intelligent Business and Edge-Computing Research (ISIBER)
abstract: Three-phase induction motors play a crucial role in industrial manufacturing due to their durability, cost-effectiveness, and minimal maintenance needs. Nevertheless, instances of overcurrent in inverters are still prevalent, particularly in deep frying applications, which can lead to motor impairments, unanticipated operational interruptions, and resultant production deficits. Between the years 2021 and 2023, there were seven documented overcurrent occurrences in frying motors, culminating in a cumulative downtime of 1.539 minutes, in addition to fifteen incidents involving damage to bowls and covers that caused further downtime of 2.431 minutes. This research endeavor seeks to investigate the underlying causes of overcurrent disturbances and to devise strategies aimed at enhancing machinery reliability at food processing industry. The methodologies employed encompass experimental evaluations conducted through simulations simulating no-load and blocked rotor conditions, alongside the analysis of current characteristics pertaining to three-phase induction motors. An automatic control system predicated on a mini PLC was conceived and executed to facilitate the early detection of disturbances, thereby averting potential failures. The outcomes of the implementation indicate that the developed system effectively diminishes the incidence of failures, minimizes downtime, and enhances the stability of machine operations. The incorporation of this control mechanism not only fortifies motor protection and management protocols but also prolongs the lifespan of components. This sophisticated control methodology presents a viable, applicable, and replicable solution for other sectors utilizing induction motors, while simultaneously contributing both theoretically to the evolution of control strategies and practically to industrial operational efficiency.

title: Elastic Net Regression with Bootstrap Performance Evaluation for Small-Sample Craniofacial Morphometric Prediction
authors: Mohamad Norikmal Fazli Hisam, Norli Anida Abdullah, Firdaus Hariri
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: This study applies Elastic Net regression with bootstrap evaluation to model craniofacial growth patterns using small-sample computed tomography (CT) data from 20 pediatric subjects with normal skull morphology. Five midfacial measurement models were fitted using cranial-base predictors to assess anatomical and statistical heterogeneity. Fine-grid mixing parameter selection <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\alpha \in[0, 1]$</tex>, step <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=\mathbf{0. 0 1})$</tex> revealed distinct regional regularization patterns (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\alpha=0.20-0.98$</tex>), indicating different growth behaviors between anterior and posterior facial structures. Anterior landmarks (ANS-PNS, N-ANS) achieved high predictive accuracy (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}>0.90$</tex>) with low RMSE and MAE, while posterior regions showed greater variability (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}=0.64-0.74$</tex>). Compared with existing regression-based craniofacial models, the proposed framework offers improved error stability and realistic confidence intervals through bootstrap resampling. These findings highlight the clinical potential of Elastic Net modeling for region-specific prediction and reconstruction planning in syndromic craniosynostosis.

title: Empirical Evaluation of Power Consumption of Custom Machine Learning Kernels and Cmsis Library-Based Kernels Implementations on Arm Cortex-M0 and Cortex-M4 Microcontrollers
authors: Jonathan Baraka, Mohammed Elmir, Nathan Amanquah
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: This paper presents an empirical evaluation of the power consumption of custom machine learning (ML) kernels in comparison to ML kernels developed using the ARM CMSIS on low-power microcontrollers. The study utilizes both Arm CortexM0/M0+ and Cortex-M4 architectures to examine how hardware features such as single instruction multiple data (SIMD) and floating-point units (FPUs) influence the power consumption of Support Vector Machine (SVM) and Artificial Neural Network (ANN) workloads on embedded devices. Four development boards were used: FRDM-KL25Z (Cortex-M0+), FRDM-K64F (CortexM4F), STM32 NUCLEO-F091RC (Cortex-M0), and STM32 NUCLEO-F446RE (Cortex-M4). Power measurements were obtained by recording the current consumption during execution while each board is powered with a 3.3 V supply. To ensure uniform comparison across similar vendor platforms, both FRDM boards were configured to operate at a clock frequency of 20.89 MHz, whereas the STM32 boards were evaluated across multiple frequencies (8-48 Mhz) to observe scaling effects. The results show that CMSIS libraries provide significant power savings on the Cortex-M4 due to SIMD and FPU utilization, whereas custom kernels achieve comparable or better efficiency on Cortex M0/M0+ devices. These findings highlight the importance of architecture-aware optimization when deploying ML workloads on resource-constrained embedded platforms.

title: Cyber-Creativity: Challenges and Frictions in Human-AI Collaboration
authors: Mario Mirabile, Angela Faiella, Lorenzo Campidelli, Giovanni Emanuele Corazza
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: This article addresses the challenges associated with the introduction of generative artificial intelligence (Gen-AI) in the creative process. Gen-AI yields both opportunities and diminishments to the human side of this cyber-creative collaboration. A decalogue of research challenges in cybercreativity is recollected, followed by the description of an experimental procedure in which a form of friction is introduced in the DA VINCI model for the creative process, applied to a shortstory creative writing task. The study tested the hypothesis that introducing friction in the attentional focus of the cyber-creative process would enhance self-perceived originality. Using a withinsubjects design, in the control condition participants did not modify the inputs received by Gen-AI, whereas in the friction condition they were forced to intervene on the Gen-AI input, to stimulate reflection and critical thinking. Preliminary results are presented, showing that the friction condition led to higher levels of originality with respect to the control condition, along with higher levels of perceived difficulty.

title: Advanced Hierarchical Classification Approach for Document Categorization
authors: Mamadou Alpha Hawa Balde, Pirlouit Dumez, Meriam Belgaroui, Guillaume Prevost, Salah Zidi
publish: 2025 IEEE Afro-Mediterranean Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AMCAI)
abstract: This paper introduces a novel hierarchical classification framework that effectively addresses overlapping features while enhancing interpretability, enabling users to better understand the rationale behind document categorization. The model manages semantic overlaps by constructing a dynamic tree structure that clusters related categories through iterative refinement, thereby boosting accuracy. This transparent and scalable approach consistently outperforms traditional flat classifiers. Tested on a dataset of 80,000 documents, the tree-based model achieved an accuracy of 93%, surpassing both SGD and deep learning models. Although challenges such as error propagation and data sparsity remain, the framework proves robust and adaptable for large-scale document management. Its scalability allows it to accommodate evolving and complex datasets.

title: Metaverse-New Platform for Digital Marketing to Target Generation Z
authors: Elena Zlatanova-Pazheva, Tanya Gigova
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Generation Z are immersed in the virtual world, consuming online content on a daily basis. As a result, the boundary between the physical and the virtual world is getting shorter in their minds. In order to reach and engage Generation Z, marketers need to be able to offer them an exciting type of experience tailored to their digital nature. Significant opportunities for this provide the concept of the metaverse, which is discussed. The meaning, trends and types of the Metaverse are discussed. The results of both qualitative and quantitative research methods among representatives of Generation Z in Plovdiv, Bulgaria are presented. The goal of the conducted research is to find out what is the attitude towards the metaverse of a young, receptive to new technologies generation like Z, which makes them a potential consumer of the metaverse.

title: Systematic Generation of a Dataset to Study University Dropout in Chile Using Public Data and Open Source Frameworks
authors: Alejandro Ferreira, Dabbo Campos, Dafne Lagos, Paola Leal, Carolina Reinecke
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
