abstract: In this paper, a structured and reproducible methodology is presented, to generate a dataset with a data quality that allows to study university dropout in Chile from multiple dimensions, such as academic, socioeconomic, demographic and temporal. The dropout variable was created during the process, since it was not reported in the initial sources. The methodology includes collecting and integrating multiple sources, each one composed by millions of records; cleaning and standardizing the fields, categorical and numerical (discrete or continuous); and local storage. The final dataset reached 594,224 records. This approach can be applied to other type of problems that seek to consolidate different sources in the build up of a quality dataset. An additional element is exclusive use of open source frameworks during the entire process.

title: 2025 II International Congress of Engineering with a Social Sense (CIISS)
authors: N/A
publish: N/A

title: Hierarchical Federated Multi-Cluster Fog Architecture with Dynamic Node Allocation for Massive Scale IoT Deployments
authors: Narek Naltakyan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Today's fog computing systems are struggling to meet the demands of deploying IoT systems consisting of millions of devices due to single points of failure and the fixed allocation of cloud resources. In this article, a new Hierarchical Federated Multi-Cluster Fog Architecture (HFMCFA) will be proposed to address these issues using a flexible approach to node allocation and a system of fault-tolerance with master-of-masters coordination. HFMCFA is composed of four primary layers: IoT devices, fog nodes with a gateway at the cluster level, regional masters, a master-of-masters layer, and a backup system (master-slave), which enhances the overall trustworthiness of HFMCFA. Each cluster contains one fog node and one master node, and 3-7 sleeping nodes, which can be activated instantaneously when the HFMCFA predicts an increase in the predicted load based on the HFMCFA's predictive algorithms. The size of each cluster will vary based on the frequency of requests, density of users, processing capacity, and latency limits for each model. The establishment of a master-slave layer will minimize any automatic service failure when the master node fails. The results of the tests show that the system is able to deal with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1, 0 0 0, 0 0 0 +}$</tex> IoT devices with the availability of 99 %, a response time of 67 % when compared to cloud-based approaches, and failure recovery in approximately 2.3 -seconds. Four times better scalability is shown when compared to normal single-cluster models, and it also keeps high energy efficiency due to its smart management of the sleeping node. Hence, the main problems of smart cities and industrial IoT are solved by our introduced architecture.

title: Design and Optimization of Wireless Power Transfer System with Resonant Converter
authors: Madhu B M., Sindhu K
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies in Electronics and Green Energy (ICETEG)
abstract: The present research work includes the design and implementation of a high-efficiency resonant power converter for wireless power transfer. This is developed with a series-parallel Class D topology that has been optimized to minimize the loss in power and maximize energy transfer efficiency. The proposed system is operated at a resonant frequency of 5 M H z, wherein the transmitter and receiver coils were used with an inductive coupling mechanism having mutual inductance of 3.7 \mu H to transmit the power. The main goal is to provide energy transfer through non-radiative near fields with a high probability of efficient and adaptive energy transfer under varying load conditions. The system uses MOSFET-based high-frequency switching with inductor-capacitor(LC) resonant circuitry to ensure impedance matching and resonance tuning. The experimental results show an average power transfer efficiency of ~ 90% with a DC output of 5 V, 1 A, 4.52 W, which could be used for charging mobile devices. The coupling conditions were simulated, and an embedded microcontroller-based data acquisition system was implemented for real-time monitoring of the voltage, current, and efficiency. Voltage regulation was maintained at ± 2% of the output across different operating scenarios. Thus, the real-time monitoring along with resonant power conversion technique makes this research scalable and practically viable for a wireless power transfer. The developed system was successfully implemented for wireless mobile phone charging, demonstrating its feasibility for consumer electronics applications while ensuring high efficiency and stable operation.

title: A Pilot Study for Real-Time Hunger Detection Based on EEG Signals and Visual ERPs
authors: Bechir Hbibi, Lamine Mili, Lilia Sidhom, Abdelkader Mami
publish: 2025 IEEE International Multi-Conference on Smart Systems &amp; Green Process (IMC-SSGP)
abstract: Emotions and basic needs such as fear, pain, hunger, or thirst could go unnoticed in some people due to mental or physical conditions. Yet, some of them can deeply affect well-being. When left unrecognized, these hidden states can disrupt daily life and lead to serious consequences. The detection of human needs is undergoing rigorous investigation, with applications that span the improvement of individual well-being or commercial enterprise. Electroencephalography is one of the most widely used tools in this field. In this research work, we invited 13 healthy participants after a night of fasting to investigate in real time the effects of watching a video of food images on their brain electrical activities using the EmotivPRO platform. The results show an increase in the level of attention in all participants when seeing food images; for some participants, the level of stress has also increased during this stage. This research is our first step in investigating the human brain reaction to initial needs such as thirst and hunger during event-related potential.

title: Aerospace Legacy - Pains or Opportunities
authors: Chinh Le
publish: 2025 IEEE Space Computing Conference (SCC)
abstract: Aerospace platforms have been around for decades. Their technologies are outdated legacy but continue to be adapted for use in aerospace, robotics, ground vehicles, industrial control and other applications. Their proven high reliability, fault-tolerant, deterministic capability and ability to operate in high electromagnetic interference enable them to be used for a long time to come. Legacy buses such as MIL-STD-1553 are here to stay. New aerospace standards, however, are based on Ethernetan open architecture which has been deployed everywhere. The new standards, however, are not compatible with legacy buses. Older buses operate at much higher voltage. Their protocols are more primitive and often centrally controlled rather than distributed - more parallelism. Working with them is not so easy as prime developers generally have the strangle hold on the technologies. Designers/developers for legacy systems may even no longer exist. New generations of developers do not learn about them as Ethernet is more widely taught. It is a pain point in the industry but also opportunities. This paper will discuss the applications, technologies of legacy standards such as 1553 and newer Time Triggered Ethernet (TTE) and Time Sensitive Network (TSN) defined by SAE and IEEE for future aerospace networking and other applications. It will also discuss opportunities, technical challenges for bridging new standards into legacy aerospace platforms allowing them to co-exist. Lastly, it will discuss efforts in standard organizations to standardize the interoperability of the legacy buses to newer standards.

title: Requirements Engineering with Generative AI: Improving Requirements Development Efficiency with Structured Prompt Technique (SPT)
authors: Kavish Nayeem, Kadir Alpaslan Demir, Chandra N. Sekharan, Bozhen Liu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Software requirements development is a crucial part of the software process. Requirements are among the most important artifacts of software projects. However, requirements development is challenging as requirements are commonly developed in natural language. Hence, automation in the requirements development process was limited until recently. Recent advances in Large Language Models (LLMs) and particularly in Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI) have created opportunities for software development, especially in areas that involve natural language specifications, such as requirements engineering. In this research, we focus on improving the requirements development efficiency using GenAI and structured prompts. We call this approach the Structured Prompt Technique (SPT). In the software process, the SPT can be used during the requirements development phase after the project vision is defined. For this research, we developed a case study to compare the efficiency that can be achieved in requirements development with 3 different methods: Traditional manual requirements development by humans, automated requirements development with GenAI utilizing conversational prompts, and automated requirements development with GenAI utilizing SPT. For the case study, we developed the requirements for a generic online marketplace application using these 3 different methods. Our research results indicate that we can achieve 8 times improvement in efficiency with automated requirements development with GenAI utilizing conversational prompts, and almost 15 times improvement in efficiency with automated requirements development with GenAI utilizing SPT compared to manual requirements development. Overall, the requirements development with Generative AI has the potential to reduce the traditional manual requirements development process by over <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 0 \%}$</tex>. This research shows the significant potential of utilizing GenAI in requirements engineering.

title: Leveraging AutoML and Agentic AI for Graduate University Admission Forecasting
authors: Samiksha Dattaprasad Tawde, Anjali Naik, Dhanshree Kokare
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Graduate University admission is a critical decisionmaking process for students seeking higher education, yet it is often characterized by uncertainty due to competitive selection criteria and varying admission standards across institutions. Accurate prediction of admission outcomes can significantly empower applicants by identifying universities where they have a higher likelihood of acceptance, thereby optimizing their application strategy and resource allocation. In this study, we propose an automated framework leveraging AutoML model such as AutoGluon and Agentic AI to predict graduate admission probabilities based on historical applicant data and institutional selection patterns. The system also provides actionable insights by analyzing key parameters-such as academic performance, standardized test scores, research experience, and Letter of Recommendations-that influence admission decisions. By highlighting factors that can enhance a candidate's chances, the proposed approach offers a competitive advantage, enabling students to make informed choices and improve their likelihood of successful admission. Experimental evaluation demonstrates the potential of integrating automated machine learning with agentic reasoning to support proactive, data-driven guidance for prospective graduate students.

title: Byzantine-Robust Asynchronous Federated Learning via Feature Fingerprinting
authors: Meng Shen, Bohan Peng, Yi Zhao, Yue Qin, Ming Li, Qi Li, Liehuang Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Asynchronous federated learning (AFL) accelerates collaborative training across heterogeneous devices compared to synchronous federated learning, but increases vulnerability to Byzantine attacks due to its asynchronous aggregation. Existing defenses rely on parametric similarity between models and temporal consistency of updates, which are compromised by data and device heterogeneity, leading to ineffective robustness. To address this limitation, we propose Belisa, a Byzantine-robust AFL framework that enhances fidelity, robustness, and efficiency under heterogeneous scenarios. Belisa introduces novel discrepancies between feature representations of local models to distinguish malicious models from benign ones. By leveraging a reference model trained on publicly available data, Belisa quantifies these discrepancies, referred to as feature fingerprints, and filters out malicious models through clustering. Extensive experiments on six datasets from three types of tasks under five advanced Byzantine attacks demonstrate Belisa’s superiority. Notably, Belisa consistently outperforms existing approaches across both attack and non-attack settings. Under attack scenarios, it lowers the average test error rate to 0.42× that of baseline methods. Furthermore, Belisa accelerates the aggregation process by an average of 12.3× compared to other methods. To the best of our knowledge, Belisa is the first Byzantine-robust AFL framework, which provides a broadly applicable countermeasure in heterogeneous scenarios which are more prevalent in real-world settings.

title: ACDSA 2026 Attendee List

title: Early Detection of IoT Botnet Attacks Using a Hybrid Autoencoder-GRU Model on Real-World Data
authors: Merzoug Soltane, Tabet Khaoula
publish: 2025 IEEE International Multi-Conference on Smart Systems &amp; Green Process (IMC-SSGP)
abstract: The proliferation of Internet of Things (IoT) devices has revolutionized various domains but also introduced significant security challenges, notably botnet attacks that compromise device integrity and enable large-scale malicious activities. Existing detection methods often focus on post-propagation phases and rely on simulated datasets, limiting their effectiveness in real-world scenarios. In this paper, we propose a novel hybrid detection model combining an Autoencoder with Gated Recurrent Units (AE-GRU) to identify botnet attacks at an early propagation stage. Our approach leverages temporal dependencies in network traffic behavior and is evaluated on the MedBIoT dataset, which contains real malware infections across a heterogeneous network of physical and emulated IoT devices. Experimental results demonstrate the model's high accuracy and robustness in early-stage botnet detection, high-lighting its potential for proactive IoT security.

title: Neural Network Architecture and Feature Extraction in sEMG Signals: Implications for Exoskeletons Control
authors: Ian Marciscano, Ilka Banfield, Humberto Rodriguez
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Surface electromyographic signal (sEMG) allows anticipating motor intention with low latency, a key quality for robust exoskeleton control. This paper reviews the state of the art in feature extraction and its coupling with neural network architectures for classification and regression in human-robot control. We first systematize a taxonomy of time, frequency, and time-frequency traits, and discuss practical window and overlap, preprocessing, and tagging parameters that impact end-to-end latency. We then analyze CNN, (Bi)LSTM, hybrid with attention, and TCN architectures, highlighting their trade-off between accuracy and inference time, as well as domain adaptation strategies (LOSO, transfer) for subject-independent generalization. Based on this synthesis, we propose design guidelines: window co-design and network complexity with the RT < hop criterion; Normalization by channel and window and end-to-end latency reporting. Finally, we outline the integration of a CNN model with BiLSTM with attention to a semi-active exoskeleton for agriculture developed by the Technological University of Panama, as a case of real-time application.

title: An Approach of Phishing Detection Using Representative Learning with Visual Recognition
authors: Hieu Le Ngoc
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: This paper presents an innovative approach to detecting phishing attacks by leveraging representation learning within machine learning frameworks. This study focuses on enhancing the accuracy and effectiveness of cybercrime defense mechanisms, specifically targeting phishing, where malicious actors forge URLs to deceive users and acquire sensitive information. The methodology involves transforming URLs into square matrices. These matrices are subsequently converted into grayscale images of varying sizes. These images are then utilized as input for training phishing prediction models using ResNet-18 and ResNet-34 deep learning architectures. Experimental results demonstrate promising outcomes for both models. For ResNet-18, training set accuracies range from 73.64% to 93.24%, with test set accuracies between 58.97% and 68.85 %. For ResNet-34, training set accuracies range from 76.04% to 89.41%, with test set accuracies between 63.78% and 73.14%. Deeper ResNet-34 consistently achieves better generalization, reflected in higher test set accuracies across multiple image resolutions. Findings suggest that the proposed representation learning technique, coupled with advanced deep learning architectures, offers a viable path toward improving phishing attack detection and contributes to robust cybersecurity measures.

title: Real-Time Operating Systems (RTOS) for IoT Applications: A Comprehensive Comparative Study
authors: Mohammed Altoumi, Nuredin Ali Salem Ahmed
publish: 2025 IEEE International Multi-Conference on Smart Systems &amp; Green Process (IMC-SSGP)
abstract: This comparative study provides a comprehensive analysis of three prominent Real-Time Operating System (RTOS) platforms: FreeRTOS, Zephyr OS, and RT-Thread, with specific focus on their memory footprint, scheduling mechanisms, and suitability for Internet of Things (IoT) applications including smart home devices, industrial sensors, and wearable technology. The research examines the comparative performance of two fundamental real-time scheduling algorithms: Rate Monotonic (RM) and Earliest Deadline First (EDF), providing detailed insights into their theoretical foundations and practical implementations in resource-constrained IoT environments. Performance benchmarks demonstrate that FreeRTOS achieves superior interrupt response times (223 cycles vs. Zephyr's 524 cycles), while Zephyr provides enhanced security features and broader hardware support across 600+ development boards. The study reveals that algorithm selection significantly impacts system utilization, with EDF achieving up to 92% CPU utilization compared to RM's 69 % theoretical limit. This work introduces a unified comparative framework and taxonomy for RTOS and scheduling algorithm evaluation in IoT domains, offering structured criteria and practical guidance for selecting optimal platforms across diverse application scenarios.

title: Multipath-Enhanced Measurement of Antenna Patterns: Experiment
authors: Daniel D. Stancil, Alexander R. Allen, Michael Ptak, Jacob J. Adams
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: In a companion paper we presented the theory for an antenna pattern measuring technique that uses (rather than mitigates) the properties of a multipath environment. Here we use measurements in a typical home garage, a university research lab, and an anechoic chamber with and without scattering objects to experimentally demonstrate the feasibility of the technique. A half-wavelength electric dipole with different orientations was used as both the calibration and test antennas. For simplicity, we limited the modeling of the antenna pattern to using only the three l = 1 vector spherical harmonics. Three methods were used to analyze the measurements: a matrix inversion method using only 3 sense antennas, a least-square-error technique, and a least-square-error technique with a constant power constraint imposed. The two least-square-error techniques used the measurements from 10 sense antennas. The constrained least-squareerror technique was found to give the best results.

title: Multi-layer Security for Wireless BCIs via Adversarial Chaos Synergy
authors: Majid Mobini, Georges Kaddoum, Marijan Herceg
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: While we benefit from the capabilities of Denoising Auto-Encoders (DAEs), some security risks also arise with the widespread adoption of these systems. Conventional chaos masking and jamming defenses do not protect against DAE-equipped eavesdroppers. Chaotic masking adds randomness to signals and helps with security, but it cannot stop detection by a DAE. To tackle this problem, we design a secure Brain-Computer Interface (BCI) using Long Short-Term Memories (LSTMs) that combines adversarial training and chaotic signals. We formulate an optimization problem to model the proposed BCI and generate a low-power perturbation vector. The results indicate that when the noise variance is equal to 0.2, the Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) of an illegal DAE exceeds 1.2. At the same time, the non-adversarial performance is approximately 0.5. Moreover, the performances of different Neural Networks are evaluated under the same computational complexity conditions. The best result is provided by the LSTM, with a complexity of about 3 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</sup> Real Multiplications per Symbol (RMpS). The proposed Adversarial Chaos Synergy (ACS) framework is compatible with cloud/edge infrastructure. With these features, the ACS approach is suitable for applications such as mobile medical services, lightweight bio-medical instruments, brain-to-brain networks, and smart hospitals.

title: Spectral Efficiency Considerations in 6G Networks
authors: Joseph Boccuzzi
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: As wireless connectivity continues to evolve toward 6G, the demand to efficiently deliver higher throughput, lower latency, and improved reliability increases. Efficiency has been traditionally quantified in terms of spectral efficiency (SE) and energy efficiency (EE). Herein, a new radio access technology (RAT) system metric, radio resource utilization efficiency (RUE), is introduced. RUE measures the efficiency with which the considered RAT utilizes available radio resources (spectrum, access methods, time slots, and data symbols) to meet future 6G demands for downlink (DL) and uplink (UL) transmissions. The performance of typical cellular and cell-free massive multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) deployments is compared to demonstrate the need for considering RUE when designing 6G networks. Factors influencing SE are investigated across three categories: 5G infrastructure, practical limitations (such as channel matrix rank deficiency) and implementation losses (signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio degradation). For the example RAT configuration, 5G infrastructure yields a DL-RUE of 47% and UL-RUE of 40%, revealing significant room for improvement when defining 6G. Practical limitation assumptions are based on 5G single-user MIMO and multiuser MIMO measurements conducted in commercialized deployments. The resulting SE losses are characterized to offer guidance for advanced algorithms employing machine learning–based techniques. The benefits of increasing transmission bandwidth from 100 MHz to 1.6 GHz are also demonstrated, and a next-generation radio access network architecture that can support 6G and artificial-intelligence-enabled radio access networks is described.

title: QoE-ensured DDoS Mitigation in NOMA-assisted Edge Computing: A Game-theoretical Approach
authors: Aiping Wang, Lin Liu, Guangming Cui, Yuanjiaoyang Li, Ruichen Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: In Multi-access Edge Computing (MEC) environments, Non-Orthogonal Multiple Access (NOMA) technology significantly improves spectrum efficiency and system access capacity through power-domain multiplexing. However, it also exposes edge servers to more complex resource contention and security risks. In particular, during Distributed Denial-of-Service (DDoS) attacks, conventional mitigation mechanisms often overlook users' quality of experience (QoE), which can lead to prolonged service degradation. To address this challenge, this paper investigates a QoE-guaranteed Edge DDoS Mitigation problem (QEDM) in NOMA-assisted MEC systems by jointly considering request scheduling and power allocation. First, the QEDM problem is modeled as a finite-strategy potential game, and a distributed game-theoretic algorithm, QEDMGame, is proposed. Then, through a multi-player best-response mechanism, the system converges to a Nash equilibrium, thereby achieving joint optimization of service rate and QoE. Next, experimental results on real-world base station deployment data demonstrate that QEDMGame outperforms several representative baseline methods. Additionally, theoretical analysis shows that QEDMGame exhibits strong convergence properties and performance guarantees.

title: AC Loss Calculation and Analysis in Armature Windings for Superconducting Electrical Machines
authors: Othman Taalibi, Tabea Arndt
publish: IEEE Transactions on Applied Superconductivity
abstract: AC losses in armature windings arise from time varying currents and fields, raising effective resistance and lowering efficiency. We compare three-phase armature winding losses for Litz wire and REBCO high-temperature superconductor (HTS) tapes, and examine how turn count and operating temperature affect them. For Litz windings, we use a hybrid analytical model that captures skin and proximity effects. For HTS windings, we develop a time-domain model that includes the angle and field dependent critical current density j<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">c</sub> (B,θ) and power-law exponent n (B,θ). Both models are validated against finite-element method (FEM) simulations. We then generate torque–speed loss maps under constraints from the drive and the cryocooler. The results provide a clear comparison of Litz and HTS armature windings across the entire operating speed range and offer recommendations for efficiency-oriented machine design and control.

title: EWSbp-SNN: Error-weighted STDP with Backpropagation for Data Classification in Spiking Neural Networks
authors: Jingjing Liu, Lingling Miu, Yuchun Wu, Mengjiao Li, Yanan Liu, Jianhua Zhang
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Spiking neural networks (SNNs) have exhibited remarkable potential in neuromorphic data classification, especially in processing dynamic vision sensor (DVS) data. However, SNNs still have challenges in improving classification accuracy and in mitigating vanishing and exploding gradients. To address these challenges, we propose the error-weighted STDP with backpropagation for data classification in spiking neural networks (EWSbp-SNN). It combines error-weighted STDP (EWS) with backpropagation (BP) for weight updates, and incorporates dynamic threshold batch normalization (DTBN). Firstly, in order to improve the classification accuracy, the local EWS approach utilizes a weight factor calculated based on the network prediction error. This factor integrates the importance information of connections from the previous layer to perform the first local weight update, effectively suppressing the influence of irrelevant or noisy features. Afterward, BP is applied to perform the second global weight update, ensuring consistent optimization across time steps. In addition, to address the problem of vanishing and exploding gradients, the DTBN technique is presented, which normalizes data based on dynamic threshold variations during forward propagation, ensuring timely gradient adjustments in the local weight updates. Experiment results demonstrate that the proposed method significantly improves training accuracy and achieves state-of-the-art performance in gradient smoothing for classification tasks. Especially, it achieves an average accuracy of 8.02% higher than other methods on the DVS-CIFAR10 dataset. The source code will be available at https://github.com/linglingMiu/EWSNN.

title: A Novel Approach with Channel Reduction Scheme for Depression Symptom Identification based on Resting-State EEG
authors: Qing Li, Linmin Hu, Xuan Wang, Ruiyuan Wu, Xixian Fang, Chunliang Wang, Xinjie Dai, Chunhui Yang, Ruitong Zhang, Yang Zhao, Jianbang Xiang
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Resting-state electroencephalography (EEG) offers a scalable approach to quantify affective neurophysiology. Using data from eighty-one young adults aged 18–25 years, this study proposes a systematic framework to address limitations in the integration of multidimensional EEG features and the insufficient characterization of their spatial distribution. We first extracted a comprehensive set of time-, frequency-, time–frequency, nonlinear, and connectivity features to capture the multiscale neural patterns associated with depression symptom. A statistical feature-selection procedure based on the two-sided Mann–Whitney U test was applied to identify robust discriminative EEG markers. Using the full 64-channel feature set, the constructed model achieved strong overall performance (AUC = 0.958), providing a reliable baseline for subsequent spatial analyses. The selected features were then mapped onto electrode locations to characterize their spatial distribution, and a progressive channel-reduction analysis was performed to determine the minimal electrode set needed to preserve predictive performance. The channel-reduction analysis indicates that using only 5, 8, or 12 electrodes still maintains high performance, with AUC values of 0.879, 0.918, and 0.948, respectively. This framework provides a reproducible foundation for developing low-density, wearable EEG systems for mental-health monitoring.

title: Adaptive Beamforming Method for RIS-Assisted Communication System with Interference Suppression
authors: Tao Luo, Peng Chen, Mengyao Yang, Zhimin Chen, Xianbin Wang, Fan Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: In reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS)-assisted communication systems, the amplitudes and phases of reflected electromagnetic waves are modified to enhance signal power and provide additional communication paths, particularly in scenarios lacking a line-of-sight (LOS) propagation path. However, conventional RIS-aided communication systems reflect all incident signals indiscriminately, without distinguishing between desired signals and interference. In this paper, we propose a novel RIS-assisted communication system capable of selectively reflecting only the desired signal by performing interference suppression directly at the RIS. By exploiting the degree of freedom (DOF) in spatial domain of RIS, the design of RIS coefficients is formulated as a minimum variance distortionless response (MVDR) problem, where the optimal RIS coefficients are obtained by estimating the interference subspace and the desired signal. Then, we propose a two-step iterative approach based on atomic norm minimization (ANM) to estimate the subspace and desired signal simultaneously. The resulting ANM problem is solved with alternative optimization by decomposing it into two subproblem. Each subproblem is formulated as semidefinite programming (SDP) problem and efficiently solved using the alternating direction method of multipliers (ADMM). Theoretical analysis is conducted to verify the estimations are consistent and bounded. Simulation results validate its effectiveness and superiority over existing methods.

title: Eigenvalue Patterns and Participation Analysis of Symmetric Renewable Energy Power Systems
authors: Yao Qin, Yitong Li, Wei Wang, Shaoze Zhou, Zheng Wei, Jinjun Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Systems
abstract: State-space analysis is widely employed for examining power system dynamics but faces challenges in large-scale power systems integrated with numerous inverter-based resources (IBRs), where the significant increase of system states complicates modal analysis. Notably, renewable energy power systems often consist of multiple homogeneous generation units. This uniformity, termed symmetry in this paper, can facilitate the system stability analysis. Eigenvalue patterns and participation factors in three types of symmetric renewable energy power systems are investigated, including ideally-, quasi-, and group-symmetric systems. An ideally-symmetric (or quasi-symmetric) system comprises a group of identical (or similar) subsystems connected to an external grid. A system containing multiple such groups is termed group-symmetric. In these symmetric systems, two types of modes are defined to characterize different interactions: inner-group modes, which describe the interactions among subsystems within a single group, and group-grid modes, which describe the interactions between the groups and the external grid. A new concept termed group participation factor is also proposed to extend the use of conventional participation factors for repeated and close modes. In addition, the invariance properties of the inner-group modes and group-grid modes are discussed. The findings provide insights for stability analysis and targeted optimization in power systems. Theoretical advances are validated through numerical results and electromagnetic transient (EMT) simulations on example power systems of varied types and scales.

title: A Shifted Window Attention-Based Lightweight Model for Massive MIMO CSI Feedback
authors: Jian Ma, Ran Li, Lei Xia, Nan Hu, Tong Liu, Jingwei Dai
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications Letters
abstract: In massive multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) systems, balancing Channel State Information (CSI) feedback accuracy and transmission efficiency is a critical challenge. This letter proposes a Lightweight Shifted Window Attention Network (LightSWNet), a symmetric encoder-decoder neural network built on shifted-window attention. It integrates a novel Dual Window-Shift (DWS) block to capture cross-window CSI contextual information with low complexity. The fixed-dimension design and decoupled compression in the encoder-decoder ensure stable training and high-fidelity reconstruction. Experimental results demonstrate LightSWNet outperforms baselines in normalized mean square error (NMSE) improvement and Floating-Point Operations (FLOPs) reduction for most scenarios, offering a practical solution for resource-constrained massive MIMO CSI feedback.

title: An Explainable Deep Forest Approach for Modeling Actual Evapotranspiration in Arid Northwest China Using Multi-Source Data
authors: Yuan Wu, Haibo Wang, Yi'na Hu, Xufeng Wang, Kaixuan Liu, Liying Geng, Junlei Tan
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Accurate estimation of actual evapotranspiration (ETa) in arid regions is essential for effective water resource management. Despite notable advances in ETa modeling, limited research has systematically addressed the joint optimization of variable selection, model development, and interpretability. To fill this gap, we performed variables selection through correlation analysis and design eight input strategies that integrate key meteorological and remote sensing variables. Five state-of-the-art machine learning models-Artificial Neural Network (ANN), Random Forest (RF), Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost), Tabular Neural Network (TabNet), and Deep Forest (DF)-are employed for ETa prediction. To enhance model interpretability, we apply SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) and Permutation Importance to quantify the contribution of each input variable. Our findings demonstrate that the DF model combined with the S6 input strategy (a combination of six key meteorological and remote sensing variables), denoted as DF6, delivers the best predictive performance. Leveraging this optimal configuration, we generate a high-resolution ETa dataset for Northwest China, spanning from 2001 to 2024 at 8-day intervals with 5 km spatial resolution. Interpretability analysis highlights net radiation (Rn) as the most influential factor, exhibiting a strong positive effect on ETa. Furthermore, Mann-Kendall trend analysis reveals a significant upward trend in ETa, particularly in the northwestern and southeastern subregions. This study presents a robust and interpretable modeling framework that improves ETa estimation under data-limited conditions and supports long-term hydrological analysis and sustainable water management in arid regions.

title: A Polarized Thin-Film Optical Model Based on Dual-Beam Interference for Quantitative Characterization of Marine Oil Films
authors: Qintuan Xu, Zhenduo Zhang, Ying Li, Yong Wang, Zhen Wang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Marine oil spills require reliable quantitative characterization to support accurate detection and thickness retrieval in remote sensing applications. However, existing optical approaches often treat thin-film interference, polarization, and extinction as weakly coupled processes, limiting physical interpretability across oil types and thickness ranges. This study develops a polarized thin-film optical model for air-oil-water interfaces by explicitly coupling dual-beam interference, polarization, and experimentally constrained wavelength-dependent extinction. Laboratory measurements demonstrate that oil-layer extinction exhibits pronounced nonlinear spectral dependence that cannot be captured by conventional simplified formulations. Incorporating this experimentally constrained extinction into the polarized interference framework reveals distinct interference- and extinction-dominated optical regimes, with enhanced sensitivity to thin oil films in the ultraviolet-blue spectral region. For thicknesses below ∼50 μm, coherent thin-film interference dominates, producing pronounced wavelength-dependent oscillations, whereas at greater thicknesses increasing extinction within the oil layer suppresses secondary reflections and drives polarization signatures toward saturation. The model further demonstrates that the degree of linear polarization (DOLP) shows opposite monotonic trends with increasing thickness at incident angles of 35° and 65°, highlighting the intrinsic geometry dependence of polarization-based retrievals. Comparisons with laboratory measurements at 488-590 nm show excellent agreement, with near-zero mean residuals (|μ| = 0.0001). These results establish a physically rigorous and experimentally validated framework for polarization-based remote sensing of marine oil films.

title: Baseline Design Method of GB-TomoArcSAR Based on Coding Optimization
authors: Weiming Tian, Kai Yan, Yunkai Deng, Xin Xie, Yuanhao Li
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Due to the limitation of lacking elevation resolution, ground-based interferometric radar suffers from serious layover problem when applied to building deformation monitoring. Ground-based tomographic arc-scanning synthetic aperture ra dar (GB-TomoArcSAR) enables tomography by controlling the antenna to scan multiple times in the horizontal plane of different elevation angles to form an arc-shaped synthetic aperture in the elevation direction. However, when GB-TomoArcSAR uses a baseline with uniformly distributed elevation angles, the excessive number of baseline samples leads to the low efficiency of tomog raphy. To address this issue, this paper proposes a tomographic baseline design method based on coding optimization (TBDCO). Firstly, the sampling positions in the elevation direction are bina ry coded. Then, with the elevation resolution and peak side-lobe ratio (PSLR) of the antenna pointing pattern satisfying specific constraints, a quality evaluation model of the tomographic base line is constructed by weighted summation of the sampling num ber and the Cramer Rao bound (CRB) estimation accuracy in the elevation direction. Finally, the genetic algorithm is employed to iteratively solve the optimal coding to achieve sparse design of the tomographic baseline. Validation using both simulated and measured data demonstrates that the proposed method can re duce the number of elevation samples to 42% of the original before optimization while maintaining tomographic performance, substantially shortening the time for collecting measured data.

title: An Adaptive Global-Local Collaborative Encoding-Decoding Network for Remote Sensing Change Detection
authors: Wenbin Tang, Xingming Xiao, Shuli Cheng, Anyu Du
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: With the rapid development of deep learning techniques, Transformer-based methods for remote sensing change detection (RSCD) have achieved significant breakthroughs. However, these approaches often lose subtle local change information when extracting multi-level feature maps, and the late-stage feature fusion lacks sufficient interaction between bi-temporal features. To address these limitations, we propose an Adaptive Global-Local Collaborative Encoder-Decoder Network.At each layer of the encoder, an Adaptive Global-Local Attention (AGLA) module is introduced, leveraging the powerful multi-head attention mechanism to capture global contextual information. Simultaneously, adaptive channel selection with different convolutional kernels is employed to focus on local change details, enabling multi-dimensional interaction between global and local information in the encoder.Furthermore, we propose a Multi-scale Large Kernel Channel Attention (MLCA) module, which utilizes different convolutional kernels to capture diverse spatial information while compressing feature width and height, thereby effectively fusing bi-temporal feature representations. This fusion mechanism integrates changing semantic information and enhances the collaborative representation of global and local features. Finally, the change map is generated through layer-by-layer decoding.Comparative experiments conducted on three remote sensing datasets demonstrate that the AGLEDNet model outperforms other state-of-the-art methods in detection performance.

title: Effects of Induced Current from High-Voltage Transmission Lines on Voltage-Limiting Device Operation in Electrified Monorail Systems
authors: Warayut Kampeerawat, Nakaret Kano, Kittiphan Nawakiattikorn, Chaiyut Sumpavakup
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Voltage-limiting devices (VLDs) are installed in DC railways to limit rail potential and touch voltage by momentarily bonding the negative return conductor to the station grounding grid. Although VLDs act on the return circuit, their switching changes the DC network topology and can also affect the positive-conductor voltage that supplies traction loads. In elevated urban corridors, DC feeders may run alongside high-voltage AC (HVAC) transmission lines, where electromagnetic coupling can inject an AC-induced current into the traction network. This paper studies the combined influence of VLD operation and HVAC-induced current on an operational monorail. A coupled train-movement and nodal DC-network model is developed in MATLAB and applied to the Bangkok Yellow Line. Five case studies are considered: baseline multi-train operation, forced VLD operation, magnetic-field estimation versus HVAC separation distance, induced-current levels of 50–200 A, and headway sensitivity. Results show that VLD closure clamps the negative-conductor potential near ground but can lower the local minimum positive-conductor voltage by about 30–40 V near the operating station. When induced current is present, both conductor potentials rise, with the negative conductor being more sensitive (approximately 45–178% increase over the examined range). Increasing headway reduces traction-driven rail-potential excursions and lessens the impact of VLD switching; however, induced-current effects remain noticeable under light traffic. The findings support practical VLD siting and threshold selection for monorail corridors co-located with HVAC infrastructure.

title: IEEE TechDefense 2025 in Rome, Italy
authors: Pasquale Daponte, Alfonso Farina, Giovanni Savoldelli Pedrocchi
publish: IEEE Aerospace and Electronic Systems Magazine
abstract: This report deals with the activities carried out during the IEEE International Workshop on Technology for Defense and Security held in Rome, Italy on November 5-7, 2025.

title: Juggler: A Dynamic Warp Scheduler for GPGPUs
authors: Mohammad Nasser, Nitesh Narayana GS, Abhijit Das, Debiprasanna Sahoo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computer-Aided Design of Integrated Circuits and Systems
abstract: General-Purpose Graphics Processing Units (GPGPUs) accelerate various applications, ranging from graphics rendering to scientific simulations and machine learning (ML). The warp scheduler is a critical element in GPGPUs. It needs to manage thread execution in a way that optimizes performance as well as efficiency. However, different applications, and even different phases of the same application, have varying performance requirements. Hence, fixed static scheduling algorithms are suboptimal. This brief introduces a dynamic warp scheduler named Juggler, which dynamically switches between different scheduling strategies at runtime based on phase-specific performance characteristics. Juggler predicts and switches to the most suitable scheduling mode at runtime while improving the overall performance. Evaluations demonstrate that Juggler improves performance by up to 24.84%, with an average gain of 4.66% over state-of-the-art schedulers. These results emphasize the capability of dynamic scheduling to unlock even greater computational power of the GPGPUs.

title: CNN-Based Image Recognition System for Indian Cattle and Buffalo Breed Identification
authors: Mohammed Ammaar Nalkhande, Divya Munot, Bhagyalaxmi Kanchalwar
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Multimedia, Signal Processing and Communication Technologies (IMPACT)
abstract: India is reported to have the largest number of native cattle breeds, with strong agricultural, dairy, and rural livelihood value. Due to the high similarity among breeds and cross-breeding further increases this similarity, precise breed identification remains an issue. Further, automated breed detection methods have not been established as traditional methods include the application of refined expert expertise, or pairs of individuals, such as is subjective, time-consuming. The latest developments in deep learning and computer vision could provide the solution. The work suggests the application of a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN)-oriented approach for an automated, image-based recognition method for Indian cattle breeds. The model is trained and tested on Kaggle's open-source datasets, as well as other government stores, utilizing multiple indigenous breeds such as the Gir, Sahiwal, Red Sindhi, Ongole, etc. The proposed system achieves an overall accuracy of 77.2 % which outperforms the ResNet-50 based model (73% accuracy), demonstrating generalization across visually similar breeds. The experimental outcome as well as the comparison discussion on existing methods revealed that architectures based on CNN outperformed traditional machine-algorithm methods on the grounds of accuracy and robustness.

title: A Microcontroller-Based Frequency Readout Circuit for a Vibrating Wire Sensor
authors: Shafqat Imam, Musab Bin Khaleeq, Wajahat Masood, Zeeshan Sarwar, Safia Akhtar Kazmi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Multimedia, Signal Processing and Communication Technologies (IMPACT)
abstract: A common kind of sensor used to monitor the stress placed on supports in buildings and other civil infrastructure is the vibrating wire pressure transducer-based sensor. This paper aims to develop and build a microcontroller-based readout unit specifically designed for precise measurement of the resonant frequency of a vibrating wire sensor. An experimental setup is presented with the microcontroller ATMEGA-328P, which is used as the primary control of the entire system. This includes an excitation and frequency measurement circuit with sophisticated algorithms, a sampling approach, and microcontroller integration with suitable signal conditioning circuitry. The mathematical modelling of each circuit is analysed and implemented using LTspice software before the design of the hardware readout unit circuit.

title: Intelligent Algorithm Transplant and Verification System
authors: Zhening Dong, Qing Luo, Dongyue Yang, Yang Liu
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: With the continuous emergence of unmanned platform equipment types and exponential growth in number, the demand for intelligent upgrading of unmanned aerial vehicles, rapid iterative development of intelligent unmanned platforms, tactical combat simulation deduction, etc. is increasing day by day. However, traditional waterfall development system process is lengthy and lacks integrated rapid verification means. Therefore, there is an urgent need to build a new “design-simulation-implementation-test-verification” integrated unmanned platform development system to effectively improve the development iteration speed of unmanned platforms. The intelligent algorithm transplantation and verification systemimplemented in this paper constructsalgorithm analysis component (algorithm interface tool), algorithm function development component, algorithm basic operation tool, algorithm interface conversion development support tool, intelligent algorithm data excitation equipment, intelligent algorithm transplantation running result analysis equipment, and realizes the transplantation of intelligent algorithms in airborne embedded intelligent computing environment.

title: Comparative Evaluation of Machine Learning Models and Feature Identification for ASD Detection
authors: Neeru, Prashant Upadhyaya, Gaganjot Kaur
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Multimedia, Signal Processing and Communication Technologies (IMPACT)
abstract: The rapidly advancing fields of Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning have transformed the living standards of all individuals. This transformation is reshaping everything, including healthcare. AI and ML have changed the ways of living, learning, interacting, and working in the current digital era. As a result, these changes have a substantial impact on autism-related traits and identification. Autism spectrum disorder is a set of neurological-developmental related disorders in which individuals face challenges in adapting to change. Both toddlers and adults usually experience difficulties in communication, social interaction, and behavioral flexibility. Conventional screening and diagnostic tools are often time-consuming and costly, require expertise to interpret symptoms, yet may still fail to capture the variability of symptoms across different stages of life. In contrast, Machine Learning (ML) tools offer accurate, faster, and automated detection, thereby supporting early diagnosis, improving reliability, and reducing the subjectivity commonly associated with conventional assessment techniques. Hence, ML tools contribute to improved healthcare by aligning with SDG 3 and promoting timely interventions. Further, improved lifelong health outcomes and equitable education will align with SDG 4, enabling personalized educational planning and inclusive learning environments.

title: Usability of Video Conferencing Apps: A Study Using Measured and Self-Reported Data
authors: Harshita Sharma, Ritu Sibal, Pinaki Chakraborty
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: Video conferencing applications are critical tools across domains such as education, business, and healthcare. This study presents a comparative usability evaluation of five popular video conferencing platforms, viz. Zoom, Google Meet, Cisco Webex, Microsoft Teams, and Skype, based on data from 30 participants performing five identical tasks on each app in a controlled environment. Usability was assessed across four dimensions, viz. effectiveness, efficiency, user satisfaction, and perceived cognitive workload. Skype achieved the highest effectiveness <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(98.0 \%)$</tex> and efficiency <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(72.67 \%)$</tex>. Satisfaction, measured using the System Usability Scale (SUS), was highest for Microsoft Teams (92.5). Perceived cognitive workload, evaluated using Raw NASA-TLX scores, was the lowest for Microsoft Teams (5.83). The results provide actionable insights into the usability strengths and limitations of each platform and offer a benchmark for future research and development in video conferencing apps.

title: A Bio-Inspired Soft Ankle Exoskeleton: Self-Aligning Mechanism Design and Shank-Kinematics-Based Adaptive Control
authors: Xianhao Liang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechatronics, Automation and Intelligent Control (MAIC)
abstract: Achieving seamless human-robot synchronization and kinematic compatibility remains a critical challenge for soft ankle exoskeletons. To address this challenge, this paper proposes a bio-inspired cable-driven exoskeleton featuring a self-aligning end-effector and a hierarchical adaptive control strategy. Mechanically, a novel four-bar linkage mechanism with embedded elastic elements and universal bearing is designed to accommodate the ankle's floating instantaneous center of rotation, minimizing joint misalignment. In terms of control, the system employs a sensor-fusion architecture: the high-level controller derives a continuous gait phase variable from shank kinematics using only IMUs, while the low-level controller utilizes closed-loop force feedback to compensate for cable friction. Experimental results demonstrate high tracking fidelity with a Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) of 0.5 Nm. Furthermore, physiological validation confirms significant metabolic benefits, showing an average muscle activity reduction of 41.3 %, with a peak reduction of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 6 \%}$</tex>.

title: Protocol-Standardized Data with 2.5D Ensembling for Generalized White Matter Segmentation
authors: Yash Sharma, Utkarsh Tiwari, Anushka Jain, Nidhi Pandey
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: In Alzheimer's disease the segmentation of Cerebral White Matter (WM) frequently reaches a performance limit of 0.86 to 0.87 dice coefficient. We created a cleaned ADNI-CWM dataset using a Data-Centric cleaning approach to address the persistent problem of high variability of manual annotators. We used a clinical standard FreeSurfer 7.0; the dataset was created by isolating the structure or label IDs 2 and 41, resulting in 15.788 slices. We used a weighted ensemble of ConvNeXt V2, MaxViT, SwinV2, and ResNeXt50 to train a 2.5 D volumetric framework. On the internal dataset, the method reaches 0.9150. Without finetuning, it reaches 0.9049 on OASIS-1, this external validation is much better than many internal benchmarks. This showed that improved cross-domain generalization results from training on protocol-consistent data. The current CNN (ConvNeXt V2) challenges the dominance of Transformers that use attention as the primary technique. These findings showed that careful data curation is more important than complex architecture, offering a substitute for the six-hour FreeSurfer pipeline.

title: Benchmarking Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and Vision Transformers for Pneumonia Detection in Chest X-Rays Using Combined Public Datasets
authors: Seema Malik, Vatsal, Rohit Papnai, Arjun Negi, Nishtha Kansal
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Multimedia, Signal Processing and Communication Technologies (IMPACT)
abstract: Reported pneumonia-detection results on chest Xrays (CXRs) are often hard to compare due to heterogeneous public datasets and inconsistent evaluation protocols. We benchmark five ImageNet-pretrained backbones-ResNet-50, DenseNet121, EfficientNet-B0, ConvNeXt-Tiny, and ViT-B/16-on a leakageaudited, multi-source dataset (Combined_CXR) comprising approximately 17,000 radiographs from three public repositories. Data are split at the patient level into train/validation/test to prevent cross-split leakage. All models are trained with an identical pipeline to enable fair comparison under class imbalance. On the held-out test set (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$N=2,553$</tex>), all backbones achieve strong discrimination (area under the receiver operating characteristic (ROC) curve (AUC) <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>0.96$</tex>), with ConvNeXt-Tiny performing best (AUC <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.976$</tex>). DenseNet-121 and EfficientNet-B0 provide competitive accuracy with fewer parameters, while ViT-B/16 remains comparable under the current data scale. Calibration analysis indicates mild miscalibration across models, motivating post-hoc probability calibration. Overall, this work provides a standardized, leakageaware benchmark for reliable comparison of modern CXR classifiers.

title: FPGA-Based FIR Filter for 2D Convolution in Image Processing Applications: A Review
authors: Nitin Kumar, Anuraj V, Dhandapani Vaithiyanathan, Manish Verma, Baljit Kaur, Preeti Verma
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: This paper presents a hardware-efficient implementation of Image enhancement using a Kernel-based FIR filtering approach, targeting real-time applications through FPGA (Artix7 AC701 Evaluation Platform) deployment. FIR filters provide several advantages, such as linear-phase response, guaranteed stability, and non-recursive structure, making the hardware realization easier. The system was implemented and synthesized to evaluate parameters such as LUT usage, register count, DSP utilization, block RAM consumption, power usage, and propagation delay. The image processing module includes a dedicated 16-bit signed array multiplier, along with cascaded half and full adders, to efficiently perform kernel-based convolution. The designs achieved a delay of (10.27-11.08ns), moderate power consumption, and consistent resource usage across all techniques. The PSNR and MSSIM values are calculated by comparing the hardware-processed image with the software-processed image and show that the output is indistinguishable from the softwareprocessed image. So, no distortion or quality degradation happens for hardware implementation. The design supports grayscale images <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(256 \times 256)$</tex> with clear potential for expansion to color images. Future work will focus on extending the architecture to adaptive kernel computation using content-aware mechanisms. Overall, this research establishes the kernel-based FIR filter on an FPGA as a practical, efficient, and high-performance solution for real-time image enhancement.

title: Evaluating Analytical Workloads for Migrating from Relational to NoSQL Stores
authors: Himanshu Ranjan, Shelly Sachdeva
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: The traditional relational database faces many difficulties, such as consistency and integrity issues, high read and write costs, performance degradation with large datasets, and complex queries in managing the data of today's world. The root cause of this scenario is the explosion of diverse data due to the advancements in the Internet of Things (IoT), Mobile devices, Social Networking, and Web Technology Applications. These data not only introduce costly joins, which lead to an increase in response time, but also add the risk of data redundancy. Unalignment of schema design with the query patterns is also a significant issue due to incorrect schema mapping. To overcome these challenges, many industries migrate their data from relational database systems to NoSQL document stores, which is a complex task. Such migration can be achieved through schema transformation using the three approaches: Complete Referencing, Total Embedding, and Hybrid. This paper showcases an overview of these three approaches by providing a comprehensive comparison and analytical experiments considering the workload queries to lay out a detailed description of each approach to focus on their unique properties and use cases for schema migration from relational to NoSQL stores. The results show that the hybrid approach performs better in average query execution time. In the case of overall space consumption, the total embedding approach consumes 21% less space than the hybrid approach and 31% less space than the complete referencing approach in the 10 Megabyte dataset, and for the 100 Megabyte dataset, the total embedding takes 11% and 29% less space than the hybrid and complete referencing approaches. In contrast, complete referencing shows the least data duplication in terms of redundancy. However, the hybrid approach balances the issues of data updation, high space consumption, and scalability by embedding frequently accessed data and referencing large data to limit space consumption. Thus, summarizes the potential of each approach to provide valuable insights by evaluating analytical workloads for migrating from relational to NoSQL stores.

title: Characterization of Flashover Along the Enclosure Screen Due to Moisture Dripping Along the Screen Surface
authors: Bo Lu, Wenbin Zhao, Shuang Zou
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: This study investigates the evolution of oil-paper insulation discharge triggered by severe moisture in transformers. A test model simulating water dripping along enclosure screens was developed to analyze characteristic parameters during flashover development. The step-up method simulated the complete discharge lifecycle from local breakdown to penetrating flashover, with synchronized recording of optical signals and electrical parameters. Results demonstrate that the discharge process comprises four distinct phases: weak discharge at the gap, lowenergy discharge at the needle tip, strong arc discharge, and penetrating flashover. Significant parametric differences between stages were observed, establishing a multi-parameter detection framework for early deflagration warning.

title: Genetic Algorithm-Enhanced Transfer Learning for Accurate Monkeypox Classification
authors: Vandana, Chetna Sharma
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: Skin issues are a global health concern affecting millions of people and posing a significant risk to public health. Classification and predicting these diseases is a complex process that requires a thorough physical examination, medical history review, and laboratory testing. It is harder to forecast illnesses as they get more complicated. The monkeypox virus has caused major problems for public health experts. It is spread by direct contact with sick persons, animals, or contaminated objects. Researchers have proposed AI-based techniques for the early detection and classification of monkeypox, albeit there is need for improvement. Pre-trained deep learning methods sometimes require a lot of memory and processing power, making it difficult to recognize and categorize events accurately. This study suggests a novel approach for correctly classifying a range of skin conditions, including monkeypox, melasma, and chickenpox. This framework improves the recommended method and boosts detection accuracy by utilizing a Genetic Algorithm (GA) and an attention-based Convolutional Neural Network (CNN). It first highlights and gives weights to particular areas of a picture that are essential to the technique's assessment by using the attention process. An evolutionary algorithm is used to modify hyperparameters in order to improve the method's classification accuracy and resilience. There are 847 images of different skin problems in the collection. When compared to other evaluation criteria, the suggested method performs better. It attains an accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.985, \mathrm{F}$</tex> score of 0.985, precision of 0.985, and recall of 0.984. These findings demonstrate its efficacy in correctly categorizing various skin disorders, underscoring its promise as a dependable and affordable clinical tool.

title: Blockchain Technology to Combat Fake Products in the Luxury Market
authors: Nikhil Dehal, Abhishek Kumar, Sachin Ahuja
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: Counterfeit growth in the luxury market is a major challenge to such goods that affects the brand reputation, consumer trust and industry revenues. Traditionally used anti-counterfeiting (holograms, RFID, serial number, etc.) have little effect as they are fully replicable and replaceable. This blockchain technology solves the problem by the features of decentralization, immutability and the transparency of the technology, providing secure product authentication and traceability. Blockchain based ledgers, smart contracts, NFTs have the potential for luxury brands to provide a tamper proof digital record from product's origin to final ownership. From this have seen already observed in the ability to fight the counterfeiting, the major luxury brands have already adopted into blockchain solutions. The overall integration can be widespread, though while it must be dealt with the challenges of the implementation costs, regulatory concerns and the technological adoption. Given this, the objective of this research is to study blockchain contribution to abolishing counterfeit luxury goods by examining its benefits, demerits and also potential relevance on sustaining item credibility as well as trusting buyers in the deluxe market.

title: MSO-SRGAN: A Collaborative Multi-Strategy Optimization Network for Image Super-Resolution
authors: Fengtong Wang, Zuozheng Lian, Yunlong Li, Jin Wang, Zheng Wang
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: In view of the problem of poor texture details in images constructed by classic generative adversarial networks, this study aims to improve the quality of image reconstruction by proposing a new multi-strategy collaborative optimization image super-resolution algorithm (MSO-SRGAN). The generative network adopts a deep residual dense module that combines the channel attention mechanism and the spatial attention mechanism to improve the feature extraction ability and accelerate the model convergence speed through weight normalization;the discriminative network introduces the coordinate attention mechanism in the last layer and uses spectral normalization technology to stabilize the training process. In addition, the loss function is used to improve the image reconstruction accuracy, and the adaptive activation function (MetaAconC) is selected as the activation function, and the activation method is adaptively adjusted to improve the generalization ability. Experimental results show that compared with existing methods, the MSO-SRGAN model proposed in this paper improves the average peak signal-to-noise ratio by about 2.4333 dB on four test datasets, the average structural similarity by about 0.0598, and the average natural image quality estimator by about 3.1622. It can restore the texture details of the image more finely.

title: Explainable Ensemble Machine Learning Framework for Accurate Detection of Thyroid Disorders
authors: Md. Ittesaf Hossain, Nur Hossain, Md. Mustakim Hossain, Md. Romzan Alom, Kabiratun Ummi Oyshe, Md. Ahsan Habib, Muhammad Aminur Rahaman, A B M Shawkat Ali
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology and Engineering (i-COSTE)
abstract: Thyroid disorders are a major clinical challenge, so rapid and accurate detection is important for better patient outcomes and fewer complications. Traditional diagnostic approaches are based on manual interpretation of clinical and biochemical clues, and this approach may cause delays and inconsistencies. This demonstrates the need for efficient, automated detection systems. While some machine learning models have shown promise, many still have issues with accuracy and interpretability. In this paper, we introduce the Ensemble-based Thyroid Disorder Detection (ETDD) system, an explainable AI system consisting of a stacking classifier based on Random Forest, CatBoost, LightGBM and XGBoost to detect thyroid disorders from a medical dataset containing 9,172 samples. The system contains comprehensive preprocessing steps like dealing with missing values, encoding categorical variables, normalizing data, and feature engineering. The dataset is split into 80% for training and 20% for testing. To compare performance, we trained the ETDD model alongside several leading machine learning models. We also used explainable AI techniques like SHAP and LIME to make predictions more transparent. Our results show that ETDD outperforms all other models tested, with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 6. 2 4 \%}$</tex> accuracy and an AUC of 99.03%. This demonstrates its effectiveness, transparency, and potential as a clinical decision-support tool for diagnosing thyroid disorders.

title: Quantum Simulation of a New Multivariate Prediction Model SEIRDVIms to Assess the Impacts of Social Restriction and Vaccination on Spreading Disease Transmission and Mortality
authors: Subhasree Bhattacharjee, Kunal Das, Arindam Sadhu, Debrupa Pal, Poulomi Karmakar, Aniruddha Adhikary, Priyasha Sikdar, Peu Saha
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: This study deals with the challenge posed by the emergence of multiple versions of an infectious disease, each exhibiting different transmission and mortality characteristics. A new compartmental model, SEIRDVIms, is developed to examine the influence of vaccination levels and social restriction measures while considering two virus strains. The objective is to maximize hospital bed availability and minimize mortality, and this optimization problem is reformulated as an energy minimization task using a Binary Quadratic Model and solved on a D-Wave Quantum Annealer. The outputs demonstrate that quantum-assisted social restriction strategies lead to fewer infections and deaths compared to situations without restrictions. Specifically, over a 60 -days period, the model predicts 265 deaths under quantum-guided restrictions versus 383 without restrictions. The findings also show that stricter social restrictions consistently reduce infection levels, while lower partial immunity increases infection rates under fixed restriction conditions. Overall, this study highlights the efficiency of quantum-assisted societal restriction rules in decreasing disease spread and mortality.

title: Implementation of RAMP-CNN-Based Radar-Camera Fusion Framework for Enhanced Missile Detection
authors: Omkar K Kanetkar, Bhaskar S V, Sanjay S, Shrinivas R Madiwalar, Nikhil L K, Shraddha G, Appu Rathod
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Modern defense surveillance system has gained lot of demand as their accuracy and reliability in the detection has been found to be environmentally diverse. In this work we are presenting and integrated radar-camera fusion frame work which is designed by keeping in mind about the enhance missile detection performance that uses deep learning techniques. The proposed method will be combining multi perspective radar representations at the same time visual features are extracted from the camera data which will further improve the robustness this radio information will be processed by using three-dimensional Fast Fourier Transform (3D-FFT) in order to obtain range, velocity and angular characteristics. The image data is first preprocessed and then it will go through deep feature extraction these features will be fuse together using CNN architecture based on modified RAMP-CNN and YOLOv8 framework the experimental calculation is showing that the propose fusion based model will be achieving greater accuracy, improvise localization and it is considered to be a best and robustness compared to single sensor approaches the final results indicates that integrating these modalities will significantly enhances missile detection and the reliability will be very good in especially in complex operational scenarios.

title: Enhanced Strategy for Multi-Area Power Scheduling of Hybrid Renewable and Thermal Power Plants with a Pumped Storage Hydraulic Plant
authors: S. Kar, Deba Prasad Dash, Renu Sharma
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: This paper proposes an enhanced solution methodology for the multi-area economic power dispatch (MAEPD) problem by integrating thermal, wind, solar, and pumped-storage hydraulic (PSH) power plants using the Heat Transfer Search (HTS) algorithm. The proposed MAEPD formulation incorporates practical system constraints, including tie-line power transfer limits, valve point effects of thermal units, operational constraints of PSH units, and uncertainties associated with wind and solar power generation. The HTS algorithm, inspired by physical principles of heat conduction, convection, and radiation, is implemented to determine the optimal power dispatch across interconnected areas. The effectiveness of the proposed approach is validated on two different test cases. Comparative performance analysis with two wellknown optimization methods, Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) and Differential Evolution (DE) demonstrates that the HTS algorithm outperforms DE and PSO in terms of lower operating cost and faster convergence. The suggested HTS-based approach provides a robust and efficient framework for solving the MAEPD problem involving thermal, wind, solar, and PSH plant integration, taking into account the practical limits.

title: miRNA-Guided Enhancement of Epigenetic Drug Efficacy in Cancer Treatment: A Novel Computational Approach
authors: Monika Yadav, Asmita Das
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Epigenetic drugs (epi-drugs) offer promising results in cancer therapy but suffer from limited specificity and resistance. To enhance their efficacy, we developed a computational approach that combines FDA-approved epigdrugs with rationally designed anti-miRNAs and miRNA mimics. Gene targets of selected epi-drugs were analyzed for their role in miRNA regulation. A network-based screening identified 21 key miRNAs. Consensus-based anti-miRNAs and mimics were designed, validated via RNAhybrid (Minimum Free Energy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\leqslant$</tex> -10 kcal/mol), and structurally assessed using RNAfold. This dual-targeting strategy enhances epigenetic modulation and expands the applicability of existing epi-drugs to a broader range of cancer types. Our framework offers a scalable computational pathway for designing synergistic miRNA and epi-drug therapies in oncology.

title: Automating Wrestling Judgement with AI Vision: A Multi-Angle Deep Learning Approach
authors: Pranjal Pandit, Atharva Chinchole, Dhananjay Jagtap, Anuradha Yenkikar, Nilesh P. Sable, Amar Buchhade
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: This work introduces an AI vision system for automated wrestling judgment that integrates multi-angle video synchronization with deep learning models for detection, pose estimation, activity recognition, and temporal classification. The system includes detection, pose estimation, activity recognition, and temporal classification with spatio-temporal models such as 2D/3D video CNNs (MoviNet, SlowFast, I3D), Frame Wise CNNs, and sequence models (LSTM-transformer hybrids). The introduced system includes three major breakthroughs: (1) a dynamic multi-camera synchronization system to minimize occlusion with clearer motion, (2) an interpretability layer that helps the referees see the timelined explanation and step-by-step description of activity, and (3) a human-AI refereeing system that provides real-time scoring with the flexibility of overriding by explanation from human referees. The system is optimized for low latency to ensure realtime assistance during matches. The proposal integrates automated scoring with a human oversight mechanism that is envisioned to make wrestling refereeing more fair, consistent, and transparent with applicability to sports analytics.

title: Malware Cryptographic Security Architecture Attack Analysis Using Machine Learning
authors: Himanshu Sharma, Aman Kumar Singh, Aastha Sharma
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: Malware attacks are expanding dramatically, yet traditional malware detection fails to keep up with the rapidly increasing malware threats. This paper proposes a Malware Cryptographic Security Architecture Attack Analysis framework that integrates cryptographic hashing, fuzzy hashing, and machine learning-based behavioral analysis to provide a more reliable and accurate malware detection system. The framework combines fast signature-based identification with dynamic behavioral monitoring to improve detection of both known and unknown malware.
