The novelty of this work lies in the design of a hybrid, multi-stage malware detection architecture that integrates cryptographic hashing, fuzzy similarity analysis, and machine learning-based behavioral classification within a single operational pipeline. Unlike conventional approaches that rely either on signatures or deep learning, the proposed system dynamically applies cryptographic techniques for known malware and their variants while utilizing computational intensive machine learning only for previously unseen threats. This multistage detection strategy achieves both high accuracy and realtime operational efficiency. Cryptographic hashes are used to rapidly detect known malware, while fuzzy hashing enables the identification of polymorphic and modified variants. Suspicious behaviors such as abnormal network activity, file access, memory behavior and other indicators are analyzed using a Random Forest classifier. The proposed system reduces false positives and improves detection of zero-day and unseen malware. Experimental results demonstrate an overall detection accuracy of 96.8 % on a dataset including various malware families, showing that the proposed architecture provides an effective, efficient, and scalable solution for malware detection in modern cybersecurity environments.

title: AI-Enhanced Routing Protocols for FANETs: Recent Trends, Explainability, and Future Directions
authors: Mansi Dubey, Kuldeep Narayan Tripathi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical, Communication, and Computing Technologies (iCONECCT)
abstract: Flying Ad-hoc Networks (FANETs) capitalize on infrastructure-free aerial communication, forming the basis of cooperative aerial networks by numerous UAVs in a three-dimensional medium. These networks are imperative in particular domains such as disaster management, environmental monitoring and defence. Nevertheless, challenges of high mobility, dynamic topologies and short link lifetimes undermine traditional source routing protocols. The application of Artificial Intelligence (AI) methods such as Reinforcement Learning, Deep Q-Learning, Federated Learning and swarm intelligence improve scalability, reliability and energy efficiency to meet these challenges. The opaque and black-box nature of these models brings about issues in mission-critical domains, where accountability and trust are paramount. It is therefore of increasing significance to apply Explainable AI (XAI) methods such as SHAP, LIME, and rule-based models to enhance transparency and trust in these decision-making processes. In this survey, we examine the routing paradigms of FANETs driven by AI, provide a taxonomy of current protocols, and highlight current challenges such as scalability, energy issues, security, and real-world validation.

title: Predictive Analytics-Driven CI/CD Pipelines for Personalized Frontend Deployment in Multi-Tenant Web Application
authors: Ayush Shukla, Kaavyansh Jain, Oshin Sharma
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: The efficiency of multi-tenant architectures often comes at a cost: a uniform and generic user experience. While serving multiple tenants from a single shared system reduces infrastructure overhead, it overlooks the heterogeneous ways in which tenants interact with applications. This limitation is particularly evident in continuous integration and continuous deployment (CI/CD) pipelines, which are designed to deliver standardized code updates efficiently, but implicitly assume that all tenants share identical needs. In this work, we propose a novel approach to deployment pipelines that incorporates predictive intelligence. Specifically, we embed a forecasting model into the CI/CD workflow, enabling the system to analyze historical tenant usage patterns and anticipate future demands. The model predicts which features are likely to be used, the intensity of their use, and the corresponding resource requirements. We evaluate our prototype against conventional uniform deployments and observe notable improvements. Personalized frontends reduced load times, optimized cloud resource consumption, and yielded higher tenant satisfaction in user studies.

title: Heart Attack Prediction using Machine Learning and Ensemble Methods
authors: Kajal Chaudhary, Rashmi Bhardwaj
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: One of the severe diseases under the category of cardiovascular diseases is heart attack. Early diagnosis of this disease requires timely understanding of different signs and symptoms, along with physical examination of patients. Machine learning (ML) algorithms and ensemble methods have become most prominent approaches for creating predictive models in healthcare sector. This research work comprises various machine learning classifiers like Logistic Regression, Support Vector Machine, Naïve Bayes, Decision Tree; bagging ensemble classifiers like Extra Trees, Random Forest; and boosting ensemble classifiers like Light Gradient Boosting Machine, Extreme Gradient Boosting, Gradient Boosting. All these classifiers are trained with hyperparameter optimization and stacked using stacking ensemble method. The proposed Bagging Boosting Ensemble with Machine Learning (BBE-ML) classifier gives the highest ROC-AUC (Area under the receiver operating characteristic curve) score of 89.12% over other conventional classifiers.

title: Environmental Monitoring and Smart Automated Cleaning for Sustainable Dairy Farming
authors: Teena S, Lekshmi V Pillai, Pravin Kumar Sah, Lekshmi Vijayan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical, Communication, and Computing Technologies (iCONECCT)
abstract: India's economy significantly depends on livestock, with millions of farmers engaged in dairy farming and cattle management for income and sustainability. Despite its socioeconomic importance, dairy farming often faces challenges such as poor barn hygiene, excessive ammonia accumulation, inadequate ventilation, and heat stress, all of which adversely affect animal health and productivity. To address these issues, this paper proposes an IoT-based automated waste management and environmental monitoring system for modern dairy farms. The system employs multiple sensors to continuously track temperature, humidity, light intensity, and harmful gas concentrations within the barn. A ventilation fan and servo-controlled roof dynamically regulate airflow and lighting conditions based on sensor feedback, while a slanted floor, water sprayer, and conveyor mechanism automate waste removal and direct it to a biogas unit. The proposed design not only ensures a hygienic and safe environment for livestock but also promotes sustainable waste-to-energy conversion, reducing manual labor and enhancing farm efficiency. This scalable, energy-efficient approach demonstrates a step toward intelligent and eco-friendly dairy farm automation.

title: Power Modeling Framework for Euclidean Distance Algorithm using FPGAs
authors: Anu Chaudhary, Gaurav Verma
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: In the modern era of technology, euclidean distancemetric plays a vital role in many applications like in the field of clustering algorithm, machine learning, template matching and image processing due to its geometric consistency and simplicity. Due to the proliferation of edge computing devices, the high level computations need to be performed on edge devices like FPGAs. FPGAs are having numerous advantages over other edge computing platforms in terms of execution speed. However, due to its generic architecture, it suffers from high power consumption. The implementation of euclidean distance on FPGAs needs more careful power handling as it comprises multipliers, adders, subtractors and square-root units, which are power hungry in nature. Therefore, this work presents a power modeling framework for euclidean distance algorithm using floating point 7.0 IP (Intellectual Property Core) for ZYNQ (Zed Board) FPGA.The algorithm is implemented using IP modeling approach for different I/O configurations varying from 8x8 to 39x39. The parametric equations are generated in MATLAB byapplying non-linear regression and curve fitting technique on the synthesis data obtained from Vivado Tool. The power estimated from proposed power model is validated againstVivado Tool. The estimated error is varying from 0.5 to 2.0.

title: M-SEVIQ: A Multi-Band Stereo Event Visual-Inertial Quadruped-Based Dataset for Perception under Rapid Motion and Challenging Illumination
authors: Jingcheng Cao, Chaoran Xiong, Jianmin Song, Shang Yan, Jiachen Liu, Ling Pei
publish: 2025 International Ubiquitous Positioning, Indoor Navigation and Location-Based Services Conference (UPINLBS)
abstract: Agile locomotion in legged robots poses significant challenges for visual perception. Traditional frame-based cameras often fail in these scenarios for producing blurred images, particularly under low-light conditions. In contrast, event cameras capture changes in brightness asynchronously, offering low latency, high temporal resolution, and high dynamic range. These advantages make them suitable for robust perception during rapid motion and under challenging illumination. However, existing event camera datasets exhibit limitations in stereo configurations and multi-band sensing domains under various illumination conditions. To address this gap, we present M-SEVIQ, a multiband stereo event visual and inertial quadruped dataset collected using a Unitree Go2 equipped with stereo event cameras, a frame-based camera, an inertial measurement unit (IMU), and joint encoders. This dataset contains more than 30 real-world sequences captured across different velocity levels, illumination wavelengths, and lighting conditions. In addition, comprehensive calibration data, including intrinsic, extrinsic, and temporal alignments, are provided to facilitate accurate sensor fusion and benchmarking. Our M-SEVIQ can be used to support research in agile robot perception, sensor fusion, semantic segmentation and multi-modal vision in challenging environments.

title: Hybrid Collaborative-Noncollaborative Algorithm for Robust Target Localization in Wireless Networks
authors: Behzod Mukhiddinov, Di He, Wenxian Yu
publish: 2025 International Ubiquitous Positioning, Indoor Navigation and Location-Based Services Conference (UPINLBS)
abstract: This paper introduces a hybrid target localization algorithm for wireless networks that unifies collaborative and non-collaborative measurement strategies. Unlike conventional time-of-arrival (TOA) methods that are vulnerable to multipath effects and non-line-of-sight (NLOS) conditions, the proposed algorithm exploits a multi-level fusion of base station (gNB)–gNB, user equipment (UE)–UE, and gNB–UE collaborative measurements. A novel geometric reliability weighting scheme adaptively adjusts the contribution of each measurement type, while an iterative refinement process with outlier rejection further enhances robustness. Simulation results indicate that the proposed method achieves <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$20 - 30 \%$</tex> lower localization error compared to state-of-the-art baselines, with improved resilience in sparse anchor deployments and limited line-of-sight conditions.

title: An End-to-End Deep Learning Architecture of Automated Insulator Detection During Power Line Inspection
authors: Manisha Gojiya, Rimpal Gojiya, Vatsalkumar Patel, Nishant Kothari, Uvesh Sipai
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: One of the most important elements of the power grid is high-voltage insulators, which must be inspected with the help of reliable automated inspections. Nonetheless, aerial inspection using computer-vision is a very difficult task because aerial backgrounds are complicated, the environmental factors are very varied, and the insulators are considerably diverse in type and construction. The greatest weakness with most of the available inspection systems is that they do not effectively locate insulators precisely before defect analysis and this results in ineffective overall performance. To solve this underlying problem, this paper presents a two-stage solution with the main concentration on the first stage- accurate insulator detectionbeing the basis of any further defect analysis. A curated dataset of 8791 images, with a high level of diversity was constructed to be used in this study. The data set contains porcelain, glass, and composite insulators, which are recorded in a great variety of practical conditions, including thick vegetation and complicated transmission tower designs. YOLOv8 object detection model was trained on this dataset with 50 epochs on a workstation with NVIDIA RTX 2050 GPU. The trained model archives an average precision (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{m A P} \boldsymbol{@} \mathbf{0. 5}$</tex>) of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 9 4 6}$</tex> on a test set. The highest F1-score of 0.93 was achieved with confidence of 0.479, which represents a good balance between the precision and the recall. These findings indicate that the proposed YOLOv8-based detection system is a practical and a strong underlying system of constructing a complete automated power-line inspection system. (Abstract)

title: Impact of Cyber-Attacks on Frequency Profile of an AC Microgrid: LFC Improvement by T-III Fuzzy Approach
authors: Sabita Tripathy, Prakash Chandra Sahu, Sunil Kumar Bhatta, Buddhadeva Sahoo, Sarat Chandra Swain
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: The power system of today's electric networks largely incorporates micro-grids. Cyber-physical attacks and parameter uncertainties are directly affecting the microgrid's resilience and are becoming a major worry for load frequency regulation. This paper presents a resilience-based frequency regulation technique for a microgrid under different operational conditions, such as step and random load fluctuations and fluctuating wind speed patterns. When framing the problem, a cyber-attack model is also considered in order to fortify the system against external attacks. To protect against cyberattacks and uncertainty in system parameters, a strong type-III (T-III) fuzzy control method is employed. For this investigation, an intelligent Clean Fish Optimizer (CFO) technique with an emphasis on optimal controller design is advised. Furthermore, the proposed control techniques are validated using a real-time T-HIL test bench. As said, the proposed T-III fuzzy controller offers the most significant improvement in dynamic performance for frequency regulation. In comparison to fuzzy PID and PID controllers, the suggested T-III fuzzy control system dramatically reduces the settling time by 36.84 % and 168.42 %, respectively, according to experimental results from the areal frequency response.

title: Identification and Prediction of Traffic and Accident Based on ML and DL Approaches
authors: Sunil Kumar, Premananda Sahu, Rajesh, Mahesh Pal, Akash Rajak
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: This paper proposes an enhanced version ML and DL based algorithm for predicting, detecting, and instantaneous identification of traffic congestion and accidents, aiming to improve road safety and traffic efficiency. Historically, the exponential growth of vehicular traffic has posed substantial challenges, justifying the proactive need for data-driven solutions in traffic management. The framework combines heterogeneous data sources, e.g., historical traffic volumes, vehicle speeds, meteorological conditions, and temporal features, accident-severity data, and a high-resolution video frame to perform a variety of machine-learning tasks. The congestion-prediction component utilizes advanced ensemble regression models, specifically the LightGBM algorithm optimized with GridSearchCV. The test accuracy achieved was 97.48 %, and the root-mean-square error was 3.595. A Random Forest classifier is used in the accident severity prediction module with an accuracy of 97.58 %. Recognition of vehicles are achieved using YOLOv8 architecture with the DeepSORT tracking algorithm, that delivers high average-precision and recall scores. Accurate real-time accident detection shows 0.89, 0.90 and 0.89 F1-scores, respectively. An evaluation metric of system efficacy and detection quality demonstrates highlypromising improvements in the predictive and detection power of this system. The results determine the efficiency of the framework by the help of extremity of correct prediction of congestion, the localization of areas of accidental likelihood and the speed of correct response to incidents. The overall approach should contribute notably to the evolution of traffic management systems, accident prevention and road safety.

title: Adaptive Delay-Aware Super-Twisting Sliding Mode Control for Networked Microgrid System under Communication Delays
authors: Ramesh Chandra Khamari, Pravat Kumar Ray
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: Communication networks in networked microgrids introduce time-varying delays and data loss that degrade control performance and compromise system stability. Addressing this challenge, this paper investigates the effects of communication-induced delays in inverter-based microgrids and proposes an Adaptive Delay-Aware Super-Twisting Sliding Mode Controller (ADAST-SMC). The proposed controller explicitly accounts for stochastic delays through delay-aware state prediction and adaptive super-twisting gain adjustment, effectively compensating uncertainties while significantly reducing chattering. A practical inverter model is employed for state and delay estimation, with estimation mismatches treated as bounded disturbances to enhance robustness. The main objectives are to achieve robust voltage tracking, finite-time convergence, and improved resilience under communication impairments. Simulation results obtained in MATLAB/Simulink demonstrate superior tracking accuracy and faster convergence under varying delay conditions. The real-time feasibility and effectiveness of the proposed approach are further validated through hardware-in-the-loop experiments using the OPAL-RT OP-4510 platform.

title: Conventional versus Explainable Artificial Intelligence-Driven Threat Analysis in SIEM Systems: A Comparative Study
authors: Atul Kumar Chauhan, R.S. Jadon, Mir Shahnawaz Ahmad
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical, Communication, and Computing Technologies (iCONECCT)
abstract: Security Information and event management (SIEM) systems have evolved from rule-based threat detection to the implementation of advanced machine learning techniques. The continued development of Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) enhances transparency and clarity in the complicated processes of identifying the threat, analysis, despite conventional SIEM systems being optimized for detection accuracy and processing efficiency. In the context of SIEM systems, this paper offers a thorough comparison of explainable AI-driven approaches with traditional approaches. The study breaks down the methodological differences, assesses performance indicators, and identifies the advantages, limitations, and issues of the application of both approaches and, therefore, reveals the research gap, namely the lack of standardized measures to evaluate the explanation, adversarial robustness, and obstacles in operational integration. The paper concludes by highlighting some future research directions that emphasize the importance of human-AI synergies, explainable models, and enhanced XAI methods in enhancing cybersecurity activities.

title: Enhancing Writing and Voice-Based Communication with Automated Feedback Generation Using Genai
authors: Rakhi Wajgi, Krutika Umale, Omkar Umale, Sahil Kale, Anshul Bhute, Sushrut Wadhankar
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: Effective communication is essential for academic success, but traditional grading systems often suffer from delayed feedback, inconsistency, and high manual workload. To address these challenges, this paper introduces 'WriteEdge' a web-based platform that leverages Generative AI with optimized prompt engineering to provide personalized feedback on written communication. Preliminary results demonstrate a functional system integrated with Gemini 2.5 Flash to provide responsive feedback, dashboards, and AIgraded reports. This web-based platform includes modules with student and admin portals which have practice modules, and test assessment modules and personalized AI generated feedback reports. Future scope of WriteEdge includes customization for institutional rubrics, AI-bot for doubt resolution, and voice-based test assessments. Our work highlights the potential of GenAI with prompt-engineered modules to transform learning and grading communicationbased content both written and verbal which will reduce instructor workload and provide students with detailed insights to help them smoothly transition from academia to professional careers.

title: Energy-Efficient Farming Practices for a Sustainable Future
authors: Suwarna Shete, Pranjal Jog, R. K. Kumawat, Manish Shrivastava, Ritu Tak, Vaishali Sonwane
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: The global agricultural sector faces a critical imperative: to enhance productivity for a growing population while drastically reducing its energy consumption and environmental impact. This paper synthesizes findings from recent research to provide a comprehensive review of advanced energy-efficient farming practices. We analyze four pivotal domains: (1) AI-driven Precision Agriculture, which leverages machine learning and IoT for hyper-optimized input management; (2) On-Farm Energy Generation and Management, focusing on the synergy between renewable sources like agrivoltaics and smart microgrids; (3) Advanced Conservation and Biological Systems, exploring the energy benefits of no-till farming enhanced with microbial soil amendments; and (4) Intelligent Water Management, using sensor networks and predictive analytics for optimized irrigation. The paper examines the underlying technologies, quantifies potential energy savings based on recent studies (often exceeding <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$30-40 \%$</tex>), and discusses the economic viability and scalability of these systems. Furthermore, we address the significant barriers to adoption, including data integration challenges and high initial capital costs. Finally, we conclude by outlining a forward-looking perspective on the autonomous, energy-positive farm of the future and proposing policy frameworks to accelerate this critical transition.

title: A Pulsed Strong Light Disinfection and Sterilization Equipment
authors: Jianjun Liu, Haibing Li
publish: 2025 IEEE Sustainable Smart Lighting World Conference &amp; Expo (LS25)
abstract: Pulse light technology, with its advantages of speed and efficiency, holds great potential for application in sterilization and disinfection in fields such as healthcare and food. This article introduces the development of a pulse light disinfection device, which relies on a pulsed light module as core component and achieves efficient killing of pathogenic microorganisms.

title: Intelligent Multi–Objective Energy Management of PV-Battery-Integrated Smart Homes via CFGWO
authors: Nilesh Suresh Mahajan, Prashant J. Gaidahane
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: The research provides a rational cooperative foraging based grey wolf optimizer (CFGWO) which will optimize both user dissatisfaction and electricity cost of an intelligent home energy management system (HEMS), using cooperative foraging and benchmarked against other well established algorithms such as multi-objective particle swarm optimization (MOPSO) and artificial bee colony (ABC). A simulation study was conducted for a duration of 24 hours, that includes actual appliance models, rooftop solar photovoltaic (PV) system, and a lithium-ion battery. The results demonstrated the ability of the CFGWO to optimize solutions much faster than both MOPSO and ABC methods. Additionally, it provided better solutions, and the best tradeoff between energy cost and comfort. The results demonstrate cost savings of up to 10.5 % when comparing the results of the CFGWO method to the MOPSO method, and a cost savings of up to 18.9 % when comparing the results of the CFGWO method to the ABC method. This framework provides a practical solution for the development of a real time, multi-objective energy management strategy for residential applications.

title: Smart IoT Based Solar PV Fault Detection System
authors: Nabeel. S, M.L.N. Vital, Y. Vasanthi, M. Kusuma Priya Lahari, A. Govindu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: Solar energy is a leading renewable energy and a crucial alternative to reduce dependence on non-renewable energy. To maximize energy output, solar panels require easy maintenance and monitoring. IoT technology can significantly improve solar panel performance, monitoring, and maintenance. This paper proposes an IoT-based system architecture for remote monitoring of real-time solar panels, enabling early detection of malfunctions and increasing efficiency. This technology can ensure the long-term dependability and effectiveness of solar energy systems by providing IoT integration to optimize power generation.

title: Economically Homogenized Virtual-Impedance Droop Control for Parallel Multi-DC/DC Converters
authors: Zhijun Wang, Lipeng He, Changyu Bai, Guotao Shi, Chengwei Wu, Detao Xu
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: To address the challenge of voltage regulation and circulating current suppression in parallel DC/DC converters, this paper proposes an economically homogenized virtual impedance droop control strategy. The approach first determines the initial virtual impedance according to the principle of port current equivalence. Then, it unifies the device thermal aging cost caused by circulating current and the copper loss energy cost into a common economic dimension, forming a comprehensive cost function as the optimization objective. Based on this, the virtual impedance is adaptively tuned in real time, allowing variables with different physical meanings and magnitudes to be optimized within a unified economic framework. Equivalent models for twoand three-converter parallel systems are developed to derive the mechanisms of circulating current formation and analyze the influence of load and sampling deviations on power-sharing errors. The analytical optimal solution of the cost function and the practical feasible domain selection method are further presented. MATLAB/Simulink simulations of a three-converter system verify that the proposed method maintains bus voltage stability (deviation <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\leqslant 2 ~\mathrm{V}$</tex>) under load transients, significantly reduces the overall economic cost, and effectively suppresses both steady-state and dynamic circulating currents. Compared with conventional fixed-virtual-impedance droop control, the proposed approach achieves more balanced power sharing and superior life-cycle cost performance.

title: From Field to Model: An Explainable Swin Transformer Framework for Robusta Coffee Leaf Disease Diagnosis in Real World Settings
authors: Zeba Anwar, Sarfaraz Masood
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: This paper introduces an explainable image classification framework for real-world settings using the use case of Robusta coffee leaf dataset (RoCoLe), which comprises three categories: healthy, red_spider_mite, and rust. The framework employs a Swin-Base transformer as the foundational architecture. The training process is executed in two distinct stages: initially concentrating on head-only feature extraction, followed by extensive fine-tuning of the entire model. This was accomplished through the application of label smoothing, the AdamW optimizer, and CosineAnnealingLR scheduling. To enhance interpretability, high-resolution Grad-CAM overlays were generated from an advanced Swin stage, allowing the activation maps to accurately identify leaf lesions. The results indicate high classification accuracy and provide visually significant saliency maps that highlight the infected areas in real-world settings. The proposed model achieved an accuracy rate of 93.25%.

title: A Decentralized Framework for Verifying the Integrity of DNA and Toxicology Reports Using Blockchain
authors: Janani Rakshitha C., Hanumanthu Brahmini, S. Manimaran
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: In medicine and forensic science, integrity of digital evidence such as DNA match reports and toxicology reports is critical since any tampering can result in severe clinical and legal consequences. This paper presents a blockchain-based integrity verification system, providing traceability and tamper evidence of such sensitive reports. The system presented here, based on Ethereum smart contracts and SHA-256 hashing, provides real-time verification without disclosing the file contents, thus preserving confidentiality. This approach minimizes dependence on centralized authorities, offering a scalable and secure alternative to verify evidence integrity. Experimental validation confirms the system's effectiveness in terms of transaction cost as well as the feasibility of its real-world deployment. To ensure that sensitive reports are safely validated without jeopardizing patient or case confidentiality, this system can be integrated into forensic labs, healthcare facilities, and law enforcement organizations.

title: PhantomPulse: An AI-Based Vulnerability Scanner with Predictive CVSS Scoring using Ensemble Learning
authors: Arij Aissa, Mayssa Emmales, Farah Jemili, Ouajdi Korbaa
publish: 2025 IEEE Afro-Mediterranean Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AMCAI)
abstract: PhantomPulse is an advanced AI-based vulnerability scanner designed to automate both the real-time detection and the prediction of Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) scores for Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVEs) of software vulnerabilities. Utilizing a stacking ensemble of a deep neural network (DNN), LightGBM, and XGBoost, the system leverages natural language processing (NLP) with Term Frequency-Inverse Document Frequency (TF-IDF) vectorization, target encoding, and feature scaling to achieve a test Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of 0.3758 and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> of 0.9242 for CVSS score prediction. Integrated with Nmap for scanning and Streamlit for an intuitive web interface, PhantomPulse delivers actionable reports with description, details, severity levels and CVSS scores. The ensemble model achieves 93.2 % accuracy and 0.92 F1-score for severity classification, outperforming traditional tools like Nessus. This dual approach enhances cybersecurity efficiency by combining predictive modeling with practical scanning, addressing the escalating complexity of cyber threats. Opportunities exist for improving prediction accuracy and integrating the predictive model into PhantomPulse for dynamic scoring.

title: Ai-Driven Strategies for Reducing Carbon Footprint and Energy Use in Big Data Infrastructures: A Cross-Domain Review
authors: Emna Hosni, Abderrazek Jemai
publish: 2025 IEEE Afro-Mediterranean Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AMCAI)
abstract: In recent years, with increasing global concerns about the environment and the need to minimize carbon footprints, this objective has become a key priority for industries and scientific research. In this context, artificial intelligence (AI) is playing a very significant role in transforming how carbon emissions are monitored and reduced. This paper explores recent work that illustrates the potentially impactful role of artificial intelligence (AI) in carbon footprint management and intelligent energy efficiency approaches by reviewing its applications in high-impact domains such as healthcare, smart grid and supply chain, and critically considers the challenges raised by the massive data generated in these domains, which require Big Data infrastructures; therefore, it also analyzes the papers that have driven the optimization of these infrastructures using AI, which can reduce energy usage. Furthermore, the AI-driven strategies mentioned in our review are classified as Green-by AI approach a term recently introduced to describe intelligent solutions that contribute to environmental sustainability challenges (as described, e.g., in the UN's Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs)<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup><sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>https://sdgs.un.org/goals. The special feature of this systematic literature review is that it includes 47 recent articles between 2017 and 2025 using artificial intelligence as a lever to reduce carbon footprints and energy use in various domains. This paper also highlights AI's ability to improve the sustainability of Big Data infrastructures are responsible for managing and hosting the massive volumes of data generated by these fields. Finally, we present a discussion on challenges in AI-Driven sustainability and their future directions.

title: A Data-Driven Framework for Prioritizing Climatevulnerable Farmers Using Topsis and Clustering Algorithms
authors: John Rey A. Alipe, Ramil G. Lumauag
publish: 3rd 2025 IEEE International Conference on Agrosystem Engineering, Technology &amp; Applications (AGRETA)
abstract: Climate variability and extreme weather in the Philippines threaten agricultural productivity and rural livelihoods, compounded by inefficient targeting in farmer extension programs. This study introduces a comprehensive framework that employs Multi-Criteria Decision-Making (MCDM) through TOPSIS and Jenks Natural Breaks clustering to identify and classify climate-vulnerable farmers for resource allocation. A vulnerability assessment was conducted on 250 rice-farming households across 18 barangays in Banate, Iloilo, focusing on climate risk exposure, agricultural resource accessibility, and socioeconomic capacity. Clustering analysis utilized Jenks Natural Breaks, K-Means, and Hierarchical clustering, with validation metrics such as the Adjusted Rand Index, Silhouette Score, Davies-Bouldin Index, and CalinskiHarabasz Index. Results show full agreement between Jenks and K-Means (ARI <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=1.0000$</tex>) compared to moderate agreement in Hierarchical clustering (ARI <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.7466$</tex>). The combination of Jenks and K-Means yielded better clusters with distinct priority levels, while Hierarchical clustering provided more cohesive internal clusters. The framework effectively categorized farmers into high, medium, and low priority groupings, establishing a scalable and repeatable process for targeting climate adaptation services. This multi-dimensional assessment promotes resilient farming, food security, and supports national development and sustainability commitments.

title: Characterization of D-Shaped Fiber Optic for the Early Development of a Surface Plasmon Resonance (SPR)-Based Fiber Optic Biosensor for Early Salmonella Detection in Poultry
authors: Muhammad Fahmi Naqib Nashron, Salmah Karman, Wan Safwani Wan Kamarul Zaman, Rozalina Zakaria, Sharifah Norsyahindah Syed Nor, Sulaiman Wadi Harun
publish: 3rd 2025 IEEE International Conference on Agrosystem Engineering, Technology &amp; Applications (AGRETA)
abstract: This study presents the early-stage development of a surface plasmon resonance (SPR)-based fiber optic biosensor for rapid and sensitive detection of Salmonella in poultry and agricultural products. Single-mode optical fibers were polished to an optimal D-shape depth of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 6. 5 - 6 7. 5} \boldsymbol{\mu} \mathbf{m}$</tex> using a Dremel 3000 rotary tool with a bulk aluminium oxide grinding stone, achieving ideal attenuation loss of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.5-3.0 ~\text{dB}$</tex>. The most effective polishing parameters were <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{a} \mathbf{1 0 0 0} \boldsymbol{\mu} \mathbf{m} \mathbf{y}$</tex>-axis knob rotation and a speed setting of 4, ensuring consistent fiber bending and contact. This platform offers adaptability for broader pathogen monitoring in agricultural supply chains providing insights into advancing portable, cost-effective, and field-deployable biosensing technologies in innovative food safety solutions and public health protection.

title: Blockchain-Enabled Multi-Channel Structure Selection for Data-Driven Logistics Supply Chain Platforms
authors: Songbo Guo, Yuhong Zhao, Zhaohui Guo
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Logistics Engineering, Supply Chain and Digital Transformation (LESCDT)
abstract: With the rapid development of artificial intelligence, cloud computing, and data factor markets, logistics supply chain platforms are evolving from matching models toward data-driven intelligent decision-making systems. To characterize the decision-making logic and profit differences across different platform structures, this paper constructs a multi-channel game model encompassing intermediary, hybrid, and resale structures. By introducing processing externalities, competition intensity, and net externalities advantage indices, it analyzes the equilibrium mechanisms among platform processing capabilities, structural costs, and market competition. Based on Stackelberg game theory, equilibrium strategies and profit boundaries for each structure are derived. Combined with information system environments, the model's implementation pathways are proposed. Results indicate that the resale structure is optimal when processing capabilities are strong and costs are controllable; the intermediary structure is robust under high-cost scenarios; and the hybrid structure typically serves as a transitional form under most conditions.

title: Optic Disc and Cup Segmentation with Bilinear-ResUNet for Automated Glaucoma Screening Using Cup-to-Disc Ratio
authors: Mohamed Mouhafid, Yatong Zhou, El Moundher Aouiche, Yassine Jaouad, Poornima Lankani Perera, Abdelmounim Boudi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Recent Advances in Mathematics and Informatics (ICRAMI)
abstract: Glaucoma remains a primary cause of irreversible visual impairment, often progressing without symptoms during its initial stages, which complicates early detection. Accurate segmentation of the optic disc (OD) and optic cup (OC) in retinal fundus images is essential for calculating the cup-to-disc ratio (CDR), a widely recognized biomarker in glaucoma diagnosis. Manual segmentation methods are time-intensive, subjective, and prone to inconsistency. Additionally, existing automated approaches frequently encounter difficulties related to limited feature extraction, image quality variability, and artifacts generated by transposed convolutions. In this study, we implement and evaluate a ResNet-34-based U-Net with bilinear upsampling for OD/OC segmentation, demonstrating its advantages for glaucoma screening. Experimental evaluation indicates that the model achieves improved performance in OD and OC segmentation compared to several conventional architectures, based on Intersection over Union (IoU) and Dice similarity metrics. Furthermore, the model demonstrates reliable glaucoma detection (GD) using area-based CDR (ACDR), vertical CDR (VCDR), and horizontal CDR (HCDR), with respective area under the curve (AUC) values of 0.9139, 0.9147, and 0.9591. These results suggest that Bilinear-ResUNet provides an effective solution for automated GD.

title: From Grid Following to Grid Forming in PV Grid Tied Single Phase Inverter
authors: Mourad Zebboudj, Syphax Ihammouchen, Toufik Rekioua, Djamila Rekioua, Ali Chebabhi, Mohammed Said Ouahabi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy (AIESRE)
abstract: This research focuses on photovoltaic systems connected to single-phase inverters operating in two distinct modes: isolated operation using grid-forming inverters (GFMi) and grid-connected operation using grid-following inverters (GFLi). Given the increasing proportion of inverter-based energy sources in modern grids, GFLi systems can contribute to grid frequency stability under normal conditions. However, the primary aim of this study is to address challenges and failures that can occur in the electrical grid, such as disturbances, voltage sags, and distribution blackouts, by employing GFMi technology to supply loads independently during grid outages. The study was conducted using MATLAB/Simulink software, comparing the performance of damped and undamped LCL filters, with the damped configuration demonstrating superior performance. The results confirm the effectiveness of the GFMi system in supplying loads during disconnection from the electrical grid, achieving a THD of 2.63 % at the inverter output for islanded operation. For grid-connected GFLi operation, the damped LCL filter achieves a THD of 1.74 % for the current injected into the grid, meeting power quality standards.

title: The Design and Construction of a Drone-to-Drone Battery Swapping System
authors: Haoyuan Deng, Takyiu Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Intelligence, Security, and Artificial Intelligence (IntelliSecAI)
abstract: The paper explores a range extension concept for drones, in which one drone replaces the battery of another mid-mission. Previous research had explored portable battery-swapping stations and drone-to-drone battery charging, and this project extends the versatility further as the battery swap can be completed quickly and on any flat terrain, thereby combining the speed of battery swapping and the versatility of drone-to-drone charging into one system. Experimental results confirmed the viability of the concept for fast and versatile range extensions.

title: Using a New Hybrid Conjugate Gradient Method with Descent Property
authors: Amira Hamdi, Alaa Luqman Ibrahim
publish: 2025 International Conference on Recent Advances in Mathematics and Informatics (ICRAMI)
abstract: Conjugate gradient methods are a well-known group of methods for optimizing without any restrictions, and they work especially well for big problems. Recent research has focused a lot on conjugate gradient methods. This study presents a novel hybrid conjugate gradient method aimed at addressing the shortcomings of the Dai-Yuan, Conjugate Descent, and Fletcher-Reeves algorithms. The suggested hybrid conjugate gradient approach, together with the Wolfe line search, makes sure that each search direction has the descent property and shows that it converges globally. The numerical results further show that the approach works and is possible.

title: Development and Efficacy Evaluation of a Lotus Root-Inspired Surface Plasmon Resonance Sensor Using Photonic Crystal Fibers
authors: Assia Hamzaoui, Abdelaziz Aouiche, Soraya Gouder, Yazeed Alkhrijah
publish: 2025 International Conference on Recent Advances in Mathematics and Informatics (ICRAMI)
abstract: This work introduces a highly sensitive SPR sensor based on a lotus-root-inspired PCF structure. The optical response and sensing characteristics of the proposed sensor were analyzed numerically using the FEM. Two structural configurations were examined: a conventional lotus-root-inspired PCF and a modified design incorporating horizontally oriented elliptical air holes to enhance light-analyte interaction and plasmonic coupling. The sensing performance was evaluated using both wavelength interrogation and amplitude interrogation techniques. Simulation results demonstrate excellent sensing capability, with a maximum sensitivity of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4000 \text{nm} /$</tex> RIU at an analyte refractive index (RI) of 1.39. Furthermore, the sensor exhibits a high amplitude sensitivity of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$77.26 \text{RIU}^{-1}$</tex>, a figure of merit of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$39.285 \text{RIU}^{-1}$</tex>, and an RI resolution of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.5 \times 10^{-5} \text{RIU}$</tex>, indicating precise detection capability. To confirm the practical robustness and reliability of the design, parametric studies were carried out to analyze the influence of key structural parameters, including metal layer thickness and the separation between the fiber core and surrounding air holes.

title: Curriculum-Guided Ensemble Learning for Stage Classification of Liver Cirrhosis
authors: Rishabh Raj, Deepanshu Baghel, Partha Sarthi Bishnu, Vandana Bhattacharjee
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: For accurate classification of liver cirrhosis, which is vital for its early diagnosis and treatment, a novel approach for the classification of liver cirrhosis into different classes using structured clinical data, referred to as “Curriculum Learning,” has been developed. For this purpose, a dataset of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 5, 0 0 0}$</tex> patient records with 18 clinical features, which was used in this study, was curated, and there were no missing values in the dataset, which was balanced and consisted of three classes of liver health: healthy liver, fibrosis, and cirrhosis. In this study, two of the most popular “Curriculum Learning” approaches, referred to as “Easy-to-Hard” (ETH) and “Hard-to-Easy” (HTE), were used depending on the confidence level of the model, which was predicted by a base classifier and a logistic regression classifier. Several machine learning algorithms were evaluated, including Logistic Regression, SVM (linear, polynomial, RBF), KNN, Decision Tree, Random Forest, AdaBoost, Gradient Boosting, and LightGBM. Our results indicate that the curriculum-guided training significantly improves model performance, with LightGBM achieving up to 95.90% accuracy under ETH regimes. The curriculum-based models showed improved generalization as compared to conventional training, especially in under-represented classes. The proposed approach demonstrates the efficacy of curriculum learning and confidence-aware data structuring in improving clinical decision support systems for liver diseases. This is a generalizable approach and can be applied to other progressive disease classification tasks.

title: Load Frequency Control in Standalone Microgrid Integrating Electric Vehicles with State of Charge Control Using Marine Predator Algorithm
authors: Jasmine Hansda, Prakash Kumar Ray, Shiba Ranjan Paital
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: Standalone microgrids (SMGs) experiences frequency deviations primarily due to the intermittent nature of renewable energy sources (RES) such as wind power and solar power, combined with their inherently low inertia. Although energy storage systems (ESSs) can partially mitigate these deviations, their widespread deployment is limited by high costs and low power density, necessitating alternative solutions to balance power generation and demand. In recent years, electric vehicle (EV) batteries have attracted significant attention for frequency regulation applications. Accordingly, this work presents the incorporation of electric vehicles (EVs) through state-ofcharge (SoC) synchronization control, combined with Marine Predators Algorithm (MPA) tuned a Tilt Integral Derivative (TID) controller, to achieve effective load frequency controller (LFC) in the SMG. Through comparative analysis, integrating EVs with SMGs using MPA-tuned TID controllers exhibits superior performance in frequency regulation, showcasing reduced peak overshoot, undershoot, and setting time across diverse operational scenarios. Simulation data underscores the efficacy of this approach, highlighting its potential to enhance SMG stability and reliability.

title: Control of Parallel Inverters Based Three-Phase Microgrid with Multiple Solar and Battery Sources for Uninterrupted Electric Vehicle Charging
authors: Sarthak Mohanty, Shubhranshu Mohan Parida, Pravat Kumar Rout, Buddhadeva Sahoo, Konneh Boakai Zinnah, Indu Sekhar Samanta
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: This paper proposes a control strategy for a three-phase microgrid (MG) utilizing parallel inverters, multiple solar power conversion systems (SPCS), and a battery energy conversion system (BECS) to ensure uninterrupted electric vehicle (EV) charging. The proposed system integrates multiple voltage-source converters (VSCs) to operate in dual mode: grid-following and grid-forming, also maintaining optimal power quality (PQ) in compliance with IEEE-519 standards. A novel time-domain-based frequency decomposition method (DM) assisted by a recursive discrete Fourier transform (rDFT) tracker is used to accurately extract the fundamental component of currents drawn by loads connected to the MG at the respective point of common coupling (PCC). The system supports seamless transitions between gridconnected mode (GCM) and islanded mode (IM) while maintaining a stable DC bus voltage and ensuring reliable operation of the critical EV charging loads. Simulation results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed control approach, highlighting its capability to handle varying irradiation conditions, load variations, and grid disturbances without compromising EV load performance or grid stability.

title: Machine Learning-Based Early Detection Framework for Alzheimer's Disease
authors: Kushagra Singh Bhadauria, Ashmit Naik, Aryan Kumar, Shivam Tiwari, Kalicharan, Anurag Rai
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Alzheimer disease (AD) is a prevalent disorder that causes dementia in the world and a significant neurodegenerative issue. The possibility to diagnose it early and with accuracy (preferably in prodromal or preclinical stages) is essential to respond to it in time and achieve better patient outcomes. Even though existing statistical models have helped to comprehend disease progression, more significant and correct techniques of predicting the occurrence and progression of AD are possible with the latest development of machine learning (ML). The current paper targets the history and development of ML-based methods of early diagnosis that might prevent the formal clinical diagnosis of the high-risk individuals in advance. The suggested framework combines multimodal data, such as biomarkers, neuroimaging, and a detailed history of clinical history to improve predictive performance. It uses probabilistic modeling to enhance interpretability and solve diagnostic uncertainty. The experimental findings show that singlemodality models which are based on biomarkers and clinical variables work best when applied to individual data sets. In addition, the ML-based predictions are more effective in capturing trends of diseases in the long term compared to traditional predictive models. The study uses the multimodal synergy of data to show how AI models can reveal latent biological and clinical associations which would otherwise be challenging to identify. The probabilistic structure also gives estimates of uncertainty, which is in favour of making better informed choices.

title: Microgrid Frequency Profile Under EV Charging and Discharging Operation: Optimal FO-T2 Fuzzy Controller
authors: Jyoti Ranjan Padhi, Prakash Chandra Sahu, Sarat Chandra Swain, Sunil Kumar Bhatta, Buddhadeva Sahoo
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: This research study demonstrates the simulation of a solo AC microgrid in penetration with several distributed generation (DG) based power producing equipment including an electric vehicle (EV). The EV charging, load dynamics, and wind and solar power variability all have a negative effect on microgrid frequency. The microgrid must use a suitable control approach to maintain a steady frequency in such unfavourable conditions. For the suggested experiment, a dependable fractional ordered-type-2 fuzzy control strategy (FO-T2FCS) has been predicted to monitor microgrid frequency in different electrical disturbances. An important source of uncertainty in the inertia-less microgrid system is dealing with non-linearity. The FO-T2FCS controller was chosen because of its improved control action and strong supremacy over such uncertainty. This work has suggested using a golden eagle optimization (GEO) to build the FO-T2FCS controller optimally. The efficacy of the proposed FO-T2FCS controller is compared with that of the PID and standard type-1 fuzzy controllers in order to highlight its benefits. The novel GEO:FO-T2FCS strategy is employed for keeping steady frequency of the microgrid both in charging and discharging operation of the electric vehicle.

title: Z-Score Based Fault Detection and Location in Microgrids Using PMU-Assisted Positive Sequence Differential Currents Angle
authors: Chinmayee Biswal, Pravat Kumar Rout, Binod Kumar Sahu, Subhransu Ranjan Samantaray
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: The development of an effective protection scheme for microgrids is a challenging task, mainly due to the addition of distributed generations (DGs). Fault detection in microgrids is a major challenge due to their complex and dynamic operating conditions. This article presents a quick and robust protection method utilizing a statistical Z-score of differential current angle (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{Z}-\Delta \boldsymbol{I}$</tex>) for both fault detection and location. The proposed method works on synchronized voltage and current data extracted from phasor measurement units (PMUs) at multiple buses. By evaluating the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{Z}$</tex>-score of observed parameters at each bus, the bus with the maximum Z-score is recognized as the fault location. The proposed method demonstrates resilience across high-resistance fault (HRF), DG penetration, including fault resistance, fault type, and fault location. The results analysis has been performed using the MATLAB/SIMULINK platform to validate the proposed approach across a wide range of microgrid operating conditions, making it suitable for real-time microgrid protection.

title: Low-Light Outdoor Single View Heritage Images Enhancement Using MIRNet V1
authors: Sandhya M. Chitnis, Prachi Mukherji
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: Cultural heritage image documentation and preservation play a vital role for our future generations, tourism development, and storytelling. However, during image acquisition, inappropriate illumination conditions and practical limitations of photographic devices adversely affect image quality, often resulting in low-contrast heritage images. Natural light is especially important in outdoor spaces where many temple statues and monuments are located, and its absence or insufficiency further deteriorates the visual quality. Therefore, there is a need for curation of heritage images, where image-based curation algorithms are employed to recover low-light images without compromising their intricate details and cultural relevance. The curation algorithms are applied to synthetically simulated low-light heritage images of the benchmark Tanks and Temples dataset. Algorithms are also applied on database collected by the researcher through a mobile camera. In this work, the classical image-based algorithms applied - Histogram Equalization (HE) variants and Retinex-based approaches are compared with the deep learning-based model MIRNet v1. The deep learning MIRNet v1 is trained and tested on a combination of both datasets. The MIRNet v1 outperforms traditional algorithms in terms of SSIM by 0.9825 and PSNR by 32.44dB, resulting in improved quality of the processed images for human visual perception.

title: Beyond Traditional Methods: Harnessing Deep Learning to Revolutionize Weather Forecasting
authors: Sakshi Priya, Aditi Shukla, Sakshi, Rashmi Sharma, Maria Jamal
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: -weather has changed our lives in all the ways possible. From everyday decisions to long term planning it affects everything. As it is vary difficult to predict it becomes vary important to have prior knowledge about its pattern for safety and further scientific predictions. This research proposes Deep Learning(DL) methods for weather forecasting which uses models that can learn patterns and relationships from past long term data to make accurate predictions. This study focuses on techniques that can give optimal results even in the low resource environment. Various models like Convolutional Neural Networks(CNNs), Long Short-Term Memory(LSTM) and Transformer based architectures are studied, datasets like ERA5 reanalysis, Global Circulation Model(GCM) and observations from satellite like Integrated Multi-satellite Retrievals for Global Precipitation Measurement (IMERG). As traditional Numerical Weather Prediction(NWP) is computationally complex DL methods have shown much more optimal performance and high accuracy which makes the researchers lean towards data driven forecasting.

title: A Causal-XAI Framework for Retail Sales Forecasting: Addressing Transparency and Lack of Robustness in Deep Learning Models
authors: Somil Khade, Kavita R. Singh, K Karule
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: Accurate sales forecasting in retail is very useful when managing inventory, planning replenishment strategy, and helping the business make good decisions in a retail environment. Various methods including Deep Learning (DL) and Ensemble techniques, have improved accuracy of sales forecasting in general [1] however, they do not distinguish between correlation and causal inference. Moreover, they cannot provide information on the underlying reasoning or rationale about the problem. Hence, these methods will produce a sales forecast and not provide actionable recommendations that would improve Return on Investment (ROI) related to pricing strategies or promotion strategies [2], [3]. Proposed new Causal-XAI framework guarantees reliable, trustworthy explanations for its predictions by using Double Machine Learning (DML) to correctly identify the true causes and Causal Transformer (CT) to use those causes for accurate and automatically clear predictions [4]. To apply our proposed framework, we are going to use Corporación Favorita Grocery Sales Forecasting dataset from Kaggle and evaluate the performance using the NWRMSLE [5]. The proposed method achieves not only superior forecasting accuracy but also presents causal information that directly leads to business actions aimed at improving marketing outcomes.

title: A Rules-Based Intelligent Assistant for Personalized Student Learning and Support
authors: Ali Seridi, Yamina Bordjiba, Riad Bourbia
publish: 2025 International Conference on Recent Advances in Mathematics and Informatics (ICRAMI)
abstract: In online education, learners are often faced with feelings of isolation and disorientation, which can hinder their engagement and increase the risk of dropping out. This article presents the development of a rule-based intelligent assistant, designed to provide personalized and continuous support to learners. This system interacts dynamically with students, guiding them through educational content and adapting it to their specific needs and progress. Using a knowledge base enriched with data on learner behavior and performance, the assistant offers real-time feedback and recommendations. By employing a rule-based approach, the system delivers consistent, personalized assistance, significantly improving student engagement and reducing dropout rates. Furthermore, the automation of specific pedagogical tasks reduces educators' workload while maintaining high-quality personalized guidance for students.

title: AI-Based Chatbot for Managing Undergraduate Academic Inquiries in the University Sector
authors: Kiara Maryfe Guerrero Rodrigo, Gabriel Omar Taipe Quito, Jymmy Stuwart Dextre Alarcón
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: The timely resolution of academic inquiries represents a challenge in higher education, particularly in research-oriented courses where students face difficulties accessing key information about guidelines, deadlines, and rubrics. This situation leads to delays, demotivation, and overload in faculty communication channels. Therefore, an artificial intelligence-based academic chatbot is proposed to manage frequent inquiries from undergraduate students in the university sector. The proposal is structured into four phases: requirements analysis, conversational flow design, technological integration using tools such as React.js, Node.js, MongoDB, and the OpenAI Assistants API, and validation through student testing. The solution is expected to reduce response times, increase student satisfaction, and promote more equitable access to academic resources. Experimental results showed that the AcadIA chatbot achieved high levels of effectiveness and acceptance, with average ratings above 4.5/5 in ease of use, clarity, and relevance, and an autonomous resolution rate exceeding 80 %. In the pedagogical dimension, a positive impact was observed on students' autonomy and understanding, as they valued its usefulness in resolving doubts immediately and accessing official resources. Overall, the results confirm its potential as an academic support tool that enhances the learning experience and optimizes interaction between students and the institution.

title: Embedded AI: Integrating TinyML in Resource-Constrained IoT Devices
authors: Amel A. Triesh, Nuredin Ali Salem Ahmed
publish: 2025 IEEE International Multi-Conference on Smart Systems &amp; Green Process (IMC-SSGP)
abstract: This paper presents a targeted benchmarking study on model size reduction for deploying Tiny Machine Learning (TinyML) on resource-constrained Internet of Things (IoT) devices. Through systematic experiments on a convolutional neural network, we quantitatively evaluate the efficacy of post-training quantization and aggressive pruning. Our primary contribution is a novel size-based benchmark demonstrating that an aggressive pruning strategy applied to a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5. 4 ~ M B}$</tex> baseline model achieved a compressed size of 1.99 KB a 76.53% reduction. This result surpassed the outcomes of both Dynamic Range Quantization (2.02 KB) and Full Integer Quantization (2.58 KB), highlighting pruning's significant and often underutilized potential for minimizing the memory footprint of models destined for extreme-edge deployment. All code, models, and data for reproducing these size-reduction benchmarks are made publicly available on our GitHub repository. Furthermore, we provide a comparative analysis of leading TinyML frameworks-TensorFlow Lite Micro, Edge Impulse, and uTensor-focusing on their support for these optimization techniques and their usability. Our findings provide developers with clear, data-driven guidance on achieving maximum model compression for ARM CortexM microcontrollers and conclude with a discussion on future research directions.

title: Deductive Processing of Digital Signals and Its Practical Application
authors: Adeliya Yu. Burova
publish: 2026 28th International Conference on Digital Signal Processing and its Applications (DSPA)
abstract: This article discusses a possibility of recurrent converting and processing digital signals without algorithmic multiplication operations. The increasing amount of information that needs to be processed by electronic computing devices on unmanned aerial vehicles actualites digital signal processing without hardware-intensive multiplication operations. The objective of this research is defining a concept of digital signal deductive processing without such operations and possible areas of its potential applications. The study used the finite difference methods for digital signal processing. The concept for so processing is defined and described. It is determined that directed search of the permissible coefficients for deductive algorithms is acceptable. It is noted that computational algorithms of such processing by using recurrent algorithms can be used for vibration diagnostics of aircraft, automobile and liquid rocket engines.

title: Nano Cerium Oxide Co-Doped with Strontium Nanofillers in Zinc Oxide Nanostructures for Varistor Applications
authors: Parvathy Bhaskar, M. G. Veena, B. S. Madhukar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies in Electronics and Green Energy (ICETEG)
abstract: Electrical and electronic circuits make use of Zinc oxide (ZnO) -based varistors as protecting devices against overvoltage, which can range from a few to several millions of volts. Varistors with high breakdown voltages can be created by manipulating the microstructure by chemical synthesis and adding a small quantity of nano fillers like, rare earth or alkaline earth elements. In this work, the synthesis of nano cerium oxide (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CeO}_{2}$</tex>, 10 % by wt) doped ZnO (90 % by wt) and cerium oxide (6 % by wt) and strontium oxide (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{SrO}, 4 \%$</tex> by wt) co-doped with ZnO (90 % by wt) are obtained through eco-friendly solution combustion synthesis process. The prepared nanoparticles were subjected to X-Ray diffraction analysis, high resolution transmission electron microscopy with selected area electron diffraction, atomic force microscopy, field emission scanning electron microscopy with energy dispersive X-Ray spectroscopy to examine the structural, morphological and elemental analyses. X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy, was conducted for chemical analysis to measure the elemental composition as well as the chemical and electronic state of the atoms within the sample. The electrical characteristics was carried out using PE Loop Ferroelectric Test System. XRD analysis proved that the ZnO particles have hexagonal wurtzite structure and nano <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CeO}_{2}$</tex> has cubic structure, with excellent polycrystallinity and phase mixing. The XRD patterns and the TEM images confirmed that the average crystalline size of the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CeO}_{2}$</tex> added ZnO samples decreases from 42 nm to 17.9 nm. We showed that there is a strong correlation between the number of grains and the breakdown voltage. Pure zinc oxide has a breakdown voltage of 320 V; when nano fillers were added the breakdown voltage increases to 3958 V for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$90 / 10 \% \text{ZnO} / \text{CeO}_{2}$</tex> and 3854 V for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$90 / 6 / 4 \% \text{ZnO} / \text{CeO}_{2} / \text{SrO}$</tex>. These findings demonstrated that the particle size decreased with the addition of cerium and strontium oxides, increasing the number of grains and, consequently, the breakdown voltage. These findings provide new insight into the potential of rare earth/alkaline earth doped ZnO nanostructures as varistor materials for use in electrical and electronic devices.

title: Design and Implementation of a Dual-Stage Non-Contact Power Interface with Simplified X-Type Converter for Portable Devices
authors: K Saranya, Gowtham C, M Deepak, Harish Ragavendar P, Vasanth P
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies in Electronics and Green Energy (ICETEG)
abstract: This paper presents a compact and efficient wireless mobile charging system intended for mobile devices and embedded systems, enabling safe, contactless, and reliable power delivery. Conventional wireless charging solutions often encounter issues such as output instability, reduced efficiency under varying loads, and bulky circuit designs. To address these challenges, a high-frequency wireless power transmission (WPT) system is proposed, employing a single-frequency LCCS compensated network integrated with a simplified x-type DC-DC converter. The converter uses a single power switch and diode to regulate the output, reducing component complexity. The system is modelled and simulated using ANSYS, with analysis performed through MATLAB integration. Key performance metrics evaluated include output voltage stability, power transfer efficiency, magnetic coupling effectiveness, air gap tolerance, and load adaptability. Experimental results demonstrate a peak efficiency exceeding 80% across a 10 mm air gap, with a consistent power delivery of up to 10 W suitable for mobile charging applications. Hardware validation is carried out on a microcontroller-based platform, confirming the feasibility and practicality of the proposed WPT architecture.

title: When Modalities Agree Without Meeting: Empirical Consistency in Unpaired CAPTCHA Learning
authors: Michele Giuseppe Di Cesare, Sara Quattrocelli
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Conventional multimodal learning typically relies on paired and synchronized data to enforce alignment across sensory domains, an assumption that can limit scalability and data flexibility in practice. This work examines whether independently trained unimodal decoders, optimized under shared symbolic objectives and alphabets but without paired supervision, can nonetheless converge to compatible outputs. Two Convolutional Recurrent Neural Network models with Connectionist Temporal Classification loss were trained separately on the Text-and-Audio CAPTCHA dataset, which encodes identical alphanumeric strings through distorted images and synthetic speech, using disjoint training splits for each modality. Both models exhibited stable convergence without overfitting, achieving an accuracy of 0.985 with a character error rate (CER) of 0.0027 on images, and 0.800 accuracy with a CER of 0.062 on audio. A pooled performance summary across 10,000 test instances reports an average accuracy of 0.893 and CER of 0.032, reflecting aggregate task performance rather than explicit cross-modal alignment. While no direct alignment metrics are evaluated, the results indicate that independently optimized decoders can exhibit compatible symbolic mappings under shared decoding constraints. The study provides a reproducible baseline for analyzing empirical consistency in unpaired multimodal settings and serves as a controlled testbed for future investigations of alignment, complementarity, and privacy-compatible learning strategies.

title: AI-Based Framework for Real-Time Livestock Health Monitoring in Cloudless IoT Systems
authors: Ajna Shukkoor, Wesam Rohouma, Shady S. Refaat, Rachid Benlamri, Amith Khandakar Md. Abdullah, Mohamed Arselene Ayari, Rezaul Alam Reza, Fida Haq, Usama Mokashi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: This paper presents an AI-based framework for realtime livestock health monitoring implemented within a cloudless Internet of Things (IoT) architecture. The proposed system operates entirely on localized infrastructure and eliminates dependency on external cloud services, ensuring continuous functionality in connectivity-constrained agricultural environments. The proposed architecture integrates Apache Kafka for high-throughput data streaming, PostgreSQL for structured data storage, and modular machine learning (ML) inference services for real-time data analytics. Two machine learning models are used including a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) Autoencoder that performs behavioral anomaly detection and an Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) classifier for health condition prediction are deployed as Kafka consumers that predicts livestock health conditions from continuous sensor streams. The proposed architecture supports asynchronous, faulttolerant communication among system components, achieving low latency, scalability suitable for edge and on-premise deployments. The proposed framework enables seamlessly data acquisition, storage, analysis, and visualization through an interoperable analytics platform. It uniquely combining dual-model ML inference with a fully cloudless, streaming-based architecture. This design ensures adaptable, autonomous, privacy-preserving, and resilient livestock health management suitable for edge and on-premise environments.
